I can confidently say I have not researched on 80% of these dasha
systems. Perhaps in my future life I will be able to spend more
time. Just the mastering of one Dasha to 99.9% accuracy can take
an entire life time. I usually work with Chara Dasha, and Yogini
Dasha. These two dasha systems require their own book due to the
vast amount of information around them.

## Chart Study:

This is a chart of random person from my Facebook page. I asked
people to send me their birth information without telling me any
details, and I would do their chart studies in my book. Due to
privacy issues their birth details and names are not being disclosed.
As much as the birth information is not private information in a
general sense, it's an extremely private issue in the astrological
sense. It's like taking someone's personal journal without their
permission and showing it to the world.

## Example 1:

## BIRTH CHART

## MOON CHART

## NAVAMSA CHART

## DASHAMSHA CHART

We will begin the study of this chart with the first full MD of
Venus, which began from 1960-1980, when they were only 3 years
old. Venus is in the 9th house in Ashwini Nakshatra, conjunct
Mercury, Sun, and Ketu, while receiving the aspect of Jupiter.
Venus is also in an exchange yoga with Mars as they both have
exchanged each other signs. When the 9th Lord and 10th Lord
exchange signs it's known as a Dharma Karma Adipathi yoga.
Venus also rules the 3rd and 10th house in this horoscope, and
creates a Raj yoga with the Sun, as well as a Raj yoga by the
aspect of Jupiter, the 5th lord.

Venus is also less than 2 degrees apart from the Sun, being burnt. I
do not like using the term combust because it hasn't proven to me
by the sheer meaning of it, but being burnt means Venus is
extremely HOT, but can still perform it's duty.
The 20 year period of the native would not have any material
pleasure, but they began their spiritual journey from a very early
age. Venus would initiate the native in higher studies, learning
philosophy, devotion to the father and God, and traveling to long
distance places to learn higher dharma.
This is a day birth and that means Venus automatically becomes
strong. Venus would provide the native with passion towards
higher knowledge and understanding the discipline which comes
through gurukul.
Venus being in Ashwini Nakshatra, which is ruled by Ketu, with
Ketu sitting with Venus indicates that the native would have great
enthusiasm towards leaning about medical science, tarot, and
occult matters.
The divine grace of Jupiter upon the 9th house suggests that the
native walks on a dharmic and pure path of learning, even though
Venus is the lord of a trik bhava (the 3rd Lord), which the classics
say is a malefic house. Knowing this we can see that one will have
to put their own effort into attaining knowledge and that nothing
will come easy. Venus ruling the 3rd and 10th houses and sitting in
the 9th house shows that the agenda of the two houses will be
displayed in the 9th house. The native will have a distant
relationship from their nearby families, cousins, and siblings.
Venus being so close to the Sun shows that one may not enjoy the
company of younger siblings, maybe getting separated from them
very early on, or they may not have younger siblings at all. Rahu
in the 3rd house usually shows that the native is the youngest in
the family.

Venus naturally will make the native creative regardless of what
they study in their life. There will be a natural tendency to express
themselves creatively. They would naturally love to travel and
experience different cultures and ethnic backgrounds. The karaka
of the 9th house, Jupiter, is extremely well placed, and even
aspects the 9th house. This makes the foundation of Venus even
stronger.
Due to the extreme close conjunction with the Sun, the native will
feel overpowered by the demands of the father. The Father, Guru,
or fatherly figure would play the major role in the native's
development. Duty and discipline would be the first and foremost
law to learn, as Saturn also aspects the 9th house via Rashi aspect.
In this chart we see a yoga occurring known as “Virinchi Yoga”, a
yoga of a person who will eventually walk the path of spirituality.
This yoga occurs when both Jupiter and Saturn are in kendras and
the 5th lord is strong in the 5th, 1st, or 9th house. In her case,
Jupiter is the 5th lord in the ascendant, which is also a kendra
position. Saturn is in the 4th house in a kendra position as well.
Kendras are the 1st, 4th, 7th and 10th houses in Vedic Astrology.
The Dharma Karma Adipathi yoga between Mars and Venus
shows that one will take up religious or spiritual responsibilites as
part of their work.
We must also look at the dispositor of Venus to see how the
foundation of Venus is. Venus is in the sign of Aries, ruled by
Mars. Mars is in the 10th house, the most powerful house, where it
naturally feels exalted. This shows Venus's path would be aiming
towards career and an attaining an authoritative position in life.
Venus is conjunct Ketu, Mercury, and the Sun. This means the 1st,
2nd, 4th, and 11th houses will also be a focal point during this
period. Mercury holds the 2nd house of family, earnings, and

wealth; as well as the 11th house of gains, hopes, wishes, and
desires. The family of this native would travel to a distant place to
provide nourishment to the native in their early stages. Their desire
will be to learn higher knowledge and attain post a graduate
education. They will also make friends in distant places, especially
in religious, spiritual, or educational environments. Ketu co-ruling
the 4th house of happiness, mother, and the homeland shows there
will be separation from family and the things familiar to them.
Saturn sitting in the 4th house shows there was a lack of happiness
and nourishment from the home or mother, but the mother was a
highly spiritual person, due to the Moon being in the 5th house
with the aspect of Jupiter. There would be too much discipline and
rules that the native would have to follow within the home.
Venus is 5th from the Moon which again shows the 20 year period
will focus on education, creative self-expression, and learning
subjects where one can become a counselor, like a psychologist,
lawyer, or a healthcare consultant. There is a natural need for
romance from an early age. It's the hope of romance, love, and
creative self-expression that keeps the native going during this
period; as Venus (from the mind's perspective) becomes a great
creative savior.
The reason we can see great needs for romantic encounters from
an early age is because in the navamsa chart, Venus is in the 7th
house with the aspect of Jupiter (karaka of husband) and this is the
chart of a female.
With respect to career, the native wants to express themselves
creatively in an exotic and unique way as Venus is conjunct Rahu
in the D-10 chart of the career. Regardless of what the native is
doing the first 20 years of their lives, they would dream about
doing creative things.
Finally, in the D60 chart, known as the Shashtiamsha, which holds
the final say on any and everything, Venus is sitting in the 12th

house in Gemini… if the time of birth is accurate, because the D60
chart changes every 2 minutes. Venus here shows that for 20 years
the native will be all about creative imagination, communication,
and traveling to foreign places.
Venus is in the hora of the Sun, which shows Venus is more
receptive towards the father during the 20 year period. From
Venus's perspective (making Venus the ascendant) the 2nd, 5th,
7th, 9th and 12th houses become strong, and so the native doesn't
need to put too much effort in dealing with these houses. This is
another point we must know, the “Dasha Lagna". The Dasha
Lagna is when we make the current MD planet as the ascendant.
Wherever they are placed, take their sign as the ascendant and see
the chart from that perspective.
From Venus's perspective relationships and the need for them
becomes very important and the native does attract people for love
interests even at an early age, though they might not do anything.
Since Saturn is in the 8th from Venus, this shows one will have a
hard time expressing their emotions. One may carry plenty of
psychological baggage during this period on behalf of the mother
and home, since Saturn originally is sitting in the 4th house from
the ascendant.

## SUN MAHADASHA:

After Venus would be the Sun MD for 6 years from 1980-1986.
The Sun is in the 9th house with Venus, in the nakshatra of
Ashwini, which shows the energy of the Venus MD would
somewhat carry on in the Sun period, due to them being in the
same house. But this period is going to be all about ego
development and becoming authoritative. The native would shape
their personality after the father. They will be eager to learn about
self-development and becoming Sattwic towards their health; this
is because the Sun rules the ascendant, the 1st house, which is the
body, self, ego, self-esteem, and health.

The development of the ego will come with a price of hard
discipline, duty, and commitment to higher studies and religious
activities, as Saturn aspects the 9th house with it's rashi aspect. The
father would gain high honors and positions in society, but will
also suffer through ego battles with the spouse and the condition
within the home itself. The Sun is beautifully exalted so the native
will have an easier time adjusting to the life of learning and
attaining post-grad education.
From the Moon, the Sun is in the 5th house indicating strong
achievements in education and creative activities. One becomes
quite competitive with their class mates, and wants to be the best at
what they do.
In relationships of all kinds, the native will suffer from a scattered
ego and arguments due to self-pride. The 24th year may see the
native get married, but it has nothing to do with the Sun MD itself.
It's another technique of Bhrigu jyotish I am applying. But the Sun
conjunction with Rahu in the navamsa will crash the ego, or make
the native disillusioned about marriage.
Career wise, the native would shine and be honored in their field
as the Sun is conjunct Jupiter in the ascendant of the D10 chart.
They would've found great inspiration during this period towards
their professional goals and agendas. Being a Rajasic sign, it will
greatly help the native enjoy their position. Being born in the
daytime, the Sun automatically becomes strong.
Overall a period of great responsibility is upon the native and their
father, as well as dealing with foreign gurus and teachers as the
Sun is with Rahu and Saturn in the D60 chart. Due to the aspect of
Jupiter upon the 9th house, however, suggests their will be more
achievements than punishment.

The Sun is in the hora of the Sun itself, so it will overall bring a
good period for academic success. Making the Sun the ascendant
shows one will be prideful during the time of the Sun, and the
aspect of Saturn and Jupiter through Parashara and Rashi aspects
will maintain the Sun's sanity.

## MOON MAHADASHA:

The next period will be that of the Moon, from 1986-1996. The
Moon is in the 5th house in Mula nakshatra, receiving the aspect of
Jupiter, Mars, and Ketu. The 10 year period of the Moon will be
quite mixed. First due to being in the 5th house, there will be great
focus and enjoyment from children and in having children. There
will also be feelings of isolation and abandonment from the family
members, this is due to Saturn being 12th from the moon. The
subconscious mind will always be troubled by thoughts of home,
the mother, and dealing with issues of the mother.
Sitting in Mula nakshatra, the mind naturally feels isolated but will
always be attracted towards doing research based studies, as the
nakshatra lord is in the 9th house. Jupiter's aspect will always keep
the native hopeful of the future and inspired to raised children
along with pursuing higher education. But the 10 year period of the
Moon will keep the native occupied within the home, and putting
all their energy and courage into raising children, as Mars aspects
the Moon. This also gives the Moon the stubbornness to deal with
matters of children and education.
The relationship of the native would be okay in standings. The
Moon sitting with Venus in the navamsa will suck the energy of
Venus, making Venus dry like a desert. This is especially so since
the dispositor of Venus and Mercury in the Navamsa is in the 12th
house, with Ketu, in the karmic sign of Scorpio. Jupiter aspecting
the 7th house becomes a saving grace, where even feeling
abandoned in marriage, the native would still be able to carry on
the marriage.

In the career front, there will be great scientific research being
done, and an interest in higher philosophical learning becomes an
addendum to the native's profession.
Overall, the Moon will feel introverted, hiding emotions, taking
great interest in the occult and mystical studies, but being
emotionally drained. The focus of life will be two-fold, children
and studies, as from the Moon chart itself, Jupiter is in the 9th
house, and Ketu is in the 5th house. These are the Moon's two
dispositors. The native would act as a teacher and counselor to
people from all walks of life during this time period.

## MARS MAHADASHA:

The Mars period would run from 1996-2003 for this person, as
Mars MD runs for 7 years. This will be the time when the native
will put a lot into the workplace. They will be energetic,
competitive, and willing to take on any authoritative task
professionally. Mars receives the aspect of Saturn and Rahu via
rashi aspect. This is the period of Raj yoga status, as Mars, the
yogakaraka, single handedly creates a Raj yoga. In this chart
particularly, it also becomes part of a dharma karma adipathi yoga,
and creates a Raj yoga with Saturn. Although from Parashara this
is not the strongest of Yogas, as the best yoga for a Leo ascendant
is between the Sun, Mars, and Jupiter. Saturn does give a burst of
workaholic energy to Mars, but Mars keeps its composure, as
being in the rajasic sign of Taurus, Mars takes pleasure and
comfort in the workspace and doesn't feel the need to throw others
from their throne. Mars is at a medium temper but is willing to
work. The dispositor of Mars, Venus, is in the 9th house, which
shows Mars will be involved in higher education or working in a
university type of setting.

Mars is in its own nakshatra of Mirgshira, which is the searching
star. In this period Mars puts it's energy into searching for meaning
at work, and in researching in a scientific field. Sitting in it's own
nakshatra, Mars becomes strong and provides stability to the
native.
Mars is in the hora of the Sun due to its degree placement, but
Mars is in the 10th house which is the house controlled by the
Moon hora; so Mars gains strength but feels the need to work
during the night with such a placement. The native would feel
more energetic after sunset.
The native takes a center stage in their marriage and relationships
but this is due to the respect they get at work, since Mars is in the
10th house of the Navamsa. There would be an increase in
reputation for the person, and a lot of time spent at work, as
opposed to dealing with the task of marriage. Mars finds a great
deal of strength in controlling the lives of children and in bringing
transformation to them by making them competitive. This is due to
Mars being in the 5th house in Scorpio in the D60 chart. Mars
finds a great amount of joy in service and in creating professional
relationships, as it sits in the 6th house of the D10. This is the
placement where Mars destroys his enemies at work by working
with diplomacy.
During Mars's period, the major focus and attention will go
towards working behind the scenes in hospitals or laboratory
settings and places, as Ketu, Mercury, Venus, and the Sun go into
the 12th house. Marriage brings in a lot of responsibilities and a
lack of passion, as Saturn goes to the 7th house in Scorpio. There
is more attention being paid to building joint assets and finances,
then there is to passion.

## Study from the Moon:

From the Moon, Venus is in the 9th house as well, so it will give
the same results from her mind's perspective and from her
emotional self. The same goes for the Sun, Mercury, and Ketu
period. From the ascendant and the Moon chart, they are
controlling the same houses. The Moon MD would also be the
same since the Moon is in the ascendant during her time of birth.
From the Moon, Mars goes into the 6th house. Being the lord of
the 4th and 9th from the ascendant would give struggles with the
home life and higher education. Mars will prevail victoriously
though, as being a malefic in the 6th, he would destroy all
obstacles and enemies. But from the Moon, Mars controls the 5th
house and the 12th house. Lots of effort would be needed to raise
children during this period. Raising children would affect the
native's muscles, but children would be very competitive during
this period, regardless of their own dasha at work. The native will
also focus on their health through spiritual sourness and will be
dealing with healing others. Saturn looking at Mars from 12th
house will bring money through service and work. Lots of effort
would be required to maintain work. There would also be foreign
trips taken, or the native will feel a bit lost in regards to their
family.
From the Moon, Rahu is in the 11th house. Rahu would bring
about unusual friendships and connections with people, both
professionally and personally. The native's material desires would
also be accomplished during this period, as Rahu, the great
malefic, is in an upachaya house from the Moon. Co-ruling the 3rd
house from the Moon, Rahu will bring gains to and from siblings if
there are any.

## The Dasha Lagna:

If we take this native’s Mars and make it the ascendant and study
the chart from Mars, we will see naturally that Mars is on the head.
Ketu, Venus, Mercury, and the Sun go into the 12th from the

Moon. This period will show great energy being put behind the
scenes working in either hospitals, laboratories, or isolated places.
One will feel a loss of self due to work, since Taurus is the sign
from the Dasha lagna.
Saturn being the 7th from Mars shows a lot of discipline, structure,
and duty will be required to maintain marriage and deal with the
spouse. There could be a sudden attainment of property or a move
from the dwelling place, due to Jupiter being the 4th from Mars
and ruling the 8th and 11th houses. There will be overall optimism
within the home, which will balance out relations with the spouse.
Rahu as the dasha lagna will show great passion and love for the
spouse and a main focus will be in building financial assets with
the spouse. The Moon being 3rd from Rahu shows that the mind
will be focused on information, writing, and media. There will be
lots of short travels during the 18 year period, knowing that the
Moon is the karaka of travel, and the 3rd house is a house of travel.
There will be gains through service, jobs, and writing, as Jupiter
will be 11th from Rahu, ruling the 3rd and 6th house. The Family
will be on a budget and one will be implementing rules and laws
within the home, which will be followed with discipline as Saturn
goes to the 2nd from the house. There will be an attainment of
jewelry and gems, while gains will also come through chemicals,
medicine, and dealing with research.

## Antar Dashas:

Let's take some antar dashas from each MD I mentioned so that
you can understand how to study sub-periods.

## The Moon-Mars period from March 1987- October of 1987:

This period will have a tendency towards getting pregnant as Mars
rules the 9th house of children and aspects the Moon back in the
5th house. There could be a focus on work and research, but not

for long. The native will be quite agitated and aggressive towards
work conditions since Mars is directly influencing the mind. There
can be success at work but the main focus of the native would be
to have children.
This antar dasha between the Moon and Mars is natural, for this is
why it will give neutral results. In studying dashas, Mars and the
Moon become temporal enemies, but since they are good friends,
they stay neutral to each other. This concept is part of Vedic
Astrology, as any planet 3 places from the left and 3 places from
the right become temporary friends, and the rest are enemies.
When two best friends are near each other, (less than 3 houses
apart) then that major and sub-period brings great results. If
enemies become temporal friends, then they stay neutral to each
other. If two enemies become temporal enemies as well, then the
period can bring great challenges and struggles. There will be
issues related to the mother's health as well, since the 6th lord is in
the 4th house, looking at Mars, while sitting in Mars's house.

## The Moon-Rahu period from 10-21-1987 to 4-21-1989:

Rahu and the Moon are 3 houses apart, we know the Moon is a
bitter enemy of Rahu, but in this case they are temporal friends;
they both become neutral to each other. This period of Rahu and
the Moon will be all about doing creative and artistic things. Rahu
will make the native attain creative skills with their hands, as well
as get interested in media, movies, and art. The native will also
take trips to exotic places within the city or state limit.

## Moon-Jupiter from 4-21-1989 to 4-21-1990:

This is perhaps the best period of the native's life. There will be the
birth of children, and feelings of inspiration, wisdom, optimism,
and hopefulness. The native will be recognized in their field of
work and have great luck if children are born. The spouse's career
will also flourish. The native can also discover things at work
which others may not be able to find, as the 8th lord is in the 1st

house. Jupiter will help expose hidden enemies during this period,
but mainly it's a period of prosperity.

## Rahu-Saturn from 6-26-2008 to 5-2-2011:

During this period, the native will be forced to deal with
psychological issues related to the Mother. The Mother's health
will also be in question, as Saturn rules the 6th house. There will
be a battle fought within the home or relating to the mother.
Extreme amounts of competitive spirit will also be there at work,
and long hours of work may keep the native away from home. The
spouse may also get involved in the mess and fight related to the
native's mother or home. Interest in politics will surely be there.
There will be a pilgrimage taken during this time as well, since the
nakshatra lord of Saturn is in the 9th house.
I hope this one example has given an in-depth understanding of
how you can judge a mahadasha. The study of this concept cannot
be more simplified. If you know even the basics of astrology up to
the nakshatra then you can easily see a the current planetary time
period and see how it's operating in the native's life.

What are Conjunctions in Astrology?
Conjunction simply means union of planets. In any birth chart,
when two or more planets are sitting in the same house, they are
considered in conjunction. There are all types of conjunctions:
loose conjunctions, exact conjunctions, close conjunctions and
virtual conjunctions.
What does a conjunction really do in an astrological birth chart?
They give meaning to your life and set a purpose. They either take
away things or give you things. There are positive and negative
effects of conjunction. Positive effects are known as yogas and
negative effects are known as doshas.
Planets are simply energies, and when two different types of
energy come together, they create a new type of energy or a
mutant energy. The new type of energy brings a situation in your
life that fulfills the destiny of that conjunction.
How is a conjunction mathematically explained?
Each zodiac sign is about 30 degrees long or you can think of it as
30 feet long. In those 30 feet sections, you can cramp up to 9
planets. Since there are 12 zodiac signs which are 30 degrees long,
it equals the 360 degree sky around us (12 x 30).

## Image 1:

In Image 1, you see the zodiac sign of Aries which is 30 degrees
long. In that zodiac sign, you have two planets: Venus and Mars.
Those planets are standing in a certain position within the zodiac
sign of Aries. The way you can tell exactly which position they are
standing in is by looking at their degrees. This way, you can safely
say that Venus is in the sign of Aries at 10 degrees, while Mars is
in Aries at 15 degrees.
Planets have been moving through these invisible sections of the
sky for eons. The only reason why astrologers used zodiac
symbols was to map out the stars in the sky with ease. If all the
stars vanished from the sky today, we would still be able to detect
which portion of the sky a planet is moving through, or which
zodiac sign they are moving through. Just because the Aries
symbol is a ram, which was drawn by using the pointers of the
stars in the sky, doesn't mean that the definition of Aries will
vanish if that star pattern was to disappear tomorrow. Stars are not
important when it comes to astrology. The only thing that is
important is the measurement of the sky and where the planets are
traveling.

This is why it’s so important to learn the true meaning of
astrology. Most people believe that astrology only exists due to the
stars in the sky. They don't realize that stars are just a way to
separate those 30 degree sections we called the “zodiac belt.”

## Image 2:

In Image 2, Venus and Mars are in conjunction in the sign of
Aries. What does that mean?
Let's break it down.
Ariesaction, youth, impulsive, energetic, independent, active,
masculine, fiery nature, athletic, egoistic
Venuspleasant, beauty, feminine, sensual, balanced, relationship,
love, luxury, performing arts, wife, girlfriend, art, music, signing
Marssoldier, athlete, male friends, brother, anger, sports, energy,
driven, passion, sex
When you combine these 3 factors together, the conjunction would

## defined as:

"An impulsive lover who is very passionate in relationships yet is
egoistic."
For a man this can show: A love marriage that may be impulsive
rather than logical. He may have a very aggressive wife with a
fiery nature.
For a woman: A love marriage that may impulsive rather than
logical. She may be a very aggressive woman when it comes to
business, love, and self. She may be extremely active and athletic,
with little time to waste.
General translation for both sexes: An energetic artist who may
be a singer, actor, or painter. There is energy in their artistic
talent.
(Please note that example #3 is incomplete because you have to
know the house as well.)
Since we know that Venus and Mars are in conjunction in the
zodiac sign of Aries, we know their degrees, and we know what it
means. But now, it’s time to talk about houses.
Houses are fixed in an astrological birth chart, which I clearly
explain Astrology @ The Speed of Light.
There are 12 zodiac signs and 12 houses, and every 2.5 hours the
zodiac sign changes houses. At one time of the day you might have
Aries rising in the 1st House, and 6 hours later you might have
Gemini rising in the 1st House. By the time Gemini is rising in the
1st House, Aries would be in the 11th House. Houses are fixed like
the roman numerals on a clock, and act similar to when the needles
(planets) move through those numerals. Let’s use this analogy to
help us understand the movement of planets and zodiac signs
through houses.

## Image 3:

In Image 3, Mars is at 1 o’clock while Venus is at 6 o’clock. If we
used the numerals as symbols of the 12 Houses, Mars is in the 1st
House, while Venus is in the 6th House. The signs are also moving
like the hands on a clock. You can also say Venus is in the 6th
House in the sign of Virgo, while Mars is in the 1st House in
Aries. The numbers do not move, what moves are the hands of the

clock. Again, houses are fixed, while the planets and the zodiac
signs are in motion.

## Image 4:

This is the true image of the zodiac in a clock. There would be 12
sets of hands, like the 12 numbers. I drew half to demonstrate.

## Image 5:

In Image 5, the moving parts are the planets and zodiac signs. The
houses are fixed. Venus and Mars are in conjunction in the 1st
House in sign of Aries.

## Image 6:

Now we can interpret that Gemini is rising in the 1st House while
Mars is in the 2nd House in the sign of Cancer. Venus is in the 6th
House in the sign of Scorpio.

I hope this clearly demonstrates how houses are fixed, while signs
and planets move constantly. This is a very basic concept which
must be understood in order to move forward with this book.

## Types of Conjunction:

There are several types of conjunctions in astrology.

Exact conjunction

Close conjunction

Loose conjunction

Virtual conjunction

## Exact Conjunction:

What is exact conjunction?

## Image 7:

In Image 7, Mars and Venus are in conjunction in the sign of Aries
at the same exact point of the zodiac. They are both at 15 degrees.
This is known as exact conjunction. Anytime two or more planets
are at the exact same point in any zodiac sign, it is an exact
conjunction. This can happen at any degree. Mars and Venus could
be at 25 degrees Aries or 1 degree Aries, it does not matter. There
could even be 4 planets in the same sign at the exact same degree.

## Close conjunction:

What is close conjunction?

## Image 8:

In Image 8, Venus is at 13 degrees while Mars is at 15 degrees,
creating a 2 degree difference between them. This is known as
close conjunction. A close conjunction happens when 2 planets are
less than 5 degrees apart from each other on either side.

## Image 9:

In Image 9, you see Venus is at 17 degrees while Mars is at 15
degrees. Again, there is less than a 5 degree difference between
the two. A planet can be on either side of the other planet, but as
long as they are less than 5 degrees apart, they are in close
conjunction.

## Image 10:

In Image 10, Mars is at 12.1 degrees and Venus is at 17 degrees.
There is a 4.9 degree difference between the two creating another
close conjunction.
Loose conjunction
What is loose conjunction?
A loose conjunction happens when two planets are apart by 10
degrees or more. In a loose conjunction, the two planets will still
influence each other, but not as strongly as the exact and close
conjunctions.

## Image 11:

In Image 11, Mars and Venus are 15 degrees apart. This is
considered a loose conjunction, because even though they are in
the same house, they are not influencing each other significantly.

There still will be some impact, but it will be closer to a
conjunction influence of 25% versus 100%.
Conjunctions are like two planets living in the same house.
Sometimes they might be in the same room or in different rooms.
Sometimes one is entering the house while another is leaving.

## Image 12:

In Image 12, Mars is at 2 degrees Aries, meaning he just entered
the sign of Aries, while Venus is at 29 degrees, meaning she is
exiting Aries very soon and will be entering Taurus next. There is
a good 27 degree difference between the two planets. This is
technically a conjunction, but a very slight one. There is probably
a 10% conjunction influence. In this example, the two planets are

really not doing anything to each other because one is very young
and the other is very old.
In astrology, planets have life cycles as well. A planet will go
through the stage of infant, young, teen, adult, mature and elderly.
When a planet enters a zodiac sign, it's considered an infant if it is
under 1 degree. Any planet under 1 degree is powerless because
it’s a baby. It will take a long time for it to awaken and mature.
When planets are in young stages, they don't mature up until the
age of 28.

## Charging Army of Planets:

Before we move further, it is very important to understand the
concept of wars in conjunction, and who impacts who.
When you look at a conjunction, always look at the planet with
lowest degrees, as this will show which planet is coming from
behind with their charging army to conquer and impact the other
planet.
For example, Jupiter is at 15 degrees and Saturn at 13 degrees. If
they are in conjunction, how do we determine the impact one
planet has on another? First, look at the planet with lowest
degrees. In this case, it's Saturn. This means while Jupiter is
moving ahead, Saturn is coming from behind to influence Jupiter.
Jupiter is also aware of Saturn's presence. When this happens, the
optimism and preaching qualities of Jupiter will be more dark,
dull, and disciplined. Reality will be based on being more spiritual
and esoteric. This may be the case with someone who is a strict
teacher at school. He may administer the most difficult tests
because he wants his students to learn.
Now, let's flip the scenario and give Jupiter the lowest degrees and
Saturn the higher number of degrees. What will happen then?

In this case, Saturn's disciplined sense of judgment, work ethic,
and belief system will be far more loose and optimistic than the
reverse. This teacher would still be strict but not as intense, and he
would be more relaxed about testing and may discuss some parts
of the test before final exams. This individual would handle
responsibility in a far more spiritual and joyful way rather than
dull and sorrowful. There would still be the impact of Saturn's
nature in this person, since this is similar to twilight versus a bright
day. Saturn's mahadasha would make this individual take a more
serious approach to life than when they were in Jupiter's dasha.
What about the Sun? Don’t planets usually combust when they are
close to Sun? How does that impact the game or the army? Well,
this is where we have to be careful. No planet can charge the Sun
regardless of big and strong it may be. Charging towards sun is
simply suicide. However, the degree can still play a part in this.
Instead of being burned, a planet’s quality can be brightened due to
the Sun's influence. If a planet is coming from behind, then they
will get burned because they will have to eventually pass the Sun.
If a planet has passed the Sun, then it’s coming out of combustion,
and is receiving the positive side of the Sun.
If Venus is at 10 degrees and the Sun is at 15 degrees Leo, then
Venus is coming towards the Sun and getting burned in the
process. This means it's a combust Venus, and has lost all of its
positive qualities. This is why many people get divorced with the
Venus and Sun conjunction in their horoscope. If Venus was at 20
degrees Leo, and the Sun was at 15 degree Leo, Venus would
come out of combustion. It would move forward and become
cooler, but at the same time have its quality brightened by the
Sun's reflection behind it.

## Age of planet:


Infant

Young

Adult

Old
Infant: The infant stage of a planet is between 0:00 and 00:59:00
degree. Any planet that has less than 1 degree in your horoscope is
an infant. This planet is like a baby who sleeps all day. They tend
to act without direction, maturity, and sense. However, the infant
planets awake between the ages of 28-35.
If Mars is an infant in one’s horoscope, and there is a Taurus
ascendant, then Mars is ruling the 7th House of marriage and legal
partnerships. It also rules the 12th House of foreign lands, jail,
hospitals, hidden treasure, and isolated places. If Mars is sitting in
the sign of Cancer in the 3rd House, with less than a 1 degree
position in the Taurus ascendant horoscope, then it will do several

## things:

### 1. Delay the marriage
2. Make the native lazy regarding marriage and goals in life.
### 3. The native will wander like a lost soul when it comes to
siblings, higher learning, and dealing with enemies. They may end
up in jail due to their own stupidity or behavior.
### 4. They will also have trouble keep a steady job.
Why does this occur? First, Mars is in the 3rd House of siblings,
courage and effort. Secondly, Mars from the 3rd House aspects the
6th House of enemies, and the 9th House of long distance travel,
higher learning, and your teachers. Thirdly, Mars aspects the 10th
House of career. Anytime the native visits or moves to a distant
land, they will feel like a lost soul. Mars is an infant, and Mars
represents energy and will power. Since Mars is debilitated and is
less than 1 degree, it's like a double edged sword. Although the
native might have will power, it is used inappropriately. They have
an abrupt, uncanny anger, and a short fuse.

Now, let's create an example of the infancy of Mars and
conjunction. Let's assume Mars in the 3rd House of Taurus
ascendant and is less than 1 degree, but this time Venus is sitting in
the 3rd House at 3 degrees with Mars. This forms a close
conjunction between the two planets.

## Image 13:

The image above shows Mars and Venus in conjunction while
Mars is less than 1 degree.
What would this tell us about the conjunction? Remember, just
because a planet is an infant doesn't mean it's not working and not
influencing anything. It does have an effect, but the energy is
chaotic.

In this situation, Mars may not do much damage, but it will still
cause some harm. It would give immature tendencies to Venus,
and make Venus do things that are completely opposite to its
qualities, causing Venus to be lost. This simply means that a
person may engage in sexual activities with many unhealthy
people. They will fall for the wrong person, and have male or
female friends that have no direction in life. This is especially true
when it comes to male friends. They may create a lot of enemies
by hurting people in relationships, love, and friendships.
If a man has this conjunction, he will come across women who
were abused in their lives or abused others. Their marriage might
be full of physical violence up until the age of 35. The wife may
have an affair and could suddenly leave him since Venus in the 3rd
House usually shows a very unpredictable break up. This can also
produce drug addiction or sex addiction for a man. He might have
weak sperm cells since Venus represents sperm in a man's chart. In
conjunction with a debilitated Mars, it can damage the quality of
these cells. He can also fall into bad relationships based on deep
rooted emotions. Perhaps he was damaged emotionally from his
mother in childhood, and always picks the wrong women.
In a female's chart this would also show a similar pattern, but as
far as marriage is concerned, the husband would be seen from the
placement of Jupiter. However, his passion, energy, and sexual
behavior will be seen through Mars. This conjunction can clearly
indicate someone who might be abusive to women, or may have
problems with his sperm and might be too sexual for his own
good. People might think being too sexual means Mars has a lot of
energy, but that's not the case. Mars being weak by degree and
sign shows the energy is very young, impulsive, and defensive.
Since Mars is in an infant stage, its energy is not as strong Venus
when it's at 2-25 degrees Cancer. The example I gave above will
be far less impactful than if Mars was strong because then Mars
would completely dominate Venus. In the conjunction of infant

Mars versus young Venus, we still see impulsiveness in love and
relationships, but on a scale of 1-10 it would be a 3 or 4.
But what if Mars was at its mature or young state in the same
conjunction? What will happen then? It would show the same
picture, but with more intensity.
What is missing from this conjunction? There is a very important
piece missing from the true nature of this conjunction, and that is
nakshatra. Yes, nakshatras will also make a difference; not just
who Mars and Venus are, but what they will do in such a
conjunction. You may already know of them through my videos
and previous book. Here is a brief introduction of what nakshatras
really are.

## Nakshatras & Conjunctions:

In astronomy, there are points in the sky which make the Aries
goat, the Taurus bull, and other zodiac symbols. There are 27
additional constellations in the sky which Greeks forgot to note
down when they were copying the eastern astrology guide. These
27 constellations are again ruled by planets.
Nakshatras provide a deeper look into our mind, and our future.
The nakshatras give a detailed analysis on how this person’s life
will be in all aspects. Nakshatras, according to my studies, show a
much better outlook of a person than your 12 zodiac signs. You
may be a Scorpio Sun or a Gemini Moon, but your nakshatra at the
time of your birth is what will give you a 100% accuracy when it
comes to personality traits.
What determines a Nakshatra?
Nakshatra is determined by your Moon sign. The zodiac sign your
Moon occupies at a certain degree will tell you the nakshatra you
are ruled by.

Remember what I said about planets always moving in degrees.
They are never 100% in just one house. Their bodies will be
somewhat in the next house or the house before. For example, the
Moon is in 15 degrees Taurus, which means it is 50% in Taurus,
and 50% in Gemini which is the next sign. But, how do you
determine the nakshatra through this? Well, it’s simple.
30 degrees is the size of one zodiac sign, and contains 2.5
nakshatras. The numbers may differ due to yearly cycle. Each of
the nine planets in astrology control the 27 nakshatras, and each of
them have 2 rulers over a nakshatra, except the Sun and Moon,
which only occupy a single nakshatra.
I’ll give you an example of how you find a nakshatra.
The Moon from 10 degrees Taurus to 23 degrees Taurus, means
the Moon is in the nakshatra of Rohini (the Moon Goddess), and
when the Moon goes into the 24th degree Taurus, all the way to 6
degrees Gemini, it's in the nakshatra of Mrigashira (Deer's Head).

## Image 14:

In Image 14, you see the three nakshatras inside the zodiac sign of
Aries. The three nakshatras are Ashwini, Bharani and Krittiak.
Each one of these nakshatras have their own meaning, symbol,

deity, and planet that controls them. In reality, a single zodiac sign
is ruled by 4 planets: the lord of the zodiac sign and the 2.5
Nakshatra rulers (we say 2.5 because the 3rd nakshatra spills onto
the next zodiac sign).
The nakshatra of Bharani, where Mars and Venus are in
conjunction, is ruled by Venus. This is the reason why Venus
sometimes does good and bad things in Aries. You may think this
is a great position for Venus because she's in her own nakshatra,
but that's not the case all the time. We will discuss this in a bit as
to why.
Through the eye of Vedic astrology, we can now say that Mars and
Venus are in close conjunction in the nakshatra of Bharani.
What does this conjunction mean?
Ariesaction, youth, impulsive, energetic, independent, active,
masculine, fiery nature, athletic, egoistic
Venuspleasant, beauty, feminine, sensual, balanced, relationship,
love, luxury, performing arts, wife, girlfriend, art, music, signing
Marssoldier, athlete, male friends, brother, anger, sports, energy,
driven, passion, sex
Bharanipersonal transformation after much struggle, oppression
by others, feeling criticized by others; through which one finally
learns the lesson of life and death
With this kind of a close conjunction between Venus and Mars,
you can see a very passionate person who is active in love and
relationships. However they find themselves in sudden break ups,
abrupt down turns, and much struggle in relationships. Eventually,
through these experiences, they will transform themselves into a

person who will not look through the mirror of illusion but through
their very own soul.

## Loose conjunction and Nakshatras:

As you know, in loose conjunctions two planets are in the same
house but quite far apart from each other in terms of degrees. In
such a scenario, the two planets can have different effects as a
conjunction because of the nakshatra. Each zodiac sign has 2.5
nakshatras, and if two planets are sitting in two varying nakshatras,
the conjunction would operate quite differently.

## Image 15:

In Image 15, there is a close conjunction between Mars and
Venus. However, this time they are in different nakshatras. Mars
is in Ashwini nakshatra while Venus is in Bharani nakshatra. What
would this mean in terms of conjunction?
Ariesaction, youth, impulsive, energetic, independent, active,
masculine, fiery nature, athletic, egoistic

Venuspleasant, beauty, feminine, sensual, balanced, relationship,
love, luxury, performing arts, wife, girlfriend, art, music, signing
Marssoldier, athlete, male friends, brother, anger, sports, energy,
driven, passion, sex
Ashwinistubborn, working with speed, enthusiastic about life,
very impulsive, love of travel and exercise
Bharanipersonal transformation after much struggle, oppression
by others, feeling criticized by others; through which one finally
learns the lesson of life and death
In this type of a conjunction, the energy of quickness,
stubbornness, and adventure created by Mars will make Venus
transform in love and relationships.

## Virtual Conjunction:

Virtual Conjunctions are quite a hidden in astrology. Professional
astrologers know about it, but many never apply it. Virtual
conjunction simply means if two planets are in different houses
they are still considered in conjunction.
If Mars is in the 5th House and Venus is in the 6th House, and
Mars and Venus are less than 5 degrees apart, it would still be
considered a conjunction. Since they are not in the same house, we
cannot really say they form a true conjunction, but rather, they are
in virtual conjunction. The reason why I use the word “virtual” is
because in cosmology, “virtual particles” tell protons to act
positive, neutrons to act neutral, and electrons to act with a
negative charge. Virtual in this context means that something is
influencing something else, yet it cannot be measured or seen. It's
just there.

In astrology I use the same concept. If two planets are less than 5
degrees apart yet in different houses, they are still influencing each
other via invisible force or virtual force. If Mars is at 28 degrees in
the 5th House while Venus is at 2 degrees in the 6th House, they
are both less than 5 degrees apart in the sky. In this case, Mars will
still have some effect on Venus though Mars is at a very old age
and is virtually powerless.

## Young Stage of Planets:

Planets are considered young between 1-5 degrees. In this stage
they act like a teenager, with lots of energy, versatility, and
eagerness to learn. This is considered quite a good stage for
planets. Some of them, like the Moon and Mercury, both need
maturity and development in their own life to have a stable mind
and intelligence.
When two planets are in conjunction at a young stage, they both
act with a lot of ignorance like any young child. A child is ignorant
up until he or she is given the right guidance in life.
Now remember, planets have a positive effect in the young stage,
mature stage, and even in the old age stage sometimes, especially
planets like Jupiter, Saturn, and the Sun. They are mature and older
planets, and they come into their own between 22-27 degrees, but
after 27 they tend to become lazy and slow in productivity.

## Adult Planetary Stage:

The adult planetary stage happens between 6-25 degrees in any
sign. This is when planets are the strongest and give solid yet
grounded results based on their placement. But even these planets
can give negative results. Whatever the outcome, they'll be felt
stronger than at other stages. When these planets are natural, they
tend to think before acting. They like to analyze things before
making a rational decision. This theory can be applied to a serial

killer, drug dealer, or any other convict. Just because you have a
strong planet doesn't mean you will do well in life; it simply means
whatever steps you take, you will act with more maturity.
When two adult planets are in conjunction, they become powerful
and active. If Mars and Saturn are in conjunction in the 10th House
between 6-25 degrees, it shows someone who can become a
successful surgeon, engineer, weapons specialist, drug dealer,
pharmacist, or a murderer. Mars and Saturn conjunctions show a
war zone wherever they sit together. This can cause battles at work
and instability in one’s professional status. If the same conjunction
happened during the infant or young stage, it would not be as
strong as an adult degree conjunction.

## Old age of Planets:

Planets in conjunction in old age are between 26-30 degrees. They
become weak, but not powerless. They are very experienced yet
slow, and they take their time in doing things. An old man has the
most wisdom, and when he works or takes on a task, he completes
it; regardless of the time it takes. This phase requires strong
discipline and a solid work ethic. Natives tend to be more spiritual
if the planet has late degrees, or conjunctions occur at late degrees.
Think of a wizard from your favorite movie: long beard, long robe,
with a stick; a very old man who looks powerless yet is the wisest
of all and has hidden strength. The wizard knows about life and
how to approach it. Late degrees are not a bad thing. Planets in late
degrees tend to teach you lessons based on their house placement
and the signs they rule. They help you grow and become mature.
Jupiter and Saturn in conjunction at late degrees makes a person
grow faster than their colleagues. These people are disciplined
about knowledge and education. They do not like to waste time
with small talk; they want to share ideas about philosophy and the
reality of things.

The negative side of having planets in late degrees is that it makes
one impulsive about things concerning the aging process. The
planet has a last card it can draw; otherwise the opportunity will be
lost. These people rush into things knowing time is running out. If
Venus is in late degrees, this person will tend to jump into a
relationship and fail repeatedly, until their spiritual lesson is
learned. At the end, it all comes down to the lesson of the wise
wizard. The wizard was young at one point, and he made mistakes
and learned from them. In a way, this placement is like the infant
stage of a planet. The native can be ignorant, impulsive, and wants
to explore everything new.
The Moon is the only planet unaffected by these degrees because it
has the quickest orbital cycle. In 2 1/2 days the Moon changes
sign, making it difficult to assess any of the stages it goes through.
The south and north node of the Moon (Rahu & Ketu) are no
different. Even though they move backwards into a sign, they are
still considered strong or weak depending upon their degree. When
they move from 30 degrees to 26 degrees in any zodiac sign, they
are young. This is why the cusp of nodes are never considered
good. They are either childish or ignorant at the early stage, or
impulsive and ignorant at the old stage. Even if a successful public
figure has such planets, they will go through these ups and downs
of degree. Never look at a rich public figure and compare the
effects of their planetary alignments. It is impossible to determine
how happy or miserable they are, if the wealth they have is just
borrowed money, or if they have really earned their way.
If you look at the birth chart of Bill Gates, his Saturn is in the old
age stage. He is around 28.32 degrees Libra, and Venus is at 26.2
degree Libra. Bill Gates was born on October 28th, 1955 in Seattle,
Washington at 9:35pm. If you look at his ascendant, it is 28
degrees. This means between 25-30 degrees, any planet becomes
the most prominent point of that house. His Venus and Saturn
being around the 5 degree mark of the ascendant gave his Venus

and Saturn a big boost. Even his Mercury is at 23 degrees, again
within the 5 degree mark of his ascendant. Due to such late degree
factors, Bill Gates even got married late with a very mature
partner, Malinda Gates.
This is a unique and impactful way of looking at planets. By
analyzing the degree of your rising sign, and seeing if any planet is
at that degree in any house, one can determine whether it will act
in a positive or negative way. In Bill's case, he has a Gemini
ascendant where Mercury, Venus, and Saturn are not functional
malefic.
Planetary Elements and their Comfort Zone
Each planet in Vedic astrology has sign placements that are within
their comfort zone; where they feel at ease and not tense.
Saturn feels comfortable in earth and air signs because those are
friendly and familiar. They are ruled by his best friends Venus and
Mercury. However, if Saturn was to be in fire signs and water
signs, he would feel drained and burned. These signs are ruled by
the planets that are his enemy or have different point of views.

## The elements in astrology are as followed:

Fire
Air
Earth
Water
FireAries, Leo and Sagittarius
AirGemini, Libra and Aquarius
EarthTaurus, Virgo and Capricorn
WaterCancer, Scorpio and Pisces

The Sun does well in Fire and Water signs because those signs are
ruled by him and his close friends: Mars, Jupiter, and the Moon.
The Sun feels uncomfortable in Earth signs and some Air signs
like Taurus, Virgo, Capricorn and Libra.
When planets are in conjunction, they will either work together, or
one will be more hostile or more powerful than the other.
Each zodiac signs is ruled by a certain planet and certain element.

Saturn, Sun
Mars, Saturn
Jupiter, Mercury
Jupiter, Venus
The Moon is neutral towards everyone, or friendly.

Another thing to remember is the lordship for each ascendant. For
each rising sign or ascendant, a planet will rule a certain house and
become lord of a certain house. What you need to watch out for is
the 6th, 8th and 12th House. These are obstacle houses, or the
houses that go against our balance of mind.

Now that you know which planet rules which house for each
ascendant, here is an example of using such lordship of houses.
For Capricorn ascendant, Venus and the Sun were in conjunction
in the 4th House. The Sun, who is the functional malefic for
Capricorn ascendant, will overpower the yogakaraka planet Venus
due to its exaltation. The result of the conjunction will be dramatic
ups and downs in marriage, home, career, and child rearing. This is
due to the fact that the Sun rules the 8th House of sudden events,
ups and downs, and things that are hidden from us but can
suddenly erupt like a volcano.
If the same conjunction was occurring in the 10th House, then
Venus would have more power over the Sun due to the Sun being
debilitated while Venus is in its own mooltrikona sign. Here,
Venus will be able to overcome the sudden events of the Sun and it

will reduce their number. The person will become disciplined and
will fulfill their duty despite the turbulence in their life.
If you are new to astrology, the next chapter will discuss the
definition of functional malefic and functional benefic.
What are Functional Malefic and Functional Benefic?
What makes a certain planet produce good or bad results in one’s
horoscope? For this you must first learn about functional benefic
and functional malefic planets for every ascendant.
Each ascendant has a set of planets that actually become adverse
for the native even if they are benefic.
For example, let’s examine Aquarius ascendant. In this case, the
Moon and Mercury become functional malefic planets, meaning
even though they are naturally benefic, they will still cause
hardship if they are in conjunction or aspecting the benefic planets
in the horoscope. Anytime the Moon comes within 7 degrees of
any functional benefic planets for Aquarius ascendant, it will make
that particular planet weak, and reduce its own significance.
Let's find out what the FM and FB are of all ascendants.

Now that you know the functional malefic and benefic, you'll be
able to understand why certain planets become strong and weak
due to conjunction and aspect.

## Planetary friendship in conjunctions:

Another basic element to focus on when judging conjunctions is to
see whether the planets in the house are natural enemies or friends.
It's quite simple to determine friends and enemies in astrology.
Natural friends set 1: Sun, Mars, Jupiter and Moon
Natural friends set 2: Saturn, Mercury, Venus
Rahu and Ketu are not friendly to anyone since most planets
represent physical and material qualities. Ketu is about letting go
of those two qualities to attain spirituality. Rahu is the eclipse,
similar to Ketu, that overshadows all other planets.

## Natural enemies:

Saturn: Sun, Mars, Moon, Ketu
Mercury: Jupiter, Moon, Mars
Venus: Sun, Jupiter, Moon
Sun: Saturn, Venus, Rahu, Ketu
Jupiter: Mercury and Venus.
Mars: Saturn, Mercury
Moon: friendly to everyone except Rahu and Ketu
Neutral to each other: Saturn and Jupiter, Mars and Venus.
What is a virtual conjunction?
The definition of virtual is derived from cosmology. We are
referring to “virtual particles” that cannot be measured or seen, but
can affect the charges of electrons, neutrons, and protons. They tell
these particles how to behave: positive, negative, or neutral. In
astrology, we have virtual conjunctions. If you look up at the sky,
there is no line dividing one house from the next. The houses have
virtual boundaries, and astrological software tells us which sign a

planet is placed in. If two planets are within 5 degrees, and in two
different houses, they are still influencing each other.
For example, Saturn is in the 6th House at 28 degrees Virgo, while
the Sun is at 2 degrees in the 7th House in the sign of Libra. In an
astrological chart, these two planets are in two separate houses so
they shouldn't be affecting each other. However, if you look at the
same conjunction up in the sky, it will look like the Sun and Saturn
are in conjunction. This is why we have to consider the existence
of this conjunction, though it may not be real in astrological terms.
Virtually, it is still able to have an impact. How would you read
this conjunction with one planet in one sign and the other in a
different zodiac sign?
SaturnVirgo
SunLibra
When a planet comes in contact with the Sun, it will combust.
Saturn is hard working, disciplined, and structured, and is
becoming lazy, dull, and weak due to its close proximity to the
Sun. The Sun itself is debilitated in the 7th House of marriage.
This can indicate a lazy spouse who might not take their
responsibilities and duties seriously.
The Saturn and Sun
conjunction represents duty, discipline, and order within a person.
Since Saturn is approaching the Sun and not passing the Sun, it's
losing those qualities represented by Saturn.
Because Saturn is in the 6th House of obstacles, enemies,
opposition, litigation, and diseases, one might lose in a lawsuit, be
defeated by enemies, or could face problems with their joints,
knees, and teeth.
You can now see how two planets in two separate houses are being
affected by each other in this world of virtual conjunction.

## Retrograde planetary conjunctions:

Many people ask me about the effects of retrograde planets in their
horoscope and how they impact their lives. First, retrograde
planets bring in what is known as a strong atmabala, which shows
that the soul is here to learn its lesson more intensively than others.
If it's a good lesson, it will receive all that it can and more. If it's a
bad lesson, the soul will keep repeating its mistakes until it has
learned from them. When a planet is retrograde, that means it is
closer to earth. The planet creates heavy magnetic waves, which is
why people born during this time will feel the intensive effect of
that planet, especially during its mahadasha. If Mars is retrograde,
it shows that one needs to be very careful when taking action
because whatever they decide to do will have an intensified effect
upon them. The person must look within to determine if the action
they are taking is right or wrong; meaningful, or meaningless.
Jupiter retrograde can show intensified spiritual awareness, which
can either damage a person's material life and take them on a
spiritual path, or can increase material wealth. This all depends on
the house placement of the retrograde planets. Venus retrograde in
a birth horoscope means that your relationships with others are
going to be karmic lessons to learn. Relationships will be
intensified by Venus. It may also manifest by becoming an
extremely creative artist who might be ahead of their time.
How much of an effect does a retrograde planet have on a
conjunction with other planets?
When retrograde planets are in conjunction with other planets, all
that happens is that their influence upon the other planet is
intensified. If Venus is in conjunction with the Moon while it is
retrograde, then the desire for relationships, romance, and
creativity will be stronger than in other conjunctions.

If Saturn is retrograde in your horoscope, then Saturn's qualities
will either be overwhelming, or you will feel lack of motivation
depending upon the house and sign. Either you will be extremely
disciplined, structured, and orderly in your approach to work, or
you will feel lazy, unmotivated, and become unsocial. Saturn
either will delay things for you more intensely or bring them to
you quicker than it would normally. Retrograde Saturn in
conjunction with Mercury can either make you super disciplined
about studies and extremely careful and serious in communication,
or it might give you a speech impediment disorder where words
hiccup out of you. Also, you may be extremely shy and reserved in
your communication versus a normal Saturn and Mercury conjunct
person.
The house positions are very important to evaluate, and the house
the retrograde planet rules is significant. If it rules the 6th, 8th or
12th House, then the result can be more problematic.

## Hora Chart and Conjunctions:

Let's talk about a unique aspect of conjunctions. With the method
of Hora spacing, we can quickly determine if the conjunction will
be beneficial, problematic, or neutral for the person.
Hora means hour, and is quite an important part of Vedic
astrology. When you get your full horoscope by an astrologer,
you'll see something called a Hora Chart. This chart determines
which areas of your life need work, and which areas of your life
give you the best result without asking.
All people are either born in the Hora of the Sun or Hora of the
Moon. You need to determine which Hora you were born into.
Hora of the Sun is about daytime, and Hora of the moon is about

night time. Planets that are strong in Hora of the Sun are the Sun,
Venus, and Jupiter. Planets that are strong in Hora of the Moon are
the Moon, Mars, and Saturn. Mercury is strong depending upon
the time you were born. If the birth took place during sunrise or
sunset, then Mercury will be strong for both Horas, otherwise it's
usually neutral.
To determine your Hora, look at your ascendant or 1st House, and
look at the degrees of your rising sign. All horoscopes show the
degree table of your ascendant and planets. People are born
between 0-30 degrees; nothing below and nothing above. The
Horas are divided into 2 parts: the first 15 degrees and the second
15 degrees. People born in the first 15 degrees of the rising sign
will be part of Hora 1, while people born in the second half of the
degrees (from 16-30), will be part of Hora 2.
How do you find determine if you are in the Hora of the Sun or
Moon? If you are born in even rising signs like Taurus, Cancer,
Virgo, Scorpio, Capricorn, and Pisces, then your first 15 degrees
will be ruled by the Moon. This means that the Moon, Mars and
Saturn are strong for you. The original houses they rule, meaning
for all the even rising signs the 1st, 4th, 8th, 10th, and 11th House
will be strong since they are originally ruled by Mars, Moon and
Saturn.
If you were born in odd rising signs like Aries, Gemini, Leo, Libra,
Sagittarius, and Aquarius, and if you were born in the first 15
degrees of the rising sign, you are ruled by the Hora of the Sun. If
you were born in the second half of the degrees, then you were
born in the Hora of the Moon.

## Example 1:

If an Aries ascendant native was born in the Hora of the Moon,
they would run into problems related to planets which are
controlled by the Hora of the Sun like the 2nd, 5th, 7th, 9th and

12th House which are ruled by Sun, Venus, and Jupiter. One can
judge the 3rd and 6th House by seeing the time of birth, and if the
native was born during sunrise or sunset. Otherwise, these houses
will be neutral for the native.

## Example 2:

If the Aries ascendant native was born in the first half of the
degrees, then they were born in the Hora of the Sun. The problem
houses would be ruled by the Hora of the Moon like the 1st, 4th,
8th, 10th, and 11th House, which are houses ruled by the Moon,
Mars and Saturn.

## Example 3:

If you are born under the Aries ascendant and Moon Hora, then the
2nd, 5th, 7th, 9th and 12th House become a source of struggle and
problems, while the 1st, 4th, 8th, 10th, and 11th House are a source
of help. If Jupiter, the original lord of 9th and 12th House, is sitting
in the 4th House, it helps the 9th and 12th House, and is less
problematic because the 4th house is good for Hora of the Moon.
If this was Hora of the Sun, and Jupiter was in the 4th house, then
problems would arise.
If you are born at night while in the Hora of the Moon, then it is
icing on a cake because throughout your life you'll have support of
your mother, in-laws, bosses, and social network. This also applies
to being born in the day time while in the Hora of the Sun. There
will be support from family, ability to save money, have children,
and gain a supportive spouse. There will also be support from
one’s father and teachers.
Rahu and Ketu give results according to the same concept. For the
Moon Hora, houses 1, 4, 7, 8, 10 and 11 are good. Houses 3 and 6
depend upon Mercury. The same goes for the Sun Hora. This is

why half the population will get good results of Rahu in 5th House
while other half will get bad results.
Image A depicts the Sun Hora. The black houses are the ones that
are supported by Hora of the Sun. The green or lighter shade
houses are the ones that only show results per Mercury's situation.
Image A
Image B below shows the houses that are supported by the Hora of

## the Moon:

Image B

## Moon Hora and Conjunction Example:

Image C

In Image C, you can see Saturn and Mars sitting in the 7th House
in the Moon Hora. Saturn and Mars create a war zone whenever
they sit. However, sometimes they might not shed blood, but just
have a cold war between them. When they are sitting in a house
not supported by the Hora, war can occur and marriage can break.
This can show someone who might gain a mature, older,
dominating, strict, disciplined, and organized spouse. Since they
are overbearing, the marriage may not work out.

## Sun Hora and Conjunction Example:

Image D
In Image D you have the same conjunction, but now Saturn and
Mars are in a house supported by the Hora. This shows the
individual may gain an older, very dominating spouse who might

be strict, disciplined, and organized, however the marriage could
work out.
The Hora is only a small part of the entire picture. Knowing the
Hora will quickly tell you which houses the native will need to
work on the most. Even though this conjunction holds the same
meaning in both Horas, one marriage might make it in one Hora,
while it may not last in the other Hora.
Image E
In Image E you can see that even though Saturn and Mars are
trying to end the marriage, there is still a saving grace from Jupiter
in the 11th House. Though Jupiter is in an unsupported house for
the Hora, it's still giving his original significance upon the 7th

House. In this case, the marriage may not become a war zone, but
a neutral or average marriage.
This is why it’s very important to look at all aspects of reading a
chart .To judge marriage, one will need to see the condition of the
7th House, 7th lord, planets in 7th House, aspect and conjunction
of the 7th House or 7th lord, condition of the karaka of 7th House,
all the yogas being formed by the planets, and then finally looking
at the D9 chart with the same eye and draw a final conclusion.
The purpose behind teaching you about the Hora is so you can
quickly judge which houses a person will need to work on the most
and which houses will be more of an interest for the native. Since
we are studying conjunctions in this book, I just wanted to show a
small example of how the same conjunction might have a different
results based on the Hora.
If you were born during sunrise or sunset, then a conjunction in
3rd and 6th House will produce good, bad, or neutral results.

## Conjunctions through signs:

When you are reading a conjunction, you need to give the houses
and sign placement just as much importance as planets that are
involved. Below is a table of signs and the type of conjunction you
will experience.
Aries: aggressive, impulsive and self centered
Taurus: sensual, extravagant and pleasant
Gemini: communicative
Cancer: emotional

Leo: dynamic and creative
Virgo: analytical and critical
Libra: peace and harmony
Scorpio: hidden, occult and secretive
Sagittarius: divine, religious, spiritual
Capricorn: workaholic, disciplined, organized
Aquarius: worldly, eccentric
Pisces: spiritual, imaginative

## Conjunctions through Houses:

The table below shows the effect of conjunctions through houses.
1st house: related to self
2nd house: family, speech, and wealth
3rd house: siblings, communication, and self motivation
4th house: homeland, mother, and early childhood
5th house: academic, creative, and related to children
6th house: obstacles, diseases, medicine and service
7th house: others, partner, spouse

8th house: occult, hidden, related to spouse's family
9th house: teachers, higher education, and religious
10th house: father, career, and status
11th house: network circle, hopes, and dreams
12th house: foreign lands, isolation, and spirituality

## Let's Start on Conjunctions:

If we are going to start on conjunctions, we will have to start with
the king of all kings, Amun Ra, or the Sun God, revered by ancient
Egyptians.
When talking about the Sun, we think of heat, power, life, and
energy. At the Vedic level, the Sun is jyotish, or the soul of a
person. As the saying goes, "Home is where the heart is.” The Sun
represents where your heart lies in your chart. The Sun in
astrology represents your father or father figures, government,
politics, creativity, leadership, ego, and your soul. I used “ego” and
“soul” in one sentence even though those two words are miles
apart in their approach to life. The conjunction between ego and
soul comes into play with the planets. The soul needs a shell or a
suit in order to survive on this plain; and the ego becomes that
perfectly fitted suit for the soul. On earth, the soul becomes a
delicate entity that needs protection so no one can hurt her. She is
female because the soul is a divine goddess, and this entire
universe was created by a female energy. The Sun is our ego and
the soul becomes the manager of these two aspects of our life. The
level of ego versus the level of humility is determined by the
position and condition of the Sun in our horoscope. The Sun is fire

and heat. The Sun is your vitality and controls the zodiac sign of
Leo, which is everything that the lion represents in a jungle. This
is why we call him “King of the jungle.” Whenever the Sun comes
into conjunction with other planets, he increases the influence of
that planet versus when the Moon is in conjunction. The Moon
gets influenced by other planets, because the Moon is a receptor
and Sun is the leader.
The Sun in conjunction with Saturn will increase the qualities of
Saturn. If the Sun is in conjunction with Mars, then he will
increase the qualities of Mars.
We are going to assume that these conjunctions are not combust in
one’s horoscope. Every planet, except Mercury is combust within
7 degrees of the Sun's range. If you see a symbol of a bomb next to
a planet with the Sun in your birth chart, it usually means its
combust. Combust planets lose all their qualities and are unable to
give any result from such a placement. It becomes indifferent.
During the dasha of a planet that is combust, one will feel lost and
unsatisfied.
Let's talk about the Sun and Moon conjunction in a horoscope.
In each planetary conjunction section I will keep reminding you of
what each planet represents. This way, you can thoroughly
understand what each planet signifies.
If you only have 3, 4, 5, 6 or 7 planetary conjunctions, I would still
recommend reading the 2 planetary conjunctions as a pair with
planets that are closest to each other. This will give you a far better
concept of the native than just going straight to your multi-
planetary conjunctions.

## Size & Nature Impact:

The biggest planets in the conjunction will have the most influence
upon others. These are the planets from biggest to smallest in
astrology: Sun, Jupiter, Saturn, Venus, Mars, Mercury, and Moon.
Rahu and Ketu can become the biggest planets in the conjunction
if the Sun is present and being eclipsed by the nodes. But Rahu and
Ketu cannot eclipse Saturn, Jupiter, Mercury, Venus, or Mars.
They only eclipse the Sun and Moon. If the Sun, Saturn, and Rahu
were present in a conjunction, and Rahu was eclipsing the Sun,
then Saturn would become the biggest planet in the conjunction.
Saturn will put pressure on the Sun and further darken its qualities.
After determining which planet is the biggest, analyze which
planets are natural malefic and natural benefic in the conjunction.
Malefic planets will always infuse their malefic qualities upon the
benefic rather benefic infusing their qualities onto the malefic. If
natural benefic planets infused more of their qualities onto the
malefic, then the native will have very few problems in their
horoscope.

## Example 1:

Saturn will never be influenced by Venus, but Venus would be
influenced by Saturn in a conjunction. The only time Venus would
impact Saturn in a very small way is if Venus wins the war by
degree and sign placement.

## Example 2:

A Jupiter and Saturn conjunction is another good example. Since
Jupiter and Saturn are similar in size, the true impact of the
conjunction will be seen through their nature. Since Saturn is the
natural malefic, Saturn would infuse his qualities upon Jupiter.
However, Jupiter is still the biggest planet, so Jupiter will not take
on all of Saturn’s qualities.

If two planets are similar in size, then one should see their degree
and sign placement.
Saturn and Jupiter in Capricorn will have Jupiter submit to the will
of Saturn. If Jupiter has the lowest degree, then Jupiter will only
take on certain qualities of Saturn. The native would become
victorious by being optimistic, law abiding, and God fearing. They
will be a knowledgeable preacher who is disciplined, organized,
and structured, and enjoys life when away from work.
If Saturn and Jupiter are in Capricorn but Saturn has the lowest
degree, then Jupiter's optimism would be darkened. Jupiter will be
serious about work and will hardly allow time to enjoy life. They
will be involved in studies, law, religion, and constant organization
of life. Even if such person is on vacation, Saturn will keep them
working.
A good example of this would be President Obama. He has Saturn
at 2.3 degrees and Jupiter at 7 degrees in Capricorn. When he goes
on vacation, he hardly enjoys it as work follows him everywhere.
His work ethic is impossible to match, which is why he is
successful. If his Jupiter had the lowest degree, he would be more
relaxed. He wouldn't let work follow him, and he would enjoy life
and be happy.

## Example 3:

The Sun is not influenced by Saturn. The Sun will simply brighten
the quality of Saturn, and this is why we think Saturn is
influencing Sun. Since Saturn is more malefic than the Sun, its
qualities shine brighter.
If Rahu and Ketu have eclipsed the Sun or Moon by having the
higher degree, then the Sun and Moon will eventually come out of
the eclipse by the native’s thirties.

## Example 4:

In a Jupiter and Mars conjunction, Jupiter is the biggest planet so
he will infuse his qualities upon Mars. Since Mars is a natural
malefic, Jupiter would only expand his malefic qualities, and Mars
would shine brighter than Jupiter. It is Jupiter's job is to expand the
qualities of the house and planet he sits with or aspects. In this
case, Jupiter expanded the qualities of Mars, so Mars comes out on
top and shows his malefic personality. Mars calms down a lot due
to Jupiter expanding its qualities. It's guiding Mars to do the right
thing. When the conjunction becomes active through maha and
antar dasha, qualities of Mars will overtake Jupiter. If Mars has
the lowest degree, then Jupiter's qualities will be more forceful,
rude, and aggressive in a bad way. Such people will use physical
force to teach their lessons.

## Example 5:

If Rahu is sitting with any planets besides the Sun or Moon, it will
amplify the qualities of that planet. However, that expansion is
only an illusion. Rahu blows planets up like a balloon, so what we
see on the surface is a huge quality of a planet, but inside there is
nothing but air. Even if the Sun and Moon are eclipsed, they are
still shining brightly. As soon as the eclipse passes, we see rest of
the light.
If the Sun is eclipsed and being amplified by Rahu, it shows an
ego blown out of proportion in front of the world. However, deep
within, the native lacks true confidence and self-esteem.

## About The Sun:

One thing to remember about the Sun in conjunction is that it
brightens the quality of the planet it comes in conjunction with. It
does not get influenced by other planets, but rather illuminates

their qualities through sheer brilliance and brightness. This is
especially true when the Sun is behind the other planet or has the
lowest degree in the conjunction.

## Sun and Moon conjunction:

We know what the Sun represents. What about the Moon?
The Moon in astrology represents your mother, or motherly
figures, your emotional response to your environment, and your
imagination, since the Moon is your mind. The Moon shows the
way you think and react to situation. How emotional or un-
emotional you are depends upon the condition of the Moon. The
Moon is also a watery planet, as it controls the sign of Cancer.
The Moon controls government. Since it is not as strong as the
Sun, it represents our local or state government versus the federal
government.
When the Sun and Moon are in conjunction, think of it as fire and
water coming into conjunction. First, whenever someone has the
Sun and Moon in conjunction in their birth chart, it shows that this
person was born under “no moon,” meaning that the Moon wasn't
visible in the sky as the Earth was between the Moon and Sun.
This conjunction shows a lot of steam, because that is what fire
and water create. In other words, they are very determined people.
They stick to their decision, and they know what they want. If the
soul (Sun) and mind (Moon) come together in one house, this
person is quite determined about their status in life. They do not
struggle with their personality since the Sun sign and Moon sign
are the same. In Aries, Scorpio, and Libra ascendants, this
conjunction can form a raj yoga if it happens in the kendera house
or trikon houses. This is one of the most powerful yogas for
political success. If this conjunction occurs where the Moon is far

weaker than the Sun, the relationship with parents, especially the
mother, can suffer.

## Sun and Mercury Conjunction:

This conjunction in Vedic Astrology is known as budha aditya
yoga. Yogas are formed when two or more planets are in
conjunction in a certain situation. But before we go into this Yoga,
let's define Mercury.
