Mercury is known as the prince in Vedic astrology. He is the royal
messenger for the king. He delivers the important messages of the
king to others. He is the karaka of communication of all kinds.
Mercury also represents speech and our inner playfulness, since
princes love to party and have a good time. Since he is a royal, he
takes his duty as the messenger seriously. This is why Mercury is
also intelligence, because in order for anyone to have good
communication, one needs to be intelligent. Mercury is also our
business and management skills due to his calculative and logical
nature, and love for the marketplace.
One thing you must remember about Mercury is that because it is a
neutral natural, it can become benefic and malefic at the same
time. If Mercury is in conjunction with a natural benefic, then it
acts like a benefic planet. If Mercury is with a natural malefic
planet, then it acts like a malefic. If Mercury is with both natural
malefic and benefic as in Venus, Mercury, and Saturn, then look at
the difference in degrees in all three of them. The closest degree to
Mercury will influence Mercury more, however if Venus and
Saturn are both within 5 degrees of Mercury, then it gives mixed
results.
The Sun and Mercury conjunction makes a strong, opinionated
person. This royal union may benefit a person in a position of
authority which these natives strive for. This conjunction shows

strong intellect in a person, especially in an academic sense. When
these natives go to school and read a book, they are able to quickly
comprehend what they are reading and learning. This conjunction
manifests itself very strongly in kendera and trikon houses, and is
quite common in people. Why? Because Mercury and Venus are
always close to the Sun due to their orbital distance from the Sun.
Mercury and the Sun are mostly 1-2 houses apart, and most people
have this in their birth chart. Does this mean they are all intelligent
and have the ability gain authority? No. This comes down to the
difference of degrees between these two planets. When Mercury
and the Sun are within 7 degrees of each other, this is considered a
powerful budha aditya yoga. Budha simply means Mercury in
Hindi, while aditya means Sun.
Since the Sun represents the father, this yoga shows that much of
the intelligence in the native came through the guidance of the
father. Since the father has a direct impact and teaching method,
these people tend to be direct in their communication and have no
problem expressing their opinion, especially in politics. Most
successful politicians have such a yoga occurring at less than 10
degrees. If this conjunction occurs in the 10th House while in a
good sign like Aries, Gemini, Leo, Virgo, Sagittarius, or
Capricorn, the native may have a government service job or
become a CEO of a company that might deal with the government
on a daily basis; like a weapons manufacturer. Remember, this
conjunction has a strong effect if the Sun and Mercury are in close
conjunction. Even though planets tend to “combust” in astrology
when they get too close to the Sun, Mercury circumvents this
reaction because of its permanent close settlement near the Sun.
When these natives speak, it is with purpose. They speak with
authority in order to sell an idea, product, or vision.

## Sun and Venus conjunction:

Let's talk about Venus first. What is Venus in Astrology?

First and foremost, Venus represents relationships. It shows the
value we put on relationships and how we react to them. The main
focus is on romantic relationships. Venus is also about comfort, as
relationships and love give us that feeling. Further, Venus also
represents the convenience of luxurious items like a car, air
conditioning, clothing, jewelry, wealth, and beauty inside the
home. Since Venus signifies pleasure, it can be any kind of
pleasure: sex, art, entertainment, interior decoration, beauty
pageants, or anything else related to having fun. Venus in a man's
chart also shows his wife, or the type of women he comes across in
life. Venus signifies marriage for both men and women.
When the ego comes in contact with pleasure, the native shares the
relationships they create with the world. The brightness of the sun
gets an extra spark by Venus, which makes these people quite
charming and charismatic. The Sun's ego and power is reflected
through love, relationships, and diplomacy. However, this
conjunction can also bring about impulsiveness in relationships,
especially if Venus is too close to the Sun where it becomes
combust. This is when Venus loses all its positive qualities. In
Vedic astrology it is said that this conjunction is a strong indicator
of divorce. When people have this close conjunction of Venus and
the Sun, the best trick to save a marriage is to remarry your partner
within couple of years of marriage. When Venus does combust,
this can create a very selfish person. They may be seeking a
relationship for their own benefit and pleasure, or someone who
uses relationships to get to attain status, similar to the Sun King. In
the mutation with Venus, he climbs to the throne through
relationships. The Sun and Venus can also create someone who is
oversexed due to the fact that Venus' pure quality can go haywire
in combustion.
Due to the ego of the Sun in conjunction with Venus, men with
this alignment like to have women pursue them rather than chase
women. They have too much pride in the area of love and

romance, and only bet on sure things. The Sun and Venus also
show a lot of creativity in a person if they are placed in the 1st,
2nd, 3rd, 4th, 5th, 7th, 8th, 10th and 11th House. Since Venus
represents art and beauty, and the Sun is creativity, the mix of
these two can produce a great actor, artist, theater performer, or
even a great charismatic athlete. This can also be someone who
takes part in beauty pageants or organizes them. They shine in
government jobs, especially when it comes to politics. Politicians
with such a conjunction can be quite charming and can naturally
attract a crowd.
The wife of this native would be very dynamic, egoistic, and
controlling. Besides her natural beauty, she would have the gift of
the gab. In a woman's chart this can also mean a lot of ego, even
bigger than a man. The woman would want everything to be equal
or more of a partnership. In a traditional marriage, this can create
disharmony which would ultimately lead to divorce. But, if a
woman has a loose conjunction of Venus and the Sun, then she
would not be as affected negatively.
The Sun is a masculine energy while Venus is a feminine energy,
and this conjunction occurring in a man's 1st, 5th, 7th, 8th, and
12th House can lead to a decline in masculine energy. It can
produce someone who might be gay or loves feminine things in
life even if they are straight. A good example would be a fashion
designer or home decorator.

## Sun and Mars conjunction:

Who is Mars in Astrology?
Let's talk about Mars. Mars is a soldier who stands for his beliefs.
Wherever it is placed in your horoscope, you stand for those
beliefs, and that's where you energy goes. Mars is energy. It's our

will power and vitality. Mars represents our ability to take action
towards something. It also represents the anger we carry within us,
as a soldier needs to have a certain level of anger within him to
fight and win the battle. In this day and age, Mars represents
policemen, soldiers, athletes, warlords, arms dealers, and fighters,
along with manufacturing, accidents, injuries, and dealing with
explosives. Mars also represents male friends and the Sun
represents royalty, so these people can be in contact with friends
who are of a higher status in society, perhaps sons of politicians
and celebrities.
When the Sun and Mars are in conjunction, it gives a very fiery,
intimidating, and bold persona to the person, especially in the area
of life where the Sun and Mars are sitting. These people are
determined because the will of Mars is now at the soul level which
the Sun represents. Being courageous is nothing new to these
people as they dive into challenges in the heat of the moment. This
combination of the king and soldier shows someone dedicated
towards serving the kingdom or government. You will find these
people not only in political office, but they can be working as
policemen, generals, emergency physicians, or as a secret agent.
These are very confident people who walk with pride. The ego of
this person emits a vivid energy, because the Sun is ego and Mars
is energy. So the ego is filled with energy. When the ego sets a
goal, in this case the Sun, then Mars, the soldier planet, goes and
fights the battle to achieve that goal. You will see a lot CEOs and
venture capitalists with this conjunction as they are brave enough
to take chances on the things their ego believes in.
But one has to remember, these two are very hot planets in
conjunction. If these two planets sit or aspect the ascendant, 2nd
House and 12th House, this person can be very angry with a short
fuse. When they walk into a room angry, the temperature goes up.
If the Moon is sitting with this conjunction, making it a triple
conjunction, you can bet this person is always screaming and
yelling at their employees, family, and subordinates. The level of

anger will depend upon the sign they are placed in. If either one is
debilitated, it will make the person constantly on edge.

## Sun and Jupiter Conjunction:

What is Jupiter in Astrology?
Jupiter is the knowledge we gain in this lifetime. It is the
concentration we put into learning something. Jupiter is the
teaching of our father and educators. Since the father is the first
teacher for a child, Jupiter automatically becomes the teaching and
preaching of fathers and father figures. Jupiter also represents our
belief system and our ability to follow the law. Jupiter is the
lawyer in astrology; he writes the law and either makes the person
follow it or resent it depending upon its condition. Jupiter also
represents a husband in a female's chart. Mars is not the husband,
Mars represents male friends. Jupiter is the guiding force in every
woman's life. He is also wisdom. We learn from our teachers,
whether its Nazi theology, Christian theology or Vedic theology.
The source of wisdom and belief is controlled by Jupiter in our
chart. Our higher learning is dependent upon Jupiter, from basic
education to a Master's degree and PhD. It represents the rituals of
our culture, our traditions, and ability to follow them. Jupiter is the
biggest planet in our solar system, which is why it represents the
expansion of things related to where Jupiter sits in your horoscope.
Now if that expansion will be positive or negative, early or late,
depends upon other factors and planets. Whenever Jupiter is in the
ascendant, 5th House and 9th House, it shows a person is capable
of learning several languages.
Jupiter in jyotish is also an indicator of wealth, finance, children,
luck, travel, and gains in life. It is pretty much the karaka of the
2nd, 5th, 9th, and 11th House in a horoscope. This is why Jupiter is
one of the most important planets after the Moon. Jupiter is the
source of optimism in astrology.

When the Sun and Jupiter are in conjunction, it bestows a great
deal of wisdom upon a native. They are well behaved and their
regal status expands in society; along with their ego. The
expanding energy of Jupiter and the ego of the Sun, allows
optimistic Jupiter to bask in the radiant glow of the Sun. This
makes a person a positive soul if they are both in a good or neutral
sign placement. The confidence of this person grows with time,
and people start to recognize it as they progress in their chosen
career field. This can range from teacher, politician, restaurant
owner, or even a jeweler. The father becomes a source of
knowledge, wisdom, and ego. The relationship with the father
flourishes over time. Not only is the father figure a good teacher,
but also a close friend of the son or daughter.
One thing to keep in mind with this conjunction is that the ego
doesn't get overblown, as Jupiter loves to expand things. These
people are confident because they think they know everything in
life, including being an expert in the things they have learned over
time or in their chosen career. If this combination occurs in the
ascendant, 9th House or 10th House, this can make a person a very
determined university professor. In the house of finance like 2nd,
5th, and 11th, a person can be a financial guru or someone who
preaches about finance and financial planning. These natives have
quite an outgoing, illuminating personality. The Sun is our soul's
personality which Jupiter loves to expand. They are the center of
attention at a party and the loudest people in the crowd. They
respect their teachers and father figures and usually their educators
are of high status, like a Harvard professor, especially if the Sun is
exalted in the 9th House with Jupiter. In the 9th House, this
conjunction shows a well traveled, diversified ego. They are not so
ethnocentric due to their exposure to different cultures around the
world. The ego gets increased exposure through Jupiter.

## Sun and Saturn Conjunction:

What is Saturn in astrology?
Saturn is our limitation in life. It sets boundaries to what we can
and cannot achieve. It's the wake up call, as Saturn shows you the
truth and reality of life. Most of us are walking around day
dreaming, and have an illusion of our elicit future. But during
Saturn's dasha, and Saturn’s transit, we are awakened by the harsh
reality that exists around us. We are forced to be more practical
and disciplined about our goals rather than searching for them on
an esoteric level.
Saturn and Sun conjunctions are not as bad as you think.
Mainstream astrological media says the Sun and Saturn are
enemies because Saturn was betrayed by his father, the Sun, in
ancient mythology. Most of the time, father and son cannot get
along in real life. There is a tug of war between the Sun and
Saturn. However this war only takes place if Saturn and the Sun
are between 9-14 degrees. Within 9 degrees, Saturn is combust and
does not have the power to fight the Sun, and beyond 14 degrees,
the Sun and Saturn are too far apart in the house to really influence
each other. The delicate effect of Saturn and the Sun happens in a
gray zone, when they are neither too close or far apart in degrees.
The positive side of this conjunction is that these people are
disciplined, mature, and have a strong sense of self identity. They
do not need to work as hard as others to come into their own. They
know early on in their life who they are and what they want. They
fight for justice and fight to protect the kingdom, or in modern
times, the current government. A successful lawyer, government
official, and head of a company can be seen from this conjunction,
especially in the 6th, 7th, 9th and 10th House. They are very
mature for their age and take full responsibility of their own
actions. This conjunction means duty and serving the higher
power. This doesn't mean you are a slave. In modern times it

means you are serving the government or serving as one of the
high executives in a company. Organizing and managing larger
departments is one of their strengths, whether it's working in
government information technology, business, or even the
entertainment field. In the house where this conjunction is
occurring, there is a great deal of responsibility and duty towards
these things. If this occurs in the 4th House for example, the
person would have a lot of responsibility towards the family and
home.
If the Sun is debilitated in this conjunction, or if Saturn is in Libra
along with the Sun, then the self confidence and ego of the person
faces a constant reality check and will keep him or her grounded.
Another rule of thumb is that the exalted planet sitting with
debilitated planets will always give some of his power to his
weaker companions. In this case, the Sun will be rescued by Saturn
while Saturn will be drowning in the Sun himself.

## Sun and Rahu conjunction:

What is Rahu?
Rahu is the north node of the Moon, and is a head without a body
that keeps devouring things without ever being satisfied. It has a
desire for material things and worldly accomplishments. It wants
to achieve the highest success possible in whatever house and sign
he represents. Rahu represents the goals that have been set by the
universe for us to achieve whether we like or not. Rahu is mainly
known as the eclipse, and it eclipses the Moon and the Sun
whenever it's in conjunction with them. This means temporary
darkness upon the native's life when the Sun and Rahu are in
conjunction in a birth horoscope. Rahu also brings fear and
stressful situations into our lives. This is due to Rahu's adventurous
nature that loves surprises. With Rahu, sudden events take place;
events that may not be a real part of our lives but rather an illusion

that becomes reality. Rahu is taboo breaking and crosses all kinds
of boundaries. It wants to be unique and break tradition; to think
outside the box. He is also responsible for spiritual breakthroughs,
because only after achieving material wealth does one seeks God.
The Sun and Rahu are direct enemies. They can't stand each other
as one is the king and the other is a thief. But when they are forced
to be in one house, Rahu takes full advantage of its chameleon-like
nature to plague the Sun King. Rahu transforms himself into the
Sun which creates a double ego, or a double standard in ego. On
one hand this person has tremendous confidence and ego to be
recognized as a genius and leader in the world, while on the other
hand Rahu wants to sneak its way into power by cheating and
hiding the real truth from people. These leaders may look honest
and sincere when it comes to being concerned for other's welfare,
but there is an underlining angle of deception. This is due to the
fact that Rahu eclipses the Sun's positive behavior when Rahu
transforms himself into the Sun. This lasts till the age of 35 at
minimum, after which Rahu starts to remove his eclipse and the
real Sun shines.
Since the Sun represents our father and father figures, the
relationship with the father and his teachings are interrupted or
completely foreign when Rahu enters the picture. Rahu simply
means unusual or unusual circumstances. This could manifest as
an estranged relationship from the father; either the father was far
away from the native living in foreign lands or the relationship was
strained. A false sense of ego comes upon the native, where it not
only hurts the person professionally, but also in their personal life
as well. However, with every conjunction there is good and bad,
and the good really shines when these people are in performing
arts, politics, and or entrepreneurs. Although internally this person
may lack confidence and suffer from low self-esteem, they wear a
pretty thick veil in front of the world.

If the Sun is exalted in Aries or in his own sign of Leo with Rahu,
then the Sun will be able to handle himself far better than being in
any other sign. Here, the Sun will be able to lower the chances of
Rahu trying to be the deceiver or cheater, and the person will have
a much better relationship with their father. When a planet is
strongly placed in a sign, Rahu amplifies those qualities of the
planet rather than eclipsing them.

## Sun and Ketu Conjunction:

What is Ketu?
Ketu is the south node of the Moon and the rest of the body of
Rahu. This is a headless body which represents separation,
isolation, and the abandonment of the material world. This shadow
planet represents spirituality, nothingness, and goals that we
already achieved in past lives. In this life we are here to only rely
on those things as a last resort. The house and sign in which Ketu
sits are in things we are not fond of, and we do not obsess about
those things as much because subconsciously we know we have
already dealt with them. If we didn't accomplish those things in a
past life, that would be seen by Ketu's bad placement in a bad sign.
This will show a tug of war between both Rahu and Ketu. Ketu
also searches for truth and enlightenment. Ketu's aspect and
conjunction gives research ability to a person especially when it's
in conjunction or aspects the Moon and Mercury.
Just like Rahu, Ketu puts a shadow upon the Sun. This time the
shadow is more about easing the ego than inflating it. These people
usually become humble early on in life as the ego constantly gets
isolated in the world or separated from self. These folks feel a loss

of identity, because the Sun is simply the ego, and without ego we
cannot function in the material world. A person lacks confidence
because the spiritual planet Ketu shines its dark light upon the
bright sun and Rahu from its 7th aspect also puts his eclipse on it.
If you were born with Rahu or Ketu in conjunction less than 5
degrees apart, you were born on a solar eclipse. This conjunction
naturally makes the person shy and introverted, unless the Sun is
exalted. If the Sun is exalted sitting with the nodes, then the Sun
will shine its light even behind the clouds of nodes. This can bring
timely success to a person.

## About The Moon:

One thing to understand about the Moon is that it's the one planet
in astrology that gets influenced the most when in conjunction.
The Moon is a dark, cold, and lifeless place, but it is revived due to
the light from the Sun. This is why each planet brings his or her
own reflection upon the Moon through which it shows its color,
lust, and quality. One thing to remember about the moon is the day
of the phase you were born on. There are two phases in the
astrology that are considered malefic and benefic for the Moon.
Waxing Moon is benefic, while Waning Moon is malefic;
depending upon which phase you were born into, it will either
work for you or against you in certain cases.

## Moon and Rahu conjunction:

What is Moon?
The Moon in astrology represents your mother, or motherly
figures, your emotional response to your environment, and your
imagination, since the Moon is your mind. The Moon shows the
way you think and react to situation. How emotional or un-

emotional you are depends upon the condition of the Moon. The
Moon is also a watery planet, as it controls the sign of Cancer.
The Moon controls government. Since it is not as strong as the
Sun, it represents our local or state government versus the federal
government.
What is Rahu?
Rahu is the north node of the Moon, and is a head without a body
that keeps devouring things without ever being satisfied. It has a
desire for material things and worldly accomplishments. It wants
to achieve the highest success possible in whatever house and sign
he represents. Rahu represents the goals that have been set by the
universe for us to achieve whether we like or not. Rahu is mainly
known as the eclipse, and it eclipses the Moon and the Sun
whenever it's in conjunction with them. This means temporary
darkness upon the native's life when the Sun and Rahu are in
conjunction in a birth horoscope. Rahu also brings fear and
stressful situations into our lives. This is due to Rahu's adventurous
nature that loves surprises. With Rahu, sudden events take place;
events that may not be a real part of our lives but rather an illusion
that becomes reality. Rahu is taboo breaking and crosses all kinds
of boundaries. It wants to be unique and break tradition; to think
outside the box. He is also responsible for spiritual breakthroughs,
because only after achieving material wealth does one seeks God.
When the Moon and Rahu are in conjunction, it can amplify fear,
anxiety, and unusual depression. Depression comes from the effect
of Saturn, and Rahu is in the shadow of Saturn. It gives you
illusions about things that really do not exist. This is why these
people have unrealistic expectations for themselves and others, and
when they do not come true, they go into a depression.
Rahu is about taboo breaking and unusualness, and when in tight
conjunction with the Moon, emotions need a unique validation to

sustain the mind. This can start anywhere from the need to be in a
foreign land, to dissecting human beings, or experimenting with
drugs. Rahu is that uncontrollable desire within us, and when the
mind or Moon is sitting with him, there is an emotional need to
fulfill that desire. This can easily lead to infidelity due to Rahu's
nature to indulge without ever being satisfied. These people have
lust for life, sex, relationships and exploration. If Rahu and the
Moon are less than 5 degrees apart, then it will extremely hard for
these people to stay put, or stay and focus on one thing. Marriage
will become a chore sooner than expected if such a combination
occurs in 1st, 2nd, 7th and 8th House. Most people keep their
fetishes to themselves, but these people actually take steps to fulfill
those desires, whether it's having an affair with their married
neighbor, having sexual or violent fantasies, or the desire to hurt
someone.
Rahu, which is the energy of doing things outside the box marries
the mind (Moon). This native thinks in a unique way, and the
solution is never common and it's always unconventional.
If there is a person in your life who loves living with drama, most
likely they their moon is in a tight relationship with Rahu. The
dramatization of events and relationships become excitement for
these people because the unsatisfied Rahu craves for more
conflicts. These are the so called pioneers and innovators of their
generation. Best example: Mahatma Gandhi.

## Moon and Venus conjunction:

What is Venus?
First and foremost, Venus represents relationships. It shows the
value we put on relationships and how we react to them. The main
focus is on romantic relationships. Venus is also about comfort, as
relationships and love give us that feeling. Further, Venus also

represents the convenience of luxurious items like a car, air
conditioning, clothing, jewelry, wealth, and beauty inside the
home. Since Venus signifies pleasure, it can be any kind of
pleasure: sex, art, entertainment, interior decoration, beauty
pageants, or anything else related to having fun. Venus in a man's
chart also shows his wife, or the type of women he comes across in
life. Venus signifies marriage for both men and women.
When emotions come in contact with pleasure, it creates a self
proclaimed celebrity who craves lavishness, security, comfort, and
wealth to be emotionally balanced. In a way, they are daddy's little
princess or spoiled brats. This conjunction can spoil a child from
the start, and later in life their relationships will be based on
pleasure and sensuality.
This conjunction is not all about being a princess. To get the
lifestyle they desire, most of the time they are willing to work
hard. Their entire life's goal becomes about financial security, art,
pleasure, and enjoyment. Even though this conjunction can
produce a creative actor, model, musician, or a designer, their sole
goal in life will be to attain as much wealth through their work as
possible. For them, art may be a part of life, but it's the security
and comfort they get through art that matters the most. If this
combination occurs in a bad sign or house like Scorpio, Virgo, or
Capricorn, it can produce prostitutes, gold diggers, and very
cunning personalities who are only out there to seek pleasure.
Since the Moon and Venus are both karakas of 4th House, which
is about comfort, peace of mind, convenience, vehicles and
childhood, this conjunction can bring about dhana yoga, or wealth
yoga if this conjunction occurs in 1st, 4th, 5th, 9th and 11th House.
The reason it is unfavorable to have these two in the 2nd house is
because the Moon causes tides, waxes, and wanes; making a
person a spend thrift. They will spend more than they can afford
and empty their savings on luxurious items.

One thing to note about this conjunction is that they are natural
enemies, especially from Venus' side. This bitterness can create
fights between your spouse and your mother, especially in the case
of men, or you can get into fights with your mother-in law.

## Moon and Mercury conjunction:

What is Mercury?
Mercury is known as the prince in Vedic astrology. He is the royal
messenger for the king. He delivers the important messages of the
king to others. He is the karaka of communication of all kinds.
Mercury also represents speech and our inner playfulness, since
princes love to party and have a good time. Since he is a royal, he
takes his duty as the messenger seriously. This is why Mercury is
also intelligence, because in order for anyone to have good
communication, one needs to be intelligent. Mercury is also our
business and management skills due to his calculative and logical
nature, and love for the marketplace.
This conjunction can either produce a brilliant mathematician or
psychotic serial killer. Since Moon is the mind and Mercury is
intelligence, your emotional balance comes from intellectual
communication, games, discussions, and work. You take a keen
interest in solving problems and emotionally connecting with
people. The mind becomes hyper intuitive. They are always
thinking, calculating, assessing, analyzing, and criticizing. Their
intelligence can somewhat become irrational at times if placed in a
bad house or bad sign. It can produce an immature, stupid, or even
a mentally retarded person. The decision making will also be
impulsive because the mind and intelligence share the same
channel.

This conjunction can make for a brilliant writer, public speaker,
mathematician, teacher, journalist, reporter, and salesmen. As
much as these two hate each other, they gel quite well if placed in
an earth, fire, or air sign. In water signs like Cancer, Scorpio, and
Sagittarius, this person will make most decision based on emotions
rather than logical thinking which has its own pros and cons.

## Moon and Jupiter conjunction:

What is Jupiter?
Jupiter is the knowledge we gain in this lifetime. It is the
concentration we put into learning something. Jupiter is the
teaching of our father and educators. Since the father is the first
teacher for a child, Jupiter automatically becomes the teaching and
preaching of fathers and father figures. Jupiter also represents our
belief system and our ability to follow the law. Jupiter is the
lawyer in astrology; he writes the law and either makes the person
follow it or resent it depending upon its condition. Jupiter also
represents a husband in a female's chart. Mars is not the husband,
Mars represents male friends. Jupiter is the guiding force in every
woman's life. He is also wisdom. We learn from our teachers,
whether its Nazi theology, Christian theology or Vedic theology.
The source of wisdom and belief is controlled by Jupiter in our
chart. Our higher learning is dependent upon Jupiter, from basic
education to a Master's degree and PhD. It represents the rituals of
our culture, our traditions, and ability to follow them. Jupiter is the
biggest planet in our solar system, which is why it represents the
expansion of things related to where Jupiter sits in your horoscope.
Now if that expansion will be positive or negative, early or late,
depends upon other factors and planets. Whenever Jupiter is in the
ascendant, 5th House and 9th House, it shows a person is capable
of learning several languages.

Jupiter in jyotish is also an indicator of wealth, finance, children,
luck, travel, and gains in life. It is pretty much the karaka of the
2nd, 5th, 9th, and 11th House in a horoscope. This is why Jupiter is
one of the most important planets after the Moon. Jupiter is the
source of optimism in astrology.
When the Moon and Jupiter come together, it creates an expansion
of optimism and finding balance in the mind through preaching
and spreading optimism. However, Jupiter's nature to expand
qualities, can make someone overly optimistic, which can
eventually lead to even greater disappointment. If the Moon is not
well placed in a sign while in conjunction with Jupiter, then the
Moon's rocky emotions will be expanded through Jupiter, which
will balloon the situation even more.
This makes for a great mother who has lot of wisdom to share with
her children. These people are not only optimistic about their own
future, but they expect others to join them in their venture of
steering away from reality and creating their own rationality.
This is one of the best conjunctions for the Moon, since Jupiter is
the Moon's best friend. There is a great deal of learning and
wisdom, for such a person is capable of finding God and
spirituality in the darkest of places. The Moon receives guidance
from Jupiter during times of turmoil.
If this conjunction occurs in kendra houses, then it can lead to
fame for the native. If the conjunction occurs in trikona houses,
then it can lead to a scholarly life or a highly regarded priesthood
because trikona houses are known as dharma houses, or houses of
duty. In Hindu culture, the most important duty is towards God.

## Moon and Mars conjunction:

Who is Mars?
Let's talk about Mars. Mars is a soldier who stands for his beliefs.
Wherever it is placed in your horoscope, you stand for those
beliefs, and that's where you energy goes. Mars is energy. It's our
will power and vitality. Mars represents our ability to take action
towards something. It also represents the anger we carry within us,
as a soldier needs to have a certain level of anger within him to
fight and win the battle. In this day and age, Mars represents
policemen, soldiers, athletes, warlords, arms dealers, and fighters,
along with manufacturing, accidents, injuries, and dealing with
explosives. Mars also represents male friends and the Sun
represents royalty, so these people can be in contact with friends
who are of a higher status in society, perhaps sons of politicians
and celebrities.
When the Moon and Mars are in conjunction, it can lead to several
positive and negative attributes in life. Moon is emotions while
Mars is action, energy, and our goals. Our emotional balance
comes when we set goals and put forth our energy to accomplish
them. This means that in the corporate world, this can be an
excellent position, especially in fire or earth signs where these two
planets can be somewhat balanced and work together. Usually this
conjunction is known as chandra mangal yoga, which is a highly
auspicious yoga for wealth and leadership, especially in the 1st,
2nd, 5th, 9th, 10th and 11th House because a goal oriented mind
will achieve the things it sets out to do, and most of the world
wants to achieve wealth and higher status.
This conjunction can also be good for any kind of business related
to chemicals, whether it's pharmacy, bar owner, beer manufacturer,
wine manufacturer, business of weapons, copper, martial arts, and
sports. This conjunction gives a person a very strong will, and a
mind that has the ability to go through hardships to attain their

goals. One thing is for certain, these people are stubborn in their
decision making and they take action before thinking.
This conjunction can also lead to impulsive anger, especially if
Mars is debilitated while sitting with Moon. They would have a
hot temper, be manipulative, and cheat people to attain their goals.
These people get into fights constantly, including domestic
violence, and road rage especially in 12th, 1st and 2nd House
which represents your head and brain. If Mars and the Moon are
well balanced due to the ascendant, then in the 2nd House it can
produce a very energetic public speaker who knows how to
electrify the crowd. This conjunction also makes a person very
stubborn about their decisions. Once they make their decision, they
will go through with it, even though it will be a wrong one.
If this conjunction occurs in a woman's chart, it can make her more
masculine as Mars energy influences the Moon more than the
Moon influences Mars in a woman's chart. A good example of this
conjunction would be someone who might be a lesbian.

## Moon and Saturn conjunction:

What is Saturn?
Saturn is our limitation in life. It sets boundaries to what we can
and cannot achieve. It's the wake up call, as Saturn shows you the
truth and reality of life. Most of us are walking around day
dreaming, and have an illusion of our elicit future. But during
Saturn's dasha, and Saturn’s transit, we are awakened by the harsh
reality that exists around us. We are forced to be more practical
and disciplined about our goals rather than searching for them on
an esoteric level.
The Saturn and Moon conjunction can depress the mind or put a
burden of responsibility and feeling of heaviness on our emotional

state. The mind is limitless; it wants to continously expand; it
wants to unconditionally and uncontrollably create values of
optimism about the future. When the lord of boundaries is in
conjunction with Moon, it can create limitations in the mind or
limit things that the mind wants to do, and when we do not get
what we want, we get down and depressed. Obviously this not a
great conjunction to be in as it can bring dark and gloomy thoughts
into a person because of the realistic nature of Saturn. Saturn wants
to show “what is,” while the Moon wants to think about “what else
can be.” Our limitless mind finally gets a roof above its head that
we can't escape. But Saturn doesn't punish; it only brings about the
fruits of our karma. If our karma is neutral or mixed, it removes its
hands from the limitless mind after midlife where the Moon again
feels optimistic.
Now, the Moon can overcome this conjunction from the beginning
if the Moon is more powerful than Saturn. This can happen when
the Moon is in its own sign or exalted in the chart while
controlling the nondusthana house in the horoscope.
What truly happens with the Saturn and Moon conjunction is that
these people tend to see the harder things in life rather than the
blooming flowers. This is a far better scenario than seeing
happiness, because when hard times come, this person will be well
prepared by mid-life. A Moon and Jupiter conjunction would make
a person so optimistic and happy all the time, that when problems
arise, they may struggle psychologically. Fear and anxiety are
common with this conjunction.

## Moon and Ketu conjunction:

What is Ketu?

Ketu is the south node of the Moon and the rest of the body of
Rahu. This is a headless body which represents separation,
isolation, and the abandonment of the material world. This shadow
planet represents spirituality, nothingness, and goals that we
already achieved in past lives. In this life we are here to only rely
on those things as a last resort. The house and sign in which Ketu
sits are in things we are not fond of, and we do not obsess about
those things as much because subconsciously we know we have
already dealt with them. If we didn't accomplish those things in a
past life, that would be seen by Ketu's bad placement in a bad sign.
This will show a tug of war between both Rahu and Ketu. Ketu
also searches for truth and enlightenment. Ketu's aspect and
conjunction gives research ability to a person especially when it's
in conjunction or aspects the Moon and Mercury.
When Ketu and the Moon are in conjunction, the mind becomes
isolated and separate from the physical body and the rest of the
world. The mind is forced to let go of attachment after many
disappointments. Since Rahu and Ketu create confusion for a
person, the mind is full of turmoil. It's not able to see the single
direction that will calm it down, due to Ketu's headless body. It
steers the mind away in many directions. The native becomes very
critical of themselves and things they are trying to accomplish.
They will always question themselves and their actions; even if it's
the right one. They will analyze a situation in their life until their
head hurts. This makes them moody, because the mind has no
direction with Ketu, and when the mind is not balanced in one
direction, its emotions go haywire.
This conjunction along with Saturn and Rahu causes fear, anxiety,
depression, and perhaps even EVP (Electro Voice Phenomenon) as
in hearing spirits and beings of other realms. Could this really be
true and not just some mental illness? People who don't have this
conjunction are not able to figure it out. They see the chemical
change in the brain and which part of the brain is active when the
patient experiences this phenomenon, and they can prescribe

medication for them, but they do not know what the consciousness
of the patient sees. The mind can be scanned and scrutinized, but
not our consciousness. There will be a time when science and
spirituality will merge, but till then we have to follow our guts and
believe what our soul and consciousness wants us to believe. There
are many cases of possession of the devil or evil spirits, and only
the family and the priest sees the unthinkable things the person
does and goes through, which modern medicine cannot prove.
There are so many movies in Hollywood that are based on true
stories of ghosts and possessions, where the person's face, body,
and strength changes dramatically. In this case the Moon will be
involved with Ketu, Saturn, and Rahu in some way. If millions of
people have this conjunction, then why don't they suffer? It all
comes down to the degree, certain nakshatras and pada. This
conjunction does not only lead to a dark world. This combination
of the Moon and Ketu also leads a person to spirituality and
enlightenment. Since Ketu's job is to separate the person from the
material plane, usually the Moon and Ketu dasha allows the
person to find his or her true self.
Since the Moon represents the mother, the mother is also isolated
and detached with her emotions towards the native. The mother
might also have been separated at birth or was traveling a lot. This
not only happens with the mother, but all women who take a
maternal role in the native's life. They have an isolated relationship
with all of them.

## Venus and Mercury conjunction:

What is Mercury?
Mercury is known as the prince in Vedic astrology. He is the royal
messenger for the king. He delivers the important messages of the
king to others. He is the karaka of communication of all kinds.

Mercury also represents speech and our inner playfulness, since
princes love to party and have a good time. Since he is a royal, he
takes his duty as the messenger seriously. This is why Mercury is
also intelligence, because in order for anyone to have good
communication, one needs to be intelligent. Mercury is also our
business and management skills due to his calculative and logical
nature, and love for the marketplace.
What is Venus?
First and foremost, Venus represents relationships. It shows the
value we put on relationships and how we react to them. The main
focus is on romantic relationships. Venus is also about comfort, as
relationships and love give us that feeling. Further, Venus also
represents the convenience of luxurious items like a car, air
conditioning, clothing, jewelry, wealth, and beauty inside the
home. Since Venus signifies pleasure, it can be any kind of
pleasure: sex, art, entertainment, interior decoration, beauty
pageants, or anything else related to having fun. Venus in a man's
chart also shows his wife, or the type of women he comes across in
life. Venus signifies marriage for both men and women.
When Venus and Mercury are in conjunction, it's like Picasso and
Al Pacino are in conjunction. Both represent art, skills, music, and
romance. These two planets amplify each other's energy, but
Mercury especially becomes more creative because Mercury in a
way is like Rahu; he becomes the planet he's in conjunction with
or at least he cheers for them. Venus' artistic, romantic, and
relationship skills become refined with the assistance of Mercury.
These people are the ones that have the wit and charm.
Mercury is our communicative, written, and verbal skills, while
Venus is our creativity, appreciation of beauty, art, and the type of
relationship we get into. This conjunction shows someone who
communicates more about love and beauty than drama and
betrayal. They make brilliant romantic authors, musicians, actors,

and writers. But, the ride doesn't end there. Since Mercury is our
business and management skills, while Venus is our creativity, this
conjunction shows someone who makes a successful businessman
and their creativity leads them to the top of their game. This
conjunction in any house leads to a great businessman, but in the
1st, 3rd, 5th, 8th, 10th and 12th House shows a great artistic mind.
Why did I mention the 8th and 12th House even though they are
evil houses? Because the 8th House represents depth, and the 12th
House represents imagination along with other things. A great
artist has tremendous depth and imagination which are greatly
amplified by such houses.
When it comes to romance, Mercury has the skill and charm of
seduction, while Venus has the ability to love and sustain a
relationship. These are the people who you remember as your best
romantic experience. In the 5th House of creativity and romance,
this energy amplifies their quality. They are your hopeless
romantics who would do anything to find that great love. Usually
this conjunction shows someone who would rather have a love
marriage than arranged marriage.

## Venus and Mars conjunction:

Who is Mars?
Let's talk about Mars. Mars is a soldier who stands for his beliefs.
Wherever it is placed in your horoscope, you stand for those
beliefs, and that's where you energy goes. Mars is energy. It's our
will power and vitality. Mars represents our ability to take action
towards something. It also represents the anger we carry within us,
as a soldier needs to have a certain level of anger within him to
fight and win the battle. In this day and age, Mars represents
policemen, soldiers, athletes, warlords, arms dealers, and fighters,
along with manufacturing, accidents, injuries, and dealing with
explosives. Mars also represents male friends and the Sun

represents royalty, so these people can be in contact with friends
who are of a higher status in society, perhaps sons of politicians
and celebrities.
Venus' passion and Mars’ energy produces a very sexual,
seductive, and passionate lover, and someone who is dynamic in
their approach to love and relationships. This is a relationship of a
warrior and a princess. The energy of Venus is for better or worse.
The condition has to be seen by who is stronger by sign placement
and degree placement. If Venus is lower in degrees than Mars,
then Venus will influence Mars more in the conjunction. If Mars
has the lowest degrees, then Mars will control Venus; throwing his
energy, roughness, and aggressive behavior on the princess. This is
masculine and feminine energy coming together, which is a perfect
blend of James Bond waiting at a bar looking tough and rugged yet
the most desirable. He may be a military trained secret spy, but has
a heart of gold.
Just like when the Sun brightens the qualities of other planets in
conjunction, Mars infuses energy into the other planet. This
infusing is not just for sexual purposes, but also professionally. It
can enhance the quality of Venus which is related to acting, art,
design, beauty, fashion, and hotel management. Or Venus can
influence Mars by making Mars a bit more sexual, lovable, and
calm in their approach to work, war, and ethics. You have to see
who is winning the war, and by how much. The debilitation and
exaltation in this factor of either planet can lead to two different
spectrums of sexuality. One can make a person a sexual yet
committed partner. On the flip side, it can show sexual abuse or
someone who uses sex as their means of earning like prostitution,
escort services, or even porn. To judge the effect of sexual
transformation, one needs to look at the house and nakshatra
placement of these planets.

## Venus and Jupiter conjunction:

What is Jupiter?
Jupiter is the knowledge we gain in this lifetime. It is the
concentration we put into learning something. Jupiter is the
teaching of our father and educators. Since the father is the first
teacher for a child, Jupiter automatically becomes the teaching and
preaching of fathers and father figures. Jupiter also represents our
belief system and our ability to follow the law. Jupiter is the
lawyer in astrology; he writes the law and either makes the person
follow it or resent it depending upon its condition. Jupiter also
represents a husband in a female's chart. Mars is not the husband,
Mars represents male friends. Jupiter is the guiding force in every
woman's life. He is also wisdom. We learn from our teachers,
whether its Nazi theology, Christian theology or Vedic theology.
The source of wisdom and belief is controlled by Jupiter in our
chart. Our higher learning is dependent upon Jupiter, from basic
education to a Master's degree and PhD. It represents the rituals of
our culture, our traditions, and ability to follow them. Jupiter is the
biggest planet in our solar system, which is why it represents the
expansion of things related to where Jupiter sits in your horoscope.
Now if that expansion will be positive or negative, early or late,
depends upon other factors and planets. Whenever Jupiter is in the
ascendant, 5th House and 9th House, it shows a person is capable
of learning several languages.
When Venus and Jupiter are in conjunction, it shows the expansion
of love, relationships, and beauty at a higher and more spiritual
level which has a purpose. Jupiter is like the Junior Sun; it expands
everything it sits with and the quality of such things. This
conjunction also indicates someone who searches for love with a
bit of a philosophical view, higher knowledge, and someone who
is mature like a teacher. These qualities are attractive to Venus.
Their sense of pleasure comes from attaining higher knowledge

and wisdom. Their source of passion in life comes from a partner
who can guide them with the type of wisdom they seek.
If these people do not grow in their relationship through the
sharing of higher wisdom, then their relationship becomes
meaningless. Since both of these planets represent wealth in
astrology, there is a strong indication of a wealthy person or one
who possesses a lot of value in life. However, it may not be
monetary; it could also be knowledge. Such combinations in
kendra and trikona show a great deal of wealth through finance
and art. Jupiter expands the creativity and artistic talent of Venus,
where the artistic expression becomes spiritual. This native might
create art as an expression of spirituality, relations, and wisdom.
As far as sexual drive is concerned, Mars must be reviewed. If
Mars and Venus are kendra from each other, or Mars aspects
Venus, then it can bring sexual energy that gets expanded through
the help of Jupiter.

## Venus and Saturn Conjunction:

What is Saturn?
Saturn is our limitation in life. It sets boundaries to what we can
and cannot achieve. It's the wake up call, as Saturn shows you the
truth and reality of life. Most of us are walking around day
dreaming, and have an illusion of our elicit future. But during
Saturn's dasha, and Saturn’s transit, we are awakened by the harsh
reality that exists around us. We are forced to be more practical
and disciplined about our goals rather than searching for them on
an esoteric level.
When Venus and Saturn are in conjunction, it produces a person
that has realistic ideas about love and relationships and is
grounded, and made for rock-solid commitment. Saturn's mature

and antique impact on Venus makes it crave a mature and older
partner, especially for a male. Venus is the karaka of marriage for
both sexes. It also shows a delay of marriage up until 28 or over,
but only in a tight conjunction. Due to Saturn's malefic nature, he
is still not allowed to spare his best friend Venus. In a male's
young adulthood, he may suffer several heart breaks due to
Saturn's nature to teach through karmic law. If Saturn is in
conjunction with Venus and aspects or sits in the 7th House, then it
can also cause a late marriage of at least 32 years of age or later. If
Saturn is retrograde, it can reverse that effect and show a timely
marriage, especially if it's in an exalted sign like Aquarius.
Since Saturn represents fear, this native may be hesitant in getting
into relationship too quickly for fear of breaking up. This becomes
a tug of war when both Venus and Saturn mature up into the mid
30's. This combination also shows lot of creative talent in a person,
especially when it comes to using their hands. Since Saturn
represents manual jobs and Venus represents art, this can create an
artistic craftsman ranging from sewing, designing, painting, and
computer animation, to physical art like acting. The art of this
native would be of an old soul; someone who might be ahead of
their time creatively. If this conjunction occurs in any upachaya
house like 3, 6, 10 and 11, it shows a great deal of wealth.
However, even in other houses it can show huge amount of wealth
if one of these planets rule money.
Due to Saturn's nature of showing where our challenges are, it can
demonstrate that with characteristics represented by Venus like
relationships, ability to love, art, beauty, and creativity. Saturn
also shows where we have to be organized and become more
disciplined. These natives have to be quite patient regarding love
affairs and creativity, as there will be challenges early in life which
will eventually be overcome by disciplined and hard work.
One thing to remember is that this conjunction makes a person
more realistic about romance and love. They won't be so keen

about proposing on Valentine’s Day, singing their girlfriend a song
with a guitar in front of their window, or bringing roses on the first
date. They want to be real and act how they would six months
from now. If Venus is in her own sign of Taurus or Libra, then
Venus can overcome the strict rules set by Saturn and be a bit
more romantic.
More than the Sun, Saturn makes Venus creative because they are
best friends. Even with his harsh touch on Venus, he does leave
Venus with a blessing of tremendous creativity.

## Venus and Rahu conjunction:

Who is Rahu?
When Venus and Rahu are in conjunction, it shows amplification
and ballooning of things related to love, relationships, and
luxurious items and desires for a rich and fancy life. This
conjunction can make a person obsessed with the idea of
relationships and love. They travel to far reaching places to be with
someone, and if this means breaking taboos and cultural
boundaries they will do it. Since Rahu shows foreign items and
ideas, love and relationships can occur with someone who is of a
different cultural background or someone living in foreign lands.
Rahu likes to blow things out of proportion, and due to his nature
of not being satisfied, this person has a hard time fulfilling their
desire for a perfect partner who can provide security, love, and
physical closeness. They will always need their partner's attention,
love, and care. When they don't receive that, it can drive them to
have an affair which could eventually lead to divorce. These
people love the idea of a perfect relationship and they tend to find
it in the first thing they see. It's hard for these folks to be single for
a long time since Venus’ desire for relationships is amplified by
Rahu.

If Venus and Rahu occur in a bad sign and house, it can lead a
person, especially female, to turn pleasure into a profession. This
includes prostitution. A couple of other planetary positions must
also come into play to trigger this type of behavior. Either a female
suffers abuse which leads to such actions, or a female's mind might
find this type of work fascinating. This can easily lead to someone
in the limelight, and becoming an iconic figure because Rahu and
Venus both love the attention and glamorous lifestyle.
This is not an ideal conjunction to be with if you like a quiet and
private life. In certain houses, this can also show gains of huge
amounts of wealth and assets. A person can go into the jewelry
business, fashion, modeling and designing, while at the same time
breaking taboos in their industry.

Women with these conjunctions are quite magnetic and attractive,
especially when this conjunction occurs in the 1st, 2nd and 7th
House. A man with this conjunction can easily fall for women and
become a player in the early years of his life, at least until the age
of 42, when Rahu's desire tends to calm down.

## Venus and Ketu Conjunction:

What is Ketu?
Ketu is the south node of the Moon and the rest of the body of
Rahu. This is a headless body which represents separation,
isolation, and the abandonment of the material world. This shadow
planet represents spirituality, nothingness, and goals that we
already achieved in past lives. In this life we are here to only rely
on those things as a last resort. The house and sign in which Ketu
sits are in things we are not fond of, and we do not obsess about
those things as much because subconsciously we know we have
already dealt with them. If we didn't accomplish those things in a

past life, that would be seen by Ketu's bad placement in a bad sign.
This will show a tug of war between both Rahu and Ketu. Ketu
also searches for truth and enlightenment. Ketu's aspect and
conjunction gives research ability to a person especially when it's
in conjunction or aspects the Moon and Mercury.
When Venus and Ketu come in conjunction, there tends to be a
great deal of directionless love and sensual pleasure. The native
will feel dissatisfied not because of lack of love and lust, but lack
of understanding when it comes to the value of relationships.
Unlike Rahu, who is never satisfied with love and lust, here Venus
feels unsatisfied due to lack of feeling or desire for a union or
relationship. These people tend to force themselves into
relationships, but eventually come out walking alone.
The directionless Ketu takes Venus into many directions in love
and relationships before Venus finally realizes that there is no
simple way to reaching ones soul mate. No matter how hard the
spouse works or how loving their boyfriend or girlfriend are, they
always tend think they are the ones who are sacrificing in the
relationship, because Venus becomes blind with Ketu; it cannot
see things clearly until it's too late.
This desensitization can also lead to porn and sex addiction since
the feeling behind such privately preserved acts will no longer be
valid. This is why Rahu and Ketu are the same yet quite different
in approach. Strippers, porn stars, and women in fashion who do
take part in extensive drug use also have such conjunction.
Venus is pleasure while Ketu is the act of letting of worldly
pleasures go. The tug of war between them damages the sensual
part of Venus. After many disappoints in love, they tend to view
love and relationships as an object rather than a union of two souls.

## Mercury and Mars conjunction:

Who is Mars?
Let's talk about Mars. Mars is a soldier who stands for his beliefs.
Wherever it is placed in your horoscope, you stand for those
beliefs, and that's where you energy goes. Mars is energy. It's our
will power and vitality. Mars represents our ability to take action
towards something. It also represents the anger we carry within us,
as a soldier needs to have a certain level of anger within him to
fight and win the battle. In this day and age, Mars represents
policemen, soldiers, athletes, warlords, arms dealers, and fighters,
along with manufacturing, accidents, injuries, and dealing with
explosives. Mars also represents male friends and the Sun
represents royalty, so these people can be in contact with friends
who are of a higher status in society, perhaps sons of politicians
and celebrities.
What is Mercury?
Mercury is known as the prince in Vedic astrology. He is the royal
messenger for the king. He delivers the important messages of the
king to others. He is the karaka of communication of all kinds.
Mercury also represents speech and our inner playfulness, since
princes love to party and have a good time. But, since he is a royal,
he takes his duty as the messenger seriously. This is why Mercury
is also intelligence, because in order for anyone to have good
communication, one needs to be intelligent. Mercury is also our
business and management skills due to his calculative and logical
nature and love for the marketplace. "Mind games", is another way
to describe this conjunction. The intelligence of the native needs
constant stimulation, and it's not a surprise to see them working
even when there is no need to do so. They play brain games, read
books, and solve problems.

When the aggressiveness of Mars joins the karaka of speech, the
speaking ability of the native becomes aggressive, shrewd, and to
the point. Mars’ nature of conquering and Mercury's nature to
communicate and increase intelligence makes this person sharp
and analytical. This also helps in competitive debates and
discussions in personal and professional life. They have the gift of
convincing people about their ideas and schemes. This conjunction
excites the nervous system and they become enthusiastic about
every idea that comes into their mind. Simply put, Mars energizes
the intelligence of a person, but depending upon where this
conjunction occurs, it can be a blessing or a blunder. The blunder
occurs when either one of these are debilitated, and both are
debilitated in water signs. If Mars is in Cancer, and Mercury in
Pisces, the intelligence of this person becomes extremely defensive
and they may act impulsively. They do not think before taking
action. If they are offended, they strike without assessing if
physical or verbal violence is necessary. In Cancer, Mars
constantly defends his beliefs and the intelligence of Mercury
justifies Mars' violent actions. It's always a good thing to have
these two earth signs grounded and stabilized.

## Mercury and Jupiter conjunction:

What is Jupiter?
Jupiter is the knowledge we gain in this lifetime. It is the
concentration we put into learning something. Jupiter is the
teaching of our father and educators. Since the father is the first
teacher for a child, Jupiter automatically becomes the teaching and
preaching of fathers and father figures. Jupiter also represents our
belief system and our ability to follow the law. Jupiter is the
lawyer in astrology; he writes the law and either makes the person
follow it or resent it depending upon its condition. Jupiter also
represents a husband in a female's chart. Mars is not the husband,
Mars represents male friends. Jupiter is the guiding force in every

woman's life. He is also wisdom. We learn from our teachers,
whether its Nazi theology, Christian theology or Vedic theology.
The source of wisdom and belief is controlled by Jupiter in our
chart. Our higher learning is dependent upon Jupiter, from basic
education to a Master's degree and PhD. It represents the rituals of
our culture, our traditions, and ability to follow them. Jupiter is the
biggest planet in our solar system, which is why it represents the
expansion of things related to where Jupiter sits in your horoscope.
Now if that expansion will be positive or negative, early or late,
depends upon other factors and planets. Whenever Jupiter is in the
ascendant, 5th House and 9th House, it shows a person is capable
of learning several languages.
When Venus and Jupiter are in conjunction, it shows the expansion
of love, relationships, and beauty at a higher and more spiritual
level which has a purpose. Jupiter is like the Junior Sun; it expands
everything it sits with and the quality of such things. This
conjunction also indicates someone who searches for love with a
bit of philosophical view, higher knowledge, and someone who is
mature like a teacher. These qualities are attractive to Venus. Their
sense of pleasure comes from attaining higher knowledge and
wisdom. Their source of passion in life comes from a partner who
can guide them with the type of wisdom they seek.
Mercury is our intelligence, and Jupiter is our knowledge and
wisdom of this world. The combination of both produces a natural
teacher that thrives on every aspect of communication. They are
natural born gurus whose quick intellectual speech and
communication has depth of wisdom and optimism. A
conversation with such personalities turns into a late night tea
party. Their wisdom mixed with charming speech make them a
magnet for philosophical, religious, and new age people. They are
able to share wisdom with a spunky style. They know how, what,
and when to say something. They are the one who have big ideas
about the world and how to make it better. However, just like a

professor of business in graduate school, who is able to teach yet
has never run a multimillion dollar company, this conjunction
produces someone who may have the right blueprint of a big idea
but the implementation of it will end up eating dust on their desk.
These natives love creating a blueprint of genius that may change
the lives of others, but they leave the task to do such things to
someone who has a knack for the real world. They would rather sit
in their office reading the Vedas, Mark Twain, and Edger Allen
Poe. They rather study up on theorems in mathematics than apply
them to an engineering invention. It would be ignorant to think
they are all talk and no action. Everyone is born with Mars in their
horoscope, and depending upon their Mars, they may actually take
action.
Besides education and higher learning, they also excel in finance.
The analytical and calculative ability of Mercury with the
optimism of Jupiter in dealing with money makes them great
financial gurus and analysts. They can be great stock brokers or
genius accountants. They also make great trial lawyers. Jupiter is
law and Mercury is speech, so these people are able to effectively
make their case. Their knowledge, wisdom and eruptive speech
often wins the heart of a jury.
Public speaking would be one of their best bets, as having the
planet of intelligence, knowledge, and optimism is one way to
guarantee the victory of crowds. President Barack Obama has this
mutual aspect between Jupiter and Mercury which again influences
each other like a conjunction.

## Mercury and Saturn conjunction:

What is Saturn?

Saturn is our limitation in life. It sets boundaries to what we can
and cannot achieve. It's the wake up call, as Saturn shows you the
truth and reality of life. Most of us are walking around day
dreaming, and have an illusion of our elicit future. But during
Saturn's dasha, and Saturn’s transit, we are awakened by the harsh
reality that exists around us. We are forced to be more practical
and disciplined about our goals rather than searching for them on
an esoteric level.
When Mercury and Saturn are in conjunction, there is great order,
intelligence, speech, and communicative ability. The speech and
communicative ability becomes conservative and strict. Mercury
thinks
hard
before
speaking
because
the
quickness
of
communication slows down due to Saturn. Such communication
can also be quite rare, and the person might be quiet most of the
time. Saturn likes to speak only when it matters, and Mercury just
wants to talk. This combination not only delays the speaking
ability of a child, but can make them shy of speaking, especially in
their early days.
Saturn is a cold, distant, and dark place while Mercury is the
closest planet to the bright Sun. This can eclipse a person's
youthfulness that comes from Mercury. The native will be
disciplined, intelligent, and critical, as they will pick on small
mistakes others make, especially at the work place where
everything must be perfect and precise. This quality can also help
the person in the business world. Both Mercury and Saturn do well
in the 7th House which is business, and Saturn's discipline and eye
for detail combined with Mercury's ability to sell, communicate,
and calculate make for a very good businessman who never lets a
penny get away. To get the better side of this conjunction, one has
to see which sign and which house these two are placed in, and the
houses they both control in the horoscope. Are they functional
malefic or functional benefic for the native?

Saturn, being a slow planet, combined with Mercury the karaka of
writing, can also produce a novelist whose ability to write a 600
page book is a walk in the park. Mercury's quick nature to move
from one task to the other slows down, especially with age. This
conjunction can also slow the mind down and the learning process
down, and produce a person who may not be interested in studies
if in an unfriendly or debilitated sign for either planet. Saturn's
nature is to bring fear and anxiety upon a person combined with
Mercury in conjunction, brings fear to the native’s speech and
communication, as in "Did I say that correctly? Was I supposed to
say that? I wonder if I should've said that at all." Due to this
thinking pattern, they are very self critical and self aware of their
communicative ability, constantly correcting and thinking about
what could've been done. This is a great conjunction for detailed
work that many might not be able to handle. Saturn and Mercury
are best friends, so the negative part is rarely felt, which can lead
to one cheating in business or withholding valuable information
for their own benefit.

## Mercury and Rahu conjunction:

Who is Rahu?
Rahu is the north node of the Moon, and is a head without a body
that keeps devouring things without ever being satisfied. It has a
desire for material things and worldly accomplishments. It wants
to achieve the highest success possible in whatever house and sign
he represents. Rahu represents the goals that have been set by the
universe for us to achieve whether we like or not. Rahu is mainly
known as the eclipse, and it eclipses the Moon and the Sun
whenever it's in conjunction with them. This means temporary
darkness upon the native's life when the Sun and Rahu are in
conjunction in a birth horoscope. Rahu also brings fear and
stressful situations into our lives. This is due to Rahu's adventurous
nature that loves surprises. With Rahu, sudden events take place;

events that may not be a real part of our lives but rather an illusion
that becomes reality. Rahu is taboo breaking and crosses all kinds
of boundaries. It wants to be unique and break tradition; to think
outside the box. He is also responsible for spiritual breakthroughs,
because only after achieving material wealth does one seeks God.
When Mercury and Rahu come into conjunction, communication,
speech, intelligence, and the skills of one’s hands are amplified
and expanded quickly and drastically. Rahu likes Mercury due to
his neutral, nonjudgmental nature. This native will have an
explosive, exotic, and unique form of speech, and will talk about
things that might be very unconventional for the real world. They
are extremely clever and have excellent communication skills
which enable them to do anything they like, especially in the fields
of sales, marketing, public relations, and public speaking. They
have the gift of uniqueness, and their approach to life and its
blueprint are unconventional and taboo breaking.
Rahu acts like Jupiter when it comes in conjunction with other
planets. Like Jupiter expands the quality of other planets, Rahu,
also does the same but at a larger, quicker, scale. Jupiter expands
the sawtic qualities in a planet while Rahu expands either rajsic or
tamasic qualities of planets. Rahu makes Mercury's intelligence
not just quick, but witty and clever; clever in a sense that Mercury
is able to get what he wants when he wants from people even if the
other is not ready to give it. They are natural salesmen who will
take your money without you ever knowing you gave it to them.
Jupiter enhances the pure spiritual quality, whereas Mercury will
think of what he is doing to the other before taking advantage of
them. In today's world of 2013 and beyond, it is very necessary to
be more like Rahu than Jupiter. After all, this is the age of Rahu.
When speaking publicly, these people might talk about sex, drugs,
women, and pleasure with a sense of spirituality. They will be able
to justify and sell the idea of breaking taboos to their audience. If
they write a book, it might be about promiscuous affairs, sex,

interracial connections, and foreign lands. This not only makes a
clever salesman, but a genius computer programmer and
mathematician because Rahu in this age also represents computers
along with quick calculations. If you are looking for someone to
sell all the cars in your lot, find a person with Rahu and Mercury
conjunction in the 3rd, 6th or 10th House where salesmanship is at
its best.

## Mercury and Ketu conjunction:

Who is Ketu?
Ketu is the south node of the Moon and the rest of the body of
Rahu. This is a headless body which represents separation,
isolation, and the abandonment of the material world. This shadow
planet represents spirituality, nothingness, and goals that we
already achieved in past lives. In this life we are here to only rely
on those things as a last resort. The house and sign in which Ketu
sits are in things we are not fond of, and we do not obsess about
those things as much because subconsciously we know we have
already dealt with them. If we didn't accomplish those things in a
past life, that would be seen by Ketu's bad placement in a bad sign.
This will show a tug of war between both Rahu and Ketu. Ketu
also searches for truth and enlightenment. Ketu's aspect and
conjunction gives research ability to a person especially when it's
in conjunction or aspects the Moon and Mercury.
When Mercury, the logical, rational, analytical, and calculative
planet comes into contact with esoteric, other worldly dimensions
and figures without direction, it makes their intelligence wacky,
weird, and sometimes problematic. This can produce a sheer
genius of spirituality, psychic ability, and wisdom of alternative
healing. It can also give a person a split personality and irrational
thoughts. In Einstein's horoscope, Ketu in the 2nd House aspects
the 10th House where his Mercury was, and if you know a little bit

about Einstein, his thoughts, logic, and philosophy were wacky.
Most of his theories were a blunder of philosophy, physics, and art,
rather than of logic, calculation, and analysis. However, due to the
presence of other planets and yogas, it drove him towards success.
Since Ketu represents searching, as in a search for the truth and
spirituality, it also gives a person research ability. Through its
aspect on Mercury from the 2nd House, it gave Einstein a love for
research. Aspects in astrology are bit like conjunctions, but there is
a chance that only one party is infusing the other rather than both
infusing their qualities onto each other.
This conjunction makes a person access their intelligence in other
worlds and dimensions. Some may view this as schizophrenia or
psychosis; others may believe Ketu helps Mercury and that these
natives possess powers. If such a conjunction occurs in 8th or 12th
House, then the probability of having actual powers increases in
water signs. Since Ketu is the reflection of Mars, the speech of this
person can be vulgar and harsh; it can suddenly provoke a dirty
mouth within them. Their speech is quite aggressive and
directionless. If Mercury is exalted, then their speech may be
logical with the backing of eccentric spiritual talk.
This conjunction also shows an electronics engineer or someone
who is interested in electronics since Ketu represents electricity
and electronics in this day and age.

## Mars and Jupiter conjunction:

Who is Mars?
Mars is a soldier who stands for his beliefs. Wherever it is placed
in your horoscope, you stand for those beliefs, and that's where
you energy goes. Mars is energy. It's our will power and vitality.
Mars represents our ability to take action towards something. It
also represents the anger we carry within us, as a soldier needs to

have a certain level of anger within him to fight and win the battle.
In this day and age, Mars represents policemen, soldiers, athletes,
warlords, arms dealers, and fighters, along with manufacturing,
accidents, injuries, and dealing with explosives. Mars also
represents male friends and the Sun represents royalty, so these
people can be in contact with friends who are of a higher status in
society, perhaps sons of politicians and celebrities.
This conjunction actually forms a yoga in Vedic astrology, known
as guru mangal yoga; the Guru (Jupiter) and Mangal (Mars) equals
Yoga (union).
When Jupiter and Mars come into conjunction, it forms a warrior
with guidance and knowledge. These spiritual natives have true
leadership qualities; where straightforward action and practical
knowledge of the world guides them in the right direction. They
are not just warriors; they are the summaries, who have the
wisdom and knowledge to determine when, how, and who to fight.
They are guided by a higher power to become the savior and
protector of knowledge and guidance. However, due to Mars'
quality, these natives will punish people if their guidance is
neglected and rules are not followed. They become aggressive in
their actions towards teaching something. They speak with
authority and purpose because Jupiter is the karaka of 2nd House
along with Mercury that shows speaking ability. These natives talk
loudly because a warrior wants to make sure his voice and actions
are heard.
Due to having two separate qualities in conjunction, one which is
of a warrior, tamasic and the other of a teacher, sawtic, these
people can have multiple sources of income if the conjunction
occurs in all the wealth houses like 1st, 2nd, 5th, 9th and 11th.
There is a lot of energy within these people, and having such a
yoga in a horoscope can produce one who loves competitive sports
and competition.

## Mars and Saturn Conjunction:

When Mars and Saturn are in conjunction, there is nothing but fire
and explosions. Mars is a hot, dry, and fiery planet. Saturn is a
cold, dark and reserved planet. Mars is known to represents the
element of fire while Saturn represents oil. When fire meets oil
there is nothing but more fire.
Mars is a soldier of the army while Saturn is a general of the army.
When a soldier and general are in the same house, it symbolizes a
war zone; only inside a war zone will you find a general and
soldier at the same time. This conjunction can cause physical harm
to the native like accidents, cuts, and burns. It can also cause one
to harm others which can lead to stabbings, shootings, rape, and
killing by any means. As much as this is the most negative
conjunction in the zodiac, it can also produce a very skilled
businessman who has a forceful yet disciplined nature and can get
things done and achieved in a timely manner.
There is tendency of being a natural born rebel. These individuals
are very independent and do not like following the law; this is why
they get in legal trouble most of their life. There is a lot of tension
and fear in the house where these two reside. I've seen quite a few
charts of people who have bipolar disorder, hypertension disorder,
and excessive anger. There is a constant war happening in the area
of life where this conjunction occurs. The amount of tension with
this conjunction will be reduced when these two are in earth or air
signs. In fire signs, it is the worst, and in water signs there is a
deep sense of being an emotional time bomb.

## Mars and Rahu conjunction:

Who is Rahu?

Rahu is the north node of the Moon, and is a head without a body
that keeps devouring things without ever being satisfied. It has a
