desire for material things and worldly accomplishments. It wants
to achieve the highest success possible in whatever house and sign
he represents. Rahu represents the goals that have been set by the
universe for us to achieve whether we like or not. Rahu is mainly
known as the eclipse, and it eclipses the Moon and the Sun
whenever it's in conjunction with them. This means temporary
darkness upon the native's life when the Sun and Rahu are in
conjunction in a birth horoscope. Rahu also brings fear and
stressful situations into our lives. This is due to Rahu's adventurous
nature that loves surprises. With Rahu, sudden events take place;
events that may not be a real part of our lives but rather an illusion
that becomes reality. Rahu is taboo breaking and crosses all kinds
of boundaries. It wants to be unique and break tradition; to think
outside the box. He is also responsible for spiritual breakthroughs,
because only after achieving material wealth does one seeks God.

When Rahu and Mars come in conjunction, there is an
amplification of independence in a person with an extremely
aggressive nature. This strong willed personality will stop at
nothing to achieve its goal. The impulsiveness in a person
increases dramatically, and can also lead to an excessive sex drive
or sex addiction. But this is a great conjunction for a soldier,
policeman, and athlete. Mars energy of taking physical action is
amplified through Rahu to an extreme.
This is also a very aggressive personality that doesn't back down
from a fight. Even if they are not the right size for a fair fight, they
become like a pit bull. They will go full charge without realizing
their own strength. This quality can be great for a boxer, wrestler,
or any other kind of athletics.

## Mars and Ketu conjunction:

Who is Ketu?
Ketu is the south node of the Moon and the rest of the body of
Rahu. This is a headless body which represents separation,
isolation, and the abandonment of the material world. This shadow
planet represents spirituality, nothingness, and goals that we
already achieved in past lives. In this life we are here to only rely
on those things as a last resort. The house and sign in which Ketu
sits are in things we are not fond of, and we do not obsess about
those things as much because subconsciously we know we have
already dealt with them. If we didn't accomplish those things in a
past life, that would be seen by Ketu's bad placement in a bad sign.
This will show a tug of war between both Rahu and Ketu. Ketu
also searches for truth and enlightenment. Ketu's aspect and
conjunction gives research ability to a person especially when it's
in conjunction or aspects the Moon and Mercury.
A Mars and Ketu conjunction produces an individual who is not
able to realize his own capacity for competition, fights, and
aggression. Ketu's headless body and desire for spirituality makes
Mars' aggressiveness, impulsivity, and action oriented tasks a risky
business. This conjunction usually represents a person who doesn't
care if they get hurt, run over, or stabbed; they are willing to risk
their body to the end to accomplish their goal. Rahu desires life,
beauty and wealth, and wants to protect the physical body
represented by Mars. But Ketu's eclipse overshadows Mars’ ability
to wear armor during war. They are willing to cheat and
manipulate the competition to win; which is also the quality of
Rahu. Since Ketu shows the things we have conquered, it doesn't
really have the desire to fight, but rather sacrifices the body so the
soul can be released. Ketu keeps throwing Mars in hot water just
so it can escape the battle and desire to conquer. This also shows
impulsive anger, like someone who does not think before stabbing
someone. They just swing the knife, and Ketu has no sense of who
he is hurting and destroying because he doesn't see anything.

Similar to how Rahu is not satisfied, and keeps craving the same
thing over and over again because there is no stomach to tell him
that he's full; Ketu makes Mars dissatisfied because it's unable to
find perfection. This is why it can lead to extramarital affairs if
such a conjunction aspects or sits in the 7th House. These natives
will not be satisfied sexually with anyone. It doesn't matter if it's
Rahu, Ketu, or Saturn. Mars has the hardest time dealing with his
actions when in the company of such malefics.

## Saturn and Jupiter Conjunction:

What is Saturn?
Saturn is our limitation in life. It sets boundaries to what we can
and cannot achieve. It's the wake up call, as Saturn shows you the
truth and reality of life. Most of us are walking around day
dreaming, and have an illusion of our elicit future. But during
Saturn's dasha, and Saturn’s transit, we are awakened by the harsh
reality that exists around us. We are forced to be more practical
and disciplined about our goals rather than searching for them on
an esoteric level.
What is Jupiter?
Jupiter is the knowledge we gain in this lifetime. It is the
concentration we put into learning something. Jupiter is the
teaching of our father and educators. Since the father is the first
teacher for a child, Jupiter automatically becomes the teaching and
preaching of fathers and father figures. Jupiter also represents our
belief system and our ability to follow the law. Jupiter is the
lawyer in astrology; he writes the law and either makes the person
follow it or resent it depending upon its condition. Jupiter also
represents a husband in a female's chart. Mars is not the husband,
Mars represents male friends. Jupiter is the guiding force in every
woman's life. He is also wisdom. We learn from our teachers,
whether its Nazi theology, Christian theology or Vedic theology.

The source of wisdom and belief is controlled by Jupiter in our
chart. Our higher learning is dependent upon Jupiter, from basic
education to a Master's degree and PhD. It represents the rituals of
our culture, our traditions, and ability to follow them. Jupiter is the
biggest planet in our solar system, which is why it represents the
expansion of things related to where Jupiter sits in your horoscope.
Now if that expansion will be positive or negative, early or late,
depends upon other factors and planets. Whenever Jupiter is in the
ascendant, 5th House and 9th House, it shows a person is capable
of learning several languages.
When Saturn, the delayer and judge, is in conjunction with the
guru, law, and optimistic sage, it makes for a very old soul. These
are your true judges, lawyers, and law-abiding citizens. But there
is a tug of war between the two. One brings darkness and misery
based on karma and the other gives optimism, light, wealth, and
happiness due to past life karma. The conjunction of both gives a
native limited access to optimism, wealth, knowledge, and
happiness. However, in order to reap the results of the fruits given
by Jupiter, they must be disciplined, orderly, patient, and most of
all, respectful. Both of these planets promote respect and honoring
the elderly. Like with the Sun and Saturn, there is a sense of duty
in one’s life. This combination brings a sense of responsibility for
one to acquire wisdom and knowledge in life; especially when it
comes to laws and following a riotous path.
President Obama has this conjunction in his ascendant, which
makes a disciplined, focused, and diligent worker who brings
optimism and hope for the future of the lower class. It was no
surprise that he became a lawyer and a professor of constitutional
law at University of Chicago. Jupiter and Saturn give a sense of
responsibility related to the house they dwell in, and the house they
are lords of. This makes the native a very serious student and
seeker of knowledge, and depending upon the sign they are placed
in, it can make them a seeker of spirituality.

## Saturn and Rahu conjunction:

When Saturn and Rahu are in conjunction, there is a battle between
restrictive Saturn and the obsession of getting everything now by
Rahu. This war between the two great malefic creates fear and
anxiety about losing what you have and getting what you want. In
this conjunction, the rule breaker and taboo breaker, Rahu meets
his arch nemesis Saturn, who brings the practice of law and order.
This creates a foreign law administrator, or someone who applies
the law of foreign lands into their own life; perhaps even becoming
a lawyer in foreign country. However, do not think that this person
will be the most honest lawyer. Rahu's eagerness to break laws and
regulations make them a cunning and willful liar. They can be
caught trying to make an illegal deal or trying to escape from the
law. Since fear and anxiety is a norm with these two planets, it can
also lead to mental stress problems and eventually harming
oneself. There is always some agenda hidden beneath the surface
with these people. There is never a straight forward approach to
something unless it benefits them. This is why they can make great
real estate brokers, stock brokers or someone in finance that makes
deals with two foreign parties.
This is not a good combination in the 8th or 12th House, as it can
lead to suddenly being caught for illegal activity and even going to
jail for their misdeed. In this 6th House, it gives great courage to
fight the opposition, as any malefic planet does well in the
upachaya house. But depending upon who is influencing who in
the conjunction, it can either lead to victory or great defeat. Also in
the 12th House, it can lead to seeing things like ghost and demons,
but this only occurs in certain zodiac signs and nakshatras which I
will not discuss. Many people are born within this period and do
not see much effect. Saturn and Rahu are in conjunction for a
maximum of 18 months. They usually travel to foreign lands in
most of the houses, as Rahu and Saturn's aspect see the houses that
represent foreign lands such as the 7th, 8th, 9th and 12th House.

Most who are born in a tight conjunction can expect to have the
qualities which I just discussed.
Both of these planets represents foreign things because Rahu is the
karaka of foreign things while Saturn separates one from their
homeland or family and takes them to foreign places.

## Saturn and Ketu conjunction:

Who is Ketu?
Ketu is the south node of the Moon and the rest of the body of
Rahu. This is a headless body which represents separation,
isolation, and the abandonment of the material world. This shadow
planet represents spirituality, nothingness, and goals that we
already achieved in past lives. In this life we are here to only rely
on those things as a last resort. The house and sign in which Ketu
sits are in things we are not fond of, and we do not obsess about
those things as much because subconsciously we know we have
already dealt with them. If we didn't accomplish those things in a
past life, that would be seen by Ketu's bad placement in a bad sign.
This will show a tug of war between both Rahu and Ketu. Ketu
also searches for truth and enlightenment. Ketu's aspect and
conjunction gives research ability to a person especially when it's
in conjunction or aspects the Moon and Mercury.
When Saturn and Ketu come in conjunction, it creates a
detachment from responsibility and makes one question their
dharma, or work in life. Ketu's quality of detachment from the
world comes in contact with the heavy burden of responsibility and
duty of Saturn. This confuses the native and causes self doubt
about their destiny and vocation. They don't truly understand their
social position and cannot fully grasp the profound change they are
bringing to the world and society. This may even create a bit of
conflict with the masses, as their eccentric thoughts and behavior

in society will be unacceptable. Due to Ketu's nature to dissolve
itself into spirituality and enlightenment, one tends to eventually
go the spiritual route and tries to convert others to follow them,
which is where the major trouble lies for the native.
These individuals are never attached to their work or their place in
society, and tend to leave their positions later in life before
retirement. One of the best features of this conjunction is that the
native has the ability to become a mystic and seeker of the occult
world. Saturn is the Lord of the dark world while Ketu is the Lord
of the other realm. When in conjunction, it creates a bridge
between two worlds where the native's soul can teleport after
learning its karmic lesson and paying off its karmic debt.
Detachment from society can also lead to prison. Depending upon
the sign these two are placed in, especially in 6th and 12th House,
it can take a person to isolated, dark, and dry places, including
prisons and asylums. Just like Rahu, Ketu creates fear and illusion
within a person, and here it is more of a delusion. People with this
conjunction can see ghosts, figures, and other types of entities in
their lives, but only in a certain sign and nakshatra. Millions of
people have this conjunction, and usually it leads them to a
spiritual or mystical path rather than prison; especially in the 12th
house.

## Jupiter and Rahu conjunction:

This conjunction is known as guru chandal yoga. The Guru
(teacher) and Chandal (demon) create this Yoga (union).
Jupiter, the optimistic preacher of higher values comes in contact
with the demon of lower values plagued with lust, greed and envy.
No matter how shiny the diamond, a black spot will always lower
its value whether the blemish can be removed or not. This is what
happens to the cultured, righteous, law-abiding, and ritual

following Jupiter. The taboo and rule breaking Rahu molds
himself into Jupiter's value, and what comes out this union is a
person who not only disregards
culture, value, rules, and
regulations, but wants to implement their own uniquely
constructed laws for society and the world.
These are two planets that represent expansion and amplification
of houses they sit in, and when two explosives sit together, it can
only create a more powerful explosion. This conjunction is that of
a true scientist, skeptic, mystic, and researcher. These are the folks
who ask "Who is Jesus? Who wrote the Bible? Why is there
religion? Who said we need religion to survive?" This out of the
box thinker truly uses the world “Glocal.” The global world needs
to become local in order to take the next leap forward for
humanity.
Rahu's rush to amplify its quality while at the same time Jupiter’s
slow but profound way of expanding things becomes a
battleground for one’s belief. At a younger age, these natives take
immature actions towards their goals. They will do homework half
as fast, eat food too quickly, and will follow through with ideas
without thinking about the consequence. .But Jupiter is that one
planet that can truly control Rahu. This is why Rahu's malefic
behavior has control in Jupiter's hands. If it gets this native in hot
water for their actions and beliefs, it will not let them drown due to
Jupiter's blessings.
In their 30's, the idea of marriage, unions, and following cultural
rules take quite a turn. They might not want to legally tie the knot
due to fear brought on by Rahu and Jupiter, which in a woman's
case represents a husband. These men and women become afraid
of commitment especially if this conjunction takes place in 1st,
2nd, 4th and 7th House. Rahu feels he is tied down by a culture-
bound Jupiter who actually believes in the lawfulness of union.
When these natives believe in something, it's of their own making,
especially if they believe in occult like astrology, magic, secret

societies, and rituals of the underground. This combination
produces a mindset that is comfortable having children out of
wedlock. Financially, this is a very good combination especially in
2nd and 11th House.

## Jupiter and Ketu Conjunction:

A true seeker of spirituality, higher learning, enlightenment, and
scientific research is seen from this conjunction. Though Ketu's
nature is rejecting anything from this material plain, including
gurus and religion, here Ketu finds a sense of purpose with Jupiter.
He finds a guide to perhaps create some understanding of the
material plain. But Ketu does have a problem with the guidelines
and laws put forth by Jupiter, and is not able to relate to them. He
rejects organized religion and preaching by men. Ketu wants to
look beyond the cycle of life and death which is defined by
religion.
Jupiter is also the karaka of wealth, children, network circles, and
gains. Ketu has no interest in pursuing money, family and friends.
Since Rahu and Ketu do transform themselves into the planet they
are in conjunction with, Ketu wants to preach in a very unique
manner. This approach might be harsh, rude, and insulting to
others, however he has the ability to pinpoint problems even
within himself. This is why they can become well liked and
appreciated leaders. This person has a true sense of how the world
works and how the world should be. These are the true
humanitarians who want to do good for the world and who have
the keen eye to make something valuable from nothing.
Before we start on three planetary conjunctions, let's do some
example of conjunctions.

Example 1: 2 Planetary Conjunction
This is an Aquarius ascendant with Mercury and Mars in
conjunction in the 10th House, and the Sun and Venus conjunction
in the 11th House. How would you interpret this?
First, let’s look at the Mercury and Mars conjunction. They are in
the sign of Scorpio. Mars rules the 3rd and 10th House while
Mercury rules the 5th and 8th House.

This conjunction shows someone who is outspoken and quite
aggressive in their speech, especially at work. Since Mercury rules
the 5th House of children and 8th House of in-laws, joint assets,
and sudden events, you can say that this person will not only have
an aggressive speech towards their co-workers, but they will also
have an argumentative relationship with their children and in the
matter of joint assets. Both Mars and Mercury in the 10th House
show leadership. This means the native will have a top
management position due to their strong will, quick wit, and
intelligence. Mars being the lord of 3rd and 10th House shows
someone who can be a manager of a sales, media, marketing, or
publishing organization. The sign of Scorpio shows investigative
intelligence combined with will and energy to dig deep into things.
Since Mercury rules the house of the hidden, it can show someone
who is a detective, or someone who does investigative journalism.
Their creativity and communication is quite deep and intense.
When the native communicates, they only speak of the most
serious matters.
The Sun and Venus conjunction in the 11th House while in the
sign of Sagittarius suggests that their relationships are based on
strong, cultural, principles. Their creativity also has strong
dharmic qualities. Remember, the Sun enhances the qualities of
the planet it sits with; it brightens them up. Therefore, the social
network of such a person is mostly people who have a higher
educational background; especially strong masculine women, since
Venus is being infused by the masculine Sun. Venus controls the
4th House of comfort and the 9th House of higher learning and
luck. Their sense of comfort and wisdom comes from the company
of well educated, strong, people who possess guru-like qualities
due to the sign of Sagittarius in the 11th House.
However, this analysis is not enough. Let's look at Part 2 of this
example.

Example 2: 2 Planetary Conjunction
Now we see the actual degrees in this chart. What does that tell
you? The first thing you should notice is that Mercury is
influencing Mars more, and the Sun is influencing Venus more in
this chart.
How would you explain this conjunction?

This person's will power, aggressiveness, and impulsiveness in
career will occur more in their communication style and speech
rather than actual physical work. Mercury will be able to control
itself and suppress their dark, straight forward speech towards
others. Their actions will have more logic, calculation, and
intelligence. Mars won't be able to take any action without
thinking things through and analyzing what needs to be done first.
Will Mars always lose out due to the degree? No. There is always
going to be the influence of Mars upon Mercury, and vice versa.
The final say will be from the one with the lowest degrees.
When we look at the Sun and Venus conjunction, we see that the
Sun has the lowest degrees, which means that Venus is coming out
of its combustion and starting to cool. Now the Sun, instead of
burning Venus, is brightening the qualities of Venus. Knowing this
fact, we can assess that this person is quite creative. They may not
be an artist, but their sense of creativity will be seen by others.
They are also very dynamic in love and relationship encounters.
This is someone who is of a higher educational level, or at least
someone who has worldly knowledge. With these qualities, they
are able to make friends with people who have very high status.
What about the chart below? How would you read the Sun and
Venus conjunction now?

Example 3: 2 Planetary Conjunction
The Sun and Venus are sitting in the sign of Sagittarius who is
ruled by Jupiter. We see the position of Jupiter, and it's debilitated
in the sign of Capricorn in the 12th House of losses and foreign
lands. How would you assess this conjunction?

This person is creative, dynamic, and confident in relationship
matters. They love beauty. However, they can suddenly lose their
network circle due to foreign travel, and financial difficulties.
They may lose their spouse to someone from their network circle.
Though Venus is not combust, it's coming out of combustion, and
is still regaining its significance. The foundation on which the Sun
and Venus are sitting on is weak due to a debilitated depositor.
If this was a female's chart, it can show losing your husband in
foreign lands to someone from your network circle. In a man's
chart, this can show someone who suddenly left for foreign lands
and lost their status, wealth, and became estranged from his
network circle. When Jupiter is debilitated, it shows sudden gains
and losses of wealth; especially in Jupiter mahadasha and antara
dasha.
Let's go in more detail
Example 4: 2 Planetary Conjunction

We notice that the Sun and Venus are both in Mula nakshatra
which is ruled by Ketu. This can be determined by looking at their
degrees and referring back to which nakshatra rules that portion of
the zodiac sign.
With Ketu, this conjunction means that this person's creativity is
personified by spirituality and enlightenment. Their sense of
beauty and relationships is simple. They will be with someone who
has deep roots in religious and educational values. Their network
circle consists of people who might have given away their wealth
and material possessions. Since Jupiter is debilitated, this person
may do the same. Mula nakshatra represents deep roots and issues.
The Sun, ego, and Venus are about pleasure, which is being
brightened by the Sun's energy. This is about letting go of our ego
and material pleasure while going back to our roots of religion and
spirituality. Venus is in Sagittarius, so Mula nakshatra becomes a
humanitarian more than a wealthy individual.
Mula nakshatra is also about sudden fluctuations in wealth. The
lord of Sagittarius is also debilitated. Venus is in Sagittarius, or its
own nakshatra, showing divine love for many, and acceptance of
our shortcomings in this material world. Just by looking at these
planets, you can say this person does not mind losing their wealth
as long as they don't lose their sense of dharma. Depending upon
where Saturn, Moon, Rahu, and Ketu are, this can also show a
criminal who may scam others for money and end up in jail.
When we look at Mercury and Mars in conjunction, it occurs in the
nakshatra of Anuradha, which is controlled by Saturn. This
nakshatra is about learning karmic lessons through deep emotional
attachment which is constantly disrupted. It is far more disciplined,
calm, and dutiful than other signs because it is ruled by Saturn.
When Mars is placed here, it defends itself from emotional
turmoil. Mars is also exalted in the sign of Capricorn and ruled by
Saturn, creating structure and discipline.

Let's take a look at the chart below.
Example 5: 2 Planetary Conjunction
If you look at the chart below, we see the depositor of Anuradha
nakshatra, which is Saturn. Plus, Saturn is aspecting Mercury and
Mars.
How would you judge this conjunction now?
This native is a very energetic leader with aggressive and strong
speech that not only has depth, but has logic, discipline and
intelligence. Their mind is sharp, they have a strong work ethic,
and are determined to achieve their goals. Since the nakshatra lord
is in the 8th House, they can be a manager of a coal, oil, or iron
company, or a mechanical engineer, If they go through their
Mercury dasha, then it can show writers of deep, dark secrets of
the world. Their writing style would be meaningful and logical.
They
would
challenge
authority
in
their
speech
and
communication, and will try to expose the secrets suppressed by

others. Due to Saturn's aspect, the person is a workaholic,
disciplined, and careful in their speech. Mercury and Saturn have a
profound effect due to their degrees. Mercury wins the war of
conjunctions, and Saturn’s close friendship with Mercury will help
elevate Mercury and control Mars' impulsive nature. This mean
they will speak on rare occasions but their speech and
communication will have intensity, logic, and authority.
Example 6: 2 Planetary Conjunction
How would you judge the conjunction below?
This time the conjunction is between Mars and Mercury. The
nakshatra is again controlled by Saturn. The sign of Cancer, where
this conjunction is occurring, is controlled by the Moon. They are
both debilitated, including Mars.
What does Mercury and Mars in Cancer signify?
This represents impulsive, dirty, and aggressive speech which is
without warning and wasted on petty emotional conflicts. Their

poor communication is with people at work, maids, and with their
mother's siblings. The 6th House in the horoscope where Mercury
and Mars are sitting shows conflicts, enemies, diseases, and debts.
Pushya is the nakshatra that shows one who takes on the emotional
responsibility of others. Both Mars and Mercury do not do well
with emotions as one is logic and analysis, and the other is action.
The native’s intelligence is quick, aggressive, and their
communication and speech is very impulsive towards those who
pose threat to them. Since Cancer is the foundation, their state of
logic, anger, and speech is not thoroughly thought out. They get
angry without reason, and without assessing the situation. This
also shows blood and nervous system disorders. Mars shows the
flow of blood, while Mercury represents our nervous system and
intelligence and is deposited in the 6th House which is debilitated.
The nakshatra lord is debilitated as well, so the emotions are very
unbalanced. This can lead to a very physical and bloody encounter
during the dasha of any of these 4 planets. There is lot of debt due
education, career, marriage and siblings.
Why did I say verbal disagreements with the mother and mother's
family? Because the Moon (karaka of mother) is debilitated. While
the 4th House shows a mother's affection, 3rd from the 4th
becomes the 6th House, as in siblings of the mother, where
debilitated Mars and weak Mercury are sitting.
Since Mars controls the 10th House, it shows verbal abuse with co-
workers, and even physical fights with co-workers and colleagues.
This sample chart can easily pass for someone who would commit
a violent crime or might be hurt through violent crime or an illegal
career.
3 things are also occurring in this chart.
### 1. Neecha banga raj yoga of Mars
### 2. Parivertan Yoga between Mars and Moon

### 3. Virpreet Raj Yoga

## If you don't know these yogas, then you must read my book:

Astrology @ The Speed of Light.
Neecha banga raj yoga means cancelation of debilitation. But is it
a really strong neecha banga? Somewhat. It is quite powerful
because the depositor that is causing neecha banga is in the
strongest house (10th House). There is a parivertan yoga occurring
at the same time, and neither Mars nor the Moon are willing to
destroy each other houses. This yoga is not very fruitful because
both of these planets are debilitated. While one is a dushtana lord,
the other is in dushtana house. The nakshatra lord is also
debilitated. None of the yogas will be strong, and their effect will
be so light that it won't be felt at all. The mind, effort, and
discipline are all negative or tamasic. This can also show someone
in the drug dealing business which will eventually lead to jail time.
Example 7: 2 Planetary Conjunction

In Example 7, the Saturn and Sun conjunction is in the 4th House
in the sign of Taurus. The depositor of Taurus (Venus) is in the 9th
House in the moolatrikona sign.
How would you judge this conjunction?
Remember, the Sun always enhances the quality of any planet it
sits with, especially if that planet is ahead of the Sun and not
combust.
The Sun is our ego, vitality, father, personality of soul, and it's the
karaka of self and the 10th House.
Saturn is structure, discipline, delay, hard working, fear, and facing
reality.
When the Sun lights up Saturn in the conjunction, it enhances the
good qualities of that planet. That means hard work, duty,
responsibility, and discipline. This conjunction mainly signifies
duty.
The conjunction is occurring in the 4th House of home, homeland,
mother's nourishment, land, property, convenience, and education.
It's also occurring in the sign of Taurus that shows stubbornness
towards the things we value and our work ethic.
This native will have lot of responsibility and duty towards his or
her home. They will work hard to achieve a luxurious life for
themselves and their family. There will be discipline and structure
inside their home. As parents, they will be quite strict, but at the
same time reward their children for their effort. They are very
dependable people.
The depositor of this conjunction, Venus, is sitting very strongly in
its own sign in 9th House of dharma. That means serving and
maintaining discipline at home will become their dharma, and they

will devote their life to building strong structure. This also shows
someone’s mother was a disciplinarian, and took the role of the
father at home. She would provide all the comforts to the native
due to Taurus being in the 4th House, while its depositor is very
strong in the chart.
Now, let's look at the degrees. The Sun is at 16 degrees while
Saturn is at 24 degrees in Taurus. That means the Sun is in the
nakshatra of Rohini, while Saturn is in the nakshatra of Mrigasira.
Rohini is ruled by the Moon, while Mirgsaira is ruled by Mars.
Rohini represents a charging chariot, beauty, comfort, wealth, and
peace of mind. Mirgsaira represents a search for beauty,
wandering, creativity, and shyness while having the talent to sell
and be a skilled leader.
This conjunction shows the person will have a soft and calm ego.
They will be a leader at home who is not egoistic, but rather brings
creativity, discipline, and order. This is someone who will give
rewards for behaving well. They are the type of parents who will
give all the toys and gadgets to their kids for getting good grades,
keeping the house clean, and throwing out the trash every week.
They are not the Hitler of their home, but more like Bill Clinton; a
charming leader who know how to get things done. However due
to Saturn's nature to delay things, the home, balance of discipline,
and structure will take time. Perhaps in their 30's, this native will
be able to give 100% to their home life.
Since this conjunction also aspects the 10th House of career, they
are very disciplined, orderly, and structured about work. They
work hard till they have completed their project. They don't do
anything rashly, and they don't leave anything incomplete. It's
against their dharma. This can be someone in politics, home
decorating, a builder, or one who runs a home business.

Example 8: 2 Planetary Conjunction
What about now? How would you judge this conjunction?
We can still say this person is disciplined and dutiful regarding
their home, but as much as they try to be disciplined and provide a
luxurious life for their family, they seem to fail. Either they lack
finances, or their relationship with their spouse causes them to lose
balance at home. They are not very lavish because there is a
sudden fluctuation in wealth, and marriage is more about a
business deal than a romantic encounter.
If Saturn was combust by having the lowest degree, as in behind
Sun and within 8-10 degrees, it would destroy the qualities of
Saturn. The discipline, structure, and duty towards the home would
feel like a burden rather than dharma.

Example 9: 2 Planetary Conjunction
Jupiter - optimism, preaching, philosophical, law, higher education
and wealth
SaturnStructure, discipline, duty, organization, delay, fear, judge,
karma
7th Houserelationships, legal bindings, spouse, trading, and local
people
Leodynamic, creative, political, self expression, royal, and proud
When you combine these four energies together, you can
determine the meaning of such a conjunction.
This shows that the native's spouse will have a dynamic and
disciplined approach. They would be a proud preacher of wisdom,
truth, and belief. At the same time, they are quite controlling due
to Leo's nature of self expression and ego. The spouse would have
a very old and mature soul. They would become a beacon of hope
in the native's life. Due to Saturn's significance on the house, the

marriage would be delayed, and the optimistic nature of their
spouse would be grounded in reality. Since the 7th House planets
aspect the ascendant, the native themselves would possess the
same qualities, and will look for the same in others.
One thing we didn't touch upon in these conjunctions are the
houses each planet controls in the birth horoscope.
In our case, Jupiter and Saturn control the self, family, wealth,
gains, network circle, and foreign travel. Now you can see how
other houses will be affected by such a conjunction.
Jupiter for Aquarius ascendant controls the 2nd and 11th House. It
signifies finances. The wealth of the native will be established
through trade, and working in business partnerships. Their spouse
will also add to their gains since 11th lord is in the 7th House
showing gains through spouse and legal partnership.
Jupiter and Saturn conjunction in the 7th House of legal matters
also shows a lawyer or a judge.
Now, the depositor of the 7th House is debilitated by the Sun. The
Sun is in the 9th House which makes it strong, but weak by sign
placement. The Sun is receiving a harsh aspect from Saturn,
making this person's ego insecure, unstable, and filled with a lot of
insecurities regarding the father and gurus. This shows that
marriage will bring insecurities to the native, as their spouse will
be critical. Saturn has won the planetary war by having the lowest
degrees in the chart. Saturn is having more influence on Jupiter,
than Jupiter on Saturn.
This person will be disciplined regarding their assets and wealth
due to Saturn's control over Jupiter. However, due to a debilitated
Jupiter, the couple will have to spend money on traveling for work
and pleasure. Saturn brings its 12th House qualities and 7th lord in

the 9th House. The spouse of this native will also be from foreign
lands.
Another thing to notice is that the debilitation of the Sun won't be
felt as severely as it seems. The Sun almost has his dikbala which
he gets in the 10th House. Dikbala means directional strength. It's
sitting in the 9th House which is a dharmic placement for the Sun.
The Sun has his good friend Jupiter sitting in his house bringing
some optimism. However, married life will be cold, lifeless, and
very much like a business partnership.
Example 10: 2 Planetary Conjunction
The Sun is at 20 degrees, meaning it's in the nakshatra of
Vishakha, ruled by Jupiter. This makes the Sun's debiltation weak,
and actually brings good qualities while sitting in the house of
dharma. Also the Sun is in Pada 1 of this nakshatra, which makes
the Sun a true follower of the law and believer in God and all that
is positive. Remember, the Sun is in the sign of Libra for 30 days,
meaning the native has low self-esteem and bad relationships,
especially with the father. This is why degrees, aspects, and

conjunction are so important. The overall mundane picture for
April born people will have more sound qualities of the Sun than
October born. Each month brings a karmic lesson, and people born
in October will have different karmic debt to pay versus people
born in April.
The Sun is in a good nakshatra while its lord is in a friendly sign.
The savings, gains, and married life won't be so bad. There may
be a disciplined, structured, marriage but it won't lead to divorce.
If Venus is badly situated, that will alter the results, but our main
focus is studying the individual conjunction of Jupiter and Saturn.
Again, if you don't know about nakshatra and padas, then please
read my first book 'Astrology @ The Speed of Light'.

## 3 Planetary Conjunctions:

Before we start on three planetary conjunctions, we need to
understand how these conjunctions work. There is no one set of
meanings for three or more planetary conjunctions, because each
planet is being influenced by the other planet, and the planet with
the lowest degree is having a major impact on the conjunction.
If Venus, Jupiter, and Saturn are in conjunction, then there is no
one liner that you can say to give its definition. You have to see
what each planet is doing for the native.
You also have to know the houses they rule. Is one ruling a bad
house, or are both ruling bad houses? What effects are they
bringing in the conjunction by being lords of good or bad houses?
If you want to study Venus and what's happening to Venus in this
conjunction without looking at degrees, houses, or sign placement,
then we should break it down.

Venuslove, relationship, desire, pleasure, beauty, comfort, art,
creativity, lust and sexuality
Jupiter - optimism, preaching, philosophical, law, higher education
and wealth
SaturnStructure, discipline, duty, organization, delay, fear, judge,
karma
Knowing the qualities of each planet allows us to make an
assessment for each one.
For Venus, we can assume that Venus' desire, love, and
relationship qualities are based on discipline, structure, and
guidance. Their way of relating to others is based on spiritual,
philosophical, and religious connections.
Example 1: 3 Planetary Conjunction

Since you know who has influence in a conjunction, how would
you study this conjunction?
Saturn has the lowest degrees, meaning Saturn is having a major
impact upon Jupiter and Venus. There is 6.5 degree difference
between Saturn and Venus, while there is only 2.5 degree
difference between Jupiter and Venus. Therefore, you can analyze
this conjunction by knowing that Jupiter is being influenced by
Saturn, and Venus is being influenced by both Jupiter and Saturn,
but more influence of Jupiter is upon Venus. We can conclude
that as much as this person is going to be disciplined and organized
in their relationships and love, they will also be very optimistic,
and will be more spiritual and philosophical in their approach
rather than dark, cold, and full of responsibility.
Let's start on 3 planetary conjunctions.
For the Sun, we will assume that the Sun is not combusting any
planet and is instead brightening their qualities. Any combustion
by the Sun will simply take away the positive qualities from the
planet. Mercury is hardly ever combust, but still gets heated and
burned. Mercury is able to withstand the heat, unless it’s less than
2 degrees away from the Sun.

## Sun + Moon + Rahu conjunction:

Sun: ego, self expression, soul, personality, father, vitality and
karaka of 10th House (career)
Moon: mother, emotions, sensuality, mind, feelings, thoughts,
imagination, fertility and fame

Rahu: illusion, taboo breaker, unconventional, deceiver, magician,
foreign products and places, thief, politician, wide communication,
and computer related products.
When the Sun, Moon, and Rahu are in conjunction, this means the
native was born on an eclipse and there was no moon. If such a
conjunction occurs in an enemy sign, then it shows separation
from parents at an early age, abandonment by parents, or foreign
parents through adoption. The relationship with parents feels like
an illusion; there are no real emotions, just superficial feelings.
Since Rahu is known to amplify the nature of the planets he sits
with, he can make a person greatly emotional, egoistic, proud, and
narcissistic.
If the Sun or Moon are exalted, or in their own sign, it can amplify
love for one parent who is stronger in the conjunction than the
other, whether it's the Sun or Moon. The other parent would feel
left out due to their role being eclipsed. It's hard to escape the birth
in eclipses unless Rahu has already passed in his degrees while
both the Sun and Moon have a higher degree than Rahu. Since
Rahu moves backwards in a sign, meaning if Rahu is at 15 degrees
while the Sun and Moon are at 17-19 degrees, that means Rahu has
already passed them and Rahu will be at 14 degrees next, while the
Sun and Moon will be above 19 degrees next. However, the
shadow of Rahu would still be felt and will greatly impact the
person until mid-life. Similar to how an eclipse in the sky doesn't
last for long, the eclipse put on by Rahu doesn't last for long either.
By mid-life, the Sun and Moon both come out from the dark
shadows and brighten up. This does lead to a delusional mind, low
self-esteem, and a very arrogant nature that tries to protect its
image due to lack of self confidence.
If Rahu is 7 degrees less than the planet it’s in conjunction with,
then its effect won't be as bad if it was ahead of other planets. Even
if Rahu is ahead, as long as it's 10 degrees or more, its effects are
diminished. One thing to remember is that when Rahu is sitting

with a strong planet, it amplifies its good qualities. If Rahu is with
a badly placed planet, it amplifies its bad qualities.
Rahu with an exalted Sun or Moon will amplify positive emotions,
motherly feelings, high self-esteem, high confidence, and high
prestige in society.

## Sun + Moon + Mercury conjunction:

Sun: ego, self expression, soul, personality, father, vitality and
karaka of 10th house (career)
Moon: mother, emotions, sensuality, mind, feelings, thoughts,
imagination, fertility and fame
Mercury: intelligence, communication, speech, logic, education,
analytical skills, calculative skills, education, business, trading and
language skills
Now that the Sun and Moon are out of Rahu’s eclipse, the Sun can
go back to his job of brightening the qualities of planets he comes
into contact with. All the conjunctions with the Sun in this book
are based on scenarios in which the planets are not combust.
This conjunction in a person's chart makes their parents intelligent
and business minded. Parents could be teachers and business
people; especially in the area of sales and communication. This
person's emotions are based on logic and reason. The mind is
highly intuitive regarding the analytics of any situation. Their
speech is highly infused with emotions, yet filled with reason and
logic. In other words, their emotions have a good amount of
reasoning and analysis behind them. This is why when they get
emotional about something, there is reason behind their reaction,
and they do not fret over small things. These natives constantly
reason about every emotion that comes into their mind because

when the Moon and mind come together, and are being brightened
by the Sun's rays, their brain is on a path towards solving problems
and untangling the emotions that most people would not deal with.
The Moon is one planet in astrology that gets influenced most in
conjunction. This native’s emotional decisions are based on good
communication, intelligence, and self expression. Since the Moon
and Mercury are two planets that are really defined by the sign
they are placed in, this conjunction, signs truly mean everything. If
the Sun has the middle degree in this conjunction, then the planet
with the lowest degree would have their qualities burned; while the
other is getting their qualities enhanced.
The way to look at this conjunction is through the concept I gave
about influence and individual impact. If one is running through
the dasha of the Moon, then the Sun and Mercury will not be
active. Therefore, the Moon is still giving results based on the
mutation it has received while sitting with these two planets.

## Sun + Moon + Venus conjunction:

Sun: ego, self expression, soul, personality, father, vitality and
karaka of 10th house (career)
Moon: mother, emotions, sensuality, mind, feelings, thoughts,
imagination, fertility and fame
Venus: love, relationship, desire, pleasure, luxurious foods,
culture, family history, beauty, comfort, art, creativity, lust and
sexuality
This conjunction intensifies the creativity within a person,
especially regarding poetry, singing, music, and art. There is an
emotional need to create art because the Sun is brightening up the
emotions of the Moon, and brightening up the creativity of Venus.

Not only are these natives charming in love and relationships, but
they are also drawn towards romance; it's no longer a want but a
need. They are sensual and sensitive in the matter of romance.
Peace of mind is derived from art, creativity, cooking, and design.
Their entire life is about self expression, emotional attachment in
relationships, and trying to gain the finer things in life. Again, the
sign and house position will truly give this conjunction a meaning.

## Sun + Moon + Mars conjunction:

Sun: ego, self expression, soul, personality, father, vitality, and
karaka of 10th house (career)
Moon: mother, emotions, sensuality, mind, feelings, thoughts,
imagination, fertility and fame
Mars: efforts, energy, will power, stamina, strength, passion,
fighting ability, impulsivity, accidents, injuries, wounds, sports,
athlete, martial arts, weaponry, engineer.
When the Sun, Moon, and Mars come into conjunction, it can
make this person a very rude, egoistic, rash, rough, tough, and
active personality. This sounds a bit scary, but that's how they
operate. Their emotions are impulsive, and their emotional
response is dramatic and attention seeking. There is a brewing
anger and rage within, since the Moon is a planet that gets easily
influenced. With two hot planets like the Sun and Mars, the Moon
heats up and erupts like a volcano. If the Moon has the lowest
degree and is not combust, the person will be more emotional, and
will respond to his or her action based on emotion rather than
impulse.
This conjunction produces a person that is not about violence, but
about protection. They are very possessive because the Moon
wants to protect the kingdom of the Sun through the fighting

ability of Mars. Usually soldiers, marines, cops, and martial artists
are seen with such conjunctions. Even though this is a very
aggressive conjunction, the moon is with her friends. This is why
the mind won't be depressed, but is restless. Their peace of mind
comes from doing physical activity.
The parents of such a native are quite active with their children.
They are aggressive, dictatorial, strong willed, and want their
children to achieve the highest goals. The parents will also be
involved in their children’s activities. They are your soccer moms
football fanatic fathers.

## Sun + Moon + Jupiter conjunction:

Sun: ego, self expression, soul, personality, father, vitality, and
karaka of 10th house (career)
Moon: mother, emotions, sensuality, mind, feelings, thoughts,
imagination, fertility, and fame
Jupiter: knowledge, wisdom, wealth, optimism, hope, law,
teachers, education, religious beliefs, and long distance pilgrimage
This conjunction of true spiritual planets brings about an urge for
knowledge. The ego of this person relies on how much information
and wisdom they attain through their teachers. The mind is
optimistic about the future and they are natural preachers and
teachers. In their personal and professional life, they want to pass
on the wisdom of what they have learned. Whatever they take up
that has not worked for them is perceived as their dharma. These
tree planets signify enlightenment. The soul, mind, and knowledge
become one. In the ascendant, 4th, 8th, and especially the 12th
House, someone will strive to attain enlightenment after midlife.

The parents of this native are a major guiding force in their life.
Mother and father are both teachers for this person, and guide them
on faith, belief, and optimistic thinking.
In terms of their career path, they want to be in a position of
guidance, teaching, and mentoring. Their personality and life is
about passing on knowledge to the younger generation. This is
why they can make great professors, guidance counselors, and
religious priests. One negative aspect about this conjunction is that
this person can become too optimistic. They can be emotionally
attached, and their ego can be blown out of proportion because of
their great knowledge. The mind is not only being expanded with
Jupiter, but it's becoming brighter through the Sun. Such a mind
always needs to learn and gain knowledge, which can allow them
to acquire wealth.

## Sun + Moon + Saturn conjunction:

Sun: ego, self expression, soul, personality, father, vitality and
karaka of 10th house (career)
Moon: mother, emotions, sensuality, mind, feelings, thoughts,
imagination, fertility, and fame
Saturn: responsibility, organization, structure, delay, separation,
grief, life span as in longevity, antique items, old people, chronic
illnesses, fear, anxiety, labor work, lower class, lower type jobs,
construction
When the Sun and Moon come into contact with dark, dreary,
Saturn, there is a sense of seriousness in the mind and soul. The
mind is disciplined and needs structure, and the ego gets
suppressed. There is bit of a block in imagination and thinking, as
well as gaps in memory. The native had very disciplined parents,

who made sure everything was organized in his or her life. There
is a constant threat on one's ego, self-esteem, and peace of mind.
When darkness comes in contact with light, the light fades. The
mind can be so tough on itself that it can lead to depression. There
can be disputes with the father if the Sun is weakly placed by sign.
The Sun usually doesn't get damaged in the 6th and 8th House due
to his brightness, but a debilitated Sun and Moon can seriously
cause some tough psychological problems. The moon becomes self
critical, causing the native to become self critical; never permitting
credit for their achievements. Saturn keeps putting more work on
the Moon's desk as soon as each task is complete
There could be disputes with the parents, or a lot of responsibility
placed on the parents of the native due to their lack of health and
finances. The mind feels like it has to perform a duty in every task,
and no task is fun or light hearted. Everything needs to have a
purpose in life. These natives grow up early, and become mature
for their age. They face the harsh realities of life which keeps them
disciplined and minimizes their optimism.

## Sun + Moon + Ketu conjunction:

Sun: ego, self expression, soul, personality, father, vitality, and
karaka of 10th house (career)
Moon: mother, emotions, sensuality, mind, feelings, thoughts,
imagination, fertility and fame
Ketu: isolation, separation, ashram, spirituality, enlightenment,
detachment from material world, psychic intuition, occult and
mystical knowledge, universal
The ego, mind, and soul need to get away from the material world
and material relationships to find mental peace. There is hardly
any satisfaction attained through status, wealth, or materialism.

The mind is separated and isolated; it finds it hard to stick to one
thing. Since the Sun and Moon represents mother and father, the
native could've been separated from their parents at birth,
especially if the conjunction took place in 1st, 4th, 9th and 10th
House. The mother and father of the native were quite distant, and
did not want to raise children. If the Sun and Moon have higher
degrees than Ketu, the effects of Ketu’s eclipse on these spiritual
planets won't be felt in a negative way. With a sensitive moon,
there is a lot of fear and anxiety in a person. The ego gets
dissolved, and a person is quite humble in their life. This native
could be a very eccentric personality, one that runs away from
people and civilization yet is forced to live life with a tight hold on
his internal freedom. Also, this is one of the strongest indications
of moksha, especially in 1st, 4th, 8th and 12th House. If this occurs
in the ascendant, then the mind, body and soul receives
enlightenment. They can have very alienated emotions, or lack of
emotion.

## Sun + Mercury + Venus conjunction:

Sun: ego, self expression, soul, personality, father, vitality, and
karaka of 10th house (career)
Mercury: intelligence, communication, speech, logic, education,
analytical skills, calculative skills, education, business, trading and
language skills
Venus: love, relationship, desire, pleasure, luxurious foods,
culture, family history, beauty, comfort, art, creativity, lust and
sexuality
Creativity, performing arts, and creative communication become
front and center for this personality. The Sun brightens
communication, arts, and creativity within a person. Venus and

Mercury may bring passion for acting, musical instruments,
creative communication, and love for passionate relationships.
These natives want their relationship to be filled with lot of artistic
communication, lust, and games. Through their wit and charm,
they are able to attract anyone they desire. The Sun's ego and
confidence shine when they are dealing with a love interest. No
matter what they do, art and creativity must be present.
In Einstein’s chart, such a conjunction existed, and playing the
violin everyday was a way to enhance his creativity in physics and
mathematics. Even if the native is an accountant, he will look at
numbers, sums, and savings as an art. They will find innovative
ways to save their corporation money. They can make very clever
businessman with a knack for creative marketing, leading to many
customers. There is also a tremendous love for hearing one's own
voice, because it brings a sense of pleasure.
These natives are sexually attracted to people based on
communication and creativity. They appreciate beauty through
words, and they know when to say the right thing at the right time.

## Sun + Mercury + Mars conjunction:

Sun: ego, self expression, soul, personality, father, vitality, and
karaka of 10th house (career)
Mercury: intelligence, communication, speech, logic, education,
analytical skills, calculative skills, education, business, trading and
language skills
Mars: efforts, energy, will power, stamina, strength, passion,
fighting ability, impulsivity, accidents, injuries, wounds, sports,
athlete, martial arts, weaponry, engineer

This individual is dynamic and vibrant, with an aggressive speech
pattern that drives their ego. They take pride in their ability to
speak to others and have an authoritative communication style,
especially professionally. Even in their person life, they tend to
come out as the sexy alpha male who knows how to control a
situation with their words. The intelligence of this person shines
when they demonstrate their ability to calculate and solve
equations, and even construct weapons. These are people who can
become code breakers for the government, work in an MIT think
tank, and have the leadership skills to command their goals. A
leader must have aggressive and assertive speech. When ego,
action, and intelligence become one, it can either produce a
ruthless mastermind with brilliant mathematical skills or a talented
engineer who takes pride in his ability to construct things.
Since Mercury represents our intelligence, it can show excessive
impulsiveness in making decisions. The decision might be quick,
but may not be sound. This conjunction does well where fast
decision making and thinking are required, like a plane tower
controller, a general in a military war zone, or a game show
contestant.

## Sun + Mercury + Jupiter conjunction:

Sun: ego, self expression, soul, personality, father, vitality, and
karaka of 10th house (career)
Mercury: intelligence, communication, speech, logic, education,
analytical skills, calculative skills, education, business, trading and
language skills
Jupiter: knowledge, wisdom, wealth, optimism, hope, law,
teachers, education, religious beliefs, and long distance pilgrimage

Education, learning, and studying are the focal points for this
personality. They travel to distant lands to pursue a higher
education. Their intelligence has a sense of wisdom, which
becomes a locomotive for their ego. Fathers of such natives could
be a teacher or a government administrative worker.
This native is drawn towards spirituality, law, and philosophy.
They have the ability to write laws, contracts, agreements, and
religious texts. Whatever they do in life, it is often related to higher
education. They feel enriched when gaining a sense of wisdom.
Their personality and ego both depend upon the amount of
knowledge they acquire. These natives follow their culture,
teachers, and knowledge bestowed upon them by their father to
their grave. A father can be a great source of influence upon the
native's life because he is also seen as a guru. Since the karaka of
wealth and the karaka of calculative intelligence combines with
the Sun, their ability to handle finance brightens up. They can go
into accounting, money management, and even become stock
brokers.

## Sun + Mercury + Saturn conjunction:

Sun: ego, self expression, soul, personality, father, vitality and
karaka of 10th house (career)
Mercury: intelligence, communication, speech, logic, education,
analytical skills, calculative skills, education, business, trading and
language skills
Saturn: responsibility, organization, structure, delay, separation,
grief, life span as in longevity, antique items, old people, chronic
illnesses, fear, anxiety, labor work, lower class, lower type jobs,
construction

When you have the Sun, Mercury, and Saturn conjunction, it can
show someone who is disciplined, structured, and orderly about
their affairs with the government. They can be a communicator for
the government or a lawyer working for the government because
both the Sun and Saturn are strong indicators of government jobs.
Mercury's intelligence helps the native to achieve intellectual
status in the government. If the person is in the private sector, they
can easily become the head of a company or be in a management
position. This individual is quite serious about their work and they
expect the same from others. As much as they become
communicative, their style of speech is very formal, direct, and
always related to their profession. This native’s ego and
personality is reflected through their work ethic.
If Mercury is hemmed between the Sun and Saturn, as in less than
5 degrees within both planets, not only is Mercury hot and
burning, but it also loses the luster of speech and communicative
ability. This can interrupt the native's education and give them
some problems in speech, especially at an early age. Overall they
are very studious people, since the mind is bright, focused, and
disciplined regarding education and work.

## Sun + Mercury + Rahu conjunction:

Sun: ego, self expression, soul, personality, father, vitality, and
karaka of 10th house (career)
Mercury: intelligence, communication, speech, logic, education,
analytical skills, calculative skills, education, business, trading and
language skills
Rahu: illusion, taboo breaker, unconventional, deceiver, magician,
foreign products and places, thief, politician, wide communication,
and computer related products

The illumination of the Sun and the amplification of intelligence
by Rahu allows a person to shine on a higher plane. As much as
Rahu hates the Sun, and the Sun hates Rahu, Mercury gets the best
of both worlds. These people are confident with a huge ego and
actually have the intelligence, knowledge, and communicative
ability to believe in themselves. These natives want to
communicate in a big way. They want to be heard and they want
to be respected for their intelligence. They achieve success in
education by breaking taboos, going to foreign lands, and bringing
a change to foreign lands.
This is a strong indication of someone who is a computer guru,
programmer, or computer engineer, especially with the aspect of
Mars. Mercury is language, calculation, and organization, while
Rahu represents the computer age. If the Sun is weakened while
Rahu has eclipsed both planets, it can show someone who might be
a mute or has problems with speech, creating a weak ego and low
self-esteem. The education of this person can be halted for a long
time if these three planets are placed in bad signs like Libra,
Scorpio or Pisces. This person may speak in a vulgar manner and
might curse a lot to defend their ego and self image.
Mercury also shows working with the skill of hands, while Rahu is
illusion, and the Sun is ego and status. The native can be a great
magician who's self confidence and status rises with each
performance. Even special effects and movie editors are seen with
this conjunction since movies, editing, and effects are illusions
designed with a computer. However, "design" will also include
Venus, or aspect from Venus, to bring about involvement of the
movie industry.

## Sun + Mercury + Ketu conjunction:

Sun: ego, self expression, soul, personality, father, vitality and
karaka of 10th house (career)

Mercury: intelligence, communication, speech, logic, education,
analytical skills, calculative skills, education, business, trading and
language skills
Ketu: isolation, separation, ashram, spirituality, enlightenment,
detachment from material world, psychic intuition, occult and
mystical knowledge, universal
In this conjunction, ego and intelligence want to escape. The native
may have a very unconventional way of thinking, calculating, and
analyzing, to a point where one's action feels illogical and out of
context. As much as this person wants to have a personality and
ego, there is something pale and plain about them. Their self-
esteem is subdued due to eccentric intelligence and behavior. In
any conjunction, the nodes have more impact than the biggest
planet in astrology: the Sun. The brightness of the Sun is darkened
by the eclipse of this spiritual and transformational node. Even
communication and speech is numbed because the mind can't
figure out what it wants to say or do. The negative qualities of this
conjunction occur if the nodes have the highest degree. If they
have the lowest degree, then the person is egoless yet confident.
They may not like the role of a father or father figure because they
want isolation from responsibility. Even with Ketu having the
lowest degrees, it still has a tail that affects the planet ahead of
him. Due to Ketu's broad and universal approach, the mind
becomes intuitive and scientific. In mathematics, where various
calculations need to take place at the same time, Mercury easily
takes advantage of Ketu’s qualities and becomes great at
mathematics. Ketu is a reflection of Mars, which brings quick and
impulsive results when in contact with the mind because it speeds
it up. If the nodes are containing the lowest degrees, especially
more than 6 degrees away from the rest of the planets, then the
effects are numbed by the nodes. In this situation, the conjunction
will mostly give result of budha aditya yoga.

## Sun + Venus + Mars conjunction:

Sun: ego, self expression, soul, personality, father, vitality and
karaka of 10th house (career)
Venus: love, relationship, desire, pleasure, luxurious foods,
culture, family history, beauty, comfort, art, creativity, lust and
sexuality
Mars: efforts, energy, will power, stamina, strength, passion,
fighting ability, impulsivity, accidents, injuries, wounds, sports,
athlete, martial arts, weaponry, engineer
Passion, love, and lust run high with this conjunction.
Relationships tend to by fiery, dramatic, and sometimes traumatic.
There is a flair for the arts and performance which may even
involve one being a savior or hero. What makes one happy is when
they are protecting and serving. Venus being hemmed between two
fire planets show love for fire and desire to serve: like a firefighter.
When these natives fall in love, they do so with great passion and
aggression. Due to their impulsive nature in relationships, their ego
is injured frequently. Venus, the planet of love and relationships, is
being burned from both sides if it contains a middle degree. If
such a conjunction occurs in Taurus or Libra, then Venus has the
upper hand, and there would be a love for fighting to create
balance and harmony in life, and the world at large. They will have
the strength to fight, but their style would be one of non violence.
Sometimes these people become obsessive and dangerous in
matters of the heart, especially if Venus is combust. They are
willing to create a blood bath to gain their significant other. They
have a very possessive nature, and are willing to get in physical
fights if you were flirting with their mate. Their self expression is
very creative and dynamic. They possess this James Bond “divide
and conquer” mentality regarding affairs and relationships. They
are willing to battle anyone to attain what they desire. Their

personality and ego is very aggressive. They might have been
abused by their father, or their father could have abused other
women.
These natives, especially men, need to be careful about how they
treat woman with this conjunction, for they have the propensity to
be in a very physical and violent relationship with opposite sex.
They have the strongest will to succeed in matters of creativity,
leadership, and fulfillment of their desire if it's steered in the right
direction.

## Sun + Venus + Jupiter conjunction:

Sun: ego, self expression, soul, personality, father, vitality and
karaka of 10th house (career)
Venus: love, relationship, desire, pleasure, luxurious foods,
culture, family history, beauty, comfort, art, creativity, lust and
sexuality
Jupiter: knowledge, wisdom, wealth, optimism, hope, law,
teachers, education, religious beliefs, and long distance pilgrimage
This conjunction creates divine love in a person. The urge to have
a relationship becomes a spiritual quest for a soul connection. This
native has a very loving personality and spiritual ego. They come
across as mature teachers or preachers. The Sun and Jupiter both
brighten and expand the qualities of Venus. This means the native
has quite an appetite for forming relationships with older and
spiritual people. A man will be attracted to a well educated,
mature, and spiritual woman, and vice versa. Their love for
tradition, religion, and family history broadens to other cultures as
well. They are very welcoming, and open to foreign forms of
artistic expression. They have a zest for traveling and gaining

knowledge through beauty, culture, and exploring various
societies.
Ego, love, and pleasure for this native are driven by knowledge
and beliefs. The self-esteem of a person rises as they connect with
others who have similar belief systems. There is great optimism in
their personality and in their interactions with others. This native
may have a very loving relationship with their father. The father
may be a new age guru, and knows how to have fun while sharing
his wisdom and philosophy with his children.
This is a great conjunction for a government job, teacher, preacher,
lawyer, guidance counselor, mystic, or artist.

## Sun + Venus + Saturn conjunction:

Sun: ego, self expression, soul, personality, father, vitality, and
karaka of 10th house (career)
Venus: love, relationship, desire, pleasure, luxurious foods,
culture, family history, beauty, comfort, art, creativity, lust and
sexuality
Saturn: responsibility, organization, structure, delay, separation,
grief, life span as in longevity, antique items, old people, chronic
illnesses, fear, anxiety, labor work, lower class, lower type jobs,
construction
When the ego, personality, and sense of pleasure are mixed with
discipline, structure, and hard work, a sense of purpose is
developed. The native has a tremendous love for what they do, and
are quite determined. When two karakas of career are joined
together, one’s life becomes about creative work. This is even
more so than the Sun, Venus, and Saturn conjunction, making a
person very creative. They excel and take great pride in their work

because it is their passion; otherwise Venus will crawl back into its
shell and will resent what it does on a daily basis. Though they
may achieve career success, the native's love life and relationships
suffer. There is a cold approach to matters of the heart. They are
not romantics, and prefer something based on structure, maturity
and stability.
If this occurs in the chart of a man, then he would end up with
someone who is far more mature than him. The marriage would be
based on security, stability, and structure rather than romance.
Venus feels a bit hard when it comes to love. Saturn makes Venus
think constantly about the reality of life and relationships rather
than romance and sensuality.

## Sun + Venus + Rahu conjunction:

Sun: ego, self expression, soul, personality, father, vitality, and
karaka of 10th house (career)
Venus: love, relationship, desire, pleasure, luxurious foods,
culture, family history, beauty, comfort, art, creativity, lust and
sexuality
Rahu: illusion, taboo breaker, unconventional, deceiver, magician,
foreign products and places, thief, politician, wide communication,
and computer related products
Love, lust, pleasure, and sexuality have expanded to a point where
there is a sense of extreme obsession. There is also a need for
foreign pleasures; whether it's settling in foreign lands, constantly
traveling to foreign places, love of ethnic foods, or falling in love
with someone from another country. The person's ego is strong,
and their self expression and personality is about demonstrating
extravagance and flaunting luxurious items. Their self worth is
derived from all the fancy places they have visited.

There is a tendency to overdo everything to display confidence and
status. They want maximum pleasure, the most expensive clothing,
the wealthiest husband, or the most beautiful wife. If Rahu wasn't
at play, then the Sun would only enhance the good qualities of
Venus. If Rahu has the highest degree, then one loses the meaning
of love, romance, and their self image. They tend to be unsatisfied
with what they have, and may gravitate towards cheating and
having affairs.
Women may gravitate towards father figures who are also quite
extravagant, confident, and accomplished. A man will select a
woman who will be quite dominating in his life, and would
demand luxury and wealth. He may use his money and assets to
fulfill her desires.

## Sun + Venus + Ketu conjunction:

Sun: ego, self expression, soul, personality, father, vitality, and
karaka of 10th house (career)
Venus: love, relationship, desire, pleasure, luxurious foods,
culture, family history, beauty, comfort, art, creativity, lust and
sexuality
Ketu: isolation, separation, ashram, spirituality, enlightenment,
detachment from material world, psychic intuition, occult and
mystical knowledge, universal
In this conjunction, there is a sense of dissatisfaction from material
wealth, sexual pleasure, and partnerships. One may feel like they
do not belong to any tribe, culture, or group. The person feels lost
in their sense of pleasure, self image, and what makes them happy.
The soul (Sun) searches for truth about itself. Due to Venus, the
native examines the external world, and can never find themselves

or someone else who can understand them. Their self image is seen
through the reflection of others, especially romantic partners, but
nothing comes about. The person lacks ego, and it creates a very
isolated personality that seeks pleasure in spiritual places and
eventually looks for a spiritual partner, whether it's a man or
woman. It takes time for the identity to be established, which
eventually results in a path of enlightenment, moksha, and
spirituality. The person will be very creative and artistic, but will
never realize the impact their art has on others, and will indulge in
self destructive behavior because they are dissatisfied by what they
see.

## Sun + Mars + Jupiter conjunction:

Sun: ego, self expression, soul, personality, father, vitality, and
karaka of 10th house (career)
Mars: efforts, energy, will power, stamina, strength, passion,
fighting ability, impulsivity, accidents, injuries, wounds, sports,
athlete, martial arts, weaponry, engineer
Jupiter: knowledge, wisdom, wealth, optimism, hope, law,
teachers, education, religious beliefs, and long distance pilgrimage
These are male fire signs in conjunction. Only one thing can come
of this: an angry, macho, savage person. This is one aggressive,
dominating, possessive, wise, and powerful personality. The
energy of this native goes into higher learning and has leadership
skills. If any of the planets are debilitated here, they will still
express confidence to the world. The self image of the person
really depends upon their leadership skills. This can be an athlete,
political leader, military leader, or a director of defense in
government. This is a hot headed person who needs to dictate in
order to satisfy their self-esteem. If this same conjunction occurs in
a peaceful sign like Libra, Cancer, or Pisces, then there is a sense

of leadership that promotes peace, harmony, and love; like
Mahatma Gandhi. If such a conjunction occurs in a fire sign,
especially Aries, then you can bet this person has the skills to
become Adolf Hitler. In Sagittarius, there would be an urge to lead
the crowd through wisdom and knowledge, while in Leo there
would be an urge to lead the crowd through politics, creativity, and
cinema. This would be a great conjunction for an action hero.
Since these three planets are best friends and respect each other,
this personality takes initiative towards a positive goal and follows
the law of Jupiter. Lawyers, university deans, and athletic coaches
have such conjunctions.

## Sun + Mars + Saturn conjunction:

Sun: ego, self expression, soul, personality, father, vitality, and
karaka of 10th house (career)
Mars: efforts, energy, will power, stamina, strength, passion,
fighting ability, impulsivity, accidents, injuries, wounds, sports,
athlete, martial arts, weaponry, engineer
Saturn: responsibility, organization, structure, delay, separation,
grief, life span as in longevity, antique items, old people, chronic
illnesses, fear, anxiety, labor work, lower class, lower type jobs,
construction
From one extreme to the other, this conjunction is similar to a fire
bomb in a war zone. There is a great urge to take the right course
of action; and this often requires the protection of oneself, home,
or country. This native is a natural patriot who would be willing to
pick up any weapon for his belief, family, or country. There is a
very aggressive urge to follow law and order. In this conjunction,
fire and oil are mixing with the nuclear fusion of the Sun. There is
a great deal of tension in the house where such a conjunction

occurs. It's a war zone, and there is often a lot of struggle with the
father. Perhaps the father would be physically abusive, especially
if it's debilitated, or in an enemy sign. If the Sun is exalted, then
there would be a great deal of attention, discipline, and motivation
through the father. The native’s father would bring structure into
the child’s life and teach them how to stand tall in the world;
however the approach may be physically or verbally severe if such
a conjunction occurs in 2nd House.
The native loves behaving like a soldier. They like the idea of
discipline, order, and structure. This conjunction makes for a great
engineer, policeman, warden, miner, real estate developer, or
builder. Mars represents land, Saturn represents the conjunction,
and Sun is the karaka of career. This is why the real estate
business and ownership are very appealing to such a native. They
are willing to fight to get the land they want. If such a conjunction
occurs in Aries, Leo, Virgo, Scorpio, or Capricorn, then it can
make a person quite violent in their business dealings, like a
mobster or head of the mafia. They are shrewd individuals. If both
Mars and Saturn are combust, then the native would be lethargic,
lazy, and unmotivated to take any action. There would be a great
deal of disorganization in his or her life. As much as the person
would try to take action, circumstances would bring them down.

## Sun + Mars + Rahu conjunction:

Sun: ego, self expression, soul, personality, father, vitality and
karaka of 10th house (career)
Mars: efforts, energy, will power, stamina, strength, passion,
fighting ability, impulsivity, accidents, injuries, wounds, sports,
athlete, martial arts, weaponry, engineer

Rahu: illusion, taboo breaker, unconventional, deceiver, magician,
foreign products and places, thief, politician, wide communication,
and computer related products
This is another destructive conjunction where the ego and
personality is driven by anger and ruthlessness. This is a strong
willed person who will not take no for answer when it comes to
their goals, desires, and efforts. They have anger brewing within,
and are waiting for someone to turn the switch on, which doesn't
take much effort. Mars greatly amplifies and brightens our drive,
will, energy, and effort. There is a strong need to set goals in life,
otherwise the native feels useless and dull. Their self image
depends upon the goals they achieve. They become revolutionary
in their industry or chosen field because the desires of Rahu
become that of Mars. The obsession with attainment of goals and
desires become everything for the native.
This conjunction produces a fighter, wrestler, athlete, boxer, police
man, or soldier. The action oriented personality is driven by Rahu,
and has a need to protect and serve at a higher level, and be
recognized as hero. There is a sense of confusion about one's
ability to protect and serve their government, city, or state. They
want to serve, but they also want to achieve success by breaking
taboos and rules of society. Such actions can lead to attaining
power or entitlement through manipulation and cunningness.

## Sun + Mars + Ketu conjunction:

Sun: ego, self expression, soul, personality, father, vitality, and
karaka of 10th house (career)
Mars: efforts, energy, will power, stamina, strength, passion,
fighting ability, impulsivity, accidents, injuries, wounds, sports,
athlete, martial arts, weaponry, engineer

Ketu: isolation, separation, ashram, spirituality, enlightenment,
detachment from material world, psychic intuition, occult and
mystical knowledge, universal.
In this conjunction, the native’s actions are without purpose and
rather impulsive. This personality breeds conflict, fights, and
arguments. There is a ripple effect of Rahu and a great loss of
identity. These native’s tend to push their agenda rather than doing
