anything. Ketu's inability to think, and Mars’ quality of action
makes this personality very unpredictable. They can have outbursts
at any moment, and they will not back down from a challenge
including physical fights. Ketu doesn't allow the soul (Sun) to
think before taking actions. This person has unbelievable will
power, leading them to new heights in the corporate and
government world.
The desire to achieve goals is restricted by Ketu's desire to let go.
This creates a sense of duty and obligation towards one's actions
and goals. The personality of the native is full of frustration, but
they tend to come off as someone who is highly accomplished and
confident. However, their confidence lacks the backbone of
patience and virtue. They will hit without feeling guilty about it
afterwards. Capturing their goals is based on unseen manipulation.
This could either be a very cunning personality out there to only
accomplish their agenda, or it can be a very aggressive, assertive
and hot personality ready to take what they believe they deserve.

## Sun + Jupiter + Saturn conjunction:

Sun: ego, self expression, soul, personality, father, vitality, and
karaka of 10th house (career)
Jupiter: knowledge, wisdom, wealth, optimism, hope, law,
teachers, education, religious beliefs, and long distance pilgrimage

Saturn: responsibility, organization, structure, delay, separation,
grief, life span as in longevity, antique items, old people, chronic
illnesses, fear, anxiety, labor work, lower class, lower type jobs,
construction
There is pleasure in having a sense of duty for this personality.
They are willing to work hard to fulfill their responsibilities as a
family member. The Sun and Saturn bring in responsibility and
duty, while Jupiter brings a sense of wisdom and optimism when
fulfilling their tasks. This individual possesses maturity, wisdom,
and organization. There is a strong sense of preaching, and
following what one preaches. This native is made for government
work, law, research, and scientific research, especially with
regards to biomechanics. The native is all about putting order and
structure in the way they study and learn things. They always
create space to submerge themselves into learning, studying, and
applying their knowledge to the real world.
This is a strong indicator of someone who might be in the legal
field, because they enjoy the process of writing the law and
implementing it. In their home life this can be troublesome, as they
will enforce strong discipline and order, making their children fear
or resent them. If Saturn is influencing, both Saturn and Jupiter can
also create a workaholic for a higher entity.

## Sun + Jupiter + Rahu conjunction:

Sun: ego, self expression, soul, personality, father, vitality, and
karaka of 10th house (career)
Jupiter: knowledge, wisdom, wealth, optimism, hope, law,
teachers, education, religious beliefs, and long distance pilgrimage

Rahu: illusion, taboo breaker, unconventional, deceiver, magician,
foreign products and places, thief, politician, wide communication,
and computer related products
A rebellious thinker best describes this conjunction. This is a
person who not only rejects the way their personality is shaped by
society, but also the thought process behind culture and religion.
Rahu has a greater impact in this conjunction because he eclipses
the Sun and morphs Jupiter into his illusion, where the guru begins
to think outside the box of conformity. Rahu is deviant, unusual,
cunning, and a rule breaker. Through Rahu, many great thinkers
broke the mold to set a new standard in society. Their ego is driven
by innovation and revolution.
Sometimes, in creating or
attempting to create something original, the person can go a bit too
far in breaking taboo and barriers.
There is a great deal of passion for research in the areas of science,
spirituality, occult, and astronomical subjects. Since Rahu takes
away the positive qualities of Jupiter and the Sun, there is a great
deal of stress and conflict with the native’s father and teachers.
Their rebellious nature leads them to believe it is the only path to
achievement.

## Sun + Jupiter + Ketu conjunction:

Sun: ego, self expression, soul, personality, father, vitality, and
karaka of 10th house (career)
Jupiter: knowledge, wisdom, wealth, optimism, hope, law,
teachers, education, religious beliefs, and long distance pilgrimage
Ketu: isolation, separation, ashram, spirituality, enlightenment,
detachment from material world, psychic intuition, occult and
mystical knowledge, universal

This conjunction produces a personality that is built to search for
the truth, whether it's scientific or esoteric. Their life long journey
is about finding the truth about themselves and the world. Ketu's
way of rejecting material wealth, including religion and culture
represented by Jupiter, makes this personality want to go beyond
the superficial and artificial. They want evidence, and if it is
lacking, they move on, because truth is their only religion.
Anything written by man is questioned often by the native. Ketu's
need to search for enlightenment leads them to great wisdom.
Wisdom rather than religion is a great tool for a person with such a
conjunction, but at the end, Ketu is an eclipse and a malefic planet.
There is fear of the father and fear of accepting knowledge passed
down by their elders. Any wisdom and principles handed down to
them is stored for questioning later.
There is a great disconnect between the native and their father if
Ketu has the highest degree. The father could be a priest, teacher,
or spiritual guru who might travel around the world to provide for
their family. Public speaking is one of the strengths of this
conjunction, since Jupiter is passing on knowledge and wisdom,
Ketu is the truth, and the Sun is your self expression. There is a
great need to express one’s thoughts by preaching a crowd.

## Sun + Saturn + Rahu conjunction:

Sun: ego, self expression, soul, personality, father, vitality, and
karaka of 10th house (career)
Saturn: responsibility, organization, structure, delay, separation,
grief, life span as in longevity, antique items, old people, chronic
illnesses, fear, anxiety, labor work, lower class, lower type jobs,
construction

Rahu: illusion, taboo breaker, unconventional, deceiver, magician,
foreign products and places, thief, politician, wide communication,
and computer related products
There is tremendous pressure and stress created by the father in
this conjunction. The Sun is sitting with his two bitter enemies,
one is an eclipse and the other is darkness. This is equivalent to a
black hole for a star. The light just can't escape to shine upon the
world, until the age of 35. There are not only going to be strict
lessons from the father, but the father might be substance abuser,
or he might have been separated at birth due to travel, or simply
abandoned the native. Although the native is a hard worker and
disciplined, their efforts might go in the wrong direction. The
person might get involved with groups that are a bad influence at
young age, where their structure and morals would be eclipsed by
the smoky Rahu. What seems to be wrong will look right and vice
versa. If the Sun is in the sign of Leo or exalted while having a
higher degree than Rahu, there would be tremendous growth due
to hard work and discipline; especially when dealing with the
government. However, they are still prone to doing wrong deeds
like taking bribes, making bad connections, and doing things for
selfish reasons rather than for the people. Rahu always wants to
take the shorter route, and if the dignity of the Sun is not strong by
sign placement, it can make for a very cunning, clever, and
mischievous personality.
If the Sun is holding the lowest degree, it greatly impacts the good
qualities of Saturn. However, the eclipse of Rahu is far more
powerful than any light, which is why during an eclipse the Sun is
darkened. Therefore, this person will have very low self-esteem,
low confidence, and will be quite shy in approaching other people.
Even if the Sun is eclipsed and becomes dull, an eclipse never lasts
forever. The effects of Rahu decline after age 32. Rahu always
removes his shadow over the Sun and Moon after the age of 35.

## Sun + Saturn + Ketu conjunction:

Sun: ego, self expression, soul, personality, father, vitality, and
karaka of 10th house (career)
Saturn: responsibility, organization, structure, delay, separation,
grief, life span as in longevity, antique items, old people, chronic
illnesses, fear, anxiety, labor work, lower class, lower type jobs,
construction
Ketu: isolation, separation, ashram, spirituality, enlightenment,
detachment from material world, psychic intuition, occult and
mystical knowledge, universal.
This conjunction can cause a complete disconnect from the father.
There is no common ground between the two. Ketu's Mars-like
nature would also aggravate the father to take physical action
towards the child if Ketu has the highest degrees. The sense of
duty in the native is lost and unidentified. They don't know their
purpose, and who they are supposed to be fighting for. The native
would have a battle within their own personality. They are unable
to fit in with others, and their focus and discipline will eventually
lead them towards the occult and spirituality. The native is drawn
towards chaotic things. The directionless Ketu could cause the
native to be focused on the negative rather than the positive. This
is like a damper on the pure soul; a huge ink spot on a white sheet.
The Sun is not only being eclipsed and darkened, but completely
blinded. Rahu usually makes a person overconfident and Ketu
causes a person to lose their identity. If this native is focused on
research subjects, they would slowly start to find themselves and
indulge into things related to the Sun like creativity, art, acting,
and photography. This would be very healing for them. Doing
creative work would be like igniting the fire under the eclipsed
Sun.

## Moon + Mercury + Venus conjunction:

Moon: mother, emotions, sensuality, mind, feelings, thoughts,
imagination, fertility and fame
Mercury: intelligence, communication, speech, logic, education,
analytical skills, calculative skills, education, business, trading and
language skills
Venus: love, relationship, desire, pleasure, luxurious foods,
culture, family history, beauty, comfort, art, creativity, lust, and
sexuality.
There is creativity in the air. When emotions, intelligence, and
creativity come together, there is a deep need to express oneself
through art, beauty, and fashion. Although the Moon is sitting with
his two enemies, Mercury and Venus, they are soft enemies, who
really do not hurt each other; they just have bitterness within. The
mind, which is the Moon, becomes disturbed because too many
creative thoughts are brewing, and they are unable to focus on one.
This is a very creative mind that wants to communicate through
art, and beauty. These natives can make great actors, designers,
writers, interior decorators, musicians, and restaurant owners.
There is a lot of support from the mother as well. The mother
herself could be an artist or a designer. The mind needs creative
stimulation to maintain balance and calm. These people are quite
pleasant, and their mind is constantly occupied by art, love, and
romance. They thrive when they are near the opposite sex. In the
8th and 12th House, it can produce someone who is homosexual.
However, these three planets are in a masculine sign, and must
also be in a masculine nakshatra for this to occur, which is rare.
Having interest in the same sex also happens when a male has
many feminine qualities, and a female has many masculine
qualities. This occurs when a majority of their planets are either in
a masculine or feminine sign.

## Moon + Mercury + Mars conjunction:

Moon: mother, emotions, sensuality, mind, feelings, thoughts,
imagination, fertility, and fame

Mercury: intelligence, communication, speech, logic, education,
analytical skills, calculative skills, education, business, trading and
language skills
Mars: efforts, energy, will power, stamina, strength, passion,
fighting ability, impulsivity, accidents, injuries, wounds, sports,
athlete, martial arts, weaponry, engineer
These natives have a very aggressive, assertive, and authoritative
mother who always takes charge of their decision making;
especially in their youth. The mother must have been in control of
a family business or has a career related to management. The
native has a very aggressive mind and is unafraid to express it.
They are not only quick thinkers, they are quick speakers. Whether
they have the right answer or not, they love interrupting a
conversation and trying "win" the argument. It is Mars’ nature to
fight and control the conversation. If they are shut down, or
declared wrong in their opinion, their mind goes haywire, and a
sudden outburst of frustration and anger may occur.
There is too much happening here. The mind is not only filled with
a million ideas, but it also wants to take action towards those ideas.
If the native becomes an entrepreneur, they would most likely
succeed because their mind will always be leaning towards new
ideas and implementing them. Mercury is the enemy here, and the
Moon is the culprit of both planets. The mind is in the middle of a
war zone between Mercury and Mars. Since Mars is the only
natural malefic, the war is easily won by Mars. The native’s
speech, mind, communication, and thoughts become energized.

## Moon + Mercury + Jupiter conjunction:

Moon: mother, emotions, sensuality, mind, feelings, thoughts,
imagination, fertility and fame
Mercury: intelligence, communication, speech, logic, education,
analytical skills, calculative skills, education, business, trading and
language skills
Jupiter: knowledge, wisdom, wealth, optimism, hope, law,
teachers, education, religious beliefs, and long distance pilgrimage
These natives are quite intelligent and succeed in academics,
which becomes a source of wisdom in their life. The mind has the
need to communicate with intelligent people who are philosophical
and cultured. The native is full of wisdom. The mother could have
been a school teacher, preacher, or dedicated housewife who
provided great stimulation for her children through reading to
them, playing brain games, and talking about religious studies.
There is a need to advance academically and pursue a higher
education. This person can easily attain their PhD if these three
planets are placed in 1st, 5th and 9th House, even if such planets
are functional malefic. These are natural benefic planets, and if the
Moon is waning, making it malefic, the conjunction expands with
Jupiter, and the optimism of the mind steers intelligence in the
right direction.
Mercury does not enjoy the company of the Moon, and Jupiter
dislikes the company of the salesman Mercury. The native might
use wisdom and higher knowledge for profit. This has been the
case for centuries. In ancient times, it was on a donation basis.
Now, one can learn about a particular subject from a professor and
start teaching the same subject at another university for living. If
Mercury is impacting the Moon and Jupiter, then the person will

become a public speaker in the areas of motivation, optimism, and
spirituality. They will try to make a profit from selling books, CDs
and seminars. There is nothing wrong with going down this path,
as long as they do so with sincerity and back it up with results.

## Moon + Mercury + Saturn conjunction:

Moon: mother, emotions, sensuality, mind, feelings, thoughts,
imagination, fertility, and fame
Mercury: intelligence, communication, speech, logic, education,
analytical skills, calculative skills, education, business, trading and
language skills
Saturn: responsibility, organization, structure, delay, separation,
grief, life span as in longevity, antique items, old people, chronic
illnesses, fear, anxiety, labor work, lower class, lower type jobs,
construction
As harsh as it may look, the Moon actually does quite well here
because when thoughts, intelligence, communication, and
emotions are disciplined, organized, and structured, there's hardly
a time to grieve. This may create an image of a person who is old
and reserved towards worldly emotions. They use their logic and
analytic skills more than expressing their feelings. Saturn being the
natural malefic creates boundaries with the Moon, and limits what
the Moon can think and express, while putting positive focus and
structure on the native's intelligence. This can lead to constant
depression. The mind becomes too self critical of what it is
thinking. Saturn’s nature to delay things frustrates the mind
because it interferes with his plans. If such a conjunction occurs in
a certain upachaya house like 3, 10 and 11, then the mind is
usually focused on realistic things and achievements rather than
falling for illusion and emotions.

The mother of the native is quite judgmental and assertive in her
approach. She disciplines the children and encourages them to
focus of academic studies and communication. She will raise them
to approach life in a conservative way, even in terms of speaking
and writing. Although the mother is communicative, she chooses
her words carefully with her children, especially with the native
who has such a conjunction. If other children have different moon
signs, it does not mean astrology is not valid. Each child reflects a
certain side of the mother as we are all multi-dimensional.

## Moon + Mercury + Rahu conjunction:

Moon: mother, emotions, sensuality, mind, feelings, thoughts,
imagination, fertility and fame
Mercury: intelligence, communication, speech, logic, education,
analytical skills, calculative skills, education, business, trading and
language skills
Rahu: illusion, taboo breaker, unconventional, deceiver, magician,
foreign products and places, thief, politician, wide communication,
and computer related products
This marks a clever individual who is willing to break the rules
and sweet talk their way to success. The mind is filled with lots of
ideas; from scientific to financial. If Rahu has a great impact in
degrees to the Moon and Mercury, then his shadow will hide
behind the veil of an emotional and intellectual moon. Emotions
are too overwhelming for the person to handle, so they create this
wall of smoke and hide themselves from the world. Meanwhile, a
huge tidal wave is brewing within. Rahu not only amplifies
intelligence, calculative skills, and the ability to communicate, but
also gives a person a foul mouth since the karaka of speech,
Mercury, gets completely absorbed by the rule breaker rebel,
Rahu. Their mother could be scientist, doctor, or simply a brilliant

sales person. However, the relationship with the mother is only on
a superficial level due to verbal abuse. There is constant bickering
with the mother and sword fights of the tongue.
These individuals are top salesmen, marketing and accounting
geniuses, or financial gurus who know the shortcut to early
retirement. If Rahu is too close to the moon, there will be constant
depression and substance abuse like smoking, alcohol, and drug
use. In the 8th House, this can show that the mother was a
substance abuser whose intelligence caved into Rahu's desire for
pleasure. Such a placement can also give intense interest and
intelligence towards occultism and black magic. Mercury's
intelligence is loaded with curiosity to explore the unknown world
in 8th and 12th House. Believing in conspiracy theories is not
unusual for these natives. Anything out of the ordinary stimulates
their mind.

## Moon + Mercury + Ketu conjunction:

Moon: mother, emotions, sensuality, mind, feelings, thoughts,
imagination, fertility, and fame
Mercury: intelligence, communication, speech, logic, education,
analytical skills, calculative skills, education, business, trading and
language skills
Ketu: isolation, separation, ashram, spirituality, enlightenment,
detachment from material world, psychic intuition, occult, and
mystical knowledge, universal
This individual suffers from irrational thinking, stupidity, and
impulsive indulgence in unexplored territory. This gets them in
trouble quite often. The mind struggles when trying to
communicate properly. There is a great disconnect with one's
mother in terms of communication. The native hardly speaks, and

the mother could be so spiritually involved in her own life that she
fails to see the joy in her own children. Their speech and
communication style is very much out of context. Sometimes the
native does not know what they are talking about. They take the
conversation in different directions, and before you know it they
have lost their audience.
If this conjunction occurs in an earth or air sign, there is a great
deal of interest in scientific research and digging for the truth.
Many research students can be born with such a conjunction in 1st,
4th, 5th, 8th, 9th and 12th House. They love to communicate in
isolated places. When they have a meeting with someone they
have an urge to speak to them privately. Such an inclination comes
from having trouble speaking or pronouncing words at an early
age. They may have been ridiculed at school for this reason. If
Ketu has the lowest degree, or degree lower than Mercury, then
they can be great at mathematics. Mercury's calculative ability
becomes very intuitive, and for them the answer comes before
walking through the problem. Such people can be very well versed
in the art of multiple languages. As much as Ketu makes them
struggle in speaking, it becomes their passion to learn many
languages.

## Moon + Venus + Mars conjunction:

Moon: mother, emotions, sensuality, mind, feelings, thoughts,
imagination, fertility and fame
Venus: love, relationship, desire, pleasure, luxurious foods,
culture, family history, beauty, comfort, art, creativity, lust, and
sexuality
Mars: efforts, energy, will power, stamina, strength, passion,
fighting ability, impulsivity, accidents, injuries, wounds, sports,
athlete, martial arts, weaponry, engineer

This personality is curious about love and relationships, and is
constantly occupied with the desire for pleasure, sex, and comfort.
This is a very social placement where sensuality and lust are
energized with lot of masculine power. This person is quite active
in their social circle and surrounded by mostly successful,
independent people. Mars' impact on Venus and the Moon both
make them encounter self sufficient and confident individuals.
They enjoy the company of friends and colleagues at upscale
restaurants. If one of the planets is debilitated, there is an urgency
to socialize, especially with the opposite sex to feel mentally
balanced. Venus and Mars conjunction is making quite a
passionate stew, and the Moon makes the mind constantly think
about being in union with others, especially sexual unions. In the
sign of Gemini and Aquarius, it does tend to give bi-sexual
feelings, as the mind is being mixed with masculine and feminine
energy. The mother could have also been a social butterfly or very
hospitable to others. Venus and the Moon crave peace, pleasure,
happiness, and luxury. Mars intensifies these desires, which can
cause substance abuse, consuming unhealthy foods, and wasting
money on pleasure. The mother of the native could also be a
substance abuser and a networking queen, especially if this
conjunction occurs in the 3rd, 5th, 10th and 11th House.

## Moon + Venus + Jupiter conjunction:

Moon: mother, emotions, sensuality, mind, feelings, thoughts,
imagination, fertility and fame
Venus: love, relationship, desire, pleasure, luxurious foods,
culture, family history, beauty, comfort, art, creativity, lust and
sexuality
Jupiter: knowledge, wisdom, wealth, optimism, hope, law,
teachers, education, religious beliefs, and long distance pilgrimage

There is a great love for the creative arts, culture, history, and
higher knowledge. The mind feels balanced when in pursuit of
pleasure through learning about philosophy and wisdom. This
might make a person quite cultured and spiritual. It can also make
someone a great banker, CEO, or a hotel and restaurant manager.
The Moon, Venus, and Jupiter all represent pleasure through food,
home, and clothing. Venus originally rules the 2nd House of
consumption, and Jupiter is the karaka of the 2nd House which
represents food and the family business. The Moon represents the
home, mother's nourishment, and love. This particular conjunction
produces someone whose restaurant becomes a place where
customers feel like family. The mother of the native is very
pleasant, spiritual, and loves catering to others by providing the
best meal possible. The mother is a creative, spiritual guide, who
has the ability to indulge children in out of the box activities, like
taking a pilgrimage during summer.
If this conjunction occurs in a man's chart, then the wife would
take the role of the mother, and will provide the best nourishment
for her husband. She will have cultural and religious values, and a
sense of style. Marriage could also take place at a religious site
like a temple or foreign lands, as Venus and Jupiter conjunction
represents a love of traveling. Since these planets represent union,
the mind is always up for celebrating things on the go. When
Venus is with Jupiter, the mind automatically is inclined towards
foreign things and interest in other cultures. These natives enjoy
ethnic foods, textiles, and clothing, and may even run a business
related to these industries.

## Moon + Venus + Saturn conjunction:

Moon: mother, emotions, sensuality, mind, feelings, thoughts,
imagination, fertility and fame

Venus: love, relationship, desire, pleasure, luxurious foods,
culture, family history, beauty, comfort, art, creativity, lust and
sexuality
Saturn: responsibility, organization, structure, delay, separation,
grief, life span as in longevity, antique items, old people, chronic
illnesses, fear, anxiety, labor work, lower class, lower type jobs,
construction
This is one of the most unique conjunctions in astrology. The
combination can provide excellent status and wealth to the native,
especially in signs like Taurus, Libra, Aquarius and Pisces. The
mind of the native is fully aware of their sense of pleasure, and at
the same time they are willing to work very hard to attain the best
in life. If Venus has the lowest degrees, then there is a sense of
pleasure and comfort in organizing events, company meetings, and
running one’s own business. In my own personal experience, I
have seen quite a few millionaires with such a conjunction in the
signs I described. Here, Saturn is largely forced to lay his hands off
the Moon. Both the Moon and Venus represents similar things in
life, and if Saturn is going to shut down the Moon, he would be
forced to shut down his best friend Venus. This creates a stable
mind for the native. If the Moon is too close to Saturn in degrees
versus Venus, then you can bet that there would be lot of stress,
responsibility, and burden upon the native to cater to the needs to
Venus, who represents one’s spouse, especially for a man. They
will take on a lot of responsibility to make them happy.
Saturn is a natural malefic, and he will never be able to change that
about himself regardless of how comfortable he is with his pal
Venus. Saturn knows marriage could be delayed, more so for a
man, since Venus is the main karaka for marriage in a man's chart.
It also delays marriage for females in most cases. Another thing to
understand here is that whenever the Moon and Venus come into
conjunction, the person becomes very clever and is always in
pursuit of pleasure. Saturn is the provider of structure and

organization, so the native is capable of scheming great ideas to
attain wealth, luxury, and pleasure. This can lead to hurting others
for selfish reasons, especially if Rahu aspects this conjunction.
Also, the Saturn and Venus conjunction can make a native very
creative when the Moon is present. The native may be inclined
towards artistic ideas as well.
If such a conjunction occurs in the 2nd, 3rd, 7th, 10th and 11th
House, it can lead to a windfall of money for a long duration,
especially in the dasha of these three planets.

## Moon + Venus + Rahu conjunction:

Moon: mother, emotions, sensuality, mind, feelings, thoughts,
imagination, fertility and fame
Venus: love, relationship, desire, pleasure, luxurious foods,
culture, family history, beauty, comfort, art, creativity, lust and
sexuality
Rahu: illusion, taboo breaker, unconventional, deceiver, magician,
foreign products and places, thief, politician, wide communication,
and computer related products
This forms another interesting conjunction. As negative as it may
look, this translates into more pleasure and comfort than a person
can handle. Rahu enjoys Venus' company due to her love for
sensuality, relationships, love, art, pleasure, fame and wealth.
Rahu's ability amplify those characteristics is also quite welcomed
by Venus. Rahu gives fuel to Venus' ability to seek pleasure,
which leads a native to work hard and achieve his or her goals,
usually in a creative field. Rahu again is a natural malefic, and
doesn't like the Moon at all. For this reason, the mind can become
obsessed with the pleasure Venus seeks and can lead to dramatic

results. The native is always caught in some drama due to their
own misunderstanding and misdirection caused by Rahu.
Rahu's desires are never sated, so love, relationships, sexuality,
and lust are amplified, yet left unfulfilled. It can lead the person's
mind to think they need more, and they need something new.
Rahu and Venus conjunction usually leads to a foreign partner or
someone from foreign lands. Since the mind is involved, the native
seeks extreme pleasures in such places. If Rahu has the highest
degree in the chart for a male, then it can lead to marrying a wife
who might actually be like Rahu. This woman would be cunning,
pleasure seeking, and always looking for more. If Venus sits in a
strong sign like Taurus, Libra, or Pisces, where Venus has full
control, she will be able to stand her ground without being
influenced by Rahu’s scheming ways.
The native can also have a mother who might be a healer or doctor
if Rahu has the lowest degree. She may only seek her children’s
company for pleasure. The mother of this native may actually
indulge in her own pleasure most of the time if Rahu has an
eclipsed Moon.

## Moon + Venus + Ketu conjunction:

Moon: mother, emotions, sensuality, mind, feelings, thoughts,
imagination, fertility and fame
Venus: love, relationship, desire, pleasure, luxurious foods,
culture, family history, beauty, comfort, art, creativity, lust and
sexuality
Ketu: isolation, separation, ashram, spirituality, enlightenment,
detachment from material world, psychic intuition, occult and
mystical knowledge, universal

The body seeks pleasure but the mind does not accept it. Although
there is tremendous artistic talent and a desire to achieve material
goals, there is a constant state of dissatisfaction in life. What the
mind wants and seeks for pleasure turns out to be unfulfilling. This
makes a person feel lost in life and they question their motivation
to achieve their goals. Ketu shows the things we have
accomplished and the talent we have within. When it comes in
contact with the Moon and Venus, creativity is already embedded,
but the desire to love and create is a chore. Whether it is music, art,
design, fashion, or cinema, the task feels easy yet unsatisfying.
In love and relationships, the native can incur heartbreaks and
separation from their spouse or lover; especially in a male's chart.
It shows separation from the mother at an early age due to work or
personal reasons, and shows dissatisfaction of the spouse. There is
a constant battle to attain and release. Whatever these natives gain
in the material world is given away to achieve peace and
spirituality. Ketu's psychic powers are passed onto Venus as a
sense of pleasure and for the mind to obtain a new reality. These
people can have psychic powers, healing abilities, and a way to
connect with the unknown. Eventually, Venus finds her pleasure
through the eccentric yet powerful character of Ketu.

## Moon + Mars + Jupiter conjunction:

Moon: mother, emotions, sensuality, mind, feelings, thoughts,
imagination, fertility and fame
Mars: efforts, energy, will power, stamina, strength, passion,
fighting ability, impulsivity, accidents, injuries, wounds, sports,
athlete, martial arts, weaponry, engineer
Jupiter: knowledge, wisdom, wealth, optimism, hope, law,
teachers, education, religious beliefs, and long distance pilgrimage

This conjunction represents an active mind that is motivated to do
what they believe in. Many people take action towards their goals,
but the difference with this conjunction is that they constantly
follow the path set by Jupiter where the mind is ready to
implement without question. It's quite hard to change the mind of
these people which can be a negative thing. If someone believes
that racism and slavery in United States is necessary to preserve
the nation, they would be willing to die for such a belief no matter
what education and advice they have received.
However, if the mind is on track, it can produce a strong willed
person who fights for the underprivileged and is willing to die for
religious and cultural beliefs. The mother of the native would also
have a strong belief system and would be a guiding factor for the
person. Mars and Jupiter usually produce someone who motivates
others with their will power and wisdom.

## Moon + Mars + Saturn conjunction:

Moon: mother, emotions, sensuality, mind, feelings, thoughts,
imagination, fertility, and fame
Mars: efforts, energy, will power, stamina, strength, passion,
fighting ability, impulsivity, accidents, injuries, wounds, sports,
athlete, martial arts, weaponry, engineer
Saturn: responsibility, organization, structure, delay, separation,
grief, life span as in longevity, antique items, old people, chronic
illnesses, fear, anxiety, labor work, lower class, lower type jobs,
construction
This combination produces a very turbulent force on the mind.
Both Mars and Saturn represent a war zone, and when the Moon is
in the middle it causes a battle of right and wrong. This force may
be so strong that it can produce a native with bipolar disorder or

any other kind of mental disorder. One of the most profound
effects of this conjunction is severe anger problems. The outburst
is so intense, it can come down to killing a person if that's what
satisfies the mind and makes it feel balanced. No matter what
house this conjunction takes place in, the mind will experience
post-traumatic stress disorder in that house. This is especially true
if the Moon has middle degrees. Both Mars and Saturn are
throwing arrows at each other and most of them are landing on the
Moon.
I have seen such conjunctions quite a few times and none have
prevailed. They were all dealing with the battlefield in their mind
and surroundings. This is one of the most intense conjunctions to
have in one's life. A great escape for such conjunction would be if
the Moon has the highest degree and is away more than 15 degrees
from Mars and Saturn. Professionally speaking, this conjunction
can make for an engineer, miner, policeman, soldier, politician,
and professional fighter. They have quite an interest in the art of
weaponry like sword making, fencing, and guns.
If the Moon happens to be in an earth sign, especially its exalted
sign of Taurus, the native would be able to overcome the battle
within and steer towards mental progress. However, there will still
be broken glass on their path to redemption.

## Moon + Mars + Rahu conjunction:

Moon: mother, emotions, sensuality, mind, feelings, thoughts,
imagination, fertility and fame
Mars: efforts, energy, will power, stamina, strength, passion,
fighting ability, impulsivity, accidents, injuries, wounds, sports,
athlete, martial arts, weaponry, engineer

Rahu: illusion, taboo breaker, unconventional, deceiver, magician,
foreign products and places, thief, politician, wide communication,
and computer related products.
This conjunction goes from one extreme to the other. The mind is
not only in overdrive, but is flying high. It is similar to a volcano
that can erupt at any moment. This is another combination for
excessive anger, illusion, psychosis, and a business like mentality.
The mother can easily be the punisher in early life. Instead of
spanking, the mother would beat the native. In India, where
physical abuse in the household is normal, the native could
experience violence from the mother and male sibling. This can
happen if the Moon is having the lowest degrees in the chart. If
Moon possesses the highest degree, the mind would still be full of
tension and anger, but it would be able to overcome it, unless of
course the Moon is debilitated.
When these natives apply their mind to something, they will try to
achieve at any cost, even if it requires hurting someone else. There
is a great deal of mental tension that stems from running into
people of a shady nature. If such a conjunction occurs in the 7th,
8th or 12th House, it can show a person who might see things of
other worlds, whether it's spirits, angels, or energies of other
dimensions. These may be mirages of the mind, as Rahu is an
illusionist. Such conjunctions occurring in the 2nd and 4th House
can show a thief, or someone who steals from their mother and
other family members. To make such claims, the native’s
nakshatra has to be studied as well.
Mars represents drive, and Rahu represents breaking boundaries
and taboo. The mind may be inclined towards illegal deeds or
activities that are not accepted by society like joining gangs,
pornography, or becoming an illegal arms dealer.

## Moon + Mars + Ketu conjunction:

Moon: mother, emotions, sensuality, mind, feelings, thoughts,
imagination, fertility and fame
Mars: efforts, energy, will power, stamina, strength, passion,
fighting ability, impulsivity, accidents, injuries, wounds, sports,
athlete, martial arts, weaponry, engineer
Ketu: isolation, separation, ashram, spirituality, enlightenment,
detachment from material world, psychic intuition, occult and
mystical knowledge, universal
Ketu is an impulsive fighter, and in the reflection of Mars, it
represents what we have already accomplished. Ketu is the
victorious army while Mars is the charging army. This is another
symbol of war, and can create an out of ordinary blood bath. The
mind rejects the idea of war, but is ready to charge against others
at any moment who pose a threat. Anytime the Moon comes in
contact with two malefic planets, it's known as hemming. Here, the
Moon is hemmed between two planets. Peace of mind no longer
exists. The mind is agitated, nervous, and anxious about being
attacked. They feel like a warrior sleeping on a battlefield. There is
no time to rest as the enemy is charging, and can attack any
second. Ketu is the intuitive power, while Mars is the energy itself.
The mind can be highly charged with intuition if such conjunctions
occur in 12th House. This conjunction works best in earth signs
where both Mars and Ketu's energy is stable. They also do well
with Pisces, which is about transformation.
After battling opposition from the mother, friends and other male
colleagues, the native seeks the path of enlightenment, and it is
easily received due to Mars' highly charged attitude towards
attainment of goals. The mother might have died at a very young
age. The native may have been separated from the mother for a
long duration, either because the mother was abusive or had severe

health problems. If the mother is healthy up until age 15, then there
is no need to worry. She gains energy and intuitive powers through
the native's Mars. If Mars has the lowest degree and Ketu the
highest, this can lead to so much turbulence in the mind that the
native may commit suicide or even a murder suicide.

## Moon + Jupiter + Saturn conjunction:

Moon: mother, emotions, sensuality, mind, feelings, thoughts,
imagination, fertility and fame
Jupiter: knowledge, wisdom, wealth, optimism, hope, law,
teachers, education, religious beliefs, and long distance pilgrimage
Saturn: responsibility, organization, structure, delay, separation,
grief, life span as in longevity, antique items, old people, chronic
illnesses, fear, anxiety, labor work, lower class, lower type jobs,
construction
The mind is able to organize information, and is disciplined
enough to attain higher knowledge and wisdom. In this
conjunction, the mind is not severely attacked by Saturn's cold,
harsh behavior because expansive Jupiter is playing the role of
referee. Jupiter's optimism and light heartedness keeps the mind in
balance. It also keeps the mind organized, disciplined, and focused
on the tasks at hand. Since Jupiter represents knowledge, the native
is drawn towards learning throughout life. They may choose to
pursue law, biomedical engineering, writing, preaching, teaching
and governing.
Jupiter and Saturn's conjunction bring a sense of maturity to a
person. Since the mind is involved, they become studious, and
want to follow a well directed path and live a simple, happy life.
They
are
your
government
workers,
school
teachers,
administrators, and city employees. They like steadiness at their

workplace. This has something to do with routine and utilizing
their knowledge base. Teachers are often seen with such a
combination. The professor keeps learning while teaching the
same subjects repeatedly. Their mind is concentrating on the work
at hand, and there is little time to be depressed or to play. They are
not bothered by this, as it is part of being human.
President Barack Obama has a Jupiter and Saturn conjunction in
the ascendant while Jupiter aspects the Moon. He was a professor
at the University of Chicago teaching constitutional law. He
enjoyed educating his students on a subject matter he was
passionate about while reinforcing his belief in America and the
laws it was founded upon. This is not an exact example of this
conjunction, but demonstrates the impact that may be felt by the
native.

## Moon + Jupiter + Rahu conjunction:

Moon: mother, emotions, sensuality, mind, feelings, thoughts,
imagination, fertility, and fame
Jupiter: knowledge, wisdom, wealth, optimism, hope, law,
teachers, education, religious beliefs, and long distance pilgrimage
Rahu: illusion, taboo breaker, unconventional, deceiver, magician,
foreign products and places, thief, politician, wide communication,
and computer related products
This native engages in learning to a point of obsession; however
the knowledge may not be within the confines of society. Rahu
represents science in terms of educational subjects, and with the
companionship of Jupiter, the mind seeks knowledge that breaks
taboo, boundaries, and laws. Jupiter is the only planet that can
control Rahu, and tends to keep the mind focused on invention,
research, and studies. It avoids the distraction of other worldly

affairs. Such a conjunction can produce a scientist working on
stem cell research, artificial intelligence, and fertility issues. It can
also produce spies, occultists, and marine biologists.
The mind is filled with optimism and hope, however Rahu brings
great obsession to a good thing. A scientist, student, or anyone for
that matter, will be so obsessed with learning that it will impact
their social behavior and personal life. Rahu always wants to
obtain worldly knowledge in the company of Jupiter. At the same
time, it puts a negative eclipse upon the Moon and throws some of
his powers on Jupiter. A person can use their knowledge for a
negative purpose, and it may even involve breaking the law. For
example, a person can gain knowledge about their workplace and
since the mind is eclipsed, they will not be able to distinguish
between right and wrong. Therefore, they may steal without
remorse, or lie on documentation. Jupiter, our knowledge and
wisdom, is greatly impacted by Rahu. Since Jupiter does control
Rahu, he doesn't let him go too far. Even though Jupiter may not
be able to provide everything, he will at least keep Rahu's demonic
nature in control.
The mother of the native will also have knowledge and interest in
the occult and alternative science. If Rahu has the middle degree
while the Moon has the lowest degree, it can greatly impact the
native's relationship with mother. Also, the mother's purpose in life
may be to selfishly gain from the native while providing
superficial affection. If Rahu has the lowest degrees, then it will
have more impact on a person's interests rather than scheming and
breaking the law.

## Moon + Jupiter + Ketu conjunction:

Moon: mother, emotions, sensuality, mind, feelings, thoughts,
imagination, fertility, and fame

Jupiter: knowledge, wisdom, wealth, optimism, hope, law,
teachers, education, religious beliefs, and long distance pilgrimage
Ketu: isolation, separation, ashram, spirituality, enlightenment,
detachment from material world, psychic intuition, occult and
mystical knowledge, universal
This conjunction creates great research ability in the areas of
spirituality, enlightenment, meditation, and occult. Ketu's
relationship with Jupiter is never welcomed by the great teacher.
However, Ketu doesn't mind extracting all that Jupiter knows
about the final placement of the soul. The mind of the native tries
to separate common knowledge from unusual, more intriguing
information. They do tend to experiment with drugs to get closer to
the truth, or take trips to isolated places in the world to gain
knowledge about life after death. There is a great deal of interest in
knowing about the origins of one's desires, but there is also
dissatisfaction from teachers and gurus they come across to gain
access to the truth. Most of the information they find is quite
mundane unless the Guru has similar alignments or has the great
impact of Ketu. Although the mother is spiritual, her spirituality
would distance the native from her affection. This may be because
the native doesn't relate to the mother's teachings, or the mother
was greatly dissatisfied by her offspring and took to spirituality for
more answers. If Ketu holds the lowest degree, then the mother's
spiritual guidance would provide a great source of knowledge and
peace without separation, unless this conjunction occurs in the 4th
House.

## Moon + Saturn + Rahu conjunction:

Moon: mother, emotions, sensuality, mind, feelings, thoughts,
imagination, fertility, and fame

Saturn: responsibility, organization, structure, delay, separation,
grief, life span as in longevity, antique items, old people, chronic
illnesses, fear, anxiety, labor work, lower class, lower type jobs,
construction
Rahu: illusion, taboo breaker, unconventional, deceiver, magician,
foreign products and places, thief, politician, wide communication,
and computer related products
This conjunction is very hard on the mind and mother. It can show
lot of unusual visions, dreams and fear from such dreams. They
can have sudden panic attacks and may have fear of things related
to the house where this conjunction takes place. It's always better
to have Rahu with the lowest degree here because then it amplifies
the mind towards being very disciplined, organized and structured,
otherwise if Moon has the middle degrees then it can show sudden
fear, anxiety, nervousness etc. Saturn wants to restrict the mind
from thinking about the future or planning about things that are not
real while Rahu is forcing the mind to think outside of the
boundaries and expand. This tug of war can be a lot of pressure for
the moon and the mother herself can feel such pressure regarding
the native.
Such people are very much interested in the darker side of the
world because of the impact of two planets that bring darkens and
eclipse.

## Moon + Saturn + Ketu conjunction:

Moon: mother, emotions, sensuality, mind, feelings, thoughts,
imagination, fertility, and fame
Saturn: responsibility, organization, structure, delay, separation,
grief, life span as in longevity, antique items, old people, chronic

illnesses, fear, anxiety, labor work, lower class, lower type jobs,
construction
Ketu: isolation, separation, ashram, spirituality, enlightenment,
detachment from material world, psychic intuition, occult and
mystical knowledge, universal
This conjunction shows a person who didn't get much nourishment
from their mother. The mother was so spiritual and involved in
religious practice that she couldn't attend the native or the mother
left the native in early childhood. This person can run into lot of
traumatic experiences during childhood which will shape their
weird mind in adulthood. Such people go into tantric and occult
practice, especially in the mahdasha of Saturn and Ketu or if the
conjunction is placed in the nakshatra of Ketu. Such people have
great physic intuition if the conjunction is in 8th or 12th house, or
has an aspect from
Jupiter. They make some of the best
investigators and research scientists.

## Mercury + Venus + Mars conjunction:

Mercury: intelligence, communication, speech, logic, education,
analytical skills, calculative skills, education, business, trading and
language skills
Venuslove, relationship, desire, pleasure, luxurious foods,
culture, family history, beauty, comfort, art, creativity, lust and
sexuality
Mars: efforts, energy, will power, stamina, strength, passion,
fighting ability, impulsivity, accidents, injuries, wounds, sports,
athlete, martial arts, weaponry, engineer
There is a sense of passion, romance, lust, and promiscuity in the
way these natives communicate. There is great pleasure found in
speaking and writing about quizzical sexual behavior. If they are

writers, they would create colorful, zesty, romantic novels. They
speak in an energetic and passionate way. When they speak their
mind, there is a sense of purpose towards sex and relationships.
They can be relationship experts, dating gurus, romance advisors,
matchmakers, authors, comedians, and actors.
There is tremendous energy and will to win over people through
one's communication, logic, and sensuality. The sexual drive
within is not only about climaxing, but also a way to exert their
frustration. This is the way they relate and communicate with the
opposite or same sex. Sex and sexuality is a form of spiritual love.
These natives are often misunderstood for foul mouthed perverts.
However, when a person's sense of pleasure, purpose, and
communication becomes about passion, it's difficult not to exude
it. If you are seeking this type of fiery personality, look for this
conjunction in the 1st, 3rd, 5th, 7th, 8th, 9th and 12th House. If
one of these planets is debilitated, especially Mars, the person can
be abusive towards women, or could have faced abuse as child.

## Mercury + Venus + Jupiter conjunction:

Mercury: intelligence, communication, speech, logic, education,
analytical skills, calculative skills, education, business, trading and
language skills
Venus: love, relationship, desire, pleasure, luxurious foods,
culture, family history, beauty, comfort, art, creativity, lust and
sexuality
Jupiter: knowledge, wisdom, wealth, optimism, hope, law,
teachers, education, religious beliefs, and long distance pilgrimage
There is a great sense of wisdom in one's communication skills,
and they enjoy talking about higher knowledge related to law and

spirituality. The native takes pride in their intelligence and higher
education, and others value this quality as well. Venus' nature of
beautifying everything brings creative expression to their work,
especially preaching and teaching. They have a pleasant voice that
one can listen to for hours because they bring a sense of great
optimism and joy when communicating, especially spiritually.
These natives take pleasure in speaking with honest and sincere
communication.
In love and relationships, there needs to be an authentic,
intelligent, spiritual connection. The spouse of the native would be
very smart and cultured. When they speak, there is great wisdom
shining from their bright eyes. In terms of career path, they could
be a professor of anthropology, accountant, finance guru, or author
of non-fiction or new age books.

## Mercury + Venus + Saturn conjunction:

Mercury: intelligence, communication, speech, logic, education,
analytical skills, calculative skills, education, business, trading and
language skills
Venus: love, relationship, desire, pleasure, luxurious foods,
culture, family history, beauty, comfort, art, creativity, lust and
sexuality
Saturn: responsibility, organization, structure, delay, separation,
grief, life span as in longevity, antique items, old people, chronic
illnesses, fear, anxiety, labor work, lower class, lower type jobs,
construction
This beautiful conjunction brings three close friends together.
Saturn, Venus, and Mercury have a great bond and respect for each
other. The Saturn and Venus conjunction brings about creativity
due to Venus' comfort zone with Saturn, and Mercury's

intelligence putting focus and discipline on the art of
communication. Some of the greatest artists and personalities have
been born with such a conjunction, like Albert Einstein. While
being a physicist, he was also an artist and a musician. Einstein
was known to play the violin and seduce ladies at parties with his
great charisma. Being a Pisces, he derived his mathematical skills
and theories through spiritual and imaginative ideas.
Saturn demonstrates the positive side of discipline and structure
with Mercury and Venus. Although Mercury really enjoys such
structure, Venus finds it difficult to find true love. Here, the artistic
and creative ability of a person is not without structure. There is
always an organized idea behind each creation. Einstein's theory of
relativity came when he was watching a wall clock on a tower
while driving away on a bus. Many artists cannot organize their
ideas and focus on them; their creativity is scattered. Saturn pulls it
all together, and helps them take shape.
These people can be shy and speak quite late in life if Saturn is
debilitated. Also, Saturn's close conjunction to Venus delays
marriage for the native. If the native gets married too early, then
Saturn separates the union and usually brings about a more mature
partner later in life. At this time, marriage is more based on a solid
foundation than romance and love. Einstein's romantic life took
similar shape due to Saturn's conjunction with Venus.

## Mercury + Venus + Rahu conjunction:

Mercury: intelligence, communication, speech, logic, education,
analytical skills, calculative skills, education, business, trading and
language skills

Venuslove, relationship, desire, pleasure, luxurious foods,
culture, family history, beauty, comfort, art, creativity, lust and
sexuality
Rahuillusion, taboo breaker, unconventional, deceiver, magician,
foreign products and places, thief, Politian, wide communication,
and computer related products
Unusual creative expression comes to mind with this conjunction.
There is a great need for sexual, romantic, and lustful
communication in all aspects of life. The native takes great
pleasure in communicating unusual and taboo breaking subjects
such as sexual orientation, interracial marriage, sex therapy, exotic
dancing, and perhaps even the more dark side of human nature.
The native becomes overly obsessive in romantic relationships and
has a tremendous urge to constantly communicate with their
partner. This can create a very controlling individual due to their
overprotectiveness, lust, and sexual needs. These natives are not
ashamed to talk about sexual behavior, and will most likely engage
in all types of sexual activities.
Their sense of pleasure comes from relating and communicating
with people of different cultural backgrounds, especially from
foreign lands. Rahu creates lots of interest in finding creativity, art,
and beauty in foreign things. Mercury gives Venus exclusive
access to communicate about foreign things. On a good note, this
can make for a historian, anthropologist, or archeologist. Creativity
is amplified with Rahu and speech is used for mass
communication. If such conjunctions occur in 6th, 8th or 12th
House, it can lead to secret love affairs, pornography, and
prostitution, especially if Venus is debilitated or in the sign of
Scorpio. Such a sign placement creates a deep rooted sexual past,
which could include sexual abuse or growing up in a household
where sex and drugs were common. Rahu loves indulging in
substance abuse, and when sitting with Venus, the sense of
pleasure is derived from consuming exotic foods, drinks, and

illegal substances. Mercury's curiosity and youthfulness combined
with Rahu's nature of breaking rules leads Venus to places where it
never thought it would be. These natives are quite adventurous,
and will try anything once.

## Mercury + Venus + Ketu conjunction:

Mercury: intelligence, communication, speech, logic, education,
analytical skills, calculative skills, education, business, trading and
language skills
Venuslove, relationship, desire, pleasure, luxurious foods,
culture, family history, beauty, comfort, art, creativity, lust and
sexuality
Ketu: isolation, separation, ashram, spirituality, enlightenment,
detachment from material world, psychic intuition, occult and
mystical knowledge, universal.
In this conjunction, there is a great need to distance oneself from
worldly pleasure and mass communication after constant
disappointments. What Rahu wants, Ketu wants to let go of. For
these natives, creativity and art come easy. It is part of them, and
through Ketu's union, they achieved success in past lives. They are
also able to succeed in music and cinema through such a
conjunction, however there is dissatisfaction, especially from
speaking. Venus' pleasure of communication is constantly
challenged, questioned, and critiqued. The natives are their own
biggest critics. For this reason, they try to become perfectionist in
what they do, and such perfectionism can drive them to insanity.
To some extent the person is immune to enjoyment and pleasure.
Pleasure, especially in a romantic context, has a lot of
responsibility and burden. If Ketu holds the highest degree,
communication and pleasure may come from spiritual, occult, or
mystical things. They can be public speakers of enlightenment,

moksha, and life after death. In the 8th and 12th House, this
conjunction can produce a God gifted psychic.
Their way of seeking pleasure might be very eccentric and derived
from uncanny behavior. On the positive side, this conjunction
gives a gift of research. Whether it's an art and music historian,
designer, mathematician, or scientist, they are natural seekers of
answers, and research becomes a source of pleasure for them. In a
man's chart, this can show a great deal of long distance
communication with their wife. The wife may communicate with
foul language, or the communication may be disconnected. If Ketu
has the lowest degrees in the conjunction, then the wife may be
very spiritual, artistic, and will travel a lot throughout marriage.

## Mercury + Mars + Jupiter conjunction:

Mercury: intelligence, communication, speech, logic, education,
analytical skills, calculative skills, education, business, trading and
language skills
Mars: efforts, energy, will power, stamina, strength, passion,
fighting ability, impulsivity, accidents, injuries, wounds, sports,
athlete, martial arts, weaponry, engineer
Jupiter: knowledge, wisdom, wealth, optimism, hope, law,
teachers, education, religious beliefs, and long distance pilgrimage
This conjunction produces an intelligent and wise fighter. The
native can be a great martial artist, someone knowledgeable in the
art of weaponry, or a brilliant engineer. Mars is construction,
Mercury is hands, and Jupiter is knowledge about a certain subject.
This can also produce accountants, financiers, bankers, and project
managers. There is a great energy in one's ability to communicate.
Due to Jupiter's wisdom, the person is no longer an impulsive
speaker with a harsh mouth, but rather there is good purpose

behind their speech and communication. When they speak, they
tell the truth and have tremendous knowledge. Mars gives their
speech energy and excitement.
When playing sports, this native has great physical, mental, and
analytical skills. In basketball, this person knows how to drive,
jump, dunk, and shoot. They know exactly when to make their
move, and this requires knowledge, intelligence, and ability. In the
1st, 3rd, 5th, 6th, 10th and 11th House, you are likely to see
someone in professional sports if they do not go down an academic
path.

## Mercury + Mars + Saturn conjunction:

Mercury: intelligence, communication, speech, logic, education,
analytical skills, calculative skills, education, business, trading and
language skills
Mars: efforts, energy, will power, stamina, strength, passion,
fighting ability, impulsivity, accidents, injuries, wounds, sports,
athlete, martial arts, weaponry, engineer
Saturn: responsibility, organization, structure, delay, separation,
grief, life span as in longevity, antique items, old people, chronic
illnesses, fear, anxiety, labor work, lower class, lower type jobs,
construction
There is great stress on the native's intelligence because it is stuck
in a war between two great enemies. If the degrees from each other
are quite far apart, then it can produce a talented real estate guru,
builder, land developer, or engineer in the oil and mining industry.
They may also be capable of manufacturing guns, factory
machines, or excel at any technical skill. If such a conjunction
occurs in 6th or 12th House with one of these planets debilitated,
then there is more battle with opposition, the mind, and ability to

comprehend things. This usually happens in certain nakshatras
and degrees. Mercury is not as weak as the Moon, which is highly
influenced. Mercury is neutral in nature and able to adapt to the
energies of other planets. Nowadays, you will find people going
into the computer and civil engineering field with this
combination. The intelligence is focused and disciplined towards a
subject with enthusiasm, passion, and energy.
Just like the previous conjunction, this can also produce an athlete
on a professional level. We all can play sports to some degree, but
to take them to a professional level requires certain conjunctions
and yogas. Such conjunctions in the 2nd, 3rd and 5th House can
produce a manual instructor, or someone who shows you how to
do something, like a yoga teacher, coach, or trainer in a
corporation.

## Mercury + Mars + Rahu conjunction:

Mercury: intelligence, communication, speech, logic, education,
analytical skills, calculative skills, education, business, trading and
language skills.
Mars: efforts, energy, will power, stamina, strength, passion,
fighting ability, impulsivity, accidents, injuries, wounds, sports,
athlete, martial arts, weaponry, engineer.
Rahu: illusion, taboo breaker, unconventional, deceiver, magician,
foreign products and places, thief, politician, wide communication,
and computer related products.
The native has a very intelligent mind; perhaps too much for their
own good. This clearly shows a computer hacker, computer
programmer, or engineer. Rahu represents computers in the new
age, while Mercury represents language, and Mars represents
construction of such language. I cannot begin to tell you how many

software engineers I have seen with this conjunction or mutual
aspect between such planets. For the past fifteen years, this
conjunction and planets are playing a vital role in the information
technology industry. Depending upon the sign placement of such
conjunctions, it can either be a blessing or a curse.
This conjunction is not just about writing code for an application
or software, but it can also make the native’s intelligence sneaky
and cunning, which can lead to hacking systems and eventually
getting caught in certain time periods.

## Mercury + Mars + Ketu conjunction:

Mercury: intelligence, communication, speech, logic, education,
analytical skills, calculative skills, education, business, trading and
language skills.
Mars: efforts, energy, will power, stamina, strength, passion,
fighting ability, impulsivity, accidents, injuries, wounds, sports,
athlete, martial arts, weaponry, engineer.
Ketu: isolation, separation, ashram, spirituality, enlightenment,
detachment from material world, psychic intuition, occult and
mystical knowledge, universal.
This native possesses very disturbed intelligence that may not
make sense to others and is often directionless. Foul speech, erratic
thinking, and sometimes even cunningness run wild. In this
conjunction, Rahu is still in aspect to Mercury and Mars because
Rahu and Ketu are always 7 places from each other, meaning they
aspect each other. They are intelligent and aggressive, and may be
working 24 hours a day, 7 days a week without the right purpose.
Most of the time, such people will plan ways to scam others,
especially in the sign of Cancer and Pisces, where Mercury and
Mars are debilitated. Mars represents injuries, accidents, cuts, and

bruises. Ketu does the same but in a more severe way, like sudden
accidents. The native can get into a lot of physical trouble due to
their intelligence, and even be stabbed in their lifetime if Mars is
debilitated. The native is a natural fighter, warrior, athlete, and
may take part in any kind of physical contact sport.
On a professional front, they can make computer geniuses, as
Mars, Mercury, Ketu, and Rahu are all indicators of the computer
industry. They can be computer programmers, IT consultants, data
engineers, and software developers. They have a clever mind and
speak well. Due to Rahu's ability to disguise things, the person's
hard speech is developed by Mercury, Mars, and Ketu. In this
respect, speech is transformed, and they become a smooth talker
who always has an agenda. A brilliant computer genius can be
cunning in their personal life, and get into trouble due to their lies
and schemes.

## Venus + Mars + Jupiter conjunction:

Venus: love, relationship, desire, pleasure, luxurious foods,
culture, family history, beauty, comfort, art, creativity, lust and
sexuality.
Mars: efforts, energy, will power, stamina, strength, passion,
fighting ability, impulsivity, accidents, injuries, wounds, sports,
athlete, martial arts, weaponry, engineer.
Jupiter: knowledge, wisdom, wealth, optimism, hope, law,
teachers, education, religious beliefs, and long distance pilgrimage.
There is lust for knowledge, philosophy, culture, and religion. This
marks a passionate person who seeks knowledge through
relationships and sensuality. They fall hard for their significant
other, and are filled with optimism. They demonstrate great love
and sexuality. Jupiter always expands the quality of the planet he

sits with. Here Venus' sexual pleasure, lust, luxury, and
consumptive qualities increase. With the dose of Mars in the mix,
they not only seek pleasure aggressively, but they teach pleasure as
well. These natives can become sexologists, relationship experts,
or professors who teach subjects related to marriage and
relationships. They become very good at designing, as creativity is
a way of exerting energy and finding one's purpose.
The best way to describe this conjunction is the daughter of King
Louis XIV, Emilie Du Chatelet, who was not only a physicist in a
male dominated society in 17th century, but also sexy,
provocative, and romantic. Her pleasure was knowledge and
higher learning. She proved Sir Isaac Newton wrong at the age of
25 about his theory of gravity.
Both sexes will behave the same way. The meaning of such a
conjunction can be drastically be different if one of the planet is
debilitated, especially the one having the most impact on this
conjunction and the house placement. The negative side can
produce physical violence with women. If Mars is debilitated
without sitting in kendra houses, or the native ends up with a very
dominating and controlling spouse, they may be unhappy.

## Venus + Mars + Saturn conjunction:

Venus: love, relationship, desire, pleasure, luxurious foods,
culture, family history, beauty, comfort, art, creativity, lust and
sexuality.
Mars: efforts, energy, will power, stamina, strength, passion,
fighting ability, impulsivity, accidents, injuries, wounds, sports,
athlete, martial arts, weaponry, engineer.
Saturn: responsibility, organization, structure, delay, separation,
grief, life span as in longevity, antique items, old people, chronic

illnesses, fear, anxiety, labor work, lower class, lower type jobs,
construction.
In this conjunction, Venus suffers because it is in the midst of
war. This is similar to a princess in her gown standing in the
middle of a battlefield. The qualities of Venus have not
evaporated, but are rather confused. This can show great stress in
forming all kinds of relationships, including romantic ones. They
may end up in distorted situations where the spouse might even hit
them physically. If a man has this conjunction, his spouse can
create havoc for him or he can actually physically assault his
spouse. Venus is not itself, and loses its identity as it begins to
show great distress when getting close to someone. This
conjunction not only leads to late marriage, but shows an early
divorce, especially if it occurs in the 12th, 1st, 2nd, 4th, 7th, 8th
and 10th House. In each of these houses, Mars or Saturn aspects
the 7th House of marriage and 8th house of married life. Woman
can suffer a great deal from stress, even to a point of rape or
molestation, especially in the nakshatra of Mars and Rahu.
On a positive note, this conjunction gives love for construction,
building, and manufacturing. These natives love to be part of a
corporation where they can organize and create structure for a
company. They also have a creative flair due to the presence of
Saturn. Since Saturn is in a war with Mars, such creativity can get
lost, and the person may feel lazy and lethargic. Although bringing
organization and structure is difficult for the native, one has to see
what sign this conjunction takes place in. If it occurs in an earth
sign, there is far more calm than if it is in a fire or air sign.

## Venus + Mars + Rahu conjunction:

Venus: love, relationship, desire, pleasure, luxurious foods,
culture, family history, beauty, comfort, art, creativity, lust and
sexuality.

Mars: efforts, energy, will power, stamina, strength, passion,
fighting ability, impulsivity, accidents, injuries, wounds, sports,
athlete, martial arts, weaponry, engineer.
Rahu: illusion, taboo breaker, unconventional, deceiver, magician,
foreign products and places, thief, politician, wide communication,
and computer related products.
This is another distressed situation for gentle Venus. There is very
obsessive passion and lust within the native, almost to the point of
being a stalker. Venus' energy is not only expanded, but is given a
jet engine by Mars. Such a native can fall in and out of love easily.
Their appetite for sex, lust, and passion is so great that no one can
satisfy their needs. Such a conjunction in the 12th, 1st, 4th, 5th, 7th
and 8th House can make a person a sex addict. They may be
obsessed with pornography and may suddenly engage in it. They
feel the need to be with someone from foreign lands that is
independent and active.
If such a conjunction occurs in a woman's chart, they would love a
man who has an active lifestyle and is quite sexual. They enjoy
being adventurous and going to exotic places to explore love, lust,
and passion. They are quite extravagant, and indulge in a lavish
lifestyle. At the same time, they need to live life on the edge. A
man with such a conjunction might act similar to a woman,
however his sexual appetite won't be filled by just one. A woman
might be able to quench her thirst with one man, but a man with
this alignment is unable to do the same. This conjunction can lead
a person to commit sex crimes if it occurs in the sign of Aries,
Cancer, Virgo, Libra, and Scorpio. Other zodiac signs are also
prone, but these signs are connected with either debilitation of one
of the planets or a hard foundation for all three planets. Such a sign
placement can bring about sexual trauma in a native, especially
during childhood.

## Venus + Mars + Ketu conjunction:

Venus: love, relationship, desire, pleasure, luxurious foods,
culture, family history, beauty, comfort, art, creativity, lust and
sexuality.
Mars: efforts, energy, will power, stamina, strength, passion,
fighting ability, impulsivity, accidents, injuries, wounds, sports,
athlete, martial arts, weaponry, engineer.
Ketu: isolation, separation, ashram, spirituality, enlightenment,
detachment from material world, psychic intuition, occult and
mystical knowledge, universal.
A butcher comes to mind with such a conjunction. The native
loves to cut, sew, and shoot. There is a great love of weapons,
wars, and competition. Venus may not be in the middle of a war
zone, but might be part of an army in this conjunction, where half
the army is charging and the other half has conquered the land.
The need for passion and the need to get away from passion can
drive Venus to madness, especially in the first half of the native's
life. As much as the native wants passion in a relationship, they
find it hard to get away. There is a constant battle being fought
within Venus to decide what it really wants. Ketu's energy is
powerful enough to eclipse the Sun, and Ketu feels it has already
won the war. The way Mars might play his role is by infusing
energy in Venus' sense of pleasure towards psychic and occult
interests, as well as interest in helping others. Both Mars and Ketu
can create accidents, where Mars is planned and Ketu's is sudden.
It can show pleasure in causing accidents, love for needles, and
cutting things. The native may have an interest in the medical
field, or surgery if this conjunction occurs in the 1st, 6th, 8th and
12th House.

## Venus + Jupiter + Saturn conjunction:

Venus: love, relationship, desire, pleasure, luxurious foods,
culture, family history, beauty, comfort, art, creativity, lust and
sexuality.
Jupiter: knowledge, wisdom, wealth, optimism, hope, law,
teachers, education, religious beliefs, and long distance pilgrimage.
Saturn: responsibility, organization, structure, delay, separation,
grief, life span as in longevity, antique items, old people, chronic
illnesses, fear, anxiety, labor work, lower class, lower type jobs,
construction.
Venus' energy is not as damaged here, but is in a foreign place
where it is not used to living. There is pleasure in gaining
knowledge from religion, philosophy, and spirituality. The native
wants to structure and organize the pleasure it seeks. They do not
want to have everything at once, but rather focus on each subject
one at a time. There is a great deal to learn about one's society,
community, and country when it comes to rules and regulations.
Such pleasure can take a person into the legal field where they will
have a natural love of implementing and defending the rules set
out by Jupiter and Saturn. Such people desire the opposite sex to
be educated and cultured. Their significant other must also look
good on paper and be of status, like a CEO, Director of
Operations, or a District Attorney. Venus loves Saturn's nature of
defining status in society.
One can be a history teacher because Venus originally represents
the 2nd House of family history. Jupiter and Saturn can also bring
about a disciplined preacher. This is also a strong position for
someone to become an art historian, dean at an arts college, or a
music producer.

## Venus + Jupiter + Rahu conjunction:

Venus: love, relationship, desire, pleasure, luxurious foods,
culture, family history, beauty, comfort, art, creativity, lust and
sexuality.
Jupiter: knowledge, wisdom, wealth, optimism, hope, law,
teachers, education, religious beliefs, and long distance pilgrimage.
Rahu: illusion, taboo breaker, unconventional, deceiver, magician,
foreign products and places, thief, Politian, wide communication,
and computer related products.
This conjunction shows immense satisfaction in attaining unusual
knowledge, such as the occult, foreign cultures, and religion. The
native would not only seek pleasure, but become obsessive about
gaining knowledge, whether it's academic or spiritually related.
They want to learn about foreign lands. This can make someone a
professor of exotic art, someone teaching esoteric and occult
science to people independently, a lecturer, or a spiritual guide
who may have a very unusual method of teaching. Art and
creativity will be quite universal, and dark, and may have an alien
element to it.
If such a conjunction occurs in a man's chart, he will have a wife
from foreign lands or a different culture, cast, or creed. She would
be very exotic, sexual, and educated. The native would be quite
obsessive about his wife. Venus' qualities are now being expanded
and amplified by Jupiter and Rahu. The person loves forming
relationships with people who know of the occult, entertainment
industry, food industry, and design. This conjunction can make a
man fall in love with quite a few women of higher status, at least
in terms of wealth.
If such a conjunction occurs in a woman's chart, then she would
acquire a husband who may not be cultured and religious, but may

have tremendous interest in foreign lands, sex, loves science, and
may be an author or writer. The female native would also be very
obsessive in a relationship. She will only fall for foreign mates
who have a very extravagant and lavish lifestyle, and the desire to
acquire worldly knowledge.

## Venus + Jupiter + Ketu conjunction:

Venus: love, relationship, desire, pleasure, luxurious foods,
culture, family history, beauty, comfort, art, creativity, lust and
sexuality.
Jupiter: knowledge, wisdom, wealth, optimism, hope, law,
teachers, education, religious beliefs, and long distance pilgrimage.
Ketu: isolation, separation, ashram, spirituality, enlightenment,
detachment from material world, psychic intuition, occult and
mystical knowledge, universal.
With the energy of Jupiter and Ketu, Venus is forced into
searching for love with the divine rather than with human beings.
We know by now how Venus' love for higher knowledge, culture,
and philosophy is expanded with Jupiter, and how these people
relate to others by through their mind more than their beauty.
With the element of Ketu involved, they find dissatisfaction with
highly qualified gurus, men, and women. They look for someone
smart, and end up with someone who can hardly match their own
intellect. The native’s love for spirituality and higher knowledge is
vastly expanded, but relationships with people are somewhat
dissatisfying. They eventually find a true sense of love through
meditation, spirituality, occult, and moksha.
Women with such a conjunction will have relationships with men
who will leave them suddenly or travel a lot, and will be away
from the native most of the time. They will run into men who

might be intelligent, but fail to provide a stimulating relationship
in terms of romance and love.
A man with such a conjunction will attract a woman who may be
spiritual and divine to the point of obsession. She might be so
religious and devoted to the divine that she fails to understand the
concept of love and relationships. She may feel distant from the
male native or she may actually travel for long durations for
educational, financial, or spiritual reasons. If Ketu has the lowest
