What are Aspects?
In astrology, aspects simply means seeing something. Person A
aspects person B in the other room. This simply means that person
A is looking at person B in the other room. Planets do the same.
They look at each other in a birth horoscope. Certain planets have
the ability of seeing certain things. There are planets that can only
see planets or houses directly opposite to them. There are also
planets that can see other houses besides the one opposite to them.
When planets aspect in astrology, they infuse their qualities upon
the house and planet they are sitting in. This is a very important
part of astrology, more important than even conjunctions. People
are aware of conjunctions, but certain events happen in life which
a conjunction cannot explain, and this is where aspects need to be
evaluated.
There can be a positive conjunction of Venus and Mercury in the
2nd House, which can bring wealth and provide love and
communication between family members. If Saturn was to aspect
Venus and Mercury from another house, this will alter the
condition of the conjunction and the house. Most beginners would
fail to see this, even some intermediate astrology students.
Remember, aspect simply means a planet's ability to see other
houses and planets sitting in them in a birth chart.

## Below is the list of Aspects each planet has:

Sun- 7th House from itself
Moon - 7th House from itself
Mercury - 7th House from itself
Venus - 7th House from itself
Mars- 4th, 7th and 8th House from itself

Jupiter - 5th, 7th and 9th House from itself
Saturn - 3rd, 7th and 10th House from itself
Rahu/Ketu - 5th, 7th and 9th House from themselves.
What do I mean when I write the words "From itself" in the table?
If I was to tell you Saturn aspects 3rd, 7th and 10th House, you
would think that no matter where Saturn sits, he will always look
at the 3rd House, 7th House and 10th House of the horoscope.
However, that is incorrect. Saturn aspecting other houses from
itself means that you must go counter-clockwise to the 3rd, 7th, and
10th House from where Saturn sits to understand its aspect.
If Saturn is in the 11th House, this doesn't mean Saturn is looking
at the 3rd House, 7th House and 10th House. This means, if you go
counter-clockwise in the north Indian style birth chart of Vedic
astrology, Saturn would look at the 3rd place from the 11th House,
which is the 1st House, as in the ascendant. Then, Saturn would
look at the 7th House which is directly opposite to the 5th House.
Next, Saturn would look at the 10th place from the 11th House
which is the 8th House. This is how you judge the aspect of
planets. You count the places from where they sit.
Aspects are like a mirror image; it is the ability of a planet to
reflect back at itself through the house. This is true especially for
7th House aspect, which is known as direct opposite.
In western astrology, aspects become quite complicated and
confusing. Typical terms include: Opposite, Trine, Square, Sextile
and Quinox. Such terms operate on a degree basis, which is
cumbersome.

## Misconceptions about Aspects:

There are many who follow the rule on aspects which states that all
planets have some aspect on some house. They will tell you the
Sun has 20% aspect on the 4th House, 30% aspect on the 5th
House, and 50% aspect on the 9th House. This type of scenario
runs for all major planets in Vedic astrology. However, this is
simply not true and doesn't work. It makes astrology complicated
when its simplicity is far more powerful.

## Special Aspects:

In Vedic astrology, aspects are simple. Each planet has been given
a specific aspect and they must operate with those exact rules.
Jupiter can aspect the 5th, 7th and 9th place from itself. The Sun
can only aspect 7th place from itself. It's that simple. You don't
need to worry about those confusing terms.
Think of aspects like laser beams shooting out from a planet
towards certain houses. Earlier, you learned the aspect of each
planet. In the example below, I will use the aspect of Saturn and
Jupiter.

## Example 1:

Saturn in the 4th House in the sign of Taurus. Number 2 represents
the 2nd zodiac sign, Taurus. If you do not know how to read a
chart, please refer to my book Astrology @ The Speed of Light,
which makes analyzing a birth chart simple and easy.
In Example 1, Saturn is in the 4th House, and the blue lit house
that you see are the aspects being created by Saturn. If you
remember, Saturn aspects the 3rd, 7th, and 10th place from itself,
so if you go counter-clockwise from where Saturn is, the 3rd place
from 4th House would be the 6th House. If you look at number 4,
Saturn aspects directly opposite to the 7th place from itself, which
is the 10th house, where you see number 8 (Scorpio). Finally, it
aspects the 10th place from itself which is the ascendant, where
you see number 11(Aquarius). This means Saturn will infuse its
qualities upon the houses it aspects.

## Example 2:

Jupiter in the 1st House in the sign of Aquarius. Number 11
represents the 11th sign of the zodiac, Aquarius. If you move
counter-clockwise from 11, you will come up to the 2nd House
which is 12 (Pisces).
From the ascendant, Jupiter now aspects the 5th House where you
see number 3 (Gemini), 7th house where you see number 5 (Leo),
and 9th House where you see number 7 (Libra). Jupiter aspects
5th, 7th, and 9th place from itself.

## Example 3:

Mars in the 3rd House in the sign of Gemini is in aspect to the 6th,
9th, and 10th House. This is an Aries ascendant chart because
number 1 is in the 1st House, and each house and sign are as is.
The number 10, represents the 10th House with the 10th sign of
the zodiac, Capricorn.
Mars aspects the 4th, 7th, and 8th place from itself. From the 3rd
House, if you count 4 places, you come to the 6th House, directly
opposite is the 9th house, and 8 places from the 3rd house is the
10th house. Mars aspects the 6th, 9th, and 10th House from the 3rd
House.
Why do Saturn, Jupiter, and Mars have special aspects, and why
those particular houses? Because Mars possesses the 4th, 7th, and
8th aspect. Mars has the 4th aspect because the original 4th House
represents land or property, and Mars is the karaka of those things.
All planets aspect the 7th House, so it's no surprise that Mars
aspects directly opposite.
With the 8th aspect, Mars represents accidents, blood, surgeries,
cuts, and secrecy.

With respect to Jupiter and the 5th, 7th and 9th aspect, Jupiter
aspects the 5th House because it's the house of academic education
and children, and Jupiter is the karaka of such things.
All planets aspect the 7th House, so it's no surprise that Jupiter
aspects directly opposite.
Jupiter's 9th aspect shows wisdom, higher learning, and spiritual
knowledge. This is why Jupiter has the 9th aspect while being the
karaka of those qualities.
With respect to Saturn and the 3rd, 7th and 10th aspect, Saturn has
the 3rd aspect because the 3rd House shows our efforts, which
Saturn controls.
All planets aspect the 7th House so it's not surprise that Mars
aspects directly opposite.
Saturn has been given 10th aspect because Saturn is all about work
and status, while the 10th House is the house of work and status.

## Meaning of Planetary Aspects:

Each planet in Vedic astrology has a special meaning when they
aspect a house or planet. They exert a certain kind of influence
upon them. Below are planets and the meaning of their aspect.
Sun: When the Sun aspects, it shows straightforward direction. It
gives an authoritative influence upon the planet or house. It can
show where the person will have responsibility, and give clear
direction without wasting time.

Moon: The Moon's aspect on other planets and houses shows a
sense of comfort and welcoming nature. Such an aspect will give a
kind and motherly influence filled with emotions.
Mercury: Aspect from Mercury shows where we like to engage,
communicate, and gather information. It makes the planet or house
a source of networking, communication, and knowledge with
analytical skills.
Venus: Venus' aspect on other planets and houses gives a pleasant
feeling and desire. We gain a strong sense of finding happiness
from those things.
Mars: Mars aspect shows a straight shooter. The planet or house
becomes a direct type of environment. They want to take actions
towards those things which are aspected by Mars. They are
straightforward with aggressive behavior.
Jupiter: Jupiter’s aspect shows where we feel the need to learn and
grow as an individual. It shows our desire to achieve a higher level
of knowledge in our physical and spiritual life. Jupiter is known as
“blessings from the sky.” Jupiter's aspect saves a planet from
giving negative results. It either neutralizes the negative result, or
reverses it into positive territory. Jupiter's ability to expand can
increase the positive or negative feeling of the planet.
Saturn: Saturn's aspect shows where we have to focus our
attention. Its influence shows where we have to practice the most
discipline in order to reap the results of our labor. This is why
Saturn's aspect becomes the most important. This aspect also
brings fear and anxiety, since we feel so much responsibility in life
towards those houses.
Rahu: Rahu's aspect shows where we need to expand, conquer,
and achieve. In relation to aspect, Rahu acts like Mars. It has an
obsession with things that it aspects, and it must achieve its goals

to fulfill its hunger. The reason why Rahu is truly like Saturn is
because it brings fear towards those things as well. Rahu puts a
wrapper of illusion or foreign element upon the planets he aspects
or the houses he aspects. Foreign element simply means things we
do not understand or are unfamiliar with.
Ketu: Ketu's aspect on planets show a sense of separation and a
desire to get away. Since Ketu shows our mastery over the things
we have already accomplished in past lives, its conjunction,
placement, and aspect shows where we feel the least amount of
responsibility. At the same time, one has the ambition to
experience those things again.
Rahu and Ketu are illusionary planets, or better eclipse points of
the Moon. Below is their aspect which is similar to Jupiter.

## Rahu Ketu Aspects:

The original scriptures of Jyotish written by Maha Rishi Prashara
never talks about the aspects of Rahu and Ketu since they are blind
planets and do not have light. You cannot see them in the sky like
other physical bodies. However, you also have to understand that
Prashara Hora Shastra, the written text of original astrology, is an
incomplete book in itself. The book wasn't a manual from A-Z. It
was a code breaking text which required higher intuitive powers to
comprehend this divine science. He left it incomplete because he
wanted the rest of the information, which was just as valuable as
the physical written text, to be accessed by the chosen ones. Those
individuals practice extensive sadhna (meditation) and live a
sawtic lifestyle. Such sadhna exposed more information about
Jyotish later in the timeline of India. Rishi Bhrigu wrote about the
aspects of Rahu and Ketu in “Bhrigu Samitha.”.
The reason why the 5th and 9th House aspect has been assigned to
Rahu and Ketu is due to their karmic effect. Rahu and Ketu show
our past life karmas; of what we achieved and failed to achieve,

and what we are to accomplish in this lifetime. The 5th House is
the house of past life deeds and karma, while the 9th House shows
the deeds and karma of this lifetime.
When I first started practicing Vedic astrology, and looked at
people's chart without using the aspects, I was running into
problems, including the interpretation of my own horoscope. Once
I found out about aspects from a noteworthy astrologer, everything
started to make sense; why certain careers were chosen, why
marriages took place, and why parental relationships were so
important.
Example 4: Rahu
The image shows Rahu in the 2nd House giving his aspect upon
the 6th House, 8th House, and 10th House. Ketu does the same in
Example 5.

Example 5: Ketu
I want to discuss an additional fascinating theory about Rahu/Ketu
aspects. Many astrologers in South India and some in North India
believe that Rahu/Ketu also aspect the 12th place from themselves.
This means Rahu in the 2nd House will also aspect the ascendant,
as in the 12th place from the 2nd House. After finding out about
this theory through one of my followers, I began reviewing new
and old charts. The theory fascinated me quite a bit as it made
sense in 8 out of 10 horoscopes.
Does this mean I firmly believe that these shadow planets aspect
the 12th House from themselves? Not yet. Only after reviewing
500 or more horoscopes can one can come to a concrete decision
about their aspects. I will be doing such research, and might update
my theory on these aspects, but wanted to mention them in this
book for your personal knowledge. Remember, don't look at your
horoscope on a regular basis, just once every 6 months or so.
Better yet, if you remember the chart, just judge it in your mind.
For now, only the 5th, 7th, and 9th aspects are valid.

## Orb Effect in Aspects:

There are many methods of studying aspects. You will find
astrologers who use a full house orb aspect versus a 10-15 degree
orb aspect. What do I mean by orb? Think of an orb like a
flashlight in the hands of a planet that is looking over to another
house. There would be intense light in the center, while the sides
would fade out, however, you will still see the entire house.

## Example 6:

You can see how Mars' aspect works. There is an intense head or
aspect in the middle, while the sides are lighter. This shows that
even though Mars has a 100% aspect on the entire house, the
middle aspects are intensified.
What do I mean by middle aspect? Well, this depends upon the
degree where Mars is placed. If Mars is at 10 degrees in any sign,
then it would show an intense aspect to the other house between 5-
10 degrees, while the rest would be less intense.

## Example 7:

Mars is in the 10th House in the sign of Capricorn at 15 degrees.
The 4th House is 30 degrees as long as all the houses are. That
means since Mars is at 15 degrees, its intense light of aspect will
be between 10-20 degrees, as in 5 degrees below 15 and 5 degrees
above 15.

## Example 8:

The 10 degree orb is working in terms of aspect. Mars' red light
aspect is intense with the 10 degree orb, but less intense after the
10 degree orb. This is how aspects work.
Most professional Vedic astrologers use a full degree orb, as in
Mars being at 15 degrees, and will have an intense light over the
entire house rather than the 10 degree orb. This still works, but it
may not fine tune the meaning of aspect. Having this concept of a
flashlight scenario does not really work when a planet is looking at
a house which is empty. It only applies when a planet aspects
another planet. Having a planet aspect an empty house using the
flashlight concept doesn't really mean anything. It is similar to
throwing a light on an empty box; no matter how intense or less
intense the light is, you will see the same thing everywhere.

## Example 9:

Mercury is in the 4th House at 15 degrees and the flashlight
concept is activated. Mercury is an intense aspect of Mars. Mars is
no longer throwing his flashlight at an empty box. There is
something inside the box that will either receive intense light or
very little light.

## Example 10:

Mercury is at 10 degrees where Mars' aspect light is very faint.
Mercury is not under the spotlight of Mars.
How would you use this in real life?
Example 9: Mercury is in the intense light of Mars at the exact
degree, while Mars is in the 10th House. The speech,
communication, writing, and analytical skills of the native at home
will be professional, to the point, business-like, and they might
even be perceived as rude. Why? Because Mars is in the 10th
House of career exalted, while Mercury is in the 4th House of
home. The native would want his home to be like his work. He
would want everyone in the house to be on guard. They are
adamant about working towards their professional goals, and want
to win every argument with their family members. This also shows
a technical writer who might be working from home, or a home
based business. There would be a lot of tension between siblings
due to career matters.
Mercury = siblings

Mars = brothers, blood, fighting, will power, strength and action.
Why regarding career? Because Mars is in the 10th House of
career, in aspect to Mercury in the 4th House of home life,
mother's house, and homeland.
This also depicts a lot of rude language being used inside the home
by both parents, especially the mother because the 4th House is the
house of the mother, while the 10th House is the house of father.
The father may use intense language towards the mother.
There are a lot of things that can come out of this aspect when
applying the house karaka along with planetary karaka.
In Example 9, you also have to see which houses each planet is
ruling. We are using an Aries ascendant chart where Mars rules the
1st and 8th House, and Mercury rules the 3rd and 6th House.

## MERCURY HOUSES

3rd House = siblings, initiative, skills and short travels.
6th House = disease, enemies, debts, everyday labor.

## MARS HOUSES

1st House = self, personality, health, and body.
8th House = sudden events, surgeries, accidents, transformation,
and in-laws.
Now that we know the houses, we can make another assessment.
Mars, which is the self as the ascendant Lord, will always have a
career like a police officer, soldier, fighter, martial artist, or a
computer programmer. The native will have fluctuations with their

siblings due to career issues. They use intense forms of
communication within the home. They are courageous due to Mars
throwing his tough aspect on the 3rd Lord of self motivation.
From a karaka standpoint, the mother would be very courageous
due to the father's support. The 10th House karaka (father) is
throwing the intense heat of Mars upon the 4th House karaka
(mother).
This can also trigger sudden blood related diseases upon the
nervous system and skin.
Before we complete my analysis of Example 9, we must note that
Mercury is also giving off intense light towards Mars since planets
look directly opposite of each other.
Thus far, I have been talking about Mars' influence upon Mercury.
We will also discuss Mercury's influence upon Mars in Mutual
Aspects.

## Mutual aspects:

Mutual aspects are simply two planets looking at each other from
opposite ends or through special aspects.

## Example 11:

There is a mutual aspect between Mercury and Mars because they
are both looking directly across from each other.
A mutual aspect does several things in a chart. It gives it energy,
and brings it to life. There is a lot of activity, especially when the
two houses have mutual aspects.
The two planets are constantly looking at each other. Just as if two
people are looking at each other directly, there is bound to be some
conversation and exchange of ideas. However, the two natives
could also be bitter, and cursing each other. Similarly, the way two
planets behave in mutual aspects requires analysis of each
individual planet. Is the planet exalted or debilitated in its own
sign? What degree and nakshatra of any one of the planets
involved in mutual aspect are in conjunction to some other planet?
In Example 11, Mercury and Mars are looking at each other. Both
planets are infusing each other. Mercury is sharing his energy with
Mars, and Mars is sharing his energy with Mercury. This is quite
different from a conjunction, because the energy exchange is not

happening in the same house. They are happening in two different
houses.
Let's get back to Example 10. If two planets have a mutual aspect
but their degrees are different, how will that impact the aspect? In
Example 10 you have Mercury at 3 degrees Cancer, while Mars is
at 15 degree Capricorn. This simply means that both planets are
impacting one another about 20-30%. If Mercury was to have an ill
or aggressive speech value of 10, with an exact aspect like
Example 9, then Example 10 would have an aggressive speech
value of 4 or 5. Everything I discussed in Example 9 would
decrease by 30-40%, but it would still be present.

## Example 12:

There is a mutual aspect between Mercury and Mars, but this time
the degrees are different along with the houses. Mercury is in the
5th House, in the sign of Leo at 19 degrees while Mars is in the
11th House in the sign of Aquarius at 15 degrees. Since Mercury is
within a 10 degree orb of Mars, it’s receiving an intense aspect
from Mars. This time the aggressive speech style along with quick
thinking will occur with love affairs, academic studies, children
and creativity. The 5th House represents romance. The native is
very straightforward and passionate with their romantic partner.
Mars always adds the scent of passion along with aggression.
Since Mercury controls the 3rd and 6th, while Mars controls the 1st
and 8th, there is a creative passion within the person to write, act,
and paint. They have a natural way of mimicking other people and
can be quite comedic since the 11th House shows networking and
events, while the 5th House shows performing arts. The native can
perform and be comedic in front of a large organization. They have
a talent for graphic design, creative engineering, and architecture.
Why am I not showing the rays of Mercury? Why is Mars only
influencing Mercury? Here is a little tiny secret about aspects and

conjunction. Malefic planets will have more impact on benefic
planets than benefic on malefic. However, there are times when
natural benefic will influence natural malefic.

## Example 13:

Mercury's rays of aspect. How would you truly judge this mutual
aspect?
When it comes to thinking and calculating things in life, this native
would be impulsive, aggressive, quick, and technical. In terms of
physical action, they would use their intelligence to win the war.
Once you add the element of house rulership, you can go even
deeper into the aspect.

## Example 14:

In Example 14, we are seeing the same image as Example 13, but
this time we want to use the nakshatra of these planets since we
know their degrees. Mercury is in the nakshatra of Purva Phalguni,
ruled by Venus. Mars is in the nakshatra of Shatabhisha, ruled by
Rahu.
Mercury is acting very creatively. Mercury also wants to
experience a very energetic and communicative relationship with
the opposite sex since Venus represents relationships. Mars' aspect
is making Mercury more energized and passionate. Mars is also in
Rahu nakshatra, so he is bringing obsession with the 5th House.
This person is obsessed with sex, romance, creativity, and studies.
They can be a computer fanatic, video game addict, or
programmer. They have tremendous mimicking and speaking
skills.

## Single/Special Aspects:

Single aspects occur when only one planet aspects another planet.
This happens in special aspect circumstances with planets like
Mars, Saturn, Jupiter, Rahu, and Ketu.
When a planet has a special aspect, it’s able to see beyond the
ability of 7th aspect, which is a mutual aspect if two planets are 7th
from each other. There are many horoscopes where only one
planet sees the other planets. What impact does that have?
Saturn's Aspect
Saturn aspect: 3, 7 and 10

## Example 15:

Venus is in the 10th House with Saturn in the 8th House. We can
clearly see that both planets are not opposite of each other, so they
cannot see one another directly, but Saturn can still see Venus.
Saturn's special 3rd aspect is looking towards Venus from the 8th
House.

## Example 16:

Saturn's 3rd aspect is on the 10th House if you count 3 places
counterclockwise from Saturn. Only Saturn is impacting Venus but
Venus is not impacting Saturn. How would you read this?
Saturn = delay, restriction, discipline, structure, and organization.
Venus = love, relationship, desire, happiness, women, wife, and
creativity.
8th House = secret dealings of government issues, occult,
knowledge, in-laws, taxes, sudden events or sudden fluctuations
10th House = status in society, father, government, and career.
Saturn's 3rd aspect regarding willpower and effort is showing that
the native will have to be motivated in their career. The 10th
House related to career and Venus represents acting, cinema,
singing, modeling, fashion, and designing. Venus also represents
marriage. This chart demonstrates that a lot of effort is required in
making a marriage work, the native may marry an older partner,
and it might happen late in life.

This also shows that marriage is more about maturity, discipline,
and managing a family than romance and love. Saturn is best
friends with Venus, so in this case he also gives lot of mechanical
and creative ability to Venus. The marriage might lack warmth, but
it lasts.
Saturn in the 8th House slows down the sudden fluctuations in life,
and maintains the balance since it is the karaka of the 8th House.

## Example 17:

The sign placements of planets. We have a Taurus ascendant with
Venus in the sign of Aquarius in the 10th House, and Saturn in the
sign of Sagittarius in the 8th House. Now we can determine what
house affects these planets, and how Saturn’s aspect will take
effect.
Saturn for Taurus ascendant rules two of the most important
houses: the 9th House and 10th House. It becomes the yogakaraka
of this ascendant. Venus is the ascendant Lord and the 6th Lord.

Venus is sitting in Saturn's own house and sign. This means
Saturn's aspect is within its own house, and brings protection to the
house. Venus, being the best friend of Saturn, is becoming part of
that protection. The native may be a workaholic since it's the
ascendant Lord being aspected by Saturn, the planet of work and
effort. It will give sudden career advancement and blessings in the
status of the native’s work life.
Remember the rule of thumb. Whether planets are best friends or
bitter enemies, they will always share their natural significance
upon other planets in aspect and conjunction. Although Venus is
getting support from yogakaraka Saturn, it will still frustrate Venus
and delay things related to Venus.

## Example 18:

The degrees are revealed. Venus is in a very tight aspect of
Saturn's orb. This can demonstrate the pure hard aspect of Saturn
with regards to a tremendous work ethic, harshness in romance and
life, and the need for stability and security in marriage, career, and
all relationships. The native is always involved with professional
women. There is also lot of fear and anxiety towards the idea of
marriage, dealing with women, and female co-workers.

## Example 19:

Venus is at 26 degree Aquarius and about to exit the sign while
there is a good 11 degree difference between Venus and Saturn.
This softens the aspect of Saturn. If Venus was to work 15 hours a
day in Example 18, now it's working 10 hours a day because of
distance in degree. Although being 11 degrees apart is still not far
enough, it won't be as dominating as the previous example.

## Example 20:

Saturn at 5 degrees while Venus is at 27 degrees. There is a 22
degree difference between two planets, so the aspect is hardly felt
on Venus. Instead of working 10-16 hours a day, Venus would be
working 6-8 hours a day, and would be far more romantic and soft
compare to previous two examples.
We can also study the nakshatra from Example 20. Saturn is in the
Mula nakshatra ruled by Ketu, and Venus is in the Dhanishta
nakshatra ruled by Mars.
Saturn’s aspect is also giving the orb of Ketu. While being in the
8th House of occult, and in the nakshatra of the planet that
represents occult knowledge, Venus' pleasure and career happiness
can come from dealing with the occult and mysterious subjects.
They can be healers, doctors, astrologers, or any kind of
investigative expert.
However, the nakshatra effect is very little compared to the
planet's own power and qualities. The planet behaves like the Lord
of the nakshatra he sits in. If Saturn is giving 15% effect of the
nakshatra Lord, then 85% is its own qualities.

Jupiter's Aspect

## Example 21:

Jupiter is in the 10th House and Mars in the 6th House. Again,
there is no mutual aspect between Jupiter and Mars. However,
Jupiter is able to aspect Mars from his special 9th aspect.

## Example 22:

Jupiter is glancing at Mars from his 9th aspect. If you go
counterclockwise from Jupiter, and count 9 places with Jupiter
being the 1st place, you will end up at Mars. How would you read
this aspect?
10th House = status in society, father, government, and career.
6th House = enemies, debts, disease, obstacles and warzone.
Jupiter = knowledge, wisdom, higher learning, optimism, and
guidance.
Mars = fighting, blood, cuts, anger, action, energy, passion, and
physical work.
This aspect shows that Mars is in the 6th House, which is a very
good placement for Mars. He now has the guidance, knowledge,
and wisdom from a teacher to win battles, fight disease, and cut
through any obstacles related to career. Mars will have great
optimism within to win battles.

## Example 23:

We can see the signs the planets are placed in. We have Cancer
ascendant with Jupiter in Aries in the 10th House, while Mars is in
Sagittarius in the 6th House.
We can quickly see that Jupiter is a functional malefic for this
ascendant while Mars is the yogakaraka. We also see that Jupiter
and Mars are exchanging signs and creating a parivartan yoga,
which we will discuss later. Parivartan yoga is another type of
special aspect.
Jupiter is in the 10th House of career, creating a good status in the
community. This native is becoming very authoritative as a
preacher and teacher. The placement of Mars makes one’s career
an uphill battle, with many obstacles to overcome. Jupiter's aspect,
which is also the karaka of law, can make Mars a litigator, freedom
fighter, community leader of non-profit organization, police
officer, or a doctor.

## Example 24:

The degrees are observed. Jupiter is at 13 degrees, while Mars is at

orb of Jupiter. This shows authority in the workplace, with a great
reputation and ability to fight and win all battles related to law,
government, and medicine.
When observing nakshatras, Jupiter is in the nakshatra of Ashvini,
while Mars is in the nakshatra of Mula.
This shows strong inclinations towards medicine, surgery, and
careers related to the medical field. Ashvini nakshatra represents a
1000 doctors or the medicine man, and it's ruled by Ketu, who is
another karaka for surgery, cuts, and blood. Mars being in Mula
nakshatra ruled by Ketu again shows knowledge of medicine and
going to the root of things to obtain a solution. Jupiter's single
aspect on Mars shows that Mars will have the knowledge,
guidance, and divine grace to find the root cause of disease.

Mars' Aspect

## Example 25:

Mars is in the 5th House and Venus in the 8th House. There is no
mutual aspect between the two, but Mars is glancing over at Venus
with his 4th aspect. If you count 4 places from Mars
counterclockwise, you will arrive at Venus.

## Example 26:

Mars’ orb upon Venus. What does this mean?
Venus = love, relationship, desire, happiness, women, wife, and
creativity.
8th House = secret dealings of government issues, occult
knowledge, in-laws, taxes, sudden events or fluctuations, and
secret affairs.
Mars = fighting, blood, cuts, anger, action, energy, physical work,
and passion.
5th House = children, creativity, academic education, and
speculative business.
Venus in the 8th House shows a very amorous married life with
wealthy in-laws, and a spouse who may bring wealth. With an
aspect from Mars, it shows tremendous sexual passion, love,
romance, and relationships. Mars is transmitting a one-way energy
upon Venus and energizing it. Such an aspect can also lead to

secret affairs or even homosexuality, but other factors have to be
considered.

## Example 27:

Mars is in Scorpio in the 5th House, while Venus is in Aquarius in
the 8th House. How would you analyze this?
For a Cancer ascendant, Mars is a yogakaraka planet. Venus
controls the 4th and 11th House. This aspect can show that the
spouse may be very creative due to Mars' 5th House aspect. There
will be a love marriage rather than an arranged marriage. Since
Mars is in the sign of Scorpio which represents secrecy, occult,
and affairs, it can lead a person into having extramarital romances
with people from work or in politics.

## Example 28:

Observe the degrees of the planets. Venus and Mars have a 10
degree difference. This means Venus is still in the orb of Mars, but
it's neither heavy nor light. It's right on the border. This means that
Venus will still feel the passion, aggression, and impulsiveness
towards romance, love, and relationships. Such a native is
impulsive about falling in love with many partners and in their
sexual behavior.
We can also study the nakshatra this way. Mars is in the nakshatra
of Jyeshrha, ruled by Mercury and Venus is in the nakshatra of
Shatabhisha, ruled by Rahu. Although Mars is enforcing its own
personality upon Venus, it’s also sharing love for the underworld,
communication, and overblown ego. Such a Venus will not only be
passionate, but also be lustful about the darker side of sex. The
native might be into fetishes of some kind, and will fall in love
with men or women who have a dark past of sexual abuse, or
suffer abuse from family members.

Rahu's Aspect

## Example 29:

Rahu is in the 2nd House and Mercury is in the 6th House. There
is no mutual aspect, but Rahu has his special 5th aspect upon
Mercury. If you count 5 places from Rahu counterclockwise, you
will end up at Mercury.

## Example 30:

Rahu is exerting his influence on the 5th aspect Mercury. What
does this mean?
Rahu = foreign, extravagant, clever, liar, cheat, obsessive about
worldly desire and achievements.
2nd House = wealth, family, voice.
Mercury = communication, logic, analysis, skills, business,
manager, writer, journalist, and speech.
6th House = debts, diseases, obstacles, enemies.
This aspect by Rahu will amplify Mercury's need to compete in
financial wars and win through cheating and manipulation. The
speech of the native is utilized in arguments, breaking contracts,
litigation, and discussing disease. When Rahu aspects a planet, it
doesn't amplify the quality of the planet like it does in conjunction.
Rather, it makes the planet hungry for more of what it already
possesses, and seeks to accomplish its goals. In conjunction, two
planets are in the same house. In aspect, two planets are in two

separate houses. In the 2nd House, Rahu is hungry for wealth, and
when he aspects any planet, it makes that planet hungry for wealth
as well through where the planet is located. In Example 30, Rahu
aspects Mercury. It is making Mercury hungry to make money
through
disease,
other
people's
debts,
accounting,
and
communicating with the enemy through debate.

## Example 31:

Observe the signs. This is a Libra ascendant chart with Rahu in
Scorpio in the 2nd House, and Mercury in Pisces in the 6th House.
How would you read this?
Rahu = foreign, extravagant, clever, liar, cheat, obsessive about
worldly desire and achievements.
2nd House = wealth, family, voice.
Scorpio = secrecy, occult, hidden truth, dark passion, and deep
desires.
Mercury = communication, logic, analysis, skills, business,
manager, writer, journalist and speech.

6th House = debts, diseases, obstacles, enemies.
Pisces = imagination, spirituality, peace, and isolation.
Rahu's aspect on Mercury shows that one will have a very wicked
mind and a foul mouth with a constant need to lie. This is due to
the fact that both planets are known to be debilitated in this
ascendant. Since Mercury is debilitated and it controls speech,
Rahu from the 2nd House of speech aspects the weak Mercury,
making it more fragile. Such a person will be needy due to their
extravagant lifestyle, while lying to others to gain wealth. Mercury
will even make them capable of blackmail. This is due to Rahu in
Scorpio attaining secrets of family and in-laws. It exerts its quality
towards Mercury, forcing its intelligence to take advantage of such
secrets to gain wealth.

## Example 32:

The degrees of planets. Rahu is at 17 degrees and Mercury is at 13
degrees. Rahu is in the nakshatra of Jyeshtha ruled by Mercury,
and Mercury is in the nakshatra of Uttra Bhadrapada ruled by
Saturn.
Rahu is also transforming himself into Mercury, but the clever and
negative side of Mercury. This is why Rahu's aspect is showing
Mercury slightly in his own mirror image.
Remember, Rahu has the ability to transform itself into the planets
whose sign it sits in. In our case it's Mars. It is aggressive and
impulsive here, and can become a very clever businessman.

Ketu's Aspect

## Example 33:

Ketu is in the 11th House and the Sun is in the 7th House. As I
discussed, Ketu has a special aspect of 5 and 9. From the 11th
House, Ketu aspects the 5th and 9th House.

## Example 34:

Ketu’s impact on the 7th House.
Ketu= separation, disinterest, isolation, and sense of abandonment.
11th House = gains, friends, network circles, and elder siblings.
Sun = ego, personality, father, soul, and our dignity.
7th House= marriage, legal partnerships, other people, and
business or trade.
When Ketu is in the 11th House, the native has few friends. It also
shows unplanned wealth and profits in life which the native will
give away to the underprivileged. Ketu's aspect on the 7th House
shows a disconnect with their partner or father. They might even
battle government officials. The father would either be separated
from the native, or he would travel much of his life and not be
present.
The 7th House represents marriage, spouse, and partnerships. The
native would be very much against settling for anyone. They will
be dissatisfied with most of the people in their life, especially their
spouse.

## Example 35:

Observe the signs and ascendant. The chart is Libra ascendant with
Ketu in the sign of Leo in the 11th House, while the Sun is in the
sign of Aries in the 7th House.
Now we can understand more about Ketu's aspect. Ketu in the sign
of Leo shows no resistance to fame, politics, theater, and the arts.
It feels indifferent to such things. Ketu feels it has mastery over the
creative side of life and has a political advantage. The 11th House
shows fluctuations within network circles connected to the
government and entertainment. They reject most of their friends,
and feel that they do not need political connections to receive gains
in life. Such an aspect on an exalted Sun in the 7th House shows
that the spouse will be very spiritual, dominating, and connected to
either politics or entertainment. However, the native and their
spouse will clash due to two malefic influences on the 7th House.
One is of the Sun, and the other is of Ketu's aspect.
As mentioned in my book, “Conjunctions @ The Speed of Light,”
when exalted planets are in conjunction with Rahu or Ketu, they
receive the positive side of the nodes. If the planets are debilitated

or neutral, then such planets receive the malefic side of Rahu and
Ketu.
In Example 35, you can see that the Sun is exalted, and Ketu is in
the sign of Sun. This shows that the Sun won't be eclipsed by Ketu.
Ketu will add a sense of spirituality and occult powers within the
spouse and the native's own personality. Since Ketu is a natural
malefic, the spouse will be away from the native for long
durations.

## Example 36:

Observe the degrees of both planets. This will show you what
nakshatra they are sitting in. Ketu is at 17 degrees Leo, or in the
nakshatra of Purva Phalguni ruled by Venus. The Sun is at 18
degrees Aries and in the nakshatra of Bharani, also ruled by
Venus.
How would you read this aspect?
Ketu being in the nakshatra of Purva Phalguni shows that it will
have the tendency to reject marital bliss and satisfaction. It feels it
already has mastery over companionship.

This aspect clearly shows the native will be dissatisfied by their
spouse due to lack of romance and dominating ego.
Parivartan Yoga
Parivartan Yoga is a special mutual aspect. We will now talk about
its deeper meaning.
Yogas are simply a union of planets that create a certain type of
situation in a person's chart. It could be a good situation or a bad
situation.
What is Parivartan yoga?
Parivartan yoga occurs when two planets exchange each other's
signs. In Example 23, you had Jupiter in the sign of Aries ruled by
Mars, and Mars in the sign of Sagittarius ruled by Jupiter. How is
this a special type of mutual aspect?

## Example 37:

Mars is in the 6th House Sagittarius, and Jupiter is in the 10th
House in Aries. How is this a mutual aspect?
The reason why this is a mutual aspect is because both are in each
other's houses making a telepathic connection. They both are
aware of each other's presence in their own houses. Such planets
make a deal with each other. I won't destroy your house if you
don't destroy mine. Such a deal can go good or bad based on two

## aspects:

1. If two planets involved in parivartan yoga are natural friends or
enemies.
### 2. If two planets are in good or bad houses.
If you have read my book, “Astrology @ The Speed of Light,” you
would know that Jupiter and Mars are natural friends.
When judging parivartan yoga, one must always pay attention to
the houses more than the sign. Observe which two houses are
involved in the mutual exchange, and what two other houses the

planet rules. Each planet in astrology, besides the Sun and Moon,
rule two houses.
Since we are dealing with Cancer ascendant, we know
automatically what houses Jupiter and Mars rule.
Mars rules - 5th and 10th House
Jupiter rules - 6th and 9th House
We also have to realize that the 6th House is considered a difficult
house in astrology along with 8 and 12. This brings us to a point
where you must know 3 types of parivartan yoga (special aspect):

Dainya Parivartan yoga

Khala Parivartan yoga

Maha Parivartan yoga
Dainya Parivartan yoga occurs when the Lord of the 6th, 8th, and
12th exchange signs with any of the other houses.
If 6th, 8th or 12th exchanged houses with 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 7, 9, 10 and
11th Lord, it would be considered Dainya Parivartan yoga.
Khala Parivartan yoga occurs when Lord of the 3rd House
exchanges places with any of the other 11 houses like 1, 2, 4, 5, 6,
7, 8, 9, 10, 11 and 12. This yoga gives fluctuations in life, and
mixed results depending upon planets and the other houses they
rule.
Maha Parivartan yoga occurs when Lord of the kendra and trikon
exchanges places in House 1, 4, 7 and 10, and exchange places

with 5th and 9th House. This is the most auspicious of all special
aspects.
Knowing these three parivartan yogas will help you determine
what the result of the aspect will be.
In Example 37, we can now see that there is a Dainya Parivartan
yoga occurring. How would you read this?
When you read parivartan yoga, you must notice which house
comes first in the exchange. In other words, from the ascendant,
which house is the first to arrive. Since the 6th House comes
before the 10th House, we have to start from the 6th House.
Mars in the 6th House does very well as a natural malefic. The
native will destroy his enemies and will gain power in career
through winning many battles and oppositions. Jupiter being the
10th house shows that there is a link between 10th and 6th house,
including 5th and 9th house. This shows there will be rise in career
during Jupiter and Mars maha and antar dasha. 5th and 9th house
are both karaka of education. This shows that education will be
greatly influenced by Jupiter and Mars, that means a civil engineer,
doctor, surgeon and even law school. But since this is Dainya
Parivartan yoga, the native will face lot of struggles in the initial
stages of career and education but will prevail due to powerful
Mars in 6th house.

Parivartan yoga can be an aspect and conjunction at the same time.

## Example 38:

In Example 38, you have khala parivartan yoga occurring as the
3rd Lord Mercury has exchanged signs with 9th Lord Jupiter. The
3rd House is a mild malefic as it’s 8th from the 8th House. It's the
4th most malefic house after 6th, 8th, and 12th.
Jupiter in the 3rd House in Virgo makes a very critical and
analytical person who is a salesman that goes into too much detail
and may loses his customers or business deal. This could also be
someone who might be an author who writes critically about local
society and government. This can cause a lot of criticism by other
people towards the native, and bring about discomfort due to their
critical literary work. Jupiter will eventually make a call to
Mercury sitting in Pisces in the 9th House. This shows that
Mercury, who is the karaka for siblings and lord of the 3rd House,
will find a way to receive higher learning. They will have an open
and broad mind, and a sense of proper communication through

their younger brother or sister, or perhaps even friends. This shows
that the person's sense of higher purpose, learning, and
communication skills will come through the inspiration of youth,
imagination, art and even traveling.

## Example 39:

Observe the same ascendant, but the parivartan yoga is occurring
between 3rd and 6th Lord.
This is a mix of khala yoga and dianya yoga. Two malefic yogas
will only produce a malefic yoga. The native’s siblings become
the enemy, and relationships with teachers and gurus will be
tumultuous. They won't listen to advice from others. When Jupiter
will make the call to Mercury to help, Mercury will again backstab
Jupiter and expose his secrets. Jupiter and Mercury rule the 12th
House and 9th House. This means that energy of the 9th and 12th
are also coming into play here. Through conflict with their
siblings, the native would find spirituality and higher education as
a way to cope with domestic issues. They may pursue an education
in foreign lands to get away from siblings and enemies. This also
shows Jupiter's critical preaching and teaching behavior will

improve with time since Jupiter is in a upachaya house. The
relationship with siblings, too, will get better with time as Mercury
is also in a upachaya house. The native may find it difficult to
serve others, as too much criticism will lead to creating many
enemies, especially criticism of their siblings.

## Example 40:

Cancer ascendant with Saturn in the 4th House and Venus in the
7th House. Both planets are sitting in each other's signs. Since the
exchange is between two kendra houses, it shows a maha
parivartan yoga even though a trikona house is not involved. How
would you read this?
First, Saturn is in the 4th House of mother and nourishment. Being
a cold and malefic planet, there would be strict discipline from the
mother, which would make the native a very mature person at an
early age. Even though the native is happy about the mother's
teachings, they still feel a lack of love and motherly nourishment
from her. This why Saturn will call Venus in the 7th House and

ask for help. Venus is in the 7th House of marriage, and Venus
represents the spouse. The male native would receive nourishment
from his wife, who will fulfill the role of mother. Since Venus also
rules the 11th House of gains, friends, and elder siblings, and
Saturn rules the 8th House of death, rebirth, transformations, and
fluctuations, the native will see sudden changes in friend circles
and income. They may have sudden fluctuations with their spouse
as well.
Since Saturn represents delay, Saturn can bring marriage later in
life, and allow for a more fulfilling spouse than if married early.
The spouse will also be more mature in personality or age, which
makes it easier for them to becoming a maternal figure. Since
Venus is 4 houses away from the 4th House, as in 4th from the 4th,
it also shows foreign lands. The spouse could be of a different
culture or from foreign lands due to Saturn's special aspect upon
Venus.
A sambandha or parivartan yoga also shows the broad picture as
well. In example 40 Venus is in the 7th house of marriage and
Saturn is in the 4th house of home. This shows that there is a very
strong possibility of the native getting married in Saturn and Venus
periods.

## Sandhi Aspects:

What are Sandhi aspects?
Sandhi aspects are simply an invisible aspect that occurs in a chart
even if two planets are not in a mutual aspect.
Let me simplify it for you.

## Example 41:

Saturn aspects the 10th House while sitting in the 4th House at 2
degrees, in the sign of Libra. Venus, on the other hand, is in the 9th
House at 29 degrees in the sign of Pisces. Venus is 1 degree away
from entering the 10th House, while Saturn is at 2 degrees in the
4th House. Saturn and Venus are less than 3 degrees apart.
Anytime you have a planet 3 degrees or less away from being
directly in front of the other planet you have a sandhi aspect. This
is because there are no boundaries in the sky. House boundaries

are something that was invented by Prashara, the father of
astrology.

## Example 42:

You do not see the boundaries of the houses. This is how planets
are in the sky when you look up. One cannot determine when
Venus has entered the 10th House. The orb of Saturn is still able to
impact Venus since they are only 3 degrees apart from mutually
seeing each other. In this case, Saturn has an aspect on Venus.
Saturn will infuse his qualities upon Venus. If you have a delayed
marriage, and you can't figure out why, it's probably due to sandhi
aspect.
Sandhi aspect is a special mutual aspect that occurs when there is a
3 degree or less difference between two planets, while one planet
is in the previous house directly opposite to the other planet.

## Planets Aspect through each House:

In this chapter, I will demonstrate each planetary aspect through all
the houses, and how their aspect may impact the other houses even
though planets may not be present at the end of their glance.

Sun

Moon

Mars

Mercury

Jupiter

Venus

Saturn

Rahu

Ketu

## Sun Aspect Through Houses:

Sun: When the Sun aspects, it shows straightforward direction. It
gives an authoritative influence upon the planet or house, and can
show where the person would have responsibility and direction
without wasting time. The Sun's ability to give confidence, ego,
and self-esteem would transform the house it aspects, which is the
7th place from itself.
I will use the terms: exalted, debilitated, and own sign. This should
be taken as is. The Sun can be in good dignity, bad dignity, or
neutral. The Sun can be good in the sign of Taurus, or bad in the
sign of Taurus. The Sun can be good in the sign of Aries, or bad in
the sign of Aries which is his exalted placement. If the Sun has
conjunction of a malefic planet, then his value as an exalted Sun
can decrease. You should know these principles from my previous

two books on astrology and conjunction to assess a powerful
(exalted) Sun or a weak (debilitated) Sun.

## Sun Aspect on the 7th House:

When the Sun is in the 1st House, it aspects the 7th House. This
means that the Sun will reveal the truth regarding one’s spouse,
and will illuminate life in the 7th House. Perhaps the Sun's ego
will transform towards the partner, and becomes a life partner.
There would be a need to dominate their significant other and seek
attention. They feel like they are king, and their partner is a
servant. However, if the Sun is in his own sign or exalted in Aries,
it can bring about a partner who is truthful, follows the law, and is
highly spiritual. They will have confidence in their ability to
nourish the marriage. The spouse will have leadership qualities to
raise a family, and perhaps provide for the family. If the Sun is
debilitated around 5-12 degrees in Libra, it will show domination
over the spouse, and even publicly insulting or degrading them.
They will be short tempered over small things, and that will create
a lot of arguments in the marriage.

## Sun Aspect on the 8th House:

The Sun in the 2nd House shares an aspect on the 8th House. This
shows a great deal of respect for family. The 8th House is the
house of transformation, death, rebirth, occult, taxes, and surgeries.
The Sun's ability to illuminate brings a great deal of interest in
investigating for the government, or working undercover for
authorities.
One's
own
family
becomes
the
source
of
transformation. With the Sun's ability to illuminate, the native is
easily able to find spirituality, occult, and help from secret sources.
If the Sun is exalted in his own sign, then the Sun's aspect will
bring wealth, status, and honor through the spouse’s family. If the
Sun is debilitated, one will seek to possess everything they have
been given from their family. They can also take resources and
wealth of their spouse’s family due to its 8th House aspect. If the
Sun is neutral or in good condition, one will be devoted to their

own family and will provide resources to increase the status of
their spouse's family.

## Sun Aspect on the 9th House:

The Sun in the 3rd House shows many skill sets. One can be a
great artist, writer, engineer, actor, salesman, or government
servant. Its aspect on the 9th House shows serving a higher
purpose through their skill set. The 9th House represents teachers,
gurus, father's teaching, government principles, law, philosophy,
and higher education. The Sun from the 3rd House shows that one
will be devoted to their teachers, fathers, and law of the land. This
will happen as long as the Sun is not debilitated. A debilitated
Sun's aspect on the 9th House will try to overthrow one's own
gurus and father. They will feel threatened by higher authorities,
and not follow the law of the land. The Sun in the 3rd House can
show a government administrator due to his glance towards the 9th
House, however, most of the time it shows interest in higher
learning. This is especially true regarding skills of hands like

engineer, sculptor, or sales manager. Exalted Sun or the Sun in his
own sign aspect in the 9th House shows tremendous respect for
one's teacher and for their father's teachings. They will take great
interest in studying the law, and become the beacon of light for
higher teachings and religious purposes.

## Sun Aspect on the 10th House:

The Sun in the 4th House aspects the 10th House. This shows that
one's career will be greatly be influenced by their mother, home,
and childhood. The 10th House is career, status, honor,
government, and the relationship with one's own father. The 4th
House is the private side of life, since the Sun happens to be in the
4th House at midnight when the world is asleep. Such people love
domestic life most of the time. The aspect of the Sun on the 10th
House shows that the native is strongly inclined towards having a
home based business, or an opportunity to work from home.
Exalted Sun, or the Sun in his own sign, will show one's
attachment to their homeland and mother. Their mother will take
on the role of their father in life. This is true even for a debilitated
Sun. A debilitated Sun will be like a double edge sword. One will
be greatly influenced by their mother, but at the same time, have

an ego clash with her and constant arguments. Debilitated Sun's
aspect on the 10th House shows a bad relationship with their father
and a dislike towards taking orders from authority. In the 4th
House, the Sun loses his directional strength.

## Sun Aspect on the 11th House:

The Sun in the 5th House aspects the 11th House of network
circles, gains, elder brothers and sisters, and one's goals. The 5th
House represents children, creativity, romance, past life deeds, and
politics. This shows that the Sun's aspect on the 11th House
reveals one's dominance over their professional network circles,
friends, and elder siblings. The Sun brightens the quality of the 5th
House which is about fun, creativity, sports, and art. At the same
time, its aspect on the 11th House shows the Sun's ability to
illuminate his path and achieve his goals, hopes, and wishes. One
will have friends and a network circle in highly important places
connected to government and senior executives if it’s exalted or in
his own sign. A debilitated Sun will generate interest in art, acting,
cinema, and performing arts, but their 11th House situation would
be highly vulnerable. The debilitated Sun's aspect on the 11th
House depicts very few friends who might be of a lower class and

dishonest. The native will take advantage of his network circle
through his creative ideas and children. The overblown ego of the
debilitated Sun will try to seek control of one's friends and
networks.

## Sun Aspect on the 12th House:

The Sun in the 6th House aspects the 12th House. The 12th House
is the house of isolation, jails, hospitals, foreign lands, spirituality,
donations, and monasteries. In the 6th House, the Sun becomes
malefic, especially if in its own sign. The 6th House rules debts,
diseases, social work, everyday work life, small animals, enemies,
and obstacles. The Sun illuminates the 6th House, and exposes the
native’s enemies; reducing them to ashes. If the Sun is in good
dignity or exalted, in his own sign or neutral, then one will do
charity work related to the government, medicine, and marketing.
However, the Sun's aspect on the 12th House shows that one must
serve others selflessly in order reap the results of moksha,
enlightenment, and peace. One will have to go through many
obstacles in life in order gain a spiritual sense of their mission as a
human being. Exalted Sun, or the Sun in his own sign, can show a
great affinity towards work related to serving the underprivileged

and elderly. It can make a person a doctor, or go into the field of
medicine. Through the Sun's aspect on the 12th House, the native
may work in hospitals and in isolation. If a debilitated Sun is in the
6th House, then the native may run into defeat, hurting those who
are powerless; even killing small animals. Such behavior will land
a person into jail due to the Sun's aspect on the 12th House. With a
heavy workload in the public service sector, the native enjoys
times of isolation and visiting foreign lands.
Sun's Aspect on the Ascendant (1st House)
The Sun in the 7th House aspects the ascendant. The 7th House
symbolizes marriage, spouse, legal partnerships, other people,
public life, fame, and business. The Sun aspects the 1st House of
self, personality, and body. The Sun is the karaka of personality. In
the 7th House, the Sun is dedicated to public life and loves dealing
with other people. However, the Sun is not really happy here. It is
as if the King (Sun) has to go down to the marketplace to deal with
the public instead of them coming to his royal palace. The Sun in
the 7th can produce a very egoistic, proud, and self-centered
spouse. A dominating spouse controls the native due the Sun's
aspect on the ascendant (self). The native also wants to rule and

dominate others. The Sun's aspect on the ascendant shows that the
native's confidence and ego is derived from other people and their
spouse. The spouse will be the source of increased self-esteem for
the native. An exalted Sun can make the spouse wealthy and from
a respected family. The spouse can be a high ranking government
official or an executive in a corporation. The personality of the
native would be shaped through other people and public life. A
debilitated Sun will always look for a spouse and other types of
partnerships to develop their sense of self-esteem and confidence.
One's creative expression will only flourish through the praises of
others, rather than the native having confidence in their own work.
This is why the Sun's debilitated aspect on the 1st House of self
can create low confidence. Their dominating nature is overblown;
the Sun is trying to acquire power by looking like a king, yet
internally they are a sheep. This will create a lot of problems in
marriage and partnerships.

## Sun 2nd House Aspect:

When the Sun is in the 8th House, it aspects the 2nd House. The
8th House represents transformation, secret affairs, death, rebirth,
and occult knowledge. In the 8th House, the Sun will illuminate

the dark side of life and the individual will either be a
revolutionary, or will become the victim of revolution. The aspect
towards the 2nd House shows the native’s hidden family secrets,
which changed the course of their life. An exalted Sun in its own
sign shows that the ego, confidence, and self respect of the native
comes through hidden sources, spouse's assets, and dealing with
secrets of other people. If the Sun is debilitated as it aspects the
2nd House, then it can show secrecy within the family due to the
father, or excessive abuse by the father that was hidden in the
family. The native will also use secrets of the family against them
to gain wealth and assets. The government can also step in and
take the assets of the native due to unpaid taxes, through which
their family life would suffer.
A debilitated Sun, especially near 5-12 degrees Libra, shows a
very dark personality who stays away from the world, yet is
cunning enough to operate from underground to dominate others.
This is also the reason why they can exert overconfidence and
dominance towards their own family. They lack the true dignity of
the Sun (King).

## Sun 3rd House Aspect:

The Sun in the 9th House will aspect the 3rd House. The 9th
House is the house of higher learning, gurus, law, religious text
and scriptures, long distance pilgrimage, and is the house of luck.
The Sun in the 9th House glances over the 3rd House of siblings,
skills, media, arts, and communication. The Sun, being the ego and
confidence of the native, shows that one's self-esteem and ego are
derived from higher learning and respecting gurus. However, the
aspect of the Sun towards the 3rd House can vary depending upon
its sign placement. An exalted Sun, or Sun in its own sign, shows
someone who uses higher education and teachings to develop their
confidence, and share their knowledge and philosophy with others.
This nature transforms into the Sun's aspect on the 3rd House,
which shows taking charge of one's own skill set, dominating
younger siblings, and communicating as the head preacher to one's
close friends and neighbors.
A debilitated Sun in the 9th House shows someone who may run
into the wrong teachers and gurus. The aspect demonstrates that
such a person will teach his or her siblings the wrong things in life,
which the native themselves won't realize. They will use incorrect
information to improve their skill set in the 3rd House, which will
become a battle to fix.

## Sun 4th House Aspect:

The Sun in the 10th House aspects the 4th House. The 10th House
represents career, status, reputation, government, and father. There
are blessings in the sky, as the Sun is at its highest point at noon.
This is why it's called the mid-heaven. The Sun in the 10th House
is strong in terms of power and prestige. The Sun governs the
federal and local government, and is a professional, serious, and
direct planet whose work ethic is uncanny. Its aspect on the 4th
House brings professionalism and egocentric behavior into the
home. The aspect upon the 4th House represents the mother, home,
comfort, and mother's nourishment. The Sun, who is the father, is a
very dominating figure in the mother's life and affected her
decision making ability. The comfort of home was great, but it had
rules one had to follow.
If the Sun is exalted in the 10th House, the aspect upon the 4th
House will bring sense of prestige and higher status in the home.
The native will want his or her family members to act as if they are
part of the kingdom and cabinet. An exalted Sun, or Sun in its own
sign, shows that one will protect their home against any kind of
smearing or insults. They will bring a sense of righteousness
within home. Comforts will be regal due to the Sun's powerful rays

on the home, but at the same time will be quite strict. A debilitated
Sun aspect on the 7th House will show that the father will cause
comforts to be taken away. This will cause a great deal of damage
in the relationship between the native and their father if the Sun
happens to be between 5-12 degrees Libra. Due to problems in
their career, the native uses excessive power and ego to dominate
their family members. The demanding nature of the native due to a
debilitated Sun would also impact one's comfort, home, and
mother.

## Sun 5th House Aspect:

The Sun in the 11th House aspects the 5th House. The 11th House
represents network circles, elder siblings, hopes, wishes, and
increased authority. The Sun can burn and dominate the network
circle. There is a great interest in politics due to the Sun's aspect on
the 5th House. The Sun in the 11th House reflects someone who

loves making connections in the political world or with people of
high status in society. The Sun's aspect on the 5th House shows
that they will use network circles and friends to gain political
leadership represented by the 5th House. The native's confidence
and ego comes from making connections with influential people,
otherwise they feel weak and unimportant.
A debilitated Sun makes one follow the wrong crowd and network
circle in the 11th House, through which the 5th House aspect
would also be affected. They would give poor advice to their
children and pursue the wrong academic education that may not be
useful. Relationships with their children would also suffer due to
dominance and involvement in the wrong crowd. There would be a
tremendous lack of confidence when dealing with a huge audience
or social event. Their frustration will negatively impact their home,
children, education, and creative expression of the 5th House. The
native with a debilitated or weak Sun would derive confidence and
ego by engaging with people of high status, yet those people would
be charlatans. Through this association, the Sun would disregard
the value of 5th House with its aspect.

## Sun 6th House Aspect:

The Sun in the 12th House aspects the 6th House. In the 12th
House, the Sun loses his power. Why? Because the 12th House is
the house of losses, and the Sun is king. This means the Sun loses
his throne or is ousted from the kingdom in the 12th House.
Knowing the 12th House represents losses, moksha, spirituality,
foreign lands, hospitals, and jails, we can determine how the Sun
will act. The Sun is our personality, our identity, and our virtue.
The Sun in the 12th House shows a loss of identity. One finds their
ego and confidence in foreign places or in isolation. This can be
the case for a foreign government diplomat. The Sun's aspect on
the 6th House shows that one will deal with enemies, disease, and
obstacles while in foreign lands or places of isolation. If the Sun is
exalted, or in a good position in its own sign, it can show one who
manages to deal with obstacles effectively.
The 6th House aspect shows that for one to find their self worth,
confidence, and balanced ego, they must dive into everyday work.
They can help social groups overcome obstacles with their
leadership qualities. They must be a good employee in the private
or government sector in order to enjoy life in foreign places, or
places of peace. A debilitated Sun, especially between 5-12
degrees Libra, can show many problems with the government and
authority, which can land the native’s father in jail. If they have a
weak Sun, they will wander in foreign lands without the ability to
find their identity and value until age 35. The native may have had
to move to a foreign place due to conflicts with one's own father,
as the 6th House aspect depicts that their father might have
become the enemy.

## Moon Aspect through Houses:

Moon: The Moon's aspect on other planets and houses shows a
sense of comfort and a welcoming nature. Such an aspect will
create a kind, emotional, and maternal influence.

## Moon Aspect on the 1st House:

When the Moon is in the 1st House (Ascendant), it aspects the 7th
House. The 7th House is the house of marriage, legal partnership,
other people, trade, and business. The Moon in the 1st House is
very self aware and emotional. The mother becomes a big part of
their physical health, and influences their career. These natives are
very caring, and love giving and receiving comforts in life. The
Moon's aspect on the 7th House makes them a nurturing spouse.
They will have a maternal attitude towards their partner. The 7th
House aspect also shows that one's mother might take part in
choosing their spouse. The downside to the Moon's aspect on the
7th House is the native’s longing for emotional attachment and
stability through their partner.

## Moon 8th House Aspect:

The Moon in the 2nd House aspects the 8th House. The 2nd House
represents family, wealth, assets, food, and speech. The Moon is
emotionally attached to one's family. The native would find
emotional stability through family support. Wealth is connected to
the mother. She will be the source of money, knowledge, and food.
The Moon's aspect on the 8th House of occult, secret affairs,
surgeries, major fluctuations, death, and transformations shows
that one will be emotionally attached to learning about the secrets
of others. They may be interested in occult science and nurturing
relatives. They are emotionally attracted to secrets of other
families, which can make them prone to gossip. The 2nd House
Moon can be like an ocean wave; where things rise and fall due to
waning and waxing phases. Similarly, wealth can fluctuate for the
native, causing them to look at marriage, family life, and relatives
as a source of income. This also allows the teachings of our family
to be shared with the family of our spouse.
The Moon feels a sense of comfort when it tries to learn about
things that are hidden underground.

## Moon 9th House Aspect:

The Moon in the 3rd House aspects the 9th House. This is a place
of discomfort for the Moon. It is the Lord of the 4th House of
comfort, and it goes to the 12th from the 4th, which is about losing
comfort and focusing all of your efforts toward a goal. In the 3rd
House, the Moon (mind), is geared towards the skills of art,
painting, communication, and sales. The mind is always into
writing and working with one’s hands. They have an emotional
attachment towards artistic pursuits which brings peace. The
Moon's 7th aspect is on the 9th House. The 9th House is of higher
learning, philosophy, teachers, universities, gurus, religious rituals,
and pilgrimage. Through one's art and ability to communicate, one
has the emotional need to attach higher wisdom to their work. It
doesn't matter if they are writers, actors, anchors, journalists, or
sculptors; they must have some religious or philosophical meaning
behind what they do. In another words, you can say whatever the
Moon learns in the 3rd House, it wants to connect to the 9th House
through those things. One uses their creativity to attain wisdom.
Through the teachings of their mother, they will be driven towards
a higher purpose.

## Moon 10th House Aspect:

The Moon in the 4th House aspects the 10th House. The Moon is
very comfortable in the 4th House because it is the Moon's home.
There is a sense of emotional comfort when at home and with their
mother. The mind is at peace when it's thinking about improving
the home, comfort, property, and people of their homeland. From
the 4th House, the Moon aspects the 10th House of career, status,
and reputation. This shows that one's career will have an emotional
impact on the native. They will feel a deep sense of nourishment
towards a career involving taking care of others; like nursing, elder
care, or massage therapy. The Moon is not just about emotions.
One will feel that with the comfort and support of their mother and
homeland, they can achieve any kind of career success. Their
mother is a great influence on their path. There is a strong
inclination towards becoming a public servant and going into
politics. The Moon also represents government on a local level.
There is a sense of patriotism and emotional attachment to helping
people of one's homeland.

## Moon Aspect on the 11th House:

The Moon is in the 5th House and it aspects the 11th House. The
Moon in the 5th House shows emotional attachment to children,
creativity, and academic studies. One feels peace of mind when
surrounded by children, and learns from them. They have an
interest in political discussions, performing arts, and gambling.
Since the 5th House is intelligence, we gain from a previous life.
The Moon can quickly grasp concepts which can produce a very
involved student. The Moon aspects the 11th House of network
circles, large organizations, hopes and dreams, and gains of all
kinds. The Moon's aspect on the 11th House shows an emotional
attachment to children, creativity, performing arts, and education.
They create network circles to support their hopes and wishes.
There is an emotional need to connect with a large network when
pursuing creative projects, building schools for children, and
academic studies.

## Moon Aspect on the 12th House:

The Moon in the 6th House aspects the 12th House. This
placement shows emotional imbalance with the mother and with
the mind. The native’s mind is conflicted with society, their
mother, animals, and their mother's siblings. Effort is required to
maintain a relationship with their mother. The mind is emotionally
attached to the needs of small animals, underprivileged people, and
healing the sick, since the 6th House represents disease. The mind
is filled with debts of the mother's actions, and their own debt to
society. There native is nurturing, and wants to heal people. The
Moon is not fond of small animals if in the sign of Saturn and
without the aspect of Saturn. From the 6th House, the Moon
aspects the 12th House of isolation, peace, spirituality, moksha,
hospitals, jails, asylums, foreign lands, and astral plains. One has
an emotional need to escape from obstacles and enemies sitting in
the 6th House, and go to isolated places to find emotional peace.
They tend to gravitate towards places of tranquility and peace after
fighting so many battles with disease and opposition. They may
live through tremendous hardship, which makes their mind
inclined towards spirituality.

## Moon Aspect on the 1st House:

The Moon in the 7th House aspects the 1st House. This brings
about emotional attachment to one’s spouse, other people, or the
masses. One finds emotional security and peace of mind when
serving and loving their partner. They choose a spouse who is
motherly and nurturing. The native becomes emotionally
connected with other people, and finds satisfaction in helping the
masses find peace. The Moon from the 7th House aspects the 1st
House, which represents self, personality, and health. One's
personality waxes and wanes depending upon their partner. They
are easily influenced by their spouse, and their identity and health
is also dependent upon the nourishment they receive and public
acceptance.

## Moon Aspect on the 2nd House:

When the Moon is in the 8th House it becomes marana karaka. In
classic Vedic text, this means it comes to die in the 8th House.
Many people have great minds and great relationships with their
mother with the Moon in the 8th House. However, you need to
observe its depositor and nakshatra Lord placement. The Moon in
the 8th House shows that the mind is inclined towards things that
are hidden from the surface and buried underground; like occult,
mystery, investigation, mining, surgery, and drilling for oil and
gems. Their emotional need comes from going through many
sudden transformations which impact them psychologically and
physically. This creates an interest in learning about how to solve
these sudden, hidden transformations. Due to the condition of the
mother and her health problems, they find themselves abruptly,
emotionally, transforming to become mature and grow out of such
setbacks. Otherwise, they dissolve themselves in the black hole of
the 8th House. They may find themselves isolated from the world
of reality.
This placement increases intuition and psychic power. They have
an interest in solving their problems through occult science, or
they become creative geniuses who try to resolve their emotional
setbacks by bringing a dark side of creativity. The Moon in the 8th
House aspects the 2nd House of family, wealth, history, culture,
and food. Due to sudden events and chronic illnesses, one has a
tremendous need to be nurtured by their family. Visualize a person
reaching out from a dark hole, and you can only see their hands
trying to grasp something. If it's a debilitated Moon, then it can
create family turmoil that is mentally draining.

## Moon Aspect on the 3rd House:

The Moon in the 9th House has an emotional attachment to higher
learning, philosophy, and religious texts. Peace of mind is
achieved when one is studying scripture and the ancient past. They
love getting involved in temples, organizations related to religion,
or the pursuit of higher education. The mother becomes a source of
guidance and knowledge in one's life, and becomes a dominating
male-type figure. Her teachings become the core for one's belief
system. Since the Moon is the karaka of 4th House, it shows one
dealing with issues related to their home, homeland, and comforts
through education and the law. In other words, one is bound to use
the law and higher education in attaining comfort and land. The
Moon from the 9th House aspects the 3rd House of siblings,
media, craft, effort, communication, street smarts, and intelligence.
The mind has an emotional need to find peace through preaching
to people who are near and dear to them. They love sharing words
of wisdom with their siblings. They feel that higher education and
religious knowledge should be shared with their friends, family,
and neighbors, otherwise there is no use in attaining such valuable
information. The mind uses philosophy and religious education to
create arts and crafts. Their art work would evoke some religious
aspect within them.

## Moon Aspect on the 4th House:

The Moon in the 10th House represents status, rank, and misplaced
government. It seeks to climb the ladder. They have an emotional
need to find themselves attached to their work, corporation, or
government position. The reason why the Moon loses his strength
as in dik bala, is because the Moon is a gentle and sensitive planet.
Being in the jungle of the corporate world creates a lack of
nourishment and praise it usually has at home. The Moon brings
fame to an individual as it's positioned at the highest point in the
sky. The Moon in the 10th House also shows that the mother has
taken over the father's role and becomes the most dominating and
visible figure in the native's life. The Moon from the 10th House
aspects the 4th house. This creates a great emotional need to seek
privacy at home and comfort from the mother due to so much
pressure from the demands of public life and corporate world. One
separates their private and public life quite easily. Even as a
celebrity, the media and public would hardly get a glimpse of the
person's home and their private world. In the 10th House, the
moon doesn't find its peace of mind by climbing the ladder of
success. Rather it finds peace of mind through its aspect to the 4th
House, in the privacy of 4th House. The Moon (mind) is drawn

towards reaching high ranks which is the Moon's DNA in the 10th
House, but the Moon's mind is towards its aspect.

## Moon Aspect on 5th House:

The Moon in the 11th House aspects the 5th House. The Moon in
the 11th House has an emotional need to connect with network
circles and large organizations for the betterment of the world.
They enjoy connecting with people, are resourceful, and are
constantly
stimulated
by
being
involved
with
friends,
