organizations, and parties. There is an emotional need to be
socially recognized. This does not mean they want to be world
famous, but they need to find a way to shine in their circle of
friends. The Moon in the 11th House can sometimes be very
transformative for the native's mind and their mother, as it's 8th
from its original 4th House. The 8th from any position calls for
sudden fluctuations and transformations. This is usually the case
for Aries ascendant, where the Moon becomes the Lord of 4th
House. The 11th House represents gains, hopes, wishes, and goals.
They are achieved by either networking, or using other people if
it's in a malefic position. From the 11th House, the Moon has an
emotional need to connect with children, arts, and creativity, which

is invoked by becoming social with others. Due to the influence of
artistic circles, they automatically have a tendency to be creative
and gain through children.

## Moon Aspect on 6th House:

When the Moon is in the 12th House, it aspects in the 6th House.
The Moon in the 12th House becomes very imaginative, creative,
isolated, spiritual, and eccentric. They feel withdrawn from the
world, as their mind is always in a spiritual realm and the realm of
other worlds. They find it hard to identify with most people. An
artificial ego tells them that they are special because they are
isolated. These natives are disconnected with the world, including
their spouse. The Moon (mind) creates the need to search the
deepest places within to find their creativity; and they do. They are
some of the best authors and artists. These people are highly
spiritual, or they are introverted and can fall into a depression
because of a lack of connection with their mother and the world.
They love living in their own dream. The Moon from the 12th
House aspects the 6th House of disease, conflicts, obstacles,
underprivileged people, and people of the lower realm.

As the mind is isolated and separated from reality, they have an
emotional need to resolve other people's conflict through their
imagination, spirituality, and ability observe the world from a
distance. Their emotional need to connect to others, as in 7th place
from themselves, attracts people who are in conflict, dealing with
diseases, health ailments, or they can be medical professional
themselves. Their spouse could be a nurse, healer, caretaker, or
doctor who has dealt with personal conflicts and enemies. The
12th House Moon wants to serve the underprivileged through their
spiritual and artistic work.

## Mars Aspect through Houses:

Mars aspects the 4th, 7th, and 8th place from itself. The aspect of
Mars is with direct force. It is militaristic, and requires action
towards the house it aspects. It represents war, conflict, and
domination.

## Mars Aspect on the 4th, 7th, and 8th House:

When Mars is in the 1st House, it aspects the 4th, 7th and 8th
House. Mars in the 1st House has the desire to take charge of one's
own destiny, health, and body. Mars in the 1st House makes a
person very active in sports and physical activity because they care
about their health, which will be explained in its aspect. These
folks have a military type personality; they must take control and
have power over their own life and the lives of others. First, we
will discuss its basic 7th House aspect. Mars has aspect towards
the 7th house from the 1st House. This aspect makes them bluntly
guide other people or their spouse. They have a straightforward
nature and a need to take charge of their relationships with people.
They seek a partner who can fulfill their physical, athletic, and
sexual needs. They are argumentative with their partners due to
insecurity and instability.
Mars' 4th House aspect shows where they need to feel grounded
and stable. The 4th aspect is originally due to the 4th House
representing land, which Mars is the karaka of. Mars from the 1st
House aspects the 4th House which shows instability at home,
arguments between the native and their mother during childhood,
or a home environment that was like a military boot camp. It also

shows one has the need for their own land and home, and they can
rule like a victorious general.
Mars also has the 8th place aspect and from the 1st House it
aspects, and the original 8th House of the zodiac. Mars 8th aspect
shows one has a very aggressive relationship with their in-laws and
fluctuations in their married life that may result in physical
violence if Mars is debilitated or sitting with a malefic planet. This
placement also shows someone who takes charge of revealing
secrets of others or the government; like a whistle blower. Mars
represents cuts, bruises, burns, and accidents. Matters of the 8th
House are unpredictable, and can happen suddenly, which is why
accidents are seen through this aspect if Mars is in neutral or bad
condition. If Mars is in Aries in the 1st House, then it aspects its
own sign Scorpio with its 8th House aspect; which means it will
protect that house and the native from sudden bad events. Mars
also makes the person very sexual and animalistic due to its aspect
on the 7th and 8th House as the same time.
Mars Aspect on the 5th, 8th and 9th House.

Mars in the 2nd House aspects the 5th, 8th and 9th House. Mars in
the 2nd House shows aggression and domination over people in
their family. The person speaks with energy, enthusiasm, and
motivation because the 2nd House represents speech and intake of
food. They are usually meat eaters. The 2nd House also shows our
wealth and our ability to accumulate assets. Mars, the soldier here,
doesn't really care for money because he knows when its war time
he will be on the battlefield and must stock up on goods needed to
win. This is why Mars like to spend money in the 2nd House. He
rather own accessories than gold coins due to his war-like
mentality. Mars can also show someone who has a foul mouth.
The red hot planet shows sudden bursts of anger as well.
Mars from the 2nd House aspects the 5th House of education,
children, creativity, romance, politics, and performing arts. Mars
aspect energizes all the aspects of the 5th House. There is a need to
take charge of one's children and dominate their life with their own
wishes. This also creates an aggressive nature in romance where
the native would be very passionate, but at the same time
demanding in areas of love. Creativity is greatly energized. A stage
actor or actress will show tremendous passion for performance.

They love playing the bad guy, hero, fighter, or killer.
Academically, Mars may not be helpful unless it aspects its own
house or other planets sitting in it. In this case, Mars will invoke
interest in learning about weaponry, engineering, and computers.
Mars’ 7th house aspect is on the 8th House. The native seeks to
dominate his own family and that of his spouse, including their
assets. There is a need to dig for secrets or things hidden
underground like gold, silver, oil, and gems. Mars’ aggressive
nature also takes the person deep into the occult world. This can
also cause accidents due to the aspect on the 8th House, if left
unprotected by Mars.
Mars from the 2nd House aspects the 9th House of higher learning,
education, teachers, teachings of the father, religion, preaching,
and long distance travel. Mars is 6th from the 9th, which shows
that people with religious backgrounds and the native’s father will
become obstacles in attaining a higher education. The native will
be an atheist in the early part of life, and will reject any religious
establishment. The native will become aggressive and competitive
in terms of higher learning. They will compare themselves with
their friends when it comes to status and ability to preach.

## Mars Aspect on the 6th, 9th, and 10th House:

In the 3rd House, Mars is courageous, brave, and has the
willpower to achieve anything in life. One is aggressively
motivated to move ahead. The 3rd House represents our skills and
hobbies like acting, sewing, drawing, painting, and sculpting. Mars
puts its energy and force into these pursuits. As a media journalist,
Mars would do the kind of story that many would refuse. They are
very daring people. They are good at sports and have a tremendous
interest in competition. When it comes to sales, they are the most
aggressive. They will do whatever it takes to sell ice to an Eskimo.
They are self motivated and try to take over any team or group
project they are involved in.
Mars from the 3rd House aspects the 6th House of enemies,
diseases, obstacles, debts, small animals, and underprivileged
people. Mars’ aspect makes one fight their enemies and win
battles. Mars gives the native strength to overcome and fight all
types of diseases. There is aggressive and competitive behavior in
fighting for the underprivileged and managing debt. If Mars is
debilitated in the 3rd House, one can take advantage of the poor,
and put them in debt.

Mars’ 7th House aspect is on the 9th House. There is tremendous
courage to win battles for one's gurus and father, but at the same
time, they seek to dominate them. Controlling one's father and
teachers will always result in conflicts, which does occur with this
aspect. One creates their own higher education, philosophy, and
values to achieve because a soldier and a guru have two very
different perspectives on life.
Mars aspects the 10th House with its 8th aspect. This aspect
creates conflicts and battles at the native's workplace and in
society. This is due to the 3rd House being 6th from the 10th,
which means the 6th House energizes conflicts. This creates
sudden battles and fights with co-workers, and perhaps even using
personal or company secrets to get ahead. Mars' 8th aspect will
always try to dig deep into matters of the 8th aspect. But the great
thing about 3rd House Mars is that it wins all battles and comes
out ahead in the professional sphere. They easily take charge at
work, and become a leader of the team.

## Mars Aspect on the 7th, 10th and 11th House:

Mars in the 4th House aspects the 7th, 10th, and 11th House. Mars’
4th aspect lands on the 7th House of marriage, legal partnerships,
and other people. Mars in the 4th House brings conflict to matters
related to the native’s mother, comfort, home, homeland, and
vehicles. The mother is a pioneer and straight shooter, who is
aggressive and acts like a general towards the native. However,
Mars can also harm the health of the mother if badly inflicted.
Conflict with parents, especially the mother, can change the
outlook of the native or how he sees other people. Due to feeling
suppressed at a young age, one tends to be a fighter and a rebel in
adulthood.
The 4th aspect of Mars on the 7th House shows conflicts within
the home, which transfers towards the 7th House of marriage. The
person wants to control the spouse, and will not let them win any
battles. Although Mars in the 4th House gives a lot of discomfort
in the early part of life, they tend to find comfort of the 4th House
through domination. There is a lack of loyalty towards one’s
spouse because Mars is originally in the house where he's
debilitated.
Mars’ 7th aspect is on the 10th House of career, status, father, and
professional achievements. Mars brings about a great need for
competition at the workplace where one can deceive, control, and
manipulate opponents to win the war. It has a sneaky way of going
behind one's own group of professionals to create a shortcut for his
or her success. If they are not in a leadership role, they will be
agitated and frustrated. This position can make for a soldier,
policeman, or athlete. Whatever career they choose, they will find
a way to take advantage of its weaknesses.
Mars in the 4th House has its 8th aspect on the 11th House. This
shows sudden conflicts with friends and network circles since the
11th House is the karaka of such things; including gains, hopes,
and wishes. The gains for an individual might come from an ill

source if Mars is badly placed in the 4th House. This can depict a
robber or thief. Their source of 11th House gains will be illegal.

## Mars aspect on 8th, 11th and 12th House:

Mars in the 5th House aspects the 8th, 11th and 12th House. The
5th House is about children, creativity, romance, speculative
business, stock market, and academic learning. Mars energizes the
5th House by demonstrating straightforward behavior towards
one's children, romance, and education. The native will be
physically active with their children, and will provide an avenue
for exercise and sports. However, their children may dominate
them as adults. Mars invokes fighting skills. They are dynamic
actors and creative artists who will fight to the finish to showcase
their product, unless Mars is damaged in the 5th House. In
relationships, they are very passionate and sexual, but if Mars is
hurting, then it nullifies sexual tendencies.
Mars in the 5th House aspects the 8th House of occult knowledge,
secrecy, joint assets with one’s spouse, taxes, and fluctuations.
Mars brings about speculative assets through marriage. One has
tremendous interest in the occult or alternative healing. They also

put pressure on in-laws. They keep secrets regarding their children,
creativity, and health related to the 5th House. With this
placement, Mars can bring sudden fluctuations in health, and may
even show surgery during its mahadasha.
The 7th aspect of Mars is on the 11th House of gains, friends,
network circles, elder siblings, and organizations. One wants to
dominate their network circle, and this can lead to fewer friends
and support. Mars in the 5th House can make an aggressive stock
broker who is willing to take many risks. If Mars is well placed,
then they have much to gain.
The 8th aspect of Mars is on the 12th House of losses, imagination,
foreign lands, spirituality, and sexual pleasure. It can bring about
vivid imagination to inspire one's creativity. This is why these
natives make the best actors, especially action heroes, and dark
mafia type characters. They are able to go into the other realm and
bring about hidden talent from the 12th House. This can also spark
a sudden foreign journey due to business. Mars can bring about the
spiritual practice of yoga and martial arts to protect one's private
life. A debilitated or damaged Mars can show someone who may
cheat other people in gambling and the stock market, which can
land them jail since the 12th House represents prisons and
asylums.

## Mars aspect on 9th, 12th and 1st House:

Mars in the 6th House aspects the 9th House of higher education,
philosophy, religion, long distance travel, and gurus. It aspects the
12th House of losses, spirituality, foreign lands, other realms,
sexual pleasures, hospitals, jails, and asylums. Mars also aspects
the 1st House of self and personality. Mars in the 6th House shows
someone who was born to battle opposition throughout life,
whether it's a cop, soldier, criminal, lawyer, or doctor. They will
be battling everyday to make a living, because the 6th House is the
house of debts, diseases, enemies, and small pets. Mars in the 6th
House can make someone an infectious disease doctor, or a fighter
for the underprivileged; like a policeman or free lawyer. Sports is
another area where you compete, so athletes are also seen from this
position. Since the 6th House deals with debt, one has the ability to
rid themselves of it, and help others struggling with this issue, like
a debt counselor.
Mars’ 4th aspect is on the 9th House of guru, father's teachings,
graduate studies, religious studies, preaching, and luck. Here, Mars
goes to battle with one's guru, teachings of the father, and any
religious establishment. They cannot handle the old world of strict

spiritual and religious preaching. They want to create their own
path of philosophy and learning. They get into arguments with
their teachers, gurus, and preachers over higher learning and
religion.
Mars’ 7th House aspect is on the 12th House. Mars brings about
conflicts and with spirituality, meditation, and isolated places.
They have a way of using their imagination for defeating enemies.
They have no patience for spiritual pursuits and meditation.
However, because Mars is action oriented, one move towards
spiritual sports like yoga and martial arts. This aspect shows
conflicts, fighting, travel to foreign lands, or even prison if Mars is
weak.
Mars’ 8th aspect is in the ascendant (1st House). This aspect shows
survival, battling enemies, and using one’s physical body like a
wrestler or fighter. One will also have debt during the Mars time
period. If Mars is in a good position, it will be paid off. There will
also be sudden harm to the body during the mahadasha period, but
the native would overcome it because all malefics do well in
malefic houses.

## Mars aspect on 10th, 1st and 2nd House:

Mars in the 7th House aspects the 10th House, ascendant, and 2nd
House. In the 7th House, Mars brings energy into married life and
relationships with others. The 7th House is the house of marriage,
legal partnerships, and other people. Mars is a planet that looks to
gain power through conflict. In the 7th House, it can show that the
spouse will be domineering. They could be an engineer, firefighter,
policeman, soldier, or athlete.
Mars in the 7th House aspects the 10th House of career, status,
father, and government. If the native is in business, then Mars will
provide willpower and stamina to withstand any opposition. They
love to have competition in the business world so they can win to
benefit their own ego. The spouse is a great contributor to
business, career, and status in society. The spouse of the native
will conflict with their father if Mars is debilitated, or aspect by a
malefic. They know how to dominate others in business and fight
for profit. If there are no benefic aspects upon the 7th House, it can
cause physical fights with the spouse.

Mars’ 7th House aspect is upon the ascendant which is health,
personality, and physical body. This aspect gives the native a very
aggressive and assertive personality. They thrive through
competition, and love to get physically involved in sports related
to fighting, boxing, and martial arts.
Mars’ 8th aspect is upon the 2nd House of speech, wealth, family,
and family assets. Mars can decrease wealth due to conflicts in
business and with the spouse. One may lose income due to a
divorce. They utilize their spouse’s ability to do business with
others to create family wealth. There is a sense of dominance and
protecting their assets.

## Mars aspect on 11th, 2nd and 3rd House:

Mars in the 8th House represents sudden events, surgery,
accidents, inheritance, occult, oil, and mining. This position can
make a person a surgeon, detective, emergency responder, or
government spy. Mars dominates the 8th House, as he rules the
original 8th House of Scorpio. It has the willpower and strength to
dive into the dark world of occult science. Most would think it's
Ketu, Rahu, or Jupiter, but it's the bravery of Mars that gives the

action pentagonal to the native to start practicing tantra. As a
soldier, Mars has the strength to keep secret information and use it
to win battles in the game of life. This also includes dangerous
government projects like UFO disclosures, wars, and wiretapping.
This position can make the native highly sexual, and use sexual
power to secretly gain strength. Women with this position can use
sex as their best weapon to climb the corporate ladder. The 8th
House Mars can also cause sudden surgeries related to the heart,
other muscles, or blood clots. This position is also known for
accidents during Mars maha and antar dasha.
Mars in the 8th House aspects the 11th House of gains, network
circles, and one's hopes and wishes. Mars aspect can create sudden
conflicts with network circles in business dealings. One will be
selfish in terms of gains through their network. When it is time to
repay, Mars in the 8th House will secretly disappear. However,
this can give tremendous value through government, mining,
business, gems, medicine, and the occult. People with this
placement are better off alone than in a team of people because no
one will appreciate their intense secretive way of working.
Mars in the 8th House also aspects the 2nd House with his 7th
aspect. This aspect is a strong indicator of second marriage if Mars
is weak or a functional malefic. The person is a spend thrift and
loves putting money towards investments and the lottery. One will
do whatever it takes to protect their family and its assets. This can
make for an aggressive investment banker, or someone who
invests his family wealth into chemicals, gems, and the mining
business. In the house of transformation, Mars can suddenly gain
or lose money due to their brother or male friends. Mars’ aspect on
the house of speech and food intake can make someone very angry
with aggressive speech. Mars brings the tendency of meat eating,
but, with the aspect of Jupiter, one can easily walk away from non-
vegetarian food.

Mars’ 8th House aspect is on the 3rd House of siblings,
communication, media, hobbies, and short distant travels. Mars
can make someone a feared athlete who has the skill set of
building things. Their communication style is aggressive and
secretive with those who are close to them. They may keep
information from their siblings and close neighbors. These natives
will try to dominate siblings and become a community leader.
They are always self-reliant, and create wealth based on their own
effort and skills.

## Mars aspect on 12th, 3rd and 4th House:

Mars in the 9th House represents gurus, teachers, father's
teachings, higher knowledge, and religious rituals. It can make the
native rebel against these matters. Mars is a soldier who likes to
make his own way, and build his own philosophy to get through
problems. This is why Mars can get into competitive arguments
with teachers and fatherly figures. Mars feels it grasps higher
knowledge of life better than the people who are teaching these
subjects.

Mars in the 9th House aspects the 12th House of losses,
spirituality, foreign lands, asylums, jails, and hospitals. Mars uses
its own motivation and religious views to form a unique spiritual
pattern. The native uses intuition, higher self, and knowledge to
find moksha. Mars, the karaka of surgeries, blood, cuts, and
accidents, gives the native the ability to cure others in hospitals,
and use medical knowledge to cure those in asylums. This is a
great position for a motivational spiritual teacher, pastor, or artist
who uses philosophy to fuel imagination.
Mars from the 9th House aspects the 3rd House of siblings, close
neighbors, sales, media, marketing, initiative, courage, and
communication. The native will try to become a commander of
higher knowledge and wisdom, which will cause conflicts and with
younger siblings and close neighbors. One will try to use their
engineering ability and skilled hands towards constructing
something of higher value. It is always important to see the
depositor of Mars and the angel of his dominance. This is a great
position for small community leadership and skilled engineers.
Mars in the 9th House also aspects the 4th House with his 8th
aspect. Mars brings conflict with the native’s mother and people at
home. The mother's teachings about life can also be too
demanding. Independent thinker Mars runs into problems and
power struggles with the mother's philosophy.

## Mars aspect on the 1st, 4th and 5th House:

Mars in the 10th House aspects the 1st, 4th, and 5th House. In the
10th House, Mars feels at home. The 10th House is not just the
house of career, status, and government, but also a jungle and
battlefield. Since everyone is fighting for status, the professional
sphere becomes a battle. This position makes the native fall in love
with challenges in life, and become involved in power struggles.
They are the soldiers who accomplish their mission to go to the top
of the food chain; whether it's a policeman, soldier, or CEO. Their
energy and willpower is about striving for status.
Mars from the 10th House aspects the ascendant 1st House of self,
body, and personality. Again, we have a fighting mentality. This
aspect makes the personality extremely competitive. They have the
knack to win the war in the shortest and quickest way possible.
They are headstrong with everything in life. Since this is a very
tough personality, they usually do poorly with team sports and
anything requiring teamwork. They need to be their own boss.

Mars from the 10th House aspects the 4th House with his 7th
aspect. Mars’ aspect is on the house of home, mother, childhood,
and comfort, and brings about a dictator-like mentality in the
household. The mother could have been a professional throughout
life, and projected and expected professional behavior inside the
home. Mars is in the 10th House of the father. This eventually
leads the native to get into arguments with their parents. This is
why conflicts and power struggles with the father and mother
occur at the same time.
Mars in the 10th House also aspects the 5th House with his 8th
aspect. Mars creates conflicts with children, arguments, and power
struggles. The native would naturally dominate their children.
However, since the 5th House is also the house of creativity,
romance, and self expression, one tends to find a lot of energy and
dynamic artistic expression in their life, and it could turn into their
profession. Usually, this is a good placement for management of
all kinds; military work, sports, and a career in media if Mars is
placed in Taurus, Gemini, Leo, or Libra. This aspect will also
make their children good athletes and give them a competitive
nature, especially if the mother possesses this placement.

## Mars aspect on the 2nd, 5th and 6th House:

Mars is in the 11th House of gains, elder siblings, hopes and
wishes, and large network circles. It aspects the 2nd, 5th, and 8th
House. Mars in the 11th House makes one energized about social
networks and market trading. The native is driven towards
financial competition with peers and network circles. They want to
have more income compared to anyone around them. The native
wants to take charge of society as a whole, and rule over them.
They are very aggressive in making deals with professional
networks, but they are also very dynamic, and able to keep good
relationships. A damaged Mars or debilitated Mars will make them
try to cheat others or simply dominate others to achieve their goals.
Mars in the 11th House aspects the 2nd House of wealth and
family. One will tend lose their wealth by trying to accomplish
their hopes and dreams. They will overspend because they will
possess the confidence of a soldier. The native is very aggressive
in speaking, especially when they talk to their family about wealth
and gains. They want to dominate and win the war of wealth with

their family. This can be for good or bad depending upon Mars'
condition.
Mars in the 11th House also aspects the 5th House with his 7th
aspect. Mars creates conflicts with children, arguments, and power
struggles over finances. The native naturally wants to dominate
their children. However, since the 5th House is also the house of
creativity, romance, and self-expression, one tends to find a lot of
energy and dynamic artistic influence in their life, and it could turn
into their profession. Mars in the 11th House of controlling social
circles and society at large makes the native attracted to politics,
and giving energetic and dynamic political speeches.
Mars from the 11th House also aspects the 6th House of diseases,
enemies, obstacles, debts, and small animals. Mars provides
security and a winning plan to overcome disease and debt. One
will overcome enemies over time, as the 11th House is the
upachaya house, meaning it grows better with time. The native will
fight to make improvements in the healthcare system for
unprivileged people. One will gain through their enemies and
disease; like a salaried physician. However, Mars in the 11th
House is mainly about finance and social networking in business.

## Mars aspect on 3rd, 6th and 7th House:

Mars is in the 12th House of isolation, hospitals, asylums,
ashrams, foreign lands, jails, and spirituality. It aspects the 3rd, 6th,
and 7th House. Mars in the 12th House energizes imagination and
sexual pleasures. This placement gives unusual and secretive
sexual experiences, which can also represent childhood rape and
sexual abuse in the nakshatra of Rahu and Mercury. Mars, the
soldier and warrior planet, does not crave spirituality, or peace and
enlightenment. Being in the house of such values, it uses the
physical self to realize its potential. The native with this placement
is very much interested in practicing martial arts and yoga in
isolated places where the physical body is the main source of
concentration. There is a strong desire for a career in foreign lands,
and finding one's power there. The native must be very careful of
not landing in jail due to violent acts after harming someone. A
debilitated Mars with Rahu and Ketu, or a strong aspect from
Saturn in the 6th House can provoke the native to do such things.
Mars in the 12th House of hospitals and jails makes the native an
excellent doctor or warden.

Mars from the 12th House aspects the 3rd House of siblings, close
neighbors, media, skill of hands, hobbies, and motivation. One
tends to use their strong imaginative skills to invoke creative
energy in writing, sculpting, and painting. The imagination of
Mars takes hold of the 3rd House of skills and efforts. One is able
to use the spiritual imagination of 12th House towards their hobby,
and skill of hands. Their communication style is direct. The native
is forceful with siblings and close friends.
Mars in the 12th House aspects the 6th House of enemies,
diseases, debts, and obstacles. Mars brings about enemies in
foreign lands, including debt. One will deal with debt when Mars
is in the house of losses. However, Mars gives the ability to fend
off debts, diseases, and enemies. If Mars is debilitated or weak per
shal bala, one could face jail on behalf of enemies, or due to
criminal acts in foreign lands. Mars brings fighting skills and the
ability to heal others, while the 12th House represents jails and
mental asylums. This is a very strong placement for a doctor, or
someone working in the medical field.
Mars in the 12th House aspects the 7th House with his 8th aspect.
The 7th house represents marriage, spouse, legal partnerships,
other people, business, trade, and courthouses. This placement
causes a tremendous number of conflicts and arguments in
marriage, along with false accusations.

## Mercury Aspects Through Houses:

Mercury is a planet of intellectual conversations, communication,
marketing, ideas, and logic. Mercury represents your written and
verbal skills. When Mercury aspects a house, it becomes curious
about that house due to its personality, and wants to communicate.

## Mercury 7th House aspect:

When Mercury is in the 1st House, he aspects the 7th House of
marriage, spouse, legal partnerships, and other people. In the 1st
House, Mercury’s presence makes the native’s personality and
physical self all about communication and ideas. They are always
jumping from one idea to the next. In the 1st House, Mercury
brings great business skills and communication. They love being
youthful, and interacting with young people and children. Mercury
is also very logical, analytical, and critical. If Mercury is in any
earth sign, it looks into life with great detail.

Mercury’s aspect on the 7th House allows the native to meet
people who are just as curious and communicative as they are.
This makes Mercury in the 1st House want to explore the 7th
House. They look for a partner who is intelligent and a good
communicator. They do not want to have boring conversations
with people because they love people! They want to do business
with others. This aspect also makes the native a flirtatious. They
tend to have many relationships before settling down because
variety is what Mercury loves in the 7th House.

## Mercury aspect on the 8th House:

Mercury in the 2nd House aspects the 8th House of joint assets,
secrets, in-laws, occult, and taxes. In the 2nd House of wealth,
family, and speech, Mercury tends to be very much attached to the
home and having a very open, communicative household. They are
good with their money and family assets. Mercury, being the
karaka of speech in the house of speech, naturally gives these
people a flair for public speaking, teaching, singing, political
speeches, and business. They are curious about money and family
lineage. Mercury in the house of finance can easily make a person
an accountant or go into finance if it’s in an earth sign.
Mercury in the 2nd House aspects the 8th House. Mercury brings
about curiosity surrounding hidden worlds. One tends to spark
conversation about deep occultist secrets that no one else likes to
talk about. They have curiosity about the secrets of the world, and
enjoy discussing these topics with others. They also love exposing
truths, which can lead to negativity and gossip. They have an
interesting in writing and communicating about such matters as a
whole, including conspiracy theories.

## Mercury aspect on 9th House:

Mercury in the 3rd House aspects the 9th House of higher learning,
gurus, religion, ritual, and philosophy. Mercury in the 3rd House
of media, performing arts, skills, hobbies, journalism, sales, and
short travels feels at home here because this is the original house
of Mercury. With this placement, there are many ideas surrounding
books, sculpting, painting, and reporting. Mercury needs to gather
information in this house. He finds his interest in sales and
marketing, and wants to communicate with others about the
current status of the community. This is a great placement for an
actor due to Mercury's talent of mimicking. Mercury becomes a
natural businessman here who wants to wheel and deal with people
during short distance travel. If Mercury is debilitated, then all
these tasks will become complicated, and the mind will feel
burdened unless there is a neecha banga raja yoga. Many great
poets, writers, and artists possess a debilitated Mercury, which
provides a limitless imagination.
Mercury in the 3rd House aspects the 9th House. One tends to be
very curious about infusing higher philosophical knowledge into
their craft and sales acumen. They love marketing and creating

businesses because they have a higher purpose. Higher education
and knowledge transforms their artistic and literary skills. This
aspect makes them a natural preacher of the arts and media. They
love being in the company of gurus and teachers who can help
them gain knowledge and independence. Mercury uses his logic to
access divine intelligence. This creates a brilliant communicator
that knows how to make esoteric knowledge tangible to others.

## Mercury aspect on the 10th House:

Mercury in the 4th House aspects the 10th House of career, status,
fluctuations, government, and the native’s relationship with their
father. Mercury in the 4th House of home, mother, land, homeland
and convenience brings about tremendous intellectual interest in
matters related to home. The native’s mother makes them great
communicators, as she was an intellectual teacher. There is a need
for education and learning about one's homeland. These folks need
to have great communication within the home in order to remain
there; otherwise a damaged Mercury can make someone move
frequently. They make excellent teachers. Mercury also makes
them business savvy, and capable of starting their own home based
business. One will tend to gravitate towards writing or reporting

about their homeland, or politics related to their homeland. They
are very compassionate towards friends and other people, which
makes them great psychologist and therapists.
Mercury in the 4th House aspects the 10th House of career and
status. Mercury is curious about taking the elements of the 4th
House and transforming it into their career. This is why Mercury
brings a strong need for a home based career or business. They
want to teach or consult from home, which also can make them
great astrologers. Mercury, the communicator and karaka of
speech, helps the native make political and government issues
public. This gives them an interest in managing people from home,
like a psychologist or therapist. They want to use logic in the
house of maternal emotions to create a business.

## Mercury aspect on the 11th House:

Mercury in the 5th House aspects the 11th House of gains, hopes,
wishes, network circles, and elder siblings. Mercury in the 5th
House of education, entertainment, performing arts, and children
does very well. These folks are natural actors and writers, and have
a flair for writing books and scripts. The 5th House is 3rd from the

3rd, creating a great need for stage performance and speaking. The
5th House is also the house of romance, and romance requires a
person to be creative and a good performer! Mercury makes a
person extremely romantic, with a passion for communication in
love. Mercury in the house of children becomes a child. Such
people develop maturity quite late because there is a child inside
that refuses to grow up. They have extremely good communication
with children, even if Mercury is debilitated. A debilitated
Mercury will tend to bring more imagination into raising children
than logic, which works well. They have very talkative offspring,
and their overall intelligence is based on artistic expression.
Mercury in the 5th House of gambling will also give speculative
abilities, especially with the stock market and sports betting if
Mars and Jupiter are in conjunction, or aspect to Mercury.
Mercury from the 5th House aspects the 11th House. Mercury,
through his artistic talent and flair for romance, has curiosity to
network with people and communicate with large organizations
regarding their talent. They like to give politically charged
speeches, since the 5th House is the house of politics, and the 11th
House is of large organizations. They use their written words and
ability to perform on stage as a source of gains. There is a great
need to communicate with their network circles about art, writing,
and stories.

## Mercury aspect on the 12th House:

Mercury in the 6th House aspects the 12th House of losses,
spirituality, foreign lands, jails, hospitals, monasteries, and
meditation. The 6th House represents disease, enemies, obstacles,
small animals, crime, litigation, and disagreements. Mercury does
very well in the 6th House. It is the house of his original, exalted
point in Virgo. In the 6th House, Mercury is able to analyze and
focus on tiny details with respect to filing, financial bookkeeping,
and medicine. Mercury becomes the mediator of resolving issues
between people. He wants to settle litigation and disputes. He
understands the details and logic behind medicine, and how to
prescribe drugs as needed. Speech and communication will always
be involved in solving conflicts, or curing diseases. They suffer
from anxiety because of their analytical ability and awareness. One
small bad move can make a whole situation chaotic. As a
businessman, Mercury will tend to work as a solo player due to
arguments and disagreements with business partners. This is a
good placement for lawyers. However, they need to refrain from
getting into arguments and being cheated by siblings. Mercury
represents our nervous system, and in the house of disease, your

nervous system can breakdown due to arguments, debts, and being
betrayed by others.
Mercury in the 6th House aspects the 12th House. Since Mercury
is tied to conflict and taking care of other people’s health, it is
drawn to the peace and quiet of isolated places. They are
comfortable curing people inside hospitals and even jails. They
need to escape into fantasy or foreign places to get away from their
troubles. This can make for a great author who uses obstacles in
life to create a story that takes characters into isolated places.
There is also a need to communicate on behalf of unprivileged
people who may face poverty. They have the desire to speak about
the harsh conditions in society, and their messages spread to
foreign lands.

## Mercury aspect on the 1st House:

Mercury in the 7th House aspects the 1st House of self,
personality, body, and health. Mercury in 7th House becomes a
mediator of agreements, business partnerships, and other people.
There is tremendous communication with business partners and
their spouse. In marriage, they need lot of intellectual stimulation

and a deep friendship, which keeps them on their toes! Their
spouse can be a manager, writer, or teacher. This placement can be
very sexual if Mercury is in the sign of Scorpio or Gemini. It is
one of the best placements for business, trade, and communication
with the masses. Mercury in the 7th House can also produce an
attorney, since it requires good communication and negotiating
skills. They are very diplomatic towards other people and are
comfortable making friends with strangers.
Mercury in the 7th House aspects the self, which is the 1st House.
One's personality is dedicated towards other people. Their whole
life becomes about business and trade, and their spouse will also
become part of their business life. The native is charming and
talkative. Mercury in the 7th House makes one curious about their
own persona. They see themselves through others and their legal
matters. The native's personality is very flirtatious, talkative, and
business-like.

## Mercury aspect on the 2nd House:

Mercury in the 8th House aspects the 2nd House of family, wealth,
speech, food, assets, and ancestry. Mercury in the 8th House

becomes the messenger of secrets, occult knowledge, assets after
marriage, spouse’s family, and bookkeeper. The intellect of the
native is focused on discovering hidden secrets of the world,
government, or their own personality. The 8th House is about
fluctuations; it can cause a rift with siblings (especially younger
ones), and friends. Mercury is an excellent indicator for wealth in
the 8th House. They may gain a tremendous inheritance, or profit
from joint assets with their spouse. As a natural manager and
bookkeeper, Mercury becomes a tax agent for the government or a
financial advisor. They may write about ghosts, aliens, or the
occult world if in an air or water sign.
Mercury from the 8th House aspects the 2nd House. Mercury
makes the native curious about family. They want to experience
transformation through familial secrets, and they know how to
manage assets. Information is exchanged between family members
about secrets and the occult that are intellectually stimulating for
the native. They tend to communicate with family on regular basis.
If Mercury is debilitated, they may experience anxiety from
sudden fluctuations. Their intelligence will suffer due to
unnecessary fear of the unknown.

## Mercury aspect on the 3rd house:

Mercury in the 9th House aspects the 3rd House of initiative,
willpower, communication, media, hobbies, skills, art, and
marketing. Mercury in the 9th House is about higher knowledge
and philosophy. It is a very logical and analytical planet that wants
to create structure in the house of higher learning. He wants to
organize all religious scriptures and put them in compartments.
They have superior communication when it comes to preaching.
They are easily able to sell the word of God to people through their
quick and witty thinking. However, Mercury doesn't realize the
true nature of higher learning, which takes place in the presence of
the divine guru. It thinks the oration of scriptures is what defines
higher philosophy, and this may make a person a bit narrow
minded about religion and culture. This placement is great for a
PhD, but it may not be the right fit for a theologian without the
aspect or conjunction of Jupiter or Venus.
Mercury from the 9th House aspects the 3rd House. Mercury, the
businessman, uses the advantage of higher knowledge and
philosophy to direct his siblings and close friends towards his
value. Through their higher knowledge, they can willfully gain

through business and marketing. The 3rd House of media,
journalism, crafts, and hobbies make Mercury put a religious and
philosophical spin on art and reporting. One may become a media
savvy preacher or motivational speaker who uses the media to
share the word of God. Their speech and writing style has a touch
of divine word.

## Mercury aspect on the 4th House:

Mercury in the 10th House aspects the 4th House of home, mother,
land, maternal nourishment, childhood, and comforts. Mercury in
the 10th House of career does very well. This is a natural manager
or businessman. Depending upon which sign Mercury is placed in,
it will bring about communication. In earth signs, Mercury can
easily become an accountant, government administrator, financial
advisor, or supervisor. Mercury can become a writer in water or air
signs. In fire signs, Mercury is a dynamic communicator like an
actor or politician. Since Mercury is multidimensional, one can
become many things at once. They can be teachers, lawyers, or
even language experts. The organizational ability and attention to
detail makes one a very good secretary or public relations agent.
They are the most flexible when it comes to dealing with

professional friends and individuals. They have a pleasant
reputation unless Mercury is sitting with a malefic. Mercury shows
a lot of communication between the native and their father; the
father may be an entrepreneur or executive.
Mercury from the 10th House aspects the 4th House. Mercury's
professional communication and speech curiously transforms into
a focus on the home. Their mother may be a businesswoman or a
teacher. Status driven Mercury wants to make his home also his
workplace. They want to take government messages to the people
of their homeland. One may be a press secretary who updates
government workers with information. Their business-like
communication style fills their home, but at the same time, it's
energized with ideas. Many professional people gather at their
residence.

## Mercury aspect on the 5th House:

Mercury in the 11th House aspects the 5th House of entertainment,
education, children, creativity, romance, and ancient scriptures.
Mercury in the 11th House represents hopes and wishes, large
network organizations, and monetary gains. They may become

social magnets. Mercury finds it easy to communicate and connect
with others. It becomes the link in introducing two people; like a
matchmaker or business development strategist. There is a great
need to communicate with large organizations and corporations.
They make great motivational speaker in the financial arena. In the
house of global connections and science, Mercury wants to use its
intellect to better the world through economic and scientific
growth. Depending upon which sign Mercury is placed in, it will
either make a person think on a more economic or scientific level.
This is a great position for financial analysts and talent agents.
They think broadly even if they are close minded due to aspects or
sign placements.
Mercury from the 11th House aspects the 5th House. Through its
network, Mercury is able to gain and enjoy life. It wants to dive
into the world of creativity, playfulness, and romance. Mercury has
a lot of resources which it utilizes for studies, fun, games, and
love. Mercury is curious about gambling earnings in the stock
market, poker, and sports betting. This aspect not only makes the
person social, but also charming and artistic. They use creativity to
engage with large organizations.

## Mercury aspect on 6th House:

Mercury in the 12th House aspects the 6th House of debts,
enemies, diseases, litigation, disagreements, small animals, and
unprivileged people. Mercury in the 12th House of imagination,
spirituality, foreign lands, losses, jails, and hospitals uses its
intellect and analytical gifts to bring logic to an isolated world.
Mercury in the 12th makes one an intuitive and imaginative
thinker. The ideas are initially abstract, but Mercury makes them
tangible. Mercury, the communicator and storyteller, loves to
create spiritual concepts and tales of other realms. This placement
is ideal for novelist and writers. Their intellect is aided by other
dimensions if they concentrate and meditate. This can make one a
communicator of spirituality, meditation, yoga, and foreign affairs.
Mercury becomes the mediator between the modern world and
the hidden world. This can make the native a loner; lost in their
own world of imagination and thoughts.
Mercury from the 12th House aspects the 6th House. Through the
process of imagination and spiritual healing, Mercury wants to aid
people of lower realms of the 6th House. It wants to resolve
conflicts by providing a bite of spiritual knowledge and words of

wisdom. Mercury becomes curious about helping people who can't
help themselves. It wants to become a beacon of hope for those
who are diseased. In the sign of Virgo, Mercury can show a
spiritual healer or doctor. They may suffer an intellectual setback
due to their imagination and desire to be involved with those who
struggle with mental disturbances. The 12th House of imagination
wants to create a story of tremendous obstacles.

## Jupiter Aspects Through Houses:

Jupiter is the planet of divine grace, optimism, knowledge, true
intelligence, philosophy, preachers, quality, wealth, and children.
When Jupiter aspects, it tries to put sugar over salt; it wants to
show the brighter side of life. This can be both positive and
negative. Jupiter can make the path easy on difficult houses, but at
the same time hide the absolute truth. Jupiter's ability to expand
positive quality brings about extreme optimism that can also be
damaging with an aspect. Jupiter aspects the 5th, 7th, and 9th place
from wherever it sits.

## Jupiter aspect on the 5th, 7th and 9th House:

Jupiter in the 1st House aspects the 5th, 7th, and 9th House. Jupiter
in the 1st House of physical body, personality, and life force
makes a person a natural teacher and preacher. People with Jupiter
in the 1st House have a natural inclination towards education,
religion, rituals, and philosophy. They have a very soothing and
pleasant nature. They have a divine personality that wants to help
educate others about the true meaning of life. These folks make
natural lawyers, professors, and doctors. This is not just due to the
placement, but also the aspect of Jupiter on the most important
houses for a human being. The 1st, 5th and 9th Houses are known
as dharma bhava, meaning houses of wisdom. They show what we
should be doing in life instead of what we want to do. The female
finds her partner in a competitive environment linked to physical
activity like sports or the gym.
Jupiter in the 1st House aspects the 5th House from its 5th aspect.
Jupiter sends its optimistic and wise nature towards the house of
creativity, children, performing arts, and political affairs. Jupiter,
the natural karaka of education, brings about love for the 5th
House, and promises children and education in one's life. These
people want to become a guide for their children. When it comes
to creativity, there is always a hint of philosophy and religion
attached to it. The native possesses a lot of confidence because
Jupiter expands the 1st House of personality and ego. However, a
debilitated Jupiter can make a person overconfident and egoistic;
where their lack of wisdom would cloud their judgment.
Jupiter from the 1st House aspects the 7th House of marriage, legal
partnerships, and other people. Jupiter blesses the native with
marriage, and the spouse becomes a source of optimism, wealth,
and knowledge. The spouse would be religious, unless Jupiter is
debilitated; then the spouse may be egocentric. Legal partnerships
and marriage will be overly hopeful; which can be damaging when
Saturn kicks in. The native will become a guide for their spouse,
and a source of divine knowledge. Interaction with other people

will be very pleasant, and there is support from the court system as
well.
Jupiter from the 1st House aspects the 5th House with its 5th
aspect. Jupiter brings about tremendous hope for one's education,
and it becomes a source of passion. Children are promised through
the divine grace of the mahadasha of Jupiter, and the native will be
their guru. Creativity and romance also expand towards people of
equal intelligence. There is a spiritual or religious touch in one's
creative pursuits, and an interest in reading about ancient scriptures
and ancestry. One’s past life deeds were very good in order to have
the aspect of Jupiter on the pruva punya bhava, or the house of past
life deeds.
Jupiter from the 1st House aspects the 9th House with its 9th
aspect. Jupiter brings about tremendous joy when the native
follows higher knowledge, religion, and rituals. They respect their
gurus tremendously, especially if the person is born in the hora of
the Moon. They have a thirst for philosophy and religion. Their
father was equal to a sage to them. The teachings of the father
made them who they are; unless the chart shows absence of the
father or his teachings. In such cases, another fatherly figure would
have taken the biological father's place. This aspect pushes the
native towards advanced degrees.

## Jupiter aspect on 6th, 8th and 10th House:

When Jupiter is in the 2nd House, it aspects the 6th, 8th, and 10th
House. Jupiter in the 2nd House makes one a natural speaker with
a focus on spirituality. They have a divine, soothing voice which
helps them create wealth. The 2nd House represents speech, food,
family, wealth, and assets. They not only love collecting valuables,
but they want to gain knowledge of all things that can be valued;
from money, gems, and jewelry, to history, culture, and art.
Jupiter, the natural giver of knowledge, provides a keen interest in
creating and saving money. One has a strong inclination towards
classical singing and music. They also enjoy writing, art, and
poetry, which increases their spiritual value. This placement makes
a natural storyteller of historical events and the ancient past.
However, overly optimistic Jupiter makes a person naive towards
wealth and pleasure. One thinks that wealth is always there no
matter what the circumstance. Jupiter makes one feel they are
wealthy even if they have a dollar in their pocket. Such illusions
keep the person away from working hard in life. The female meets
her husband through familial ties and connections.

Jupiter in the 2nd House aspects the 6th House with its 5th aspect.
This aspect brings about benefits from enemies, debt, and fewer
obstacles for the native. Even if the person is living below their
means, they will not be hindered by people creating obstacles for
them. However, such an aspect makes a person overly optimistic
about getting out of debt, which is usually a trap. The native thinks
he or she can repay any loan possible, and usually ends up in
bankruptcy. This is due to the 2nd House being a marka house, or
death inflicting house. Although Jupiter provides protection, there
isn't much help provided in the mahadasha of Jupiter regarding
wealth. However, one has great protection from suffering any
serious diseases unless Jupiter is debilitated. There is love for
small animals and pets, especially dogs. One is prone to leave their
education behind due to lack of interest in the structure of school
and obtaining a degree.
Jupiter in the 2nd House aspects the 8th House with its 7th aspect.
This aspect provides great benefits from in-laws and joint assets;
unless Saturn or Ketu are involved in either of the houses. The
karaka of knowledge makes a person fall into the world of the
occult and secrets, which becomes the basis of their education
throughout life. They feel a great sense of power and optimism
when knowing the secrets of the world versus the common man.
One is protected from sudden accidents and surgeries, and blessed
with a long life. But since the aspect is coming from a maraka
house to the maraka which is 8th House, there is always a sense of
a dark cloud looming when dealing with their family or their
spouse’s family. This is a great aspect for someone to become an
astrologer.
Jupiter from the 2nd House aspects the 10th House of career,
status, and father. This aspect amplifies the hope for a good career
and reputation. One will be inclined towards a career of finance or
teaching. In the house of family, one is also strongly indicated to
take over the family business, or carry on the profession of one's
father. However, one of the best career paths would be a

motivational public speaker who focuses on life and wealth
management. If Jupiter is not affiliated, then the relationship with
the father is very good, but one has to see the condition of the Sun
and the 10th Lord placement as well.

## Jupiter aspect on 7th, 9th and 11th House:

Jupiter in the 3rd House aspects the 7th, 9th and 11th House. The
3rd House is about media, art, journalism, initiative, discomfort,
willpower, short distance travel, and close neighbors. Jupiter, the
teacher and giver of knowledge, provides all types of education to
the native through interacting with siblings and close neighbors. It
expands communication and organization skills at work. In the
house of skills and hobbies, Jupiter brings about educating others.
This is a very cooperative individual who relies on others’ effort to
create a project. Jupiter brings hope in one's effort. It also brings
the skills of writing, speaking, and communicating. There is lot of
faith in the native's words. Their marketing and selling style
becomes philosophical.
Jupiter in the 3rd House aspects the 7th House with its 5th aspect.
The 7th House is about marriage, legal bindings, partnerships,

business, trade, and other people. A female may meet her husband
through close neighbors, siblings, or even through an artistic
environment. Jupiter provides a very business minded spouse who
is a gifted communicator. The spouse becomes a source of
philosophical and entrepreneurial discussion. They enjoy teaching
their spouse new things on a regular basis. There will be the least
amount of effort needed to make the marriage work. This
placement of Jupiter shows a person who has mastered the art of
diplomacy.
Jupiter in the 3rd House aspects the 9th House from its 7th aspect.
The 9th House is about higher learning, philosophy, religion,
rituals, and long distance travel. Jupiter brings about great
optimism when it comes to education and improving one's skills in
communication, marketing, and sales. It may not make a person a
PhD, but they might pursue an MBA. The 3rd House is a more
realistic placement for Jupiter, so the native is more realistic about
what their knowledge and education means. This aspect motivates
them to attain higher knowledge, and improve their teamwork and
administrative skills.
Jupiter in the 3rd House aspects the 11th House with its 9th aspect.
The 11th House is about gains of all kinds; professional, network
circles, and dreams. Jupiter in the 3rd House of teamwork and
close friends brings the native in contact with highly spiritual,
educated, and motivated individuals who help the native improve
their projects and communication skills. Jupiter brings a lot of help
from elder siblings and network circles. One also gains higher
knowledge through their friends. The native can learn a lot when
interacting with people from a corporate environment. They gain
by taking initiative, and running their own business with a solid
team.

## Jupiter aspect on the 8th, 10th and 12th House:

Jupiter in the 4th House aspects the 8th, 10th and 12th House.
Jupiter is very comfortable in the 4th House of mother,
nourishment, early education, land, home, and conveniences.
Jupiter brings optimism and love within the home. The mother is
not only a source of affection, but also education and wisdom.
Jupiter provides a sizable home to the native while growing up. Its
mahadasha also provides the native with his own residence as an
adult. Home base is a deep rooted place where the native finds
spiritual bliss. Even after marriage, the home will be blessed with
peace and harmony. Originally, Jupiter is exalted when in the sign
of Cancer, which rules the 4th House. The mother is a very
religious figure who may also be a schoolteacher or professor. In
order to see the relationship with the mother, the Moon's condition
has to be evaluated, because the Moon controls 85% of the
outcome. This placement also attaches one to their homeland. A
female will meet her husband in a home environment like the
kitchen, living room, backyard, or any place that is like home.
Jupiter in the 4th House aspects the 8th House of sudden events,
joint assets, taxes, occult, secrets, and surgeries. Jupiter provides

protection, and one's domestic knowledge extends into learning
about the occult and medicine. Jupiter aspects bring a creative flow
towards the unknown; even if they are sour, Jupiter keeps the
person optimistic. Jupiter doesn't deny difficult times, but it
provides a natural healing light. The native is also very curious to
learn about the secrets of the world. They feel occult knowledge is
a way to understanding oneself. This placement also gives
spiritual, religious, or philosophical in-laws.
Jupiter in the 4th House aspects the 10th House with its 7th aspect.
The 10th House is the house of status, father, government, and
fluctuations in career. Jupiter brings a very positive professional
reputation to the native. They are natural teachers, and make good
university professors who actually nurture their students. They
have knowledge of domestic life and real estate, which may drive
them towards selling property with passion. Providing security to
strangers is a way of life. The aspect on the 10th House also brings
an easy and educational work environment. In this respect, the
native makes the most of their potential.
Jupiter in the 4th House also aspects the 12th House with its 9th
House aspect. The 12th House represents the psychic world,
imagination, intuition, foreign lands, spiritual, and isolated places.
This aspect of Jupiter craves to make their home a spiritual
sanctuary. They appreciate meditation, and through a secure home
life, they are able to pursue a spiritual practice. Home becomes an
oasis. Jupiter becomes a psychologist, or a guide for people in jails
and hospitals. The native enjoys travel. Jupiter wants to reject
religion and follow the true essence of humankind; the soul. They
want to connect with beings of other dimensions and gain
knowledge through them; beyond religion and rituals. These
natives are able to tap into their wisdom in the 12th House of
hidden talents.

## Jupiter aspect on 9th, 11th and 2nd House:

Jupiter in the 5th House aspects the 9th, 11th, and 1st House. The
5th House represents children, creativity, performing arts,
gambling, politics, and education. Jupiter, the great benefactor,
provides optimism towards education and creativity. The creative
part of one's life will be very philosophical and spiritual in nature.
Their creativity is geared towards achieving academic success.
Jupiter, the natural teacher and educator, tends to provide one with
knowledge by interacting with children, diving into performing
arts, and indulging in studies. Although the 5th House is a great
place for education and creativity, it tends to delay children for the
native. This is due to Jupiter's overly optimistic quality with
regards to childbearing, and a lack of effort towards development.
One tends to be overconfident about having children without
trying. This person is a great follower of religion, philosophy, and
guru’s teachings. It is a great placement for stockbrokers and
financial advisors. They are creative financial pundits who know
how attain wealth. A female will meet her husband in an
entertainment party setting, political arena, academic environment,
or at a stage play.

Jupiter in the 5th House aspects the 9th House from its 5th aspect.
Jupiter’s aspect brings enthusiasm in achieving an advanced
academic education. Jupiter's aspect upon the house that it
naturally controls makes the native respect his teachers and father.
Jupiter also brings opportunities to study abroad for graduate work.
The father will be like a guru, and provide wisdom while the
native is growing up. If the biological father is not present, then a
fatherly figure will appear.
Jupiter in the 5th House aspects the 11th House of gains, network
circles, elder siblings, and large organizations. Jupiter uses its
financial skills in the stock market to bring about gains. Through
their network circles, they expand their speculative wealth. This
aspect provides a large number of friends who assist with guiding
the native towards achieving their goals. Jupiter gives political
ambitions, and they help organize large fundraisers for this
purpose. Through performing arts, they also meet people who help
them with artistic pursuits. This is one of the best positions for
financial gains with Jupiter. The native will amass wealth through
teaching, financial management, and psychology.
Jupiter in the 5th House aspects the ascendant or 1st House with its
9th aspect. The 1st House is about the self, personality, health and
vitality. Jupiter gives the native a very pleasant and intelligent
personality. One becomes a teacher even in their personal life.
Education and children will be the core of their existence and
personality. They love classical music, movies, and art. Life
becomes about maintaining financial balance and philosophical
learning. These people follow a set of principles to accomplish
their goals. They are very generous and give sound advice, which
makes them the best counselors.

## Jupiter aspect on the 10th, 12th and 2nd House:

Jupiter in the 6th House aspects the 10th, 12th and 2nd House. The
6th House is known as one of the three evil houses. It represents
diseases, enemies, debts, obstacles, and divorces. Jupiter tries to
look at the bright side, but is forced to think rationally rather than
philosophically. Jupiter wants to preach his way through dealing
with enemies and obstacles. This placement is mostly seen with
lawyers and accountants. Jupiter is overly optimistic about relying
on faith to survive difficult times. This placement can bring about
huge debts, as the native would believe faith and God will repay
them. The stress of education, children, and debt can also harm the
health of the native, especially the liver. However, Jupiter provides
love of pets and small animals. If Jupiter is exalted, or in his own
sign of Sagittarius or Pisces, no enemy would be able to harm
them, and they will make money through their rivals. They have
trouble seeking spiritual guidance of any kind as they fear being
cheated. The native gains knowledge by struggling and dealing
with adversity. We find our divine education wherever Jupiter is
placed. A female will meet her husband in a courthouse, hospital,
non-profit organization, or charity event.

Jupiter in the 6th House aspects the 10th House of career and
reputation with its 5th aspect. Jupiter aspects give a strong
inclination towards the service industry. These people make great
government workers, lawyers, social workers, and non-profit
leaders. The reputation of the native is well perceived in society
unless Jupiter is debilitated. In such cases, the native will be
known as someone who starts arguments and fights to get ahead in
life. This aspect gives a strong inclination towards being a
government servant like a lawyer or guidance counselor for
Sagittarius, Virgo, Scorpio, and Taurus ascendant. One makes a
living through debates, dealing with disagreements, or non-profit
social groups.
Jupiter in the 6th House aspects the 12th House with its 7th aspect.
One benefits in foreign lands when dealing with enemies and debt.
There are spiritual lessons learned through enemies, diseases, and
obstacles. After going through many hurdles, they start preaching
and bring optimism to others. Providing words of wisdom or
helping hands leads them to find enlightenment and peace.
Jupiter’s aspect on the 12th House shows that spirituality is
earned by overcoming obstacles in the world. Only after dealing
with the negative side of life does one find peace, spirituality, and
abundance in foreign places. Such lessons are realized at the age of
45. Jupiter becomes a guide for people in jails and asylums. He
reassures them that it's not the end; it's only the beginning.
Jupiter in the 6th House aspects the 2nd House of wealth and
family through its 9th aspect. Jupiter provides wealth through
conflicts, dealing with enemies, and financial management. One
learns their higher purpose and knowledge of wealth by diving into
the world of disagreements and resolving them. They appreciate
this ability since the 2nd House represents what we value. This
aspect makes the native aware of family values and culture when
dealing with the harshness of the world. Their speech is bold,
brave, and determined to fix family issues, debts, and family
business matters.

## Jupiter aspect on the 11th, 1st and 3rd House:

Jupiter in the 7th House aspects the 11th, 1st and 3rd House. The
7th House represents marriage, legal business partnerships, and
trade. The 7th House treats people like strangers, and is also one of
the maraka houses, or death inflicting houses. Usually, it's not
recommended to have any planets in this house except Saturn due
to his directional strength. Jupiter is a natural giver of optimism,
healing, and faith. Jupiter is how we gain divine knowledge. In the
7th House, the native looks for a spouse to teach them the
philosophy of the world. They look for faith and optimism through
their spouse, which can be good and bad. When Jupiter is the 1st
House, one may be dependent upon the knowledge of others, while
not being able to learn on their own. Marriage becomes too
cultured and full of rituals. This can lead to lack of freedom. A
female will meet her husband through an arranged marriage or
through court.
Jupiter in the 7th House aspects the 11th House of gains, network
circles, friends, and wishes with its 5th aspect. This aspect shows
that through legal partnership and marriage, one will acquire

wealth and increase their network circles. This will enable them to
climb the ladder of success. Marriage fulfills their hopes, even
more so if Jupiter is the Lord of 11th House in the 7th House. They
start having faith in their friends and network circles after
marriage. However, they rely on their spouse and other people for
guidance. Their true education comes from wheeling, dealing, and
trading with people. This makes them blossom in business and
joint partnerships.
Jupiter in the 7th House aspects the 1st House of self, personality,
and health with its 7th aspect. Optimism is felt when other people
are praising and putting faith into the native. They have good
health and protection from diseases unless Jupiter is debilitated. If
so, one will suffer from diabetes, liver, and obesity problems. This
aspect also makes them a good advisor or teacher of marriage and
business due to their own experiences.
Jupiter in the 7th House aspects the 3rd House of siblings, media,
journalism, marketing, sales, hobbies, skills, initiative, and
communication with its 9th aspect. One will be skilled in the art of
dealing with people and doing business. Jupiter, in the house of
spouse and joint ventures, brings communication regarding the
benefit of marriage and legal bindings. One's siblings benefit with
the native's marriage, and the native finds higher philosophical
knowledge to share with their close friends and neighbors. Jupiter's
aspect shows that there is great optimism and faith in taking
initiative, leading to positive outcomes. Such willpower and ability
to put forth effort comes with the help of a spouse or team. Jupiter
makes one inspired working in a group environment.

## Jupiter aspect on 12th, 2nd and 4th house:

Jupiter in the 8th House aspects the 12th, 2nd and 4th House. The
8th House represents secrets, occult knowledge, death, rebirth,
surgeries, chronic diseases, taxes, joint assets with spouse, in-laws
and inheritance. Jupiter in the 8th House brings a natural interest
in occult knowledge and science. There is faith in keeping secrets,
and one tends to think optimistically about extramarital affairs.
Jupiter brings about an abundance of secret wealth and joint assets
hidden from the world through their spouse. The native will seek
teachers in dark, secretive places like a photo lab. They want a
widowed or divorced spouse because an experienced person
knows the value of life, death, and relationships. One also gives up
religion due to past life deeds and takes on occult knowledge and
sexually oriented values. A female will meet her husband in a
secretive setting without the world ever knowing. There will be
many sexual encounters with the potential husband before making
any kind of a bond.
Jupiter in the 8th House aspects the 12th House with its 5th aspect.
The 12th House represents spirituality, lucid imagination, sexual
pleasures, foreign lands, jails, hospitals, and monasteries. Jupiter

from the 8th House shows that one that likes to keep and expose
secrets. There is interest in learning about spirituality through
occult tantric practices. One finds their true guru in foreign lands
or isolated places far away from home. They seek spiritual
guidance by relying on secret information. They keep their
spiritual knowledge to themselves. There is a sudden move to
foreign places in the mahadasha of Jupiter. They covertly visit
hospitals and jails to guide other people, or become psychiatrists.
Jupiter in the 8th House aspects the 2nd House with its 7th aspect.
The 2nd House is about family, culture, family lineage, assets, and
wealth. The 2nd House also represents food and speech. Jupiter's
aspect shows that one will reap the benefits of inheritance through
family. Although there are many family secrets, if Jupiter is not
debilitated, those secrets are somewhat of an asset. Otherwise, a
debilitated Jupiter could show physical or verbal abuse through
family and in-laws. Family becomes a source of true knowledge
and transformation in the native's life, especially in Jupiter's
mahadasha. One has a very mature and pleasant voice, and they
benefit through hidden family resources.
Jupiter in the 8th House also aspects the 4th House with its 9th
aspect. The 4th House is the house of home, mother, mother's
nourishment, vehicles, land, and convenience in life. Jupiter’s
aspect on the 4th House can bring sudden gains of property and all
types of conveniences. However, it can also bring a sudden loss of
property if it's a more malefic than benefic aspect on Jupiter, or
Jupiter is being debilitated. This aspect gives a very optimistic
environment at home, and the mother can be a source of wisdom,
secrets, and occult education.

## Jupiter aspect on the 1st, 3rd and 5th House:

Jupiter in the 9th House aspects the 1st, 3rd and 5th House. The
9th House is the original house of Jupiter, and it represents
everything Jupiter stands for. It is the house of faith, higher
learning, and religious texts. The native may travel long distances
for their education. It's also the house of luck and feeling blessed
regardless of one’s financial situation. Jupiter feels comfortable
and at home. This placement makes one virtuous, and the native
has an amazing relationship with their father, gurus, and teachers.
However, they can be obsessed with religion, rituals, and
scriptures. They have high expectations of people and themselves.
Jupiter is a natural teacher and preacher, and in the house of God,
the native becomes a natural preacher as well; unless Jupiter is in
Pisces. In such cases, one will preach the word of spirituality,
peace, and imagination. A debilitated Jupiter can bring about a
priest who might cheat others in the name of God, or attain higher
knowledge
through
manipulation,
blackmail,
or
political
connections. This is a perfect placement to become a professor.
The female will meet her husband in a religious setting or at
graduate school. Her husband might be a professor, preacher, or
lawyer.

Jupiter in the 9th House aspects the 1st House with its 5th aspect.
The 1st House represents self, personality, health, and vitality.
Jupiter's aspect brings about a high sense of value for oneself. The
native is a natural preacher and teacher, and they feel that life is
not complete without higher education. However, in the sign of
Pisces, it can deter a person from a higher education. The father or
religious guru shapes the personality of the native more than any
other person unless other planets aspect the ascendant through
different houses. One feels very fortunate in life no matter what the
circumstance. The 9th House is also the house of law, and Jupiter
can make the native a lawyer or judge.
Jupiter in the 9th House aspects the 3rd House with its 7th aspect.
The 3rd House represents siblings, close neighbors, skills, efforts,
hobbies, media, sales, marketing, and journalism. Jupiter’s aspect
makes one a natural teacher for their younger siblings. They try to
preach the word of God and wisdom to their close friends,
neighbors, and siblings. They share the voice of God through the
help of media, sales, and marketing. This can help other people,
especially in the native's local community. The siblings will be
fortunate, and become a source of luck and knowledge. There is
optimism in teamwork and interacting with members of the local
community. They make great writers, and their work has an
undercurrent of religious philosophy.
Jupiter in the 9th House also aspects the 5th House with its 9th
aspect. This aspect upon the 5th House of children, education,
creativity, romance, and politics promises the fulfillment of such
desires. One has many children and academic achievements in the
mahadasha of Jupiter. They find creativity in a religious and
spiritual form, and love exploring their performing ability through
the word of God. This placement brings a natural passion for
education and classic creativity. They become a fatherly figure and
guru to their children. This aspect also gives a strong inclination

towards teaching in an academic environment since the 9th House
is 9th from the 9th.

## Jupiter aspect on 2nd, 4th and 6th House:

Jupiter in the 10th House aspects the 2nd, 4th, and 6th House. The
10th House is the house of status, career, reputation, father, and
government. This position of Jupiter promises a very good
reputation in the professional sphere and in the community. Jupiter
is a natural teacher and preacher. In the 10th House, one tends to
provide philosophical wisdom to their co-workers, and they may
look at their career with spiritual eyes. This pattern can hurt the
native more than help because logic, organization, and structure
are needed in this area of life. Jupiter's nature to share and provide
service can lead them to working in the government sector or
charities where they can help others. This position can make for a
senior executive in government, finance, or education. In the
natural house of career, Jupiter can create a path of teaching and
preaching. Overall, Jupiter is uncomfortable here. Jupiter is a
divine teacher while the 10th House represents rules, politics,
government, battlefields, and jungles; not an ideal place for Jupiter
to be present. This can make the native unsatisfied with their
