career unless Jupiter can provide an educational or religious
setting. Even a counselor or psychologist can be a satisfying career
choice. The native has their own belief system and higher
philosophy which can cause a problem professionally. A female
would find her husband in a professional setting.
Jupiter in the 10th House aspects the 2nd House with its 5th
aspect. The 2nd House is about family, wealth, assets, savings,
food, speech, and family business. Jupiter's aspect shows that one
provides optimism and wisdom to their family through their
education and professional field. They tend to take over the family
business, as wisdom of the father translates to their career. The
family is well respected in the community because of the father's
reputation. They learn the value of hard work through family. The
native is authoritative, and will try to make their family
conservative and law-abiding. The native’s words are full of
wisdom, and there is a sense of professionalism in their voice.
Jupiter in the 10th House aspects the 4th House with its 7th aspect.
The 4th House represents mother, childhood memories, land,
home, and all types of conveniences. Jupiter brings a great sense of
optimism within the home, which is all about education and
professionalism. One's mother will push the native to become a
scholar and have a respectable career. On the negative side, there
can be a lot of pressure from both parents to reach the highest level
of education. Aspect on the 4th House also shows that one is
interested in a home business surrounding education and religious
activities. The mother always served the father, but not in a
negative way. She looked upon the father as a guru, and wanted to
take care of him till the end.
Jupiter in the 10th House aspects the 6th House with its 9th aspect.
The 6th House represents enemies, debt, diseases, small animals,
everyday work life, and the unprivileged. Jupiter's aspect from the
10th House shows that one gravitates towards serving people and
small animals. They are optimistic about helping people through

non-profit organizations and fundraising. They are able to
understand their status in life by helping those less fortunate.
Jupiter is able to attain victory over enemies through its wisdom
and diplomatic approach. Jupiter is interested in bringing value to
disagreements, and helping parties resolve their issues. They feel
that helping people will allow them attain higher divine
knowledge. A debilitated Jupiter can make a person too optimistic
about dealing with enemies and debt. This can lead to huge
financial problem or sudden debt in the mahadasha of Jupiter.

## Jupiter aspect on 3rd, 5th and 9th House:

Jupiter in the 11th House aspects the 3rd, 5th, and 7th House. The
11th House represents large organizations, network circles, older
siblings, gains of all kinds, hopes, and wishes. This is a great place
for Jupiter in terms of giving tremendous gains in his mahadasha
since Jupiter expands the house it sits in. The native gets most of
their hopes and wishes fulfilled if Jupiter is exalted, or the
depositor of Jupiter is in kendra. This placement brings a large
network of professional people who help the native climb the
ladder of success. The ability to work with large organizations can
result in public speaking in religious, spiritual, or educational

settings. Jupiter provides gains in every area, including financial
wealth. In an air or fire sign, Jupiter can make a great actor,
especially through its aspects. A female will find her husband
through large parties or professional network circles.
Jupiter in the 11th House aspects the 3rd House from its 5th
aspect. The 3rd House represents younger siblings, media,
journalism, writing, communication, hobbies, initiative, and
willpower. Jupiter brings tremendous optimism from the end
results of their labor. The native’s bravery and communication
skills can result in gains through reporting, acting, and public
speaking. The native is mildly religious or philosophical unless
Jupiter is in Sagittarius or Pisces. Jupiter’s aspect brings great hope
and opportunity in the media and sales industry. They are smart
and charismatic at sales. They make great writers that focus on
large organizations and making the world a better place.
Jupiter in the 11th House aspects the 5th House with its 7th aspect.
The 5th House represents formal education, children, creativity,
performing arts, acting, political arenas, and speculative
businesses. Jupiter's aspect shows gains through speculative
business and network circles. The native gains through education
and their children. Artistic talent and performing on stage also
becomes an important part of the native's life, and help them fulfill
their dreams. Children bring optimism, joy, and happiness. The
native may choose to interact with children on a regular basis, like
a preschool teacher.
Jupiter in the 11th House aspects the 7th House with its 9th aspect.
The 7th House represents marriage, legal business partnerships,
spouse, other people, business, trade, and courthouses. Jupiter’s
aspect on the 7th House shows that one gains through marriage,
spouse, and legal business partnerships. One's spouse becomes a
source of friends, professional network circles, and helps the
native achieve their dreams. They often win and are rewarded

through court cases. The spouse is also a source of higher wisdom
and helps the native start an organization in Jupiter’s mahadasha.

## Jupiter aspect on 4th, 6th and 8th House:

Jupiter in the 12th House aspects the 4th, 6th, and 8th House. The
12th House is the house of spirituality, meditation, foreign
settlements, foreign lands, jails, hospitals, asylums, and
monasteries. Jupiter in the 12th House feels at home, though it’s
not; rather, it is more of a spiritual retreat for Jupiter. It goes to the
12th House to get away from the burden of religion, rituals, and
higher philosophical learning. It wants to connect with the soul in
the 12th House, which has nothing to do with the 9th House. The
12th House is the heart of the guru due to being 4th from the 9th
House. Jupiter really connects with teachers in this house. Jupiter
has a strong inclination towards spirituality, meditation, and
imagination. One becomes a natural spokesperson for spiritual
practice. Since the 12th House represents losses, there will be a
loss of things related to the house which Jupiter rules. This also
means a disinterest in rituals and religion, and higher academic
pursuits. But, there is a great enhancement in the imagination of
the native which can make them great writers and speakers. There

is a lot of foreign travel, and even visits to hospitals, especially in
12th and 8th Lord maha and antardasha. A female could meet her
husband in foreign lands or isolated places which are engulfed
with spirituality.
Jupiter in the 12th House aspects the 4th House with its 7th aspect.
The 4th House represents mother, homeland, physical home,
childhood, and all types of conveniences. Jupiter's aspect from the
12th House may bring a loss of identity within the home even
though the environment will be conventional and spiritual. The
native will be away from the mother often due to foreign travel,
and she will be an extremely spiritual person. Jupiter brings the
knowledge of dreams and imagination through the mother. Her
nourishment is classic and creative. The true knowledge of how to
manage domestic life will be learned through spiritual practice,
finding a guru in foreign lands, and through imagination.
Jupiter in the 12th House aspects the 6th House with its 7th aspect.
This aspect can bring about a healing quality towards others. One
wants to heal and nourish people through spiritual knowledge and
access to other worlds. They find themselves teaching and sharing
knowledge to the unprivileged in isolated settings. They are
optimistic about healing and curing diseases through natural and
alternative medicine. This position can also trigger diseases related
to Jupiter during its mahadasha. One can end up in a hospital due
to this aspect, especially if Mars has an aspect on Jupiter from a
bad position. One may also incur debt due to foreign travel,
illnesses, and education. Overall, Jupiter is always a saving grace,
even during illness. Its natural significance is benefic, which is
always active.
Jupiter in the 12th House aspects the 8th House of sudden
transformation, fluctuations, joint assets, death, rebirth, and occult
knowledge. Jupiter's aspect from the 12th House brings sudden
transformations in spirituality. In a female's chart, this can show
sudden marriage or separation from the husband due to foreign

opportunities. Jupiter gives protection from accidents, but can land
a person in the hospital during its mahadasha if there is a malefic
aspect on Jupiter. The native is not really interested in the occult,
but the secret knowledge that can transform them into a more
spiritual being. One will reap great benefits from in-laws if
married in foreign lands. They can suddenly move to foreign
places for education if Jupiter rules the 5th or 9th House.

## Venus Aspects Through Houses:

Venus is the planet of love, relationships, beauty, art, and luxury.
Venus is how we express ourselves in a relationship. The way we
surround ourselves with material wealth is also seen through
Venus. It is the karaka of marriage for both men and women, but
for a man it also shows the personality and profession of their
spouse, including how they will meet. Venus is what we desire in
life. Its aspect shows the result of our desires and unions with other
people. Venus only aspects the 7th place from itself.

## Venus aspect on 7th House:

Venus in the 1st House aspects the 7th House of marriage, spouse,
legal partnerships, and dealing with others. The 1st House is about
self, personality, health, and ego. Venus in the 1st House gives one
the desire to look beautiful for themselves and society. Outside of
beauty, they also love finance, luxury, and fine foods. They use
their body to perform artistic acts like dancing, acting, and
modeling. Usually, they are self-absorbed. Regardless of sex, the
native loves to be admired and adored by others. This is more
prominent in a female's chart. The man is confident enough to
meet his wife on his own. For example, walking up to a woman in
a gym, at a beauty contest, or any physical activity is not a
problem. They don't need assistance from other people.
Venus from the 1st House aspects the 7th House. This aspect
brings tremendous desire and passion to connect with others to
find love and relationships. Venus in the house of self makes one
wants to reach out to others to feel desired and wanted. It makes
the native, especially a man, need a relationship to feel confident
and at ease. They seek a spouse who is beautiful, and who
increases their attractiveness when together. This aspect is about
caring for and providing luxury to others. The native is sensual,
and wants to fulfill their partner’s needs. They search for a partner
to feel admired and desired, which can make a relationship
difficult, as this is a two way street.

## Venus aspect on 8th House:

Venus in the 2nd House aspects the 8th House of joint assets, in-
laws, occult, death, rebirth, transformation, sudden events,
surgeries, and taxes. Venus feels very comfortable in the 2nd
house of family, home, food, wealth, family assets, valuables,
speech, and family businesses. They enjoy a close knit family
environment, and they find pleasure in domestic life. The native's
voice is very pleasant, soothing, and perhaps even feminine. There
is a natural flair for singing. One loves collecting jewelry,
gemstones, and other valuables. When it comes to food, they love
sweets. This placement can make someone overly extravagant,
where money is wasted on useless art, antique items, and cultural
decorations. Venus does not feel the consequences of its actions as
long as it possesses valuables. A woman with this placement can
have high standards when it comes to her lifestyle, even after
marriage. A man will meet his wife in a family setting, or is
introduced through a family member.
Venus from the 2nd House aspects the 8th House. Venus in the
2nd House desires joint family assets and increased wealth through
marriage. The native feels that through family, it can conquer in-

laws because of the value brought to the table. There is also a
desire to negotiate with money and assets in a secretive manner.
Venus makes married life and relations with the spouse's family
very pleasant. Venus also finds pleasure through occult
knowledge. This may not make the native an astrologer or tantric
guru, but they feel that occult knowledge is important. Venus’
desire to gain wealth through valuables can take the native into
mining for gems and gold.

## Venus aspect on the 9th House:

Venus in the 3rd House of younger siblings, media, acting,
hobbies, initiative, journalism, and skills aspects the 9th House of
higher education, divine knowledge, teachings of guru and father,
religion, scripture, and long distance travel. Venus finds pleasure
in artistic talents like painting, sculpting, and acting. They enjoy
reporting and announcing events. Communication becomes their
passion. Venus brings great relationships among siblings, close
friends, and neighbors. This is also a great placement for having
skills in design and sewing. Venus brings a sensual look to
marketing and public relations work. The native can creatively

present any kind of story. A man will meet his wife through a
sibling, or an artistic or media related event.
Venus in 3rd House aspects the 9th House. This aspect shows
Venus' desire to use higher education and philosophical knowledge
to be more creative with one's skills in art and media. Venus from
the house of hobbies and artistic talent wants to learn religious text
and philosophy in an artistic manner. They want to make their
work of higher spiritual value. Venus gives the native an
inclination towards finding a guru or mentor to improve their
work. They also want to travel to foreign places to enhance their
artistic mind and improve upon their skills by learning about
different societies.

## Venus aspect on 10th House:

Venus in the 4th House aspects the 10th House of career, status,
reputation, and relationship with the father. Venus in the 4th House
has full directional strength since they both represent comfort and
luxury. The 4th House is also land, mother's nourishment, and the
native’s
childhood.
This
placement
brings
comfort
and
appreciation for art and beauty through one’s mother. They

discover creative talent and artistic skills through her presence, and
she may be into designing, modeling, or interior decorating. The
mother makes the native a beautiful person. There is great passion
for making home a sanctuary where relationships can develop and
flourish. A man could meet his wife in a domestic setting, through
his parents, or even art school.
Venus in the 4th House aspects the 10th House. This aspect makes
Venus want to utilize its creativity within a home setting and make
a career of it; like a real estate agent, landscape artist, or interior
decorator. One has a great need to form professional relationships
and climb the ladder of success; which generally happens unless
Venus is damaged. In this case, the aspect will make people insult
the native professionally. Venus’ aspect shows there is great
accommodation in the professional sphere, and beautiful
relationships are formed that allow one to excel and make home
a beautiful place of comfort.

## Venus aspect on 11th House:

Venus in the 5th House aspects the 11th House of gains, hopes,
network circles, large organizations, and increased authority. The

5th House represents creativity, academic achievement, children,
politics, and performing arts. Venus brings great passion and
interest in the arts, but specifically performing arts. Venus, the
natural actor, brings a talent and flair for the stage and seeks
attention and praise. There is tremendous desire for romance and
love as well. This position brings many romantic affairs which
inspire creativity and self-expression. Venus is not about
academics unless it's tied to creativity. Even in politics, there is a
desire to lead and be a leader. Such politicians stand out due to
their dramatic personalities. A man meets his wife in a creative
field, political arena, or at school.
Venus in the 5th House aspects the 11th House. This position gives
Venus the desire to connect with others in a large group setting
where their creative talent shines and helps them network up the
ladder of success. In the mahadasha of Venus, their wishes are
fulfilled by interacting with people and showing their creative
talent in large organizations. They want to change society and
make a difference through art. There are also monetary gains that
occur during this period.

## Venus aspect on the 12th House:

Venus in the 6th House aspects the 12th House of losses, foreign
lands, spirituality, meditation, imagination, jails, hospitals, and
monasteries. Venus in the 6th House does poorly; being the karaka
of relationships, there are constant disagreements with people,
especially with their spouse. The 6th House is about lower ranking
and unprivileged people. There is a natural desire to engage in
relationships with servants, janitors, trash collectors, or someone
living in extreme poverty. Venus wants to be a savior and show
such people the value of loving relationships. There is tremendous
love for animals, especially dogs. Such people find luxury by
being in the presence of medical professionals or those who are
willing to serve for a higher cause. Venus can make one follow the
path of medicine, healing, or acts of service. In marriage, the
native will suffer due to constant battles and arguments with the
spouse over silly things. The man will find his wife in a hospital,
non-profit organization, working with animals, or at a courthouse.
Venus in the 6th House aspects the 12th House. This aspect can
make one an alcoholic or drug abuser. They will want to escape
the battlefield and seek sexual pleasure, spirituality, and foreign
travel as way to cope with everyday obstacles. There is hardly ever
any luxury in the native's life. Even if they have everything, they
will not feel fortunate. This makes them overspend and get into
debt, since the 12th House represents expenses and losses.
Through the career choice of the 6th House, one frequently visits
hospitals and jails to heal others or provide medical assistance.
Venus' desire for food, drink, and sensuality can lead to diabetes
or sexually transmitted diseases, especially if Venus is debilitated
or in an enemy sign. Venus finds artistic talent by escaping into an
energized world of imagination in the 12th house. One is bound to
find many romantic loves in foreign lands and through travel.

## Venus aspect on the 1st House:

Venus in the 7th House aspects the 1st House or the ascendant,
which is the body, personality, health, ego, and vitality. The 7th
House represents marriage, legal partnerships, trade, business,
other people, and court. Venus is the karaka of the 7th House.
Venus brings tremendous pleasure through the spouse and all legal
partnerships. The spouse of the native will be very creative and
into designing, art, acting, or modeling. There is tremendous
growth in a man’s career after marriage, as the wife will promote
his status. Luxury is gained through signing contracts and making
agreements. For a woman, the husband will also be creative, and
may be a singer or designer unless there is an influence of another
planet upon Venus.
Venus in the 7th House aspects the 1st House. This gives the
native a desire to learn about themselves through their spouse.
Their personality is about beauty, luxury, and art. The male native
is influenced by others about women; how they should look, and
how they should behave. Women outside of the family play a huge
role in shaping the native's personality regardless of sex. The male
also will have feminine qualities. He enjoys looking his best, and

will constantly try to impress others with his physical beauty. He
may even go into fashion designing or modeling. This aspect is
mainly about the spouse’s tremendous influence.

## Venus aspect on the 2nd House:

Venus in the 8th House aspects the 2nd House of family, wealth,
savings, food, speech, culture, history and family lineage. The 8th
House represents occult knowledge, secrets, government taxes, in-
laws, sudden events, accidents, surgeries, and transformations like
reincarnation. Venus in the 8th House produces secret affairs and
relationships for the native throughout life. This is inescapable
with Venus. It’s about love, sexual relationships, and business.
They will have situations arise where business partnerships and
formal relationships would be executed in a secret manner. Venus
in the 8th House is like pouring honey in a gutter. Venus actually
does quite well in the 8th House by providing a solid relationship
and wealth through in-laws. A man would meet his wife in a very
secretive manner. However, the male native would gain wealth
through his wife's family. This placement of Venus makes a person
highly sexual and flirtatious. They are very secretive with romantic
relationships.

Venus in the 8th House aspects the 2nd House of family, wealth,
food, speech, and assets. This placement shows a desire to
accumulate family wealth through secret sources and private
dealings. They do not want to share their financial status or source
of wealth. Gains may come through dealings with the government,
underworld mob bosses, lottery, and assets of in-laws. One enjoys
the occult, and hopes to attain wealth and assets through their
belief. If Venus is in its own sign or exalted, then the native has
great healing power and the ability to transform lives. If Venus is
damaged, then they may suffer through in-laws and an abusive
married life. They will turn to their family for help. Childhood
rape and sexual assault by a family member is also seen through
this aspect if Venus is in Rahu or Mercury's nakshatra.

## Venus aspect on 3rd House:

Venus in the 9th House aspects the 3rd House of media, skills,
hobbies, sales, marketing, siblings, journalism, and initiative. The
9th House represents our higher self, gurus, teachers, father's
teachings, abiding the law, rituals, and religion. The 9th House is
about higher philosophical ideas. Venus in the 9th House gives one
great pleasure in religious knowledge and rituals. Venus makes

one sustain good relationships with religious and educational
figures. This is a great position for wealth through higher
education, religious activity, and teaching. The native is raised in a
religious family, especially if Venus is the ascendant Lord. Such
people find love in religious places and with religious figures.
They can fall in love with their guru, teachers, and meet their
spouse in an educational or religious setting. A man’s wife comes
from a very religious family. There is a natural love for rituals,
spirituality, and philosophy. Venus becomes more spiritual than
religious when it comes to advanced studies.
Venus in the 9th House aspects the 3rd House. Venus desires to
create art and music based on higher knowledge and philosophical
ideas. If the native becomes a designer, there would be a very high
value put on clothes, with philosophical and spiritual reason
behind the work. Whether they become painters or actors, they
will always want to create work of higher value with an
undercurrent of religion or philosophy.

## Venus aspect on 4th House:

Venus in the 10th House of career, status, reputation, government,
and father aspects the 4th House of home, mother, homeland, and
mother's nourishment. Venus in the 10th House takes pleasure in
climbing the ladder of corporate success. The native loves forming
relationships with professional people who bring luxury and
wealth to their lives. A man seeks a woman of status and forms a
relationship with them. A man generally finds his wife through his
profession. If Venus is placed in her own sign, or sign of Leo or
Gemini, the native may have a natural flair for cinema. Generally,
Venus leads to hotel management and graphic design. In the 10th
House of father, the native may discover his father is a creative
individual with some feminine qualities.
Venus in the 10th House aspects the 4th House. One's creativity in
the professional world translates into their domestic life. A
professional Venus aspect shows that the native’s wife will be
professional and domestic, and will enrich her home. The native’s
mother may be a creative person that invokes or provides artistic
talent within the native. Home is a beautiful place to live because
Venus desires to insulate itself from the strain of the outside world.

## Venus aspect on 5th House:

Venus in the 11th House aspects the 5th House of children,
schooling, creativity, performing arts, and romance. The 11th
House represents gains, network circles, marketplace, and large
corporations and organizations. The native has many female
friends due to Venus, and they help them achieve goals. Venus
being the karaka of luxury in the house of gains brings tremendous
wealth in life if Venus is in a good sign placement. A man meets
his wife in a large organized setting like corporate and political
events or seminars. This is one of the best placements of Venus
with regards to wealth.
Venus in the 11th House aspects the 5th House. This brings
creative ways to use network circles for gains. Through others, one
can find many opportunities in performing arts and working in
cinema. This placement also insures gains through children,
especially a female child. One tends to enjoy playing the stock
market and gambling. The native will have many romantic
encounters before setting down. There will be many opportunities
to find love through network circles.

## Venus aspect on 6th House:

Venus in the 12th House aspects the 6th House of debts, enemies,
diseases, small animals, disagreements, and obstacles. The 12th
House represents imagination, foreign lands, spirituality, and
isolated places like jails, hospitals, monasteries, and asylums.
Venus is originally exalted in the 12th House of Pisces. Without
knowing the sign placement, one can safely say that they find
pleasure in artistic imagination through yoga, meditation, and
visiting foreign lands. Such people love private and quiet places to
relax, eat, drink, and form relationships. A man finds his wife in
foreign lands or isolated places. There is tremendous artistic talent
that comes through astral imagination and connection with the
spirit world. They believe that everyone is their soul mate and love
needs to be universal and spiritual rather than physical and
material. A man may discover his wife is an enemy instead of his
fantasy woman because the 12th House is 6th from the 7th House,
and the 6th place from any house becomes an enemy of that house.
The 12th House is also about losses and expenses, and this
placement can bring monetary loss and expenses through one's
spouse, who might be a spend thrift.
Venus in the 12th House aspects the 6th House. This brings great
desire to help those dealing with obstacles and bad health by
providing healing and spiritual blessings. Venus may obtain
healing powers and want to work in hospitals and asylums to help
those who can't help themselves. When it comes to creativity, they
always add an element of sickness and health in their art. They
want to connect with people in poverty through non-profit
organizations. Divine spiritual energy transfers through a female to
give a helping hand to those less fortunate. Women with such a
placement are especially sensitive to psychic and intuitive powers
when serving society.

## Saturn Aspects Through Houses:

Saturn aspects the 3rd, 7th and 10th place from itself. Saturn
brings fear and anxiety within us because of its malefic nature.
Saturn is also a very straightforward, serious, and determined
planet. It makes us responsible and disciplined about work and
things around us. Saturn brings us hard work and delays. Whatever
house Saturn aspects, he brings frustration, delay, and fear.

## Saturn aspect on 3rd, 7th, and 10th House:

Saturn in the 1st House aspects the 3rd, 7th and 10th House. Saturn
in the first house of self, personality, and physical body brings
about responsibility and heavy burden from a young age. The
native feels a strong sense of duty towards their family and
themselves. They have incredible endurance to handle difficult
workloads. Saturn in the ascendant shows love for law and order,
and one who is striving to increase rank in government or the
corporate world. They utilize their physicality for gains and status;
like a car mechanic, athlete, medical doctor, or construction

worker. Saturn’s nature of fear and anxiety exists throughout the
native's life, especially during childhood. In the 1st House, there is
anxiety about appearance; whether it’s related to weight or looks.
They are always worried and critical of their physical self. Saturn
loves routine work in everyday life. The native may wear a
uniform that represents routine work in society. This person is
serious, and has natural leadership qualities.
Saturn in the 1st House aspects the 3rd House. The 3rd House
represents
creativity,
skills,
hobbies,
siblings,
teamwork,
willpower, and initiative. Saturn's aspect shows one will have to
overcome obstacles to gain success through taking initiative.
Artistic or media related skills display rigidness and seriousness.
There is darkness and maturity when it comes to artistic skills.
Hobbies are also routine. For example an actor may perform the
same type of role in every movie, or a painter may create the same
dark and dull design. They bring responsibility and structure when
working in a team environment as well.
Saturn in the 1st House aspects 7th House of marriage, legal
partnerships, and other people. The native has strict responsibilities
in the marriage; it is not a romantic movie but rather a legal soap
opera. The marriage lasts due to Saturn's nature to slow things
down and sustain them. This aspect also brings one who is
climbing the ladder of success through marriage. They will only
seek a partner who can provide wealth, status, and security.
Saturn in the 1st House also aspects the 10th House of career,
status, father, government, and reputation of the native. This aspect
shows the slow but steady growth in one's career and reputation.
Saturn, being the natural malefic, will draw too much criticism
from other people unless there is a positive aspect on 10th House
via a natural benefic planet. There is fear of taking a leadership
role because of criticism, yet one is forced into the spotlight,
whether it's being a government official, lawyer, judge, or doctor.

The native who pursues medicine will be more technical and
mechanical, like dentists, physical therapists, or surgeons.

## Saturn aspect on 4th, 8th, and 11th House:

Saturn in the 2nd House aspects the 4th, 7th and 11th House. In the
2nd House of family, wealth, speech, food and assets, Saturn
brings responsibility and organizational skills when it comes to
dealing with family affairs. The family environment would be very
serious and strict. The native may even grow up in poverty if there
are no aspects or yogas being formed by Saturn. Saturn limits and
sets boundaries with the family that either separates them during
youth or until they are 36 years of age. There is an unusual grey
area with family. Wealth and prosperity come after the age of 35.
Until then, Saturn makes one work for every dollar they make.
Their eating habits may be poor due to limitations of food and
drink by Saturn, unless Saturn is exalted or in his own sign of
Aquarius. This placement also makes the native frugal because
they may have suffered great poverty early in life.
Saturn in the 2nd House aspects the 4th House of mother,
homeland, mother's nourishment, peace, and all types of

conveniences. This aspect makes the native feel their mother’s
coldness. She would demand a lot from the native through hard
work and discipline. There is a sense of law and order within the
home, and luxury seems difficult to achieve. Those who have
Saturn aspect on the 4th House will gain property later in life, after
age 32. The later, the better! Saturn delays results, which means
the relationship with their mother will also improve over time, and
more nourishment will be felt.
Saturn's 7th aspect is on the 8th House of joint assets, death,
rebirth, sudden transformations, in-laws, and the occult. Fearful
Saturn brings tremendous anxiety towards having any kind of joint
assets or dealing with sudden change. Saturn delays the process of
having joint assets until after age 36. Saturn loves routine work
and lifestyle. The aspect on the 8th House will slow down the
process of the 8th House, but if Saturn is debilitated, then such a
slow transformation would be hard to grasp. This aspect also
insures long life for the native. Saturn is a very realistic planet that
wants to work. When it comes to occult science, Saturn may be
less persistent towards it unless it aspects its own house. The 8th
House is also the house of joint assets after marriage, and Saturn's
aspect limits the amount of assets one gains with joint ventures
after marriage. Saturn also limits risk taking, which prevents them
from growing any further.
Saturn in the 2nd House aspects the 11th House of gains, network
circles, elder siblings and hopes. The native fears that they are
unable to accomplish their goals and make their wishes come true
due to responsibilities of domestic life. They also become quite
fearful of making more money because Saturn brings anxiety
around network circles and creates fear of betrayal. Saturn
provides gains in the sign of Taurus, Libra, or Aquarius. One
usually acquires gains only if Saturn's mahadasha arrives during
their mid-thirties.

## Saturn aspect on 5th, 9th, and 12th House:

Saturn in the 3rd House aspects the 5th, 9th, and 12th House. The
3rd House represents our siblings, media, skills, hobbies, sales,
marketing, teamwork, and initiative. Saturn limits the amount of
siblings one has. It brings about fear and anxiety from doing things
alone, yet Saturn makes the native hate teamwork or help from
others. This fear subsides by one’s mid 20’s. They realize success
will come by taking initiative and working in solitude. The 3rd
House is known as the upachaya house, meaning it grows better
with time, and any natural malefic planets give good results. The
native learns throughout life that through taking initiative and sign
placement, they will go into media, sales, or business ventures.
One of the best things about Saturn in the 3rd House is that it gives
the native the capacity to make gold from dust through their
efforts. This is a great placement for someone who wants to be a
novelist, because Saturn slows down the process of the house
where he sits and lengthens it. The 3rd House represents
communication and writing. Communication is delayed at an early
age; one is either too shy to speak, or there is a delay in speech.

Saturn in the 3rd House aspects the 5th House of education,
children, romance, creativity, performing arts, and politics. This
aspect blocks and delays matters related to the 5th House.
Romance is turbulent until one’s mid 30's, and education is
delayed or blocked. It is next to impossible to achieve a bachelor's
degree, and, if it is obtained, it won't be used. There are also
stumbling blocks in gains through creative pursuits until after the
age of 32. Children will also be delayed for the native until after
age 32. Basically, Saturn never denies; it only delays.
Saturn in the 3rd House aspects the 9th House with his 7th aspect.
The 9th House represents higher learning, religion, philosophy,
rituals, long distance travel, and teachings of one's father and
gurus. Saturn's aspect on the 9th House delays traveling for the
native. Travel is infrequent, and if they do, they go to the farthest
places for long durations. There are setbacks when dealing with
their father or father's teachings because of travel. There is fear of
responsibility when dealing with professors at universities. Later in
life, one gains success through education, unless there is an aspect
of Jupiter and Mercury on the 9th House.
Saturn in the 3rd House aspects the 12th House of foreign lands,
imagination, medication, isolation, spirituality, losses, expenses
and modern multinational companies. Saturn's aspect upon the
12th House can restrict one’s ability to have creative ideas and feel
comfortable in a spiritual environment. They feel odd in spiritual
places, jails, and hospitals. This will change in their late 20's,
where they will find true peace in isolation and spiritual
environments. This aspect also makes the native depressed in early
life because the 3rd House represents discomfort in dealing with
everyday issues. There can be a loss of mental peace with aspect to
the 12th House of losses. This aspect can take the native to foreign
lands for business and media related work in Saturn's mahadasha.
Their ability to preach will also be criticized due to their
pessimistic outlook on religion, the world, and philosophy.

## Saturn aspect on 6th, 10th, and 1st House:

Saturn in the 4th House aspects the 6th, 10th and 1st House. The
4th house represents the mother, home, homeland, mother's love,
early education, and convenience. Saturn in the 4th House makes
the relationship with the native’s mother disciplined, organized,
and structured. The mother might be cold towards the native unless
4th Lord is sitting with Saturn or aspects the 4th House. The
mother is controlling and might act like a general at home. There is
a delay in attaining property, but one does get a home after the age
of 35, since Saturn is the karaka of construction on land. They are
able to build a custom home after Saturn's restrictive period has
passed. During the mahadasha of Saturn, one has to fully indulge
in responsibilities towards the mother, home, and maintaining the
land. Saturn's placement might bring a cold, business-like
approach at home, but the environment is stable. This can produce
a soldier, policeman, or security guard who is forced to bare the
responsibility of protecting their homeland. This position brings
slow but steady growth in life and gains through property.
Saturn in the 4th House aspects the 6th House of debts, enemies,
diseases, small animals, everyday work life, and obstacles. Saturn's

3rd aspect brings victory over enemies through a steady work
ethic. The 6th House also represents disagreements and divorce.
Saturn's aspect delays or restricts the native in getting a divorce
due to the responsibility that comes with it. One may suffer from
chronic diseases of the chest, lungs, and joints. They need to have
a routine work life in order to handle obstacles. Saturn restricts one
from going into debt. If they do, Saturn helps them legally rid
themselves of it.
Saturn in the 4th House aspects the 10th House of career, status,
father, and government. This aspect brings responsibility and
leadership. They are forced to become a leader of the workforce
which is a source of more daily responsibility. They need routine
at work and steadiness no matter what they do. They like work to
be long term and constant. One is able to cope with the burden of
work due to their mother’s teachings. An appreciation of their
mother’s teachings comes later in life. They also benefit through
the government, and may even become a government
administrator or politician.
Saturn in the 4th House aspects the 1st House of the physical body,
personality, life force, and health. This aspect brings a very
disciplined and organized personality. There is a sense of
following the rules and abiding by the law. Their persona is
influenced by the mother, and is very serious and powerful. They
have a laser-like focus on what they do in life. As far as health is
concerned, the native should beware of joint pains, bone density,
heart, and lung issues. Depending upon Saturn's sign placement,
pinpointing the cause of disease is possible.

## Saturn in aspect on the 7th, 11th, and 2nd House:

Saturn in the 5th House aspects then 7th, 11th and 2nd House. The
5th House represents education, romance, children, creativity, and
politics. Saturn in the 5th House brings responsibility through
children, romance, and education. One feels like they have to do it
all when dealing with children or their academics. There is
coldness when it comes to romance because Saturn brings
restrictions in love. As a child, the native would feel stifled during
playtime, and suffer academic stress. Saturn might restrict the
number of children they have, but won’t deny them. It's better to
try having a child after the age of 32. Otherwise, it may result in a
miscarriage. If they pursue creative or speculative businesses, they
would want it to be consistent and organized. However, this
placement can seriously restrict the native in achieving quick
creative thoughts and actions. They refrain from thinking through
things creatively unless Venus or Mercury is with Saturn.
Saturn in the 5th House aspects the 7th House with its 3rd aspect.
The 7th House represents marriage, legal partnerships, and other
people. This aspect brings late marriage and responsibility within
it. Marriage occurs for business reasons, and to keep the faith of

children intact. Although one may marry late, the marriage lasts
because Saturn slows down the effect of each house it aspects.
Saturn's aspect shows a spouse who might be very serious, older,
and disciplined; perhaps more than the native themselves. Saturn
in the 5th House creates a very immature person because they
never got to experience childhood freely. As an adult, they tend to
seek attention through other people, including their spouse. If
Saturn is in a good sign or exalted, then one will gain
tremendously through their spouse and in business partnerships.
Saturn in the 5th House aspects the 11th House of gains, network
circles, elder siblings, and wishes. Saturn’s aspect restricts their
friends and network circles that could help them climb the ladder
of success. They may have few friends, yet they are valuable.
Saturn brings fear and responsibility through elder siblings and
friends. There is a delay in gaining wealth if Saturn is alone in the
5th House and the 11th House has no planet present. Saturn brings
the need create structure when dealing with professional people
because without it, the native may miss opportunities. In Saturn’s
mahadasha, there could be frustration due to lack of support from
network circles.
Saturn in the 5th House aspects the 2nd House of family, wealth,
savings, assets, food, and speech. They feel restricted in their
speech later in life. They are shy about verbally sharing their
beliefs. There is responsibility towards family, and family assets.
Early in life, it's hard to save money unless Saturn is exalted. One
cannot attain assets until their early 30's. Saturn in the 2nd House
makes one a serious and demanding speaker in the family. They
may criticize others for having children. They consume food with
low nutritional value. Saturn's 10th aspect shows that will be
highly responsible regarding personal assets and money. They are
frugal, and demand family businesses to be more organized.
Trying to save money and manage finances will be frustrating with
Saturn’s slow pace.

## Saturn aspect on 8th, 12th, and 3rd House:

Saturn in the 6th House aspects the 8th, 12th, and 3rd House. The
6th House represents disease, debt, enemies, small animals,
service, disagreements, and unprivileged people. This is one of the
best placements of Saturn, as the 6th House is known as one of the
evil houses, and natural malefic planets in evil houses do well;
especially Mars and Saturn. Saturn's job is to suppress and slow
down the effects of all houses it aspects and the house it sits in. In
the 6th House, it suppresses and slows down the result of all other
evil houses. This a great position for success in the service industry
or as an employee. One gains through working at senior centers, in
medicine, or non-profit work. They are very obedient and work
long hours without complaining. In the house of disagreements and
divorces, Saturn tends to slow down the effect of such situations.
One might have a long divorce process, or the divorce will drain so
much from both parties that they may not pursue it. The 6th House
is about diseases, while Saturn is about chronic illnesses. The
native may develop a chronic illness, or Saturn can act as a cure
for a chronic illness. Being the house of animals and pets can
create an animal rights activist. They may also be a political
activist who fights for the underprivileged. Saturn brings law and

order, and in the 6th House of enemies, it can make a criminal
lawyer, policeman, or government administrator. This may not be
a great placement for intimacy, as Saturn will try to bring strict
ethics and principles in married life, which takes away from
romance.
Saturn in the 6th House aspects the 8th House with its 3rd aspect.
The 8th House represents death, rebirth, surgeries, accidents,
occult, secrets, transformations, and in-laws. This is the most
malefic of all houses due to its unpredictability. It can either make
a rags to riches story, or one of great tragedy. They will remain
victorious over their enemies as Saturn slows the pace down of any
sudden events, including death. They live up until their mid 80's.
Saturn also protects the native from accidents and surgeries if
Saturn is not debilitated. This aspect brings duty and responsibility
towards maintaining joint assets, dealing with in-laws, and a long
lasting married life. Saturn brings reality to marriage, and
responsibility maintains the relationship.
Saturn in the 6th House aspects the 12th House of sexual
pleasures, foreign lands, isolation, jails, asylum, hospitals,
meditation, and losses. Saturn slows the process of imagination
and intuition. The person may be frugal with their money. It also
slows down the process of losing one’s identity to the unknown
realm. The native can visit the spirit world and still recognize
themselves. Saturn helps the native become disciplined and
organized regarding their spiritual beliefs. There is a strong work
ethic in restricted environments; whether a doctor or warden.
Saturn in the 6th House aspects the 3rd House of communication,
media, hobbies, initiative, artistic skills, and siblings. Saturn brings
organization and discipline in one's ability to communicate with
others. They speak in a straightforward manner about unprivileged
people and those who are suffering due to bureaucracy. There may
be a common goal between the native and their siblings, but their
relationship leads to different paths. This aspect can also show one

who dominates their younger sibling and asks too much of them. If
Saturn is debilitated, it may create differences as well.

## Saturn aspect on 9th, 1st, and 4th House:

Saturn in the 7th House aspects the 9th, 1st and 4th House. The 7th
house represents marriage, legal partnerships, and other people.
Saturn
gains
directional
strength
because
marriage
and
partnerships are about work, discipline, and organization between
two parties that are committed through thick and thin. Saturn
brings about a strong bond between a couple or business partners.
In the house of other people, Saturn brings generosity and care
towards the public, and one becomes a lovable public figure. If
Saturn is not exalted, or in his own sign of Aquarius, then marriage
will occur late in life or with someone much older than the native.
Saturn is known as the planet of rank, and in the 7th House, the
native looks for a spouse who can elevate them. The nature of the
spouse depends upon the lordship of the 7th House. Marriage will
be more of a business venture than about falling in love. The
spouse will be organized, disciplined, and maybe more
hardworking than the native.

Saturn in the 7th House aspects the 9th House of higher learning,
gurus, teachers, father's teachings, long distance travel, religion,
and rituals. Saturn's 3rd aspect shows that one will have to work
hard to maintain a sound relationship with their teachers and gurus.
A lot of work is required in order to be accepted into a university
or be part of a religion establishment. The native may be rejected
repeatedly until their application is accepted. There might be a
strict teaching technique used by one’s father which enables them
to function in a disciplined and organized way in the world.
Saturn in the 7th House aspects the 1st House of self, personality,
physical body, and health. The native is greatly affected by others,
and only meets disciplined, organized, older people, unless Saturn
is debilitated. Saturn brings people who influence the native's
personality and perception of life. They are absolute workaholics,
which may lead to chronic health ailments like bone or joint pain.
The native needs to work hard just so others won't criticize them of
laziness. The body is greatly impacted by the burden of
responsibility from marriage and business partnerships. They are
so involved in bettering the lives of others that they forget about
their own health and vitality. The ego can be weakened by
criticism and responsibility in life.
Saturn in the 7th House aspects the 4th House of mother, land,
home, conveniences, and peace of mind. This aspect of Saturn
creates a professional mother who could be a school dean or
teacher. There is a great responsibility towards one’s mother. They
have trouble understanding emotions due to lack of nourishment
from her because of her intense professional life. The home is
more business-like, where discipline and structure prevail. This
aspect also brings an opportunity to build one’s own home in life.
The 4th House brings about peace of mind, and this is where
Saturn can bring stress due to marriage and parental responsibility.
If Saturn and its depositor are well placed, there is more structure
than stress.

## Saturn aspect on 10th, 2nd, and 5th House:

Saturn in the 8th House aspects the 10th, 2nd and 5th House. The
8th House represents death, rebirth, transformation, surgeries,
sudden events, and secrets. Saturn, being the karaka of death,
controls one's destiny and slows down the process. One has a very
long life but may feel they are always in danger. That's simply
because Saturn in the 8th House invokes such emotions and
energy. Saturn is forced to take responsibility of sudden
transformations and fluctuations. The native is tested often when
dealing with death. It's a great position for professions like an
emergency room physician or ambulance driver. Saturn is the
karaka of fear and anxiety, so these emotions arise from death
related scenarios. If the native realizes this in their horoscope, then
fear is just an illusion caused by a planet. The good side of the
placement comes with married life. Saturn maintains marriage and
relations with in-laws in a very traditional and conservative way.
The 8th House is also the house of occult, oil, coal, and things
buried underground. Saturn gives a natural inclination towards
healing through the occult, mining, and drilling.

Saturn in the 8th House aspects the 10th House of reputation,
career, status, government, and father. Saturn from the 8th House
shows fluctuations with one's relationship with their father if there
is no positive aspect on the 10th House. Saturn, the master of
routine, constantly battles their workplace, and deals with
transformations through career and status. They need motivation in
order to make it professionally, and they are disciplined and
organized when it comes to work. They dig deep in order to learn
and master the politics of their profession. In order to gain status
and climb the ladder of success, they must overcome their own
demons, and manage secrets that are hidden beneath the surface.
This aspect makes one very mechanical in the oil, mining, and coal
business. It also can make a native a secret service operative or an
administrator.
Saturn in the 8th House aspects the 2nd House of wealth, family,
assets, food, drinks, family history, speech, and culture. This
aspect shows one will go through fluctuations with their family,
and dramatic events transform the native's life. There is stability
towards maintaining a savings and protecting assets, and there will
be a slow and steady rise in wealth. They fear losing wealth or
family assets, which leads them to keep their gains hidden.
Saturn in the 8th House aspects the 5th House of children,
creativity, education, and speculative business. Saturn's aspect
shows that one wants to control their children, and increase their
status through them. The initial stages of child rearing bring fear.
Saturn also makes the native struggle in pursuit of their
undergraduate degree. They may go through a sudden
transformation in their academic field; maybe a surprise
acceptance to a prestigious university, or suddenly losing their
scholarship. These fluctuations are slowed down by Saturn, so
there are fewer ups and downs, but all the good and bad periods
last for a long time. There is great responsibility on behalf of their
children, and creativity is halted with this burden.

## Saturn aspect on the 11th, 3rd, and 6th House:

Saturn in the 9th House aspects the 11th, 3rd, and 6th House. The
9th House represents priests, gurus, teachers, higher learning,
education, religious studies, philosophy, and long distance travel.
Saturn in the 9th House restricts higher knowledge and receiving
wisdom through gurus. Saturn limits broad thinking in the native
and wants to maintain habits. In the 9th House, one tends to
perform religious actions on a daily basis. The higher spiritual and
philosophical mind is developed by one’s mid 30's because of the
boundaries of reality set by Saturn. One tends to criticize their
gurus, universities, and even their father's teachings. This position,
without any positive aspect from Jupiter or Mercury, can obstruct
the native from achieving advanced degrees. If one grows up in a
religious family, then there is a tremendous amount of
responsibility regarding rituals. The 9th house represents the
father's teaching, not the relationship with him. A native may have
been restricted in receiving their father's guidance due to work and
long distance travel.
Saturn in the 9th House aspects the 11th House of gains, profits,
network circles, and hopes. This aspect shows that one will have to

use their willpower and effort to maintain gains and relationships.
Saturn aspects delay making friends and creating network circles
which help the individual excel at work. If Saturn is in Aquarius
and Libra, then there is less delay. Growth is slow and steady in
maintaining gains in life, which usually occurs after age 36.
Motivation is needed to maintain all things related to the 11th
House, including good relationships with elder siblings. The 3rd
aspect is about your effort willpower, and motivation. Income can
be attained through religious activity and preaching to unprivileged
people.
Saturn in the 9th House aspects the 3rd House of media,
journalism, skills, hobbies, courage, willpower, motivation,
communication, sales, marketing, and younger siblings. This
aspect of Saturn makes one very cautious about pursuing things
which require courage, dealing with danger, and fighting ability.
Saturn brings fear and anxiety around taking charge and finding
courage. This is why malefic in trikona houses are not usually
welcome, especially Saturn. Saturn brings a slow communication
style, and one needs motivation to have good relationships with
their siblings. This aspect may also separate them from younger
siblings, or they may not have one. The native is unaware of their
skills and abilities until their late 20's.
Saturn in the 9th House aspects the 6th House of debts, diseases,
enemies, obstacles, small animals, disagreements, and non-profit
organizations. Saturn’s aspect defeats enemies or at least prevents
the native from being severely harmed. There is great discipline
and structure in their everyday work life. They have a sense of
higher duty and purpose when serving poor and underprivileged
people. In the 9th House of higher principles and preaching, Saturn
fights for those who are helpless. Saturn can give chronic joint
problems in old age, especially if Saturn's mahadasha comes into
play in their 40's or 50's. It also protects them from heavy debt and
helps maintain marriage as he blocks the process of divorce and

disagreements. This is a great place for government service, and
the service industry in general.

## Saturn aspect on 12th, 4th, and 7th House:

Saturn in the 10th House aspects the 12th, 4th and 7th House. The
10th House represents status, reputation, government, career,
father, and fame. In the 10th House, Saturn feels comfortable as it
originally rules the 10th House of Capricorn. This placement
brings a great work ethic along with tremendous discipline and
organization skills. One loves routine jobs. There is a possibility of
working in the steel, oil, and mining industry. The status of the
native grows slowly but steadily in their profession and social life,
unless there is an aspect of Jupiter; then it will excel the process of
moving up the ranks. Saturn makes one adore the reputation that
comes with higher status and climbing the ladder. They focus on
their career more than anything else. There is a possibility of
working for the government, preferably behind the scenes. There is
a sense of social duty, and performing good deeds publicly, unless
Saturn is debilitated. In this case, one will run away from social
responsibility and may even cheat their way to the top because
they do not want work. One’s father brings about great

responsibility, and the relationship is strict and fear based. He also
infuses organization skills and discipline in the native. Fame does
not come easy, nor do they desire it; unless there are other planets
sitting in the 10th House, or aspects the 10th House.
Saturn in the 10th House aspects the 12th House of privacy,
imagination, jails, hospitals, foreign lands, spirituality, expenses,
and multi-national companies. The native needs to take initiative
in order to have mental peace and pleasure in bed because the 12th
House also represents our private sexual life. The native is more
interested in behind the scenes work. However, the native is
forced to be in the spotlight due to the demanding position Saturn
brings, like public office for the local, state, or federal government.
Saturn in the 10th House aspects the 4th House. The 4th House
represents home, mother, land, homeland, childhood and
conveniences. This aspect of Saturn brings about a very strict
environment at home. There is lack of nourishment and love from
the mother, who demanded structure, discipline, and organization.
She is a conservative figure who hardly has fun, unless the Moon
is sitting with Mercury or Venus in a good sign. For these reasons,
the native usually prefers to be at work and outside of the house.
There is also a delay in purchasing a home. Saturn's need to devote
full attention at work slowly brings enough gains to build a home.
Saturn in the 10th House aspects the 7th House of spouse,
marriage, and legal partnerships. Saturn’s aspect means one will
look for a spouse who can increase their social and financial status.
This brings about a very professional, mature, and disciplined
spouse who is less about romance, and more about maintaining the
marriage and a stable home environment. There are additional
responsibilities after marriage, but it increases their status unless
Saturn is debilitated. This aspect also indicates that one will get
married after the age of 28, perhaps between 28-35 years of age.

Saturn’s aspect on the 7th House of other people, including family,
brings about responsibility in public life. One must be aware of
their public behavior because if inappropriate, Saturn will punish
them for it.

## Saturn aspect on 1st, 5th and 8th House:

Saturn in the 11th House aspects the 1st, 5th and 8th House. The
11th House represents gains, network circles, elder siblings, and
large organizations. Saturn feels comfortable in the original house
of Aquarius, as Aquarius is the 11th sign of the zodiac. Saturn
likes to create structure for large organizations, and help make
many corporate events happen to benefit society. One must work
hard for profit, and have a large network circle. Saturn makes the
native disciplined towards working in large organizations and
gaining from their resources. Saturn performs well 80% of the time
when in Libra, Capricorn and Aquarius. In the rest of the signs, it
becomes an obstacle and brings more responsibility. One may not
have an elder sibling if Saturn is in the 11th House, or there will be
great burden and responsibility from the elder sibling. This
placement can isolate the native from their network circle, and
they may not find happiness outside their professional life.

Saturn in the 11th House aspects the 1st House of self, personality,
physical body, and health. This aspect makes one hard working,
disciplined, patient, and well organized. In other signs besides
Libra, Aquarius, and Capricorn, the native finds a way to organize
life except when Saturn is debilitated. A strong work ethic is
required to achieve financial goals. Saturn will provide gains, but
only after hard work.
Saturn in the 11th House aspects the 5th House of children,
education, speculative business, fun, games, creativity and
performing arts. This aspect brings responsibility from having
children, and one's creativity may be blocked due to their
workload. Saturn doesn't block speculative gains if Saturn is in a
friendly sign. Saturn will give slow and steady growth in stocks,
sports betting, and other type of speculative business. The native
may be less inclined towards creativity or performing arts. If
Saturn is in the sign of Venus, then they will be pushed into the
entertainment business. This aspect will delay children and make
education difficult if there is no aspect of Jupiter or Mercury on the
5th House. One will encounter cold breaks ups in romance, even if
Saturn is in a good sign. This is due to Saturn's natural malefic
nature.
Saturn in the 11th House also aspects the 8th House of death,
rebirth, sudden events, accidents, surgeries, in-laws and occult.
This aspect of Saturn limits joint assets with the spouse in the early
stages of marriage, but increases after age 38. Saturn also slows
down the effect of fluctuations, and suppresses the negative effects
of the 8th House. They have a long life but may suffer from
chronic illnesses. There will be secret meetings with in-laws, and
hidden assets. The relationship with in-laws is distant no matter
how well Saturn is placed because Saturn concentrates on
suppressing the houses he aspects. During Saturn's mahadasha, one
will gain through secret dealings, occult, and the government.

Saturn is interested in the occult science, especially when in the
sign of Jupiter or in the nakshatra of Ketu.

## Saturn aspect on 2nd, 6th, and 9th House:

Saturn in the 12th House aspects the 2nd, 6th, and 9th House. The
12th House represents spirits, other realms, spirituality, spiritual
places, isolated places, wandering in foreign lands, jails, hospitals,
asylums, and expenses. Saturn in the 12th House blocks the
imagination and spiritual life until age 35. It restricts obtaining
spiritual knowledge unless there are other planets present in the
12th House. One also finds that their sex life is restricted by some
unknown psychological factor. Their spiritual values are set in
stone, and one will routinely follow them without anyone
disturbing the flow. Saturn brings frustration through meditation
and spiritual practice. This placement also brings loss of identity in
foreign lands. If one moves to a foreign country, they will suffer
from this until their mid 30's. A debilitated Saturn can also land
someone in jail due to stealing family assets or other criminal
activity.

Saturn in the 12th House aspects the 2nd House of family, wealth,
savings, family assets, speech, and food. One is cautious when
dealing with money or assets of the family. They do not to make
foolish investments and are very frugal. Saturn here also separates
the native from their family for certain durations. They either
move to foreign lands or to a spiritual school. If Saturn is in a
tough sign or conjunction, then one will spend more than they
make. They will run up credit cards and become bankrupt. This
also brings a delay in saving money until the age of 35, after which
Saturn will release the native from its karmic bondage. There is
also family responsibility from an early age.
Saturn in the 12th House aspects the 6th House of debts, enemies,
diseases, disagreements, divorces, and small animals. This aspect
brings empathy towards those who are diseased. They love
assisting people who are poor that cannot provide for themselves
which is also seen from the 6th House. Saturn helps deal with
enemies and keeps disagreements at a minimum. Divorce rarely
happens with this aspect. Saturn is mostly found in the horoscope
of bankers, jailers, and doctors. This placement also brings victory
over enemies and restricts debt. In Saturn’s mahadasha, one will
get rid of debts through consistency.
Saturn in the 12th House aspects the 9th House of higher learning,
teachings, gurus, religion, rituals, and philosophy. This aspect of
Saturn shows that one can be very driven towards rituals, religions,
and gurus. They have a traditional approach to religion and
philosophy. One has responsibility of the father and his teachings,
religion, and higher education. They may be blocked from
attaining a higher education unless Jupiter aspects the 9th House.
Their father might be in foreign lands, or moved permanently;
unable to teach the native about life. This placement of Saturn
brings spirituality in later years. Early on, one may find that their
living environment is about strict spiritual principles and religious
activities.

## Rahu Aspects Through Houses:

Why do Rahu and Ketu have 5 and 9 aspect? Rahu and Ketu are
two planets that can only be controlled and suppressed by Jupiter,
the ultimate super guru. Whenever Ketu or Rahu sit with Jupiter,
Jupiter gives away his good side or Rahu, and makes Rahu act like
him. This harms the native far less than if Rahu was with another
planet. The same goes for Ketu. Many astrologers do not count this
aspect, including myself. However, when I started doing
consultations, there were flaws in my predictions. Based on a
guru’s advice, I began analyzing this aspect and saw a dramatic
improvement.
Rahu (north node) aspects the 5th, 7th, and 9th place from itself.
Rahu is an obsessive energy which looks to take leaps and bounds
towards the future. It wants to be respected, nurtured, and
showered with wealth and fame. Rahu represents foreign energy as
in foreign people, objects, and places. Rahu is the illusion. It has
the nature of amplifying for good and for bad. Whoever Rahu sits
with, the aspect it brings is dynamic and obsessive. Whatever
house Rahu aspects, the native would have to impact those houses
to reap the rewards. Rahu is also known as an out of the box
thinker. It is unorthodox and breaks taboos of society.
I will not discuss the 7th aspect of Rahu or Ketu because opposite
to either one will be the other doing its job. Seventh from Rahu
will always be Ketu, and seventh from Ketu will always be Rahu.
This is why the 7th aspect should only be studied when there is
another planet present, otherwise the 7th aspect is very confusing.
The two opposite energies are creating a black hole, phantom
energy.

## Rahu aspect on 5th and 9th House:

Rahu in the 1st House aspects the 5th, 7th, and 9th, House. In the
1st House, Rahu can be egotistical, confident, and overly
ambitious if Rahu sits with the ascendant Lord, or if the ascendant
Lord aspects Rahu. This position can make a native a very driven
athlete, soldier, or politician. Rahu’s qualities are also presented if
the ascendant Lord is strong. One tends to think of themselves as
the supreme leader who deserves the best in life. Rahu is obsessed
with the self even if the ascendant is damaged. Rahu in the 1st
House can become revolutionary. It may not provide any discipline
or intelligence because Rahu is about getting things done now.
Rahu doesn't know what it means to earn something without the
support of another planet or ascendant. A weak ascendant can
show a very lazy and unmotivated personality that has been
clouded by circumstances and imagination.
Rahu in the 1st House aspects the 5th House. This aspect brings
about either great obsession or disillusionment about children,
politics, and education. They are inclined towards adopting
children from foreign lands, or children conceived outside the

boundaries of society; like having children out of wedlock. In
Rahu's mahadasha, one will conceive children in a very unusual
way. It could be anything from artificial insemination, adopting,
having a c-section birth, or a surrogate mother. Rahu also brings a
very unusual stage performer who dives deep into their craft and
becomes obsessed with perfection. However, these qualities can be
completely dead if the Lord of Rahu's sign placement is
debilitated, combust, or in a planetary war.
Rahu in the 1st House aspects the 9th House of religion, higher
learning, father and guru's teaching, wisdom, philosophy, and long
distance travel for education. Rahu's 9th aspect shows that one
wants a higher education from a university. One has a strong desire
to be known as a taboo breaking philosopher and teacher. Rahu
also brings his taboo breaking ways by making someone a fan of
foreign religion or studying in distant lands. The native can pick a
subject related to anthropology or other religions. They may
become a foreign diplomat, travel expert, or pursue mysterious
philosophical avenues like the occult. One will also seek teachers
and gurus of foreign descent.

## Rahu aspect on 6th and 10th House:

Rahu in the 2nd House aspects the 6th, 8th,and 10th House. Rahu
in the 2nd House brings a very unusual family upbringing. One
wants to be the head of the family and know everything; from
lineage to family assets. This placement brings obsession with
wealth, gems, family assets, and savings. One is always striving to
save as much as possible, but they may not be able to until their
30's. Rahu wants to have the most perfect family in society, but
because of its natural malefic effect, there is a family disturbance
due to death, tensions, or lack of wealth. If Mercury is with Rahu
in the house of speech, the native exaggerates, lies, and swears.
They love to manage money; whether it’s their own or belongs to
others. This placement also separates the native from their family
or homeland. Rahu makes one exaggerate their wealth. Such
natives love to pretend they have money, expensive clothes, and
savings, but in reality it might be the opposite. This is why Rahu is
known as the illusionist. They make good public speakers by
breaking social norms. Since the 2nd House is related to marriage
along with the 7th house, the spouse is generally foreign.
Rahu in the 2nd House aspects the 6th House. This brings enemies
in foreign lands, of different religions, and cultures. Rahu's
extravagant ways bring debt by overspending to appear rich. Rahu
can also bring unusual diseases that doctors may not be able to
diagnose. This placement can bring victory over enemies through
unusual, taboo breaking ways. All malefic aspects on the 6th
House are a good thing. Rahu's nature to amplify issues can bring
obstacles in its mahadasha. If Rahu's depositor is strong, then one
will easily prevail. They create wealth through enemies and people
of lower realms, like criminals and drug addicts.
Rahu’s aspect on the 10th House shows that one will have to make
an impact on people in order to make a living. The native has to be
the product and use an unorthodox approach for gains since the
10th House represents career along with the 2nd House. Since the
2nd House is 2nd from the 1st, it demonstrates the wealth of the

native and how it will be created. This aspect shows an unusual
career; one could be in the film industry, public speaking, singing,
or a science fiction writer. If they follow a traditional career path
like a doctor, lawyer, or engineer, they will be in unusual places
and situations. They make an impact on clients through their
personality to earn a living. They may have a good or bad status in
society, depending on Rahu's sign placement and condition of the
sign depositor.

## Rahu aspect on 7th and 9th House:

Rahu in the 3rd House aspects the 7th and 11th House. Rahu in the
3rd House brings about a personality that wants to be the best and
the first to report any kind of news, or make any kind of big
announcement. Rahu becomes obsessed with media, marketing,
reporting and the art of sales. The energy of Rahu amplifies the
willpower and ability to put tremendous effort into business
ventures and the media. This native loves boosting their image in
public life. The 3rd House represents skills and hobbies, and Rahu
can create unusual interests and skills; like magic, or an
unorthodox journalist and media personality. One would make a
good talk show host, gossip columnist, or offbeat news anchor.

Rahu is an energy that will do whatever it takes to achieve its
tasks. The native is capable of cheating or stealing other people’s
ideas and using them as their own. There is a great obsession over
the power of information.
Rahu in the 3rd House aspects the 7th House of marriage, legal
partnerships, other people, and trade. This aspect brings about a
foreign spouse. There is a great deal of interaction with the
opposite sex, sexual obsession, and the need to form a union.
Business and trade occur mostly with foreigners. Rahu’s aspect
gives one the ambition to learn about legal side of business
partnerships and how to gain resources through marriage. Rahu
does not deny nor destroy marriage. A conclusion can be reached
depending upon the aspect, conjunction, and condition of the 7th
House.
Rahu in the 3rd House aspects the 11th House. The native has
contact with many foreign friends and business minded people.
Rahu will do whatever it can to gain as much as possible through
them. Rahu in the 3rd (upachaya) House means things get better
with time, and Rahu brings a lot of gains through business, sales,
and marketing. The native wants to lead a large organization and
work in scientific think tanks. Since Rahu also aspects the 7th
House along with the 11th House, one will gain through their
business partnerships and spouse.

## Rahu aspect on the 8th and 12th House:

Rahu in the 4th House aspects the 8th and 12th House. In the 4th
House, the native may be in a foreign and unusual home
environment. They may have a foreign or adopted mother. Rahu
brings ambition to the house of home, mother, and property. The
home environment may be full of hope for the future. Parents can
be very encouraging, but the native needs to realize that hard work
is needed to fulfill desires, whatever they may be. Rahu can make
one travel frequently to foreign lands and one’s mother may also
travel to foreign lands. The Rahu native wants to be the first to
attain parental property and build a large home. Rahu is still a
natural malefic, and one may be so obsessed with attaining a home
that they may go an illegal route or steal property from others if
Rahu sits in an enemy sign, or Rahu's depositor is weak. They can
even be a home invasion robber or thief.
Rahu in the 4th House aspects the 8th House. This aspect also
brings obsession about obtaining property and assets through
marriage and in-laws. Holding onto secret information is a great
resource and will provide much in life. The native can blackmail

people for property and vehicles. During the Rahu period, one will
be interested in the occult science and tantric knowledge, but such
knowledge will be used to gain power over others. Rahu is not
sincere about information, and always wants to find a way to gain
something form divine secrets. Rahu can also bring sudden
transformations within the home, where either the native gets a
huge home, or suddenly loses a home and moves to foreign lands.
Rahu in the 4th House aspects the 12th House. This aspect can
bring frequent travel to foreign lands for pleasure, education, or for
property reasons. They find happiness away from home and in
isolated places. But such an aspect can also be dangerous, as it can
take the native into jails or mental hospitals. Rahu’s aspect also
makes one interested in the psychological side of life, and they
may become a psychiatrist or psychologist in a mental institution.
This placement is about taboo breaking situations, like working in
jails or going to jails, building an unusual house in foreign lands,
or growing up with foreign parents.

## Rahu aspect on 9th and 1st House:

Rahu in the 5th House aspects the 9th and 1st House. In the 5th
House, Rahu is obsessed with children. Rahu wants to be
privileged enough to have the best children, even if it has to adopt
them. Since Rahu is about taboo breaking, the native will have
mixed race children or children out of wedlock. They may adopt,
or have a child through a surrogate mother. If Jupiter is well
placed, then Rahu can also be obsessive about education and
wanting to be the best. Rahu doesn't like to work hard when
achieving something, which can make the native cheat in school or
in politics. Rahu makes a dynamic politician, actor, or stage
performer. It loves the attention and praise in this house. Rahu can
create a gambling addiction. If Rahu is in Venus or Mercury, then
it will help the native in speculative businesses like sports betting,
poker, or the stock market.
Rahu in the 5th House aspects the 9th House. This aspect makes
one interested in the philosophy and religion of other cultures.
They love traveling for purposes of education and learning about
other religious views and countries. Rahu always wants to be the
first at everything, for this reason Rahu wants to take the role of
preacher and teacher. Being a taboo breaker, Rahu may be
involved in occult and foreign religions that are not accepted by
society. The native may convert religions in Rahu mahadasha if it
also aspects the 9th Lord.
Rahu in the 5th House aspects the 1st House. This aspect brings
about creativity, ego, and overconfidence within the native unless
the Sun or Moon is debilitated. The native's personality is very
manipulative and clever. They feel like they have to be the pioneer
in creative or political pursuits. One can go into world politics or
become a member of the United Nations. The life of the native
becomes about illusion and delusion. They think they are above
everyone else, when they are in fact, beneath. If Rahu is in Scorpio
or the depositor of Rahu, it can spell loss of identity and ego for
the native.

## Rahu aspect on the 10th and 2nd House:

Rahu in the 6th House aspects the 10th and 2nd House. The 6th
House is one of the best placements for Rahu, Mars, and Saturn.
Rahu destroys enemies, overcomes obstacles, and manages to cure
diseases. Rahu wants to be the first to cure and operate on people,
