solve disagreements, and become the beacon of hope for the poor
and disabled. They want to be known as the one who cures others,
or solves problems of society at large. In the house of crime and
criminals, Rahu excels as the master. The native can smooth talk
their way into any deal, and take care of the ones who don’t
comply. Due to certain yogas, the native may go into the world of
crime. If the yogas are good, then the native can go into the world
of crime, but this time try to prevent it; like a police officer. This
Rahu is a calculative risk taker.
Rahu in the 6th House aspects the 10th House. This aspect makes
the native power and status hungry. One will defeat any enemy in
order gain their place in high society. The native will climb the
ladder of social success by winning the hearts of the poor and
unprivileged people. One will do whatever it takes to bring social
order and justice to the world. They will do so by providing

medical attention and housing to those who can't afford it. Such a
native is also equally cunning, for they will use anyone, including
the poor, to become rich with status. This placement insures a
public life because Rahu has to make an impact upon people to
make a living. They have to be the face of the company or their
non-profit organization. They can be an attorney, and fight and win
for the rights of non-profit organizations.
Rahu in the 6th House aspects the 2nd House. This aspect shows
one will gain money through enemies. Rahu wants to gain all types
of assets and privileges through social reform. One also gains
through providing medical attention to people. The native can lie
to achieve status, as the 2nd House shows our speech. There is
early separation from the family due to school, poverty, or
enemies. They will try to attain assets and wealth by illegal activity
or dealing with criminals in foreign countries. Many corporations
go into third world countries to find cheap labor. They either pay
the politicians or the mob that controls the area. A CEO with such
placement can easily make a deal to gain status and wealth, which
can negatively impact others.

## Rahu aspect on 11th and 3rd House:

Rahu in the 7th House aspects the 11th and 3rd House. In the 7th
House, Rahu becomes obsessed with finding self-esteem through
other people. It wants to be admired by the opposite sex, society,
and seeks a union leading to increased self-worth and gains. Rahu
is about breaking taboos and in the 7th House, and one will marry
a foreign spouse or someone from another religion. This position
of Rahu also brings massive fame. Rahu amplifies this energy and
brings about sudden and uncontrollable change if it's positioned in
the Sun, Venus, or Mercury. This person wants to be known as a
great diplomat or deal maker since the 7th House represents
business. It can provide great results in terms of marriage, but one
will marry someone outside of their religion or homeland. If
Rahu's sign Lord is weak or too strong, it can bring about a
flirtatious nature, and one will be prone to cheating on their
spouse. This is also a strong placement for foreign settlement since
7th House is 4th from the 4th House, or its second home.
Rahu in the 7th House aspects the 11th House. The main goal of
the native is to find gains through marriage and legal business
partnerships. This aspect shows that through other people, one will
gain wealth; whether it's the media business, legal partnerships, or
marriage. The spouse will bring gains, but the Lord of 7th House
should be well placed and not debilitated. Usually, this placement
shows someone who wants to give up everything in life and let
spirituality take over due to Ketu being in the 1st House. Most of
the time, there is always some aspect or planet placed in the
ascendant which mediates the spiritual outcome.
Rahu in the 7th House aspects the 3rd House. This aspect brings
interest in media and artistic pursuits. One appreciates how much
information others have, and how they can creatively present the
information. Rahu in the 7th House is about gaining through other
people's resources. This can also create some unusual
circumstances with siblings. Rahu wants to use their spouse's
resources, and do business with the local community. They market

themselves through their spouse or business partners. In the house
of skills, hobbies, and media, Rahu wants to promote its creative
abilities to the world and gain wealth. The spouse will also be a
huge factor in helping one achieve their media success.

## Rahu aspect on 12th and 4th House:

Rahu in the 8th House aspects the 12th and 4th House. In the 8th
House, Rahu is obsessed with secret documents and information
which can be used against others for its own gain. Rahu needs to
know all about government conspiracy, occult, and tantric science.
This also makes the individual fascinated with reincarnation and
transformation through great calamities. The 8th House is about
unknown secrets, and Rahu represents foreign elements. Rahu can
create unusual experiences of the 8th House, like paranormal
activity, or Rahu can bring traumatizing experiences through
sexual abuse which may be a secret. They may become treasure
seekers, ghost hunters, or paranormal researchers. Rahu brings in-
laws from foreign lands, and one becomes obsessed with
increasing joint assets. They want to be a known healer, but
without the support of other planets, it can make someone a scam
artist in the name of magic.

Rahu in the 8th House aspects the 12th House. This aspect makes
Rahu curious about other worlds and spirituality. Rahu wants to
know about foreign lands, planets, and stars. Rahu wants to expose
these secrets to others and be recognized for it. It wants to be the
leader of secret knowledge. It also wants to be known for spiritual
knowledge which the native may not fully understand. This creates
a person who will seriously seek spiritual redemption under a guru
in foreign lands. Or, their ego will be overblown, and they think
they have spiritual knowledge without properly acquiring it. If
Rahu has the aspect of Jupiter, or well placed Saturn, then Rahu
will be under control. Otherwise without a good aspect, Rahu will
cheat his way into becoming a spiritual leader.
Rahu in the 8th House aspects the 4th House. This brings about
great transformation and major fluctuations within the home. The
native and their family may move frequently, and may
permanently settle in foreign lands. Many secrets are kept within
the family that could threaten their reputation. There are unusual
situations which occur between the native and their mother, and
they may be disillusioned. This can result in the native wandering
in foreign lands, away from family and friends. If Rahu is in
Taurus, Virgo, or Capricorn, then it can achieve sudden wealth and
property because it’s in a stable and safe earth sign.

## Rahu aspect on 1st and 5th House:

Rahu in the 9th House aspects the 1st and 5th House. This makes
one seek admiration for preaching and their knowledge of
philosophy and rituals. The individual wants to be known as the
king of rituals and scriptures. They want to break taboos of society
and create their own belief system and religion based on
disillusionment. Their ego is overblown regarding teaching and
preaching, and they think they possess the most valuable and
privileged information. Often, this can create someone who may
scam others in the name of God if Rahu is in a troubled sign. The
native is inclined towards unorthodox types of teaching; like sex,
magic, and occult cultures of the world. One feels they have a
better philosophical view, and people must follow them. This
placement also brings foreign travels due to religion and education.
Rahu in the 9th House aspects the 1st House. This makes one
overconfident about what they know and how others should
behave. They fail at teamwork which requires shared information.
They want to be the first and only one with all the knowledge
required. For this reason they are too egoistic. Their entire life is
dedicated towards proving to others that they deserve to be

respected as a knowledgeable person; though they know they may
not be.
Rahu in the 9th House aspects the 5th House. The native makes
their children believe that they possess valuable knowledge and
their philosophy is the right path. This can be really good or bad
for their children. If the parents teach them bad habits, then they
will be worse off. If there is a positive aspect on Rahu, then one
will bring universal and multicultural values to their children. This
aspect also indicates that children will be born in foreign lands or
have foreign genetics. They may attain children through adoption
or kidnapping them for their own status.

## Rahu aspect on 2nd and 6th House:

Rahu in the 10th House aspects the 2nd, and 6th House. Rahu
becomes extremely powerful and inflates the status and fame of
the native during its mahadasha if positioned in the friendly signs
of Venus, Mercury, and Saturn. The 10th House represents career,
status, reputation, and father. This position has an amplified need
for status. One wants to be known as the king of their profession,
and will do whatever it takes to climb the ranks. The native can be

a flatterer, and take orders from those who can move them up the
ladder. Internally, Rahu wants nothing but power and fame, and
such superficial friendships are only for gains. The reputation of
the native increases with time as the 10th House is the upachaya
house, which means it grows better with time. This clearly shows
that one will have to make an impact or impression on others to
make a living. This could range from acting, modeling, magic,
theater performer, or widely known political figure. During the
mahadasha of Rahu, one will experiences unusual success, but if
the 10th Lord is weak, then there will be a great distance between
father and son. The father will travel frequently to foreign lands
during the native’s youth. In Aquarius, which is co-ruled by Rahu
and Saturn, they can become a lawful government agent or a
successful politician.
Rahu in the 10th House aspects the 2nd House. The 2nd House
represents profession, voice, food, family, family assets, and
wealth. Rahu easily creates liquid wealth by providing mass fame
to the native. This also means the native will lie in order to gain
status professionally, and their family will be superficial and
pretentious without help in the 2nd House. Rahu brings foreign
objects and situations in our life. In the house of family, one is
bound to marry someone outside of their cast and religion, and also
break taboos of society. It can bring a situation where one may
have to choose between their family and their spouse.
Rahu in the 10th House aspects the 6th House. This brings victory
over enemies, and through enemies one will gain status and fame.
They are forced to eliminate their enemies and obstacles, and will
have enemies in foreign lands. Rahu's aspect is actually considered
good in the 6th House, as it wants to be known as curer and helper
for the underprivileged. The native becomes charitable towards
non-profit organizations. Rahu is open minded enough to try new
and alternative ways of curing diseases. Instead of just using old
fashioned medicine, the native tries ayurveda or acupuncture.

## Rahu aspect on 3rd and 7th House:

Rahu in the 11th House aspects the 3rd and 7th House. Rahu in the
11th House is one of the best placements after the 6th and 10th
House. Rahu becomes the master of networking. It wants to be part
of large organizations, and rise to the top. Rahu amplifies the size
of the 11th House. The native may gain more than they can handle
in Rahu's mahadasha. The native has foreign friends and network
circles, and breaks taboos by joining groups that defy the laws of
society. They may be associated with a gay and lesbian network,
or they have friends who are activists against racism. Rahu
provides gains if Rahu's Lord is strong, or Rahu is part of a Raj or
Dhan yoga. Since Rahu will do whatever it takes to gain wealth,
the native may cheat people to gain money and prestige.
Rahu in the 11th House aspects the 3rd House. This brings about
courage and willpower to network in large organizations and in the
local community. The native is obsessed with announcing and
marketing their plan to make the world a better place. They love
communicating in a big way, and want to present ideas to large
organizations. One will have step-siblings and younger siblings.

The native is also curious about unusual hobbies and talents. This
brings intellect and street smarts.
Rahu in the 11th House aspects the 7th House. This brings a
foreign spouse or someone outside of their faith. Gains are attained
through their spouse. This particular aspect shows that one is
obsessed with networking and dealing with the opposite sex. This
is good and bad. It can bring foreign people of the opposite sex
who might tempt the native into having affairs if one is not
fulfilled by their current partner, and there are no positive aspects
on the 7th House. By interacting with others, they attain higher
knowledge of the world and learn how to do business. This aspect
is great for business partnerships, but one has to be careful about
their urge for sex with many partners.

## Rahu aspect on the 5th and 8th House:

Rahu in the 12th House aspects the 5th and 8th House. Rahu in the
12th House increases imagination and brings interest in the
psychic and spirit world. The native wants to be the best spiritual
and psychic guide to people. Rahu represent foreign things and
other worlds. Rahu easily makes the native intuitive about spirits,

aliens, and UFOs. The native enjoys foreign travel and culture.
They do whatever it takes to settle in foreign lands or spiritual
sanctuaries. Rahu has the tendency to take shortcuts, and cheat into
spirituality. The native may delude people and themselves into
spiritual and psychic beliefs, and may land themselves in jail. This
occurs if Rahu is in an enemy sign, or the depositor of Rahu is
debilitated and weak. If the depositor of Rahu is strong by sign
placement in the 6th or 8th House, then it can give channeling
powers. This placement of Rahu can make one a great fiction
author, filmmaker, or stage director. The imagination of the native
travels to other dimensions and creates amazing stories.
Rahu in the 12th House aspects the 4th House of home and
mother. Their mother may have traveled often to foreign lands, or
she was foreign; a stepmother, or of a different culture. This aspect
separates the native from their homeland, and brings them to
foreign lands. There is a strong desire to build a home in foreign
lands and spiritual places. If Rahu is in a harsh sign, then it makes
someone a home invasion robber, especially in foreign places.
Other planetary alignments need to be present as well, like a
debilitated Mars in the 4th House.
Rahu in the 12th House aspects the 8th House. This brings
curiosity and obsession with death. The native wants to learn about
death and the spiritual realm, and tells others that death is not the
end of the road, but a passage way. In the sign of the Moon,
Venus, or Jupiter, the native wants to be the master of occult and
gain occult knowledge. There are sudden foreign trips the native
takes due to surgeries or for investment purposes. In Rahu
mahadasha, one will find themselves traveling on behalf of in-laws
and marriage. There is also a major spiritual transformation during
the mahadasha of Rahu, but such transformation only happens
after great upheaval.

## Ketu Aspect Through Houses:

Similar to Rahu, Ketu also aspects the 5th and 9th place from
itself. Ketu brings a bit of detachment and dissatisfaction from the
houses it aspects, but such aspects gain power when there are
planets present in those houses. Ketu wants to release things in life
where he sits and in the houses he aspects. When Ketu aspects an
exalted planet, or planet in its own sign, then spiritual energy is
attained. If a planet is in an enemy sign or debilitated, and Ketu
aspects it, then it will separate your desire from that planet and
things related to it. Ketu brings unusual situations in life, but in a
bad way. If Ketu is in conjunction with Venus in the 5th House, or
aspects Venus in the 5th house, then one will have stalkers and
sexually charged people coming into their life. They may face
sexual molestation or rape in childhood. If the debilitated Sun gets
an aspect from Ketu, then their ego and reputation in society is
doomed until their mid 30's. Ketu is perhaps the most malefic, but
teaches you through loss in the material world. It shows where we
have mastered things in a previous life. We always tend to fall
back on Ketu's lap when everything else fails.

## Ketu aspect on the 5th and 9th House:

Ketu in the 1st house aspects the 5th and 9th House. Ketu brings
loss of ego and self, and the native turns to spiritual life or a life of
exile if there are no other planets present in the ascendant, or
aspect the ascendant. They do not want material comfort or
material relationships. In a way, they become loners and have
weird personalities. They love spending time in isolation, or with
people in spiritual places like monasteries. They derive their self-
esteem and personality through other people, especially their
spouse.
Ketu in the 1st House aspects the 5th House. This brings isolation
and dissatisfaction from basic education and children. They may
be in awkward situations relating to school and children. These
experiences would distance the native from striving for such
things. A woman with this aspect may be highly likely to get an
abortion in case of an unplanned pregnancy. However, with Rahu's
aspect, curiosity would lead them to carry on with the pregnancy.
Ketu in the 1st House aspects the 9th House. This brings
dissatisfaction from trying to attain higher knowledge, and now

the native also rejects strict religious and ritualistic ways of
society. One becomes a rebel, and wants nothing to do with
religion and school. This aspect also brings in bad blood with
teachers and gurus, and perhaps a lack of teaching by the father.
These experiences in early life make the native run away from
educational and cultural establishments.

## Ketu aspect on the 6th and 10th House:

Ketu in the 2nd House aspects the 6th and 10th House. In the 2nd
House, there are turbulent family experiences involving
detachment, or the family abandons the native early in life. They
are unattached to wealth or assets of material value. Money comes
easy for the native because they do not seek it. They can be very
harsh and straightforward in terms of speech, which can be hurtful
to others since the 2nd House represents speech.
Ketu in the 2nd House aspects the 6th House. This aspect can
make family a source of obstacles, and they may be their worst
enemy. Any aspect of malefic on a malefic house suppresses that
house, but Ketu brings experiences and lessons which are hard to
digest. In such instances, we hear about family murders, but this is

rare. Most of the time, there are major differences of opinion with
family members, and they present obstacles for the native. There is
no love of small animals or pets unless there is an aspect of
another planet present, or the Lord of the 6th House is strong. Ketu
also brings unusual diseases which doctors may not be able to
diagnose.
Ketu in the 2nd House aspects the 10th House. This aspect of Ketu
brings career dissatisfaction. One experiences major fluctuations
with their work and reputation, and they face hardship through the
government or from authority. One’s father may have been
traveling, and was away from the native's life. Or, the father was
simply not present. The karaka of the father, which is the Sun,
must be evaluated to determine the situation. If the Sun is well
placed, there is physical distance without any damage to the
relationship. They are unattached to work or leadership roles; they
prefer to take the back seat and stay out of the spotlight. Work can
also bring humiliation. If Jupiter is present in the 10th House, or
Jupiter aspects Ketu or the 10th House, then one may pursue a
spiritual career. Ketu can also bring a career in research.

## Ketu aspect on the 7th and 11th House:

Ketu in the 3rd House aspects the 7th and 11th House. In the 3rd
House, Ketu disrupts communication. There is always some issue,
making the native walk away from marketing, sales, and media. If
they have younger siblings, they will be detached from them. Elder
siblings bring fluctuations, distance, and are capable of physically
attacking the native. These people have mastered communication,
but in this life, they have no interest communicating unless other
planets aspect Ketu. If Jupiter or the Sun aspects Ketu,
communication can be successful for spiritual pursuits.
Ketu in the 3rd House aspects the 7th House. This aspect shows
tremendous discomfort and dissatisfaction from the first spouse.
The spouse is unable to fulfill the desires of the native unless there
is an aspect of Venus or Jupiter upon the 1st House. In this case,
the spouse will be very spiritual. Ketu shows an unusual marital
situation. One may experience sexual abuse, physical violence, or
infidelity. This is very distressing for the native.
Ketu in the 3rd House aspects the 11th House. This brings
dissatisfaction through one's network circle. The native can be
betrayed or cheated by their network. The native can also deceive
their network circle, which may distance them from others. Ketu
gives a harsh aspect to the house of gains. One may even beg for
money early on in life, but towards their mid 30's, gains begin
entering the picture. This aspect can make someone a loner in life,
as both the 7th House and 11th House represent other people and
network circles, while the 3rd House represents friends.

## Ketu aspect on the 8th and 12th House:

Ketu in the 4th House aspects the 8th and 12th House. In the 4th
House, Ketu brings detachment from the mother; she passes at an
early age, or the native is separated from her. There is adversity
within the home, and one wants to either run, or face the problem
at a deeper level. If there are supporting planets, then Ketu doesn't
leave, but provides spirituality within the home. There is a lack of
security and peace in the home, which is why the workplace gives
the native peace of mind. They move far away from home when
Ketu is in the 4th House. If they stay in the same city, they face a
lot of adversity and damage to their reputation. If there is lack of
support from other planets, they will reject ancestral property due
to their own psychological dilemma.
Ketu in the 4th House aspects the 8th House. This aspect of Rahu
brings sudden changes within the home during Ketu's maha or
antar dasha. Secrets of the home are exposed to near and dear ones.
There is dissatisfaction from in-laws, and one has to work for joint
assets with their spouse. This aspect usually makes the native the
sole breadwinner. Interest in the occult is intensified during Ketu's
maha or antar dasha in their 20's. If the native has this placement,

and Mars is transiting in the 8th House, one can face car accidents,
cuts, or burns. If Venus is in the 8th House, then there can be
sexual abuse in childhood. Due to problems at home, one becomes
fascinated with the process of death. This can make someone a
serial killer if the Moon and Mercury are also damaged.
Ketu in the 4th House aspects the 12th House. This will drive the
person towards isolation and spirituality during Ketu's mahadasha.
This also insures that one will settle in foreign lands or have
property in foreign lands. If Ketu is debilitated, the native can end
up in jail. Debilitated means Ketu is in an enemy sign like Leo,
Capricorn, or Cancer, but mainly in the Sun and Moon. The 4th
House represents peace of mind, and if Ketu is in a harsh position,
then it can create psychological issues, landing the native in a
mental hospital.

## Ketu aspect on the 9th and Ascendant:

Ketu in the 5th House can make someone a master of politics and
creativity, yet distant from academic studies and performing arts.
This also brings about miscarriages or an abortion in a female's
chart. Ketu’s placement without any aspects from other planets can

show dissatisfaction through children. They may not want to have
them at all. If Jupiter or Mercury aspects Ketu, then one goes into
educational research. There are many unusual situations that
happen in relation to the 5th House which discourages the native to
pursue things related to it. Without the support of Venus or Jupiter,
one can have bad romantic experiences to the point of being
stalked. They are lazy about creativity, children, and education.
This is also a bland and serious position. The native is constantly
looking to make network connections, and doesn't stop to smell the
roses.
Ketu in the 5th House aspects the 9th House. This aspect brings
rejection of higher education if Ketu is the only planet which
aspects the 9th House. One will get into a power struggle and
reject religious philosophy, as well as the mundane philosophy of
the world. The native hates rituals and following religious leaders
unless they promote spirituality. One lacks teachings of the father
perhaps due to traveling for work or education. If Ketu has support
from other planets, or Jupiter sits in the 9th House, then one
becomes a great researcher.
Ketu in the 5th House aspects the ascendant as in 1st House. This
aspect shows that one loses their ego and personality due to past
life deeds, children, and academic education. They feel lost, and
forced to do things they have no enthusiasm for. This aspect may
distance the native after the birth of their child because they lack
interest in raising them. If the Moon is in the ascendant, then it can
show someone with slightly low I.Q. or depression.

## Ketu aspect on the 10th and 2nd House:

Ketu in the 6th House aspects the 10th House and 2nd House. This
placement of Ketu creates many conflicts in life, and the native
doesn't want to face or resolve those problems. They tend to ignore
enemies, diseases, obstacles, and crime, leading them to lose wars
to their opponents. They have expertise in resolving conflicts and
taking care of non-profit organizations, but one doesn't have the
desire to deal with these matters. They are obsessed with the spirit
and psychic world. The native may end up in jail by associating
with the illegal business of drugs. They can also associate with
thieves, or come into contact with them in Ketu's mahadasha. The
native does not want to be involved in social conflicts, but rather
wants to escape into their imagination. If Ketu receives a harsh
aspect from Mars or Saturn, then one will be involved in some
criminal activity around drugs or chemicals. They are normal
people who refuse to partake in social revolutions and matters of
society.
Ketu in the 6th House aspects the 10th House of career. This
aspect takes the native into the field where they are forced to use
their psychological ability to heal people. They have unusual

situations at work where one can be accused of doing things they
never did, or put in a leadership role they never wanted. Usually, a
lawsuit at work is seen with this placement. This person would
rather be behind the scenes and do routine work. They have no
hope for the future, and feel their life will be spent in service and
dealing with conflicts they can't escape. The best position for this
native would be to work for themselves without anyone around.
Ketu in the 6th House aspects the 2nd House of family, wealth,
assets, and family history. This aspect shows one's family might be
involved in gambling or illegal activities, and the native goes
through experiences which psychologically separates them from
their family. The family can become the main obstacles in the
native's life. They can also suffer from diseases of the throat and
mouth. The native has no interest in family affairs or family assets.
This can also make someone an alcoholic, or curious about drugs
due to peer pressure. Peer pressure stems from enemies of the
native who challenge them in a negative way.

## Ketu aspect on the 11th and 3rd House:

Ketu in the 7th House aspects the 11th and 3rd House. The native
is able to maintain relations with society. They know how to make
deals and partnerships work, but with Ketu, they lose enthusiasm
for relationships, marriage, and business partnerships. They rather
concentrate on themselves. The native meets many partners, but
will suddenly feel dissatisfied by them. They run into situations of
physical or sexual abuse, and their partner’s ignorance makes them
sick at the idea of a union. If no positive aspect of Jupiter or Venus
is upon Ketu, then one loves living a single life. There is also a
sense of manipulating the public.
Ketu in the 7th House aspects the 11th House. One will lose their
friends due to bad relationships, and they are also dissatisfied by
their circle of friends because they feel isolated by marriage or
partnerships. Since Ketu's energy is about our expertise in past
lives, it shows that one can survive the business world by working
without the support of networks and friends. Ketu doesn't block
gains. Rather, it shows no desire to gain in life except through
knowledge and education. If they have younger siblings, there is
animosity towards them. The 11th House represents desire, and
Ketu may bring a desire for more spiritual than material
achievements unless the 11th Lord is strong or planets are placed
in the 11th House which would minimize the effect of Ketu's
aspect.
Ketu in the 7th House aspects the 3rd House. This aspect of Ketu
shows that there is discomfort from younger siblings and close
neighbors, and one has disagreements with their siblings about
who they should marry. Ketu shows separation from younger
siblings or no younger siblings in life. If Jupiter is present in the
3rd House, one will find spiritual knowledge through siblings, as
well as a spiritual spouse with a long lasting marriage. In the house
of communication, Ketu shows a lack of interest in reporting,
marketing, and gossiping with family members unless another
planet is present.

## Ketu aspect on 12th and 4th House:

Ketu in the 8th House aspects the 12th and 4th House. If you feel
like leaving and not returning, or like the world is mundane and
you don’t fit in, then you may have Ketu in the 8th House. The 8th
House represents death, secrets, underground and occult worlds,
revolution, transformation, surgeries, and accidents. Ketu may beg
for support and love from in-laws, but it may never happen. The
native might be the sole bread winner in the family, making joint
assets impossible. Ketu shows that one was an expert in the occult
in past lives, and knows what astrology, tarot, and magic is all
about. They are drawn towards such mystical sciences. There is
hardly any family inheritance, or any attraction to one's family.
They love and practice the occult, but are not attached to it like
other masters. When time comes, they let go of it immediately.
The 4th, 8th, and 12th House are about moksha.
Ketu in the 8th House aspects the 12th House. This brings a
sudden urge to be in isolation and wander in foreign lands for
spiritual pursuits. One is forced into isolation, and finds
themselves doing research on unusual subjects like UFOs, ghosts,

aliens, or predicting the future. Though Ketu's aspect does not
reflect curiosity, it shows automatic expertise and interest from
past lives. This is why the 8th House of underworld secrets brings
a desire to renounce the world, and go to the final place of souls. If
the native moves to foreign lands, they find spiritual connections
that are more interesting than staying in their homeland. There is
never peace of mind in their home or country.
Ketu in the 8th House aspects the 4th House. This brings
separation from the mother due to traveling and sudden changes in
residence in early life. There is distance and dissatisfaction from
the mother, especially if Ketu aspects the Moon or is in
conjunction with the Moon. The native had a deprived childhood,
and feels better away from their homeland. If Mars and Ketu both
aspect the 4th House, then there is physical abuse from their
parents. Since Ketu aspects another moksha house, we can be
assured that during Ketu's mahadasha, one completely transforms
their life from material to spiritual, which may trigger the life of a
yogi or of a spiritual sage. This also brings paranormal activity
during Ketu's mahadasha at home. In the 8th House, Ketu keeps
secrets while aspecting the 4th House. Ketu has domestic secrets
which they will not share with anyone, and will try to forget.

## Ketu aspect on the 1st and 5th House:

Ketu in the 9th House aspects the 1st and 5th House. In the 9th
House, Ketu rejects religious and philosophical teachings of gurus,
rituals, and belief in God. Ketu in the 9th is a rebel of religion; a
placement common among charts of spiritual people who reject
separating the value of souls based on faith. The native hates
listening to their father or guru when it comes to teachings of
society. They want freedom of expression and religion; a chart like
the United States of America. This is a great placement for
research on cultural and religious affairs, or debunking
philosophical subjects. Depending upon the sign, one can be a
researcher of science or anthropology. Ketu has no problem
preaching. In the 9th House, he preaches against the religious
establishment. This also distances the native from their father, but
it may not ruin the relationship unless the Sun is in close contact
with Ketu, or Ketu is in the 10th House. During Ketu’s period, one
can travel for education and spiritual pursuits. They will gain more
from spiritual than academic learning.
Ketu in the 9th House aspects the 1st House. This aspect makes the
person skeptical about all things known as “the establishment.”

They question the government, and feel that all institutions are
suppressing human beings. They are completely detached from
such things. There is also dissatisfaction from ritualistic endeavors.
Ketu in the 9th House aspects the 5th House. One finds distant
relationships with romantic partners and children because of their
belief system. They find lovers who are more religious, and
incorporated in religious establishments. If Ketu already makes it
difficult to achieve a higher education, it makes it even harder to
achieve an undergraduate education in the 5th House. One runs
into strict teachers, and dislikes most subjects. These natives do
well with spiritual teachings.

## Ketu aspect on the 2nd and 6th House:

Ketu in the 10th House aspects the 2nd and 6th House. Ketu in the
10th House can give someone natural leadership ability, and make
them a spiritual leader at work. These natives do not like the media
or public attention. They want to work behind the scenes at
guiding people, like a real professional. They do not care for the
limelight, but about success and winning. The problem is the
native often rejects the opportunity to succeed in status and career,

and would rather live in darkness and isolation. These leaders are
not bred in the establishment; they run against it. They are the
whistleblowers and revolutionaries who cannot stand the status
quo. The person has to face many fluctuations due to Ketu's nature
of not caring for status, career, or employment.
Ketu in the 10th House aspects the 2nd House. This brings
dissatisfaction and misunderstanding within the home and family
business. The native has no desire to deal with matters on behalf of
the family. They would rather give up family assets and not burden
themselves. Such people are very direct with family, and what they
think of them. If Ketu has an aspect of Jupiter or is in conjunction
with Jupiter, then one will try to guide the family towards charity
and spiritual pursuits. They have substantial savings in the bank,
and no one may know because they are unpretentious. This aspect
usually prevents them from receiving family assets, and they may
be detached from family early on life to make a living.
Ketu in the 10th House aspects the 6th House. This aspect of Ketu
brings many enemies and obstacles professionally, which is why
they have career fluctuations. Ketu has no desire to deal with
enemies, therefore it never cares to fight them, and surrenders
easily unless Mars is present in the 6th House, which can be a
deadly force. They may not have any opinion about those in
poverty, and do not like small pets. Ketu is annoyed because he is
forced to deal with matters of no interest. Ketu in the 10th House
might be a good placement for a doctor or healer, since they are
unattached to status or fame; they just want to do their service.

## Ketu aspect on the 3rd and 7th House:

Ketu in the 3rd House aspects the 3rd and 7th House. In the 11th
House, Ketu can beg for friends and network circles because they
were burned in the past, and pushed people away from them. The
native prefers to live an isolated life. Ketu does not prevent gains,
and the native gains much in life. However, they feel empty due to
lack of support from friends. One has a very turbulent relationship
with elder siblings who distance themselves from the native. They
feel they have no friends, and don’t need friends to survive. They
do not want any leadership roles in large organizations. The native
has no desires for material gain; which is why they acquire the
most in life.
Ketu in the 11th House aspects the 3rd House. Ketu’s aspect
brings complete dissatisfaction from media, journalism, and
artistic pursuits. They are disconnected from the media and their
own artistic skills. Ketu brings no attachment to such things, and
this is why they may pursue media and performing arts in a way
that many would reject. There is also an awkward relationship with
younger siblings. The native has no connection with their
neighbors, and if they do, they fight over very small things.

Ketu in the 11th House aspects the 7th House. This aspect of Ketu
may bring many disappointments in marriage and legal
partnerships. The native is incapable of attachment due to bad
experiences in friendships and network circles. They don’t trust
their network and they do not trust their spouses. This aspect
brings very awkward situations with one’s spouse, which
sometimes ruins the reputation of the native in public life, as the
7th House represents the masses. One will always find some
dissatisfaction which will quickly push them away.

## Ketu aspect on the 4th and 8th House:

Ketu in the 12th House aspects the 4th and 8th House. Ketu in the
12th House is perhaps one of the most positive and influential
positions in a birth chart. Ketu is like a final puzzle piece. The 12th
House is the final resting place of the soul which passes from the
material world into the spiritual world of other dimensions. Ketu in
the 12th House make the native feel most comfortable and attached
to spiritual and isolated places. One has already mastered the art of
spirituality in previous lives, whereas this life is about using
psychic intuition to defeat enemies and overcome obstacles. This

placement is about finishing last minute business with adversaries,
paying off debts, and charitable donations to the poor. They are
natural philanthropists. The subconscious mind guides them in the
right direction. Although their artistic vision and imagination
might be blocked, they still know what has to be accomplished to
follow a spiritual path.
Ketu in the 12th House aspects the 4th House. Ketu's aspect from
the 12th House means one had a spiritual upbringing at home.
Although things were simple, there was never great attachment to
the home or homeland. One’s mother was a major spiritual
influence in life, and showed the native how to improve their
intuition. This aspect can detach the native from the mother or
home at an early age due to their spiritual or artist pursuits. They
can move to any place and call it home. They always tend to leave
home during their Ketu mahadasha, and permanently settle in
foreign lands or have the spiritual life of a yogi.
Ketu in the 12th aspects the 8th House. This aspect of Ketu on the
8th House shows that one is aware of the process of death and
reincarnation, and is not bothered by it. They have a sense of
completion in life, where the 8th House becomes a 3 dimensional
game of this realm. Ketu is the natural karaka of occult knowledge,
and when it aspects the house of occult, they have a natural gift of
guiding people through special powers like psychic vision, touch
healing, astrology, tarot, or mind reading. Ketu is about
detachment, which means the native may have a distant
relationship with in-laws and assets through marriage. In the 12th
House, Ketu finally becomes a guide for those who are fearful of
sudden events like death and surgeries. Ketu eases the pain of
others.

## Alternative Ascendant perspective:

When you judge aspects of planets from a certain house. Do not
just use your ascendant as the avenue of seeing what the planet and
its aspect is doing. Look at the aspects from different ascendants.
According to Prashara there are 3 very important ascendants.
### 1. Lagna (your rising sign)
### 2. Moon Lagna (Putting your moon as the ascendant)
### 3. Sun Lagna (Putting Sun as your ascendant lord)
Example (1)

Example (2)
Example (3)
By look at chart from different perspective you can see the result
of aspects more clearly.

If you want to know whether or not you will have a child of your
own. In such a situation you will look towards the 5th house
condition, 5th lord and karaka of 5th house (Jupiter). If You have
Mars and Saturn in the 5th house from lagna without any aspect
from Jupiter or other natural benefic planets, one might think you
do not have a chance for children. But if you looked at the same
chart from the Moon lagna, Jupiter might aspect the 5th house or
might be placed in the 5th house blessing the 5th house. A positive
aspect from Venus, Moon, Mercury and Jupiter is necessary to see
the birth of a child, whether from lagan placement or aspect or
alternative lagna placement.
You will use the same method in divisional charts. If you are
looking for indication for the timing of children then you will look
towards the D7 chart as in Saptamsa chart. You will again try to
see if Jupiter and other positive or natural benefic planets aspect or
sit in the 5th house.
Even when judging the effects of planetary mahadasha, you will
always use alternative ascendants. Because in my own research
planet seem to give very strong results from Moon lagna more than
even main lagna chart.
Sun ascendant is used very last or as the 3rd alternative, but first
two are the most important.

## Mutual Aspects between Planets:

What are Mutual Aspects?
Mutual aspects are when two planets are looking directly at each
other, as most do when they are opposite each other, or are 7
houses apart.

## Example 1:

Jupiter and Saturn are both looking at each other. This is a classic
example of mutual aspects. They are both infusing their qualities
upon each other. What does it really mean to have this mutual
aspect in astrology?
The way to read this is by looking at them individually. When you
are judging how Saturn is doing, you can see Saturn is in the 4th
House in the sign of Libra, and Jupiter is in the 10th House in the
sign of Aries.
### 1. You should know what Saturn is and what Saturn is doing.

### 2. You should know What Saturn in the 4th House means.
### 3. You should know what Saturn in Libra means.
Combine the first 3 together. Once you have done that, you should
add the qualities of Jupiter upon Saturn. This means you should
add positions and optimistic qualities upon Saturn.

## When trying to judge Jupiter's situation in the chart:

### 1. You should know what Jupiter means is in astrology.
### 2. You should know what Jupiter in the 10th House means.
### 3. You should know what Jupiter in the sign of Aries means.
After knowing what Jupiter is doing in the chart, add the element
of Jupiter aspect upon Jupiter. Saturn is a natural malefic; a planet
that brings strictness, discipline, organization, and structure upon
things, including delay. Jupiter in the 10th House makes career
advancement difficult, and no one is going to spoon feed the native
with status. In Jupiter's time period, one will have to work hard
and long hours with tremendous discipline to achieve career
success. The sheer optimism and luck of Jupiter won't help this
time because Jupiter's final results are being affected by the aspect
of Saturn.
If you want to go deeper, you can see Saturn rules the 7th and 8th
House in the horoscope, which means the native will face career
fluctuations, and many transformations in life to advance their
career. Saturn’s aspect also shows that one's home will influence
discipline and hard work, including their spouse. Career success
will be delayed and will be frustrating because Jupiter in the 10th
House makes the native think they can easily achieve anything in
life, but Saturn brings them back to reality. Since Saturn is exalted
in Libra, their success will be eminent, and the native could
revolutionize their industry.

## Example 2:

Mutual aspect between Saturn and Mars. Mars and Saturn have
unique special aspects without being directly opposite to each
other. They can aspect each other like examples 2B and 2C.

## Example 2B:

In Example 2B, Mars with his 4th aspect looks at the 7th House
where Saturn is placed, and Saturn in the 7th House aspects Mars

with the 10th House in the 4th House. This is another example of
mutual aspect, as they both can see each other.

## Example 2C:

Mars and Saturn are looking at each other with their 4th and 10th
aspect, but they also have parivartan yoga between them as they
are sitting in each other's sign. This is a very strong mutual aspect.

## Example 2 (Repeated):

How would you study this?
Again, we have to judge the planets individually. Saturn and Mars
are perhaps the most destructive mutual aspects. Saturn is the
karaka of oil, and Mars is the karaka of fire. When oil and fire
meet, they create a massive fire and explosion. This mutual aspect
becomes a warzone because a battle is happening between the two.
Mars aspect on Saturn makes Saturn aggressive and forceful about
discipline and organization within the home. There is a military
environment designed by both parents. Saturn's aspect upon Mars
shows that Mars' action will be disciplined and organized to fight
any kind of battle. In the end, this can also be a blessing for a
fighter, politician, or corporate executive because they know the
art of war and how each battle is won.
If you are going to judge any aspect of Mars or Saturn, you will
still apply this theory. Mars in the 10th House aspects the 5th
House, but Mars is also being molded by the aspect of Saturn.
Now, you would read Mars aspect upon the 5th house with a bit
more detail. Mars’ aspect not only shows aggressive and
competitive behavior towards children, but Mars also wants to
apply discipline and hard work towards their children. They may
even physically abuse children in order to perfect them. The 5th

House represents education, and this can show that one will chose
a very technical field like engineering or architecture, as both
Saturn and Mars are the karakas of land. Before jumping to a
conclusion of what the aspect is doing, you have to see what aspect
that particular planet is receiving, and whose aspect you are trying
to judge. Notice the yoga that is being formed. Mars and Saturn
both are in panch mahapurush yoga, as Mars is in his own sign in
Kendra, and Saturn is exalted in Kendra. Mars is also forming
ruchika yoga. The mutual aspect between these two yogas are
extremely good for a career in real estate, government, military,
and sports. In the mahadasha and antar dasha of Saturn and Mars,
one will suddenly gain professional success.

## Example 2C (Repeated):

This particular chart has two special events happening: a mutual
aspect and parivartan yoga. How would you read this?
First we see that Saturn is debilitated in Aries, while Mars is
exalted in Capricorn. A neecha banga raja yoga occurring, which is
the cancellation of debilitation. Saturn is receiving the support of
Mars, or of males including one’s spouse to uplift their career. In
terms of parivartan yoga, the native deals with other people well,

including their spouse. They are content with their public image,
unless their career didn't go as planned. Then the native
immediately calls upon their male friends and masses to help them
get back on track. Since Saturn and Mars aspect each other with
their 4th and 10th aspect, it shows one will be very aggressive and
assertive when dealing with others. They have a straightforward,
military approach to their life and the lives of others.
Besides the neecha banga of Saturn which helps the native, it's
actually strong planets in the sign of planets which are debilitated
that really makes a difference. Even if Jupiter is present in the 7th
House beside Mars, it would still help the native since Jupiter is
sitting in Saturn's sign, Capricorn. Although Jupiter is debilitated
in Capricorn, it provides natural positive support. If Mars is in
Capricorn and Jupiter in Aquarius, there would be a much stronger
point of Saturn's support system.
I will share the meaning of basic mutual aspects between planets.
Remember, mutual aspects don't have a single meaning. You have
to look at each planet individually and see what aspects from other
planets are doing. You also have to see the planet that's affecting
the aspect. Which house does it control? Is it a functional malefic
or functional benefic? Is it a natural malefic or natural benefic?
Below we have some examples of functional malefic, but mainly
it's basic mutual aspects.

## RAHU/KETU AXIS

Both Rahu and Ketu are looking at any planet that is in
conjunction with them, which can create a tug of war between two
extreme energies. This is why you have to be careful when
drawing a conclusion about this mutual aspect. Along with a
mutual aspect, a conjunction is occurring. This is why I didn't give
you the 7th aspect, or Rahu and Ketu through houses. After the
Sun and Moon, I will not do mutual aspects of Rahu and Ketu

because this axis will mainly give the result of the conjunction and
a piece of the 7th aspect of each planet.
There are no mutual aspects between Sun, Venus and Mercury
since they are always within 48 degrees of each other.
SUNMOON - When the Sun and Moon have a mutual aspect in
any house, it means that you were born on a full moon, unless of
course the Moon's degree has crossed the Sun's, which means it's a
waning moon, yet the effects of a bright moon are always present.
This exchange of two energies shows that the mind and soul are
connected, and the ego is not easily affected. The Moon's bright
light means one can shine upon this planet due to their career
choices because the Sun is the karaka of career. Their ego and
confidence is content, and they are not shy about performing in
front of others. The native's mother is greatly affected by the
father. Both parents will be involved in the native's upbringing,
and their emotional and psychological state. It also reflects that
one's emotions are greatly impacted by the opinion of others. Their
ego relies upon the approval of their parents and other people.
SUNMARS - When the Sun and Mars have a mutual aspect, it
can create a forceful, aggressive, and assertive personality. They
are confident and hot headed. Often, this aspect makes someone
work in uniform, like the police, military, a judge, or sports athlete.
Mars represents lands, and the Sun shows authority and
government. With this mutual aspect, one will acquire government
property during the maha and antar dasha of such planets. The
father becomes a major authority figure in life, and may even
physically discipline the native. There is a major power struggle
with oneself and what their father is trying to teach them. If this
aspect occurs between 1st and 7th, 2nd and 8th, or 12th and 6th,
one will have to take anger management classes. The ego becomes

inflated and action oriented, and the native has too much
authoritative power.
SUNJUPITER – The mutual aspect between Jupiter and the Sun
creates an ego that has a soft, spiritual side. One has great respect
and understanding of culture, religion, and philosophy. They tend
to become a preacher and teacher in life to everyone they meet,
and their personality is about following the right path. This aspect
makes the native interested in the field of government law, since
the Sun is government, and Jupiter represents the law. The native
could use their knowledge to help govern the society. One’s father
becomes a great teacher and coach. No matter where Jupiter
aspects the Sun, there is always an optimistic feeling within the
native, and their confidence is never at risk. Both the Sun and
Jupiter are the karaka of spirituality, and if they happen to have a
mutual aspect in the 1st and 7th, 12th and 6th, or 3rd and 9th, they
will be interested in a spiritual path and preaching.
SUNSATURN - Saturn is our work ethic and the Sun is our
personality and ego. This mutual aspect can bring duty towards the
houses where this mutual aspect is occurring. Although these two
are enemies, they still stand for the right things in life and are
conservative. They feel that everyone needs to be responsible for
their own actions. In a way, they are your typical conservative
republican. They believe in hard work and making things happen
through taking initiative. This is a strong position for a government
worker or corporate leader. The father of the native is disciplined,
organized, and structured, and would expect the same from the
native. Saturn is a cold planet, which means the relationship with
one’s father is similar to a general or soldier.
SUNRAHU - The mutual aspect of the Sun and Rahu can either
be extremely good or bad. Rahu amplifies things and expands

them out of proportion. The good side of Rahu shines when the
Sun is exalted, or in his own sign of Leo. The bad side occurs
when the Sun is debilitated, or in an enemy sign like Taurus,
Capricorn, or Aquarius. If the Sun is strong, then one's ego is
expanded, and they feel they can achieve anything. They usually
have an extroverted personality. The relationship with their father
is friendly, and he could be a government diplomat, businessman,
or a stage performer. If the Sun is weak, then one's father was
absent due to foreign travel, or simply walked out of the native's
life. Under these circumstances, the native becomes shy,
introverted, and lacks confidence. The father becomes critical of
the native, and always finds flaws.
SUN-KETU – The Sun and Ketu mutual aspect work exactly like
the Sun and Rahu mutual aspect. If the Sun is strong, or exalted in
his own sign, then Ketu will provide spirituality in life. If the Sun
is debilitated or in an enemy sign, then one faces cruelty in their
life. If a strong and exalted Sun receives an aspect from Ketu, then
one will have a spiritual relationship with their father and their
personality is less about ego. They extend their hand in spiritual
and religious activities. If the Sun is debilitated and weak, then
one's father could be absent in life, or the native may experience
physical or sexual abuse from the father. They always think about
violence and destruction and may even turn into a criminal.
MOONMERCURY - This mutual aspect can be quite black and
white depending upon which planet is stronger. Mercury is logic,
business, and calculation, while the Moon is intuition, emotions,
and feelings. This can make someone either too logical or too
emotional about their decisions. If the Moon is in Scorpio and
Mercury is in Taurus, that means the Moon is debilitated and
Mercury is in a strong earth-friendly sign. How would we read
this? When we try to analyze the Moon, we see that though the
Moon is debilitated, there is still solid logical thinking. There is
less emotional impact of their surroundings, and their valuable
possessions have meaning. In a way, the Moon is being saved by

Mercury. If we try to study Mercury, we can see the irrational and
emotional Moon looking at Mercury. This shows that one's logic
and calculative thinking can be impacted by emotional and
impulsive decisions. They might be attached to their valuables
which can suddenly be lost. Their educational pursuits might be
very intense to the point of psychologically impacting them.
MOONVENUS – The Moon and Venus mutual aspect can show
one's thought process and emotional stability revolves around
luxury, assets, art, and beauty. The native's emotions and feelings
are now mixed with sensuality and creativity. The mind craves fine
food and dining, along with luxury in life. In a negative sense, this
can make the native snobbish and extravagant. They may
overspend, as the mind becomes greedy for material wealth. The
relationships of the native are mainly based on feelings and
emotions. They can fall in and out of love quickly as the Moon
becomes fickle minded. Creativity can make the person very
passionate about singing, acting, and performing arts. If the Moon
is debilitated, then the mind will be very sexual, and will crave
lewd acts. They form relationships with ill minded people, and
may be too rational in love matters and relationships. They choose
partners based on security and appearance.
MOONMARS – The Mutual aspect between Mars and the Moon
can be very powerful. If Mars is exalted, and the Moon is in its
own sign of Cancer, the native is driven, and will do whatever it
takes to find success in life. They are very action oriented people
who are engaged in sports and physical activity on a regular basis.
The mind is constantly active, and thinks about how to keep the
body physically fit. On the negative side, this can create anger in a
person if Mars is debilitated. They can be impulsive about many
things, especially in this house, where the Moon is placed. They
get into physical fights with others about small matters, and can
even commit criminal acts.

MOONJUPITER - This can be a beautiful mutual aspect. The
mind receives positive vibrations and hope for the future through
Jupiter. Knowing the Moon and Jupiter are best friends, the mind
is always looking at the brighter side of life. They can go through
any kind of bad circumstances, and always find a way to smile and
be happy. Jupiter expands the quality of planets so that it aspects in
a positive way. There are always good and bad sides of the
equation. If the Moon is debilitated or waning, then Jupiter can
bring expansion of emotions. If the native becomes too emotional,
they may suffer from depression. If Jupiter is debilitated, then
there could be a sudden loss or gain of peace of mind. Such sudden
fluctuations can be very daunting for the native. The native has a
beautiful relationship with their mother in most circumstances. She
can also be a guru or life teacher.
MOONSATURN – The mutual aspect between Saturn and the
Moon can be quite stressful on the Moon. The mind wants to think
positively and be free, whereas Saturn is about boundaries,
restrictions, delays, and cold reality. Such an aspect can bring a
pessimistic attitude about achieving goals. They have dark
thoughts, and are constantly depressed because the mind does not
feel free. Saturn also represents social welfare and taking care of
the elderly. This aspect can make the native fight for social
freedom and privileges in society. They love routine work, and
may not like taking on too many challenges on a regular basis. The
mind faces disappointments in life, and one builds their discipline
and organization skills by learning from these situations. If the
Moon is debilitated, then it can make someone manic depressive or
suicidal. One must also look at the nakshatra. If Saturn is
debilitated, then the mind is scattered and disorganized, and their
work makes them lethargic. The mind can also be very cold
towards others because Saturn latches onto one thing, and will not
rest until it is attained.

MOON -RAHU and MOON - KETU - A mutual aspect of Rahu
and the Moon can be problematic because there was a lunar eclipse
happening during the time when the native was born. Rahu can
make someone an innovator or revolutionary when it aspects the
mind, or Moon. If the Moon is exalted or in its own sign, then
one's emotional stability expands, and they will think outside the
box. They may bring new or foreign elements into their thought
process. However, the Rahu and Moon conjunction is better than
aspect because if Rahu aspects the Moon, that means Ketu is in
conjunction with the Moon. This creates a tug of war. Most of the
time, Rahu and Ketu aspect and conjunction make the native
hallucinate and have panic attacks. They may detach the native
from their mother or homeland. Ketu aspect upon the Moon can
bring confusion and dissatisfaction from one's mother. The native
can also suffer from autism or some other mental condition. An
exalted Moon in aspect with Ketu can bring great spiritual
teachings through their mother, and one will be a natural psychic.
MERCURYMARS - Mercury is speech, communication,
business, language, and education. When Mars aspects Mercury,
one’s speech is quite powerful and direct. They are also very
mechanical and technical people because Mercury is about
working with one’s hands, and Mars puts more action into them.
Depending upon which house this mutual aspect is occurring in,
the results will correlate. One has no problem defining orders of
authority verbally. When Mars receives Mercury's aspect, one's
actions are very calculative and logical. They make sound
decisions, and are clever enough to win battles. In their youth, the
native plays many sports, and may love martial arts if this mutual
aspect occurs in the 3rd and 9th, 12th and 6th, or 10th and 4th
house. They love engineering and computer programming, as it is
second nature to them.
MERCURYJUPITERThis mutual aspect brings a great deal of
information and knowledge. They are quite smart, clever, and have
many academic achievements. When we talk about Mercury in

astrology, the communication and speech of the native is very
articulate, intellectual, and full of philosophical ideas. They are
always willing to guide others, as they believe their intelligence is
superior. They make great mathematicians, accountants, writers,
and even businessmen. Mercury’s aspect on Jupiter shows that
one's higher beliefs are tools of communication and learning. Ideas
about religion must be practical and easy to implement in order to
better the world. When they write, a sense of higher spiritual
knowledge comes through. They make great communicative
teachers.
MERCURYSATURN – The mutual aspect of Mercury and
Saturn is positive. Mercury and Saturn are friends, so their aspect
will help the native. When Saturn aspects Mercury, one has a
logical mind and can focus on their studies. They are able to
withstand all types of challenges to achieve success in business.
One has a very strict, formal, and conservative way of
communicating. They are very organized when it comes to
collecting data, ideas, or problem solving. Mercury's aspect on
Saturn will make Saturn's skill of discipline and organization more
relaxed. They won't take themselves as seriously because Mercury
makes life about more than a routine job. The discipline and
organization skills of Saturn will have a communicative feel. They
share their ideas with others about how to make things happen.
Saturn is a natural malefic, and an aspect upon the karaka of
speech could delay the speaking ability of the native. It may give
them ill speech and the capacity to lie if Saturn is in a bad house
and sign, or rules a negative house.
VENUSMARS - Mars and Venus can create a very intense
mutual aspect. When Mars aspects Venus, one's ambition for
relationships are intensified, and passion and romance becomes
more sexual than sensual. These people have quite a high sex
drive,
and
seek
physical
interaction
more
than
verbal
communication because Mars is the planet of action more than
thought. When it comes to relationships, they can be quite

impulsive, jumping from one to the next until they find that perfect
physical match. When Venus is looking at Mars, their actions are
derived from love and passion. The action of the native becomes
more artistic and sensual. They are quite feminine in their
approach to life. If Mars and Venus are both retrograde at the same
time, it can make someone gay, as the roles of both Mars and
Venus are reversed. If either is debilitated, it can show a sexual
deviant or someone who has made a habit of extramarital affairs.
One can be physically attacked during sexual relations if Venus is
debilitated and in the 8th House.
VENUSJUPITER – A Venus and Jupiter mutual aspect can
rarely be negative unless they rule 6th, 8th or 12th House. Even
though they are considered enemies, they are still the natural
benefics in astrology. In other words, two saints won't damage
each other, but rather change each other's outlook. When Jupiter
aspects Venus, the native's sense of pleasure and lust in
relationships takes on a higher form. They do not fall in love easily
because their values and standards have now risen to a different
level. Until they have a spiritual and philosophical connection with
someone, they may not get involved. Venus receives high morals
from Jupiter, who shows that love and relationships are not only
based physical attraction, but also an inner soul connection. They
enjoy classical music, art, and movies, as they bring a sense of
value. Venus’ aspect on Jupiter brings creativity, higher learning,
and knowledge. They are able to turn the dry subjects of religion
and philosophy into something enriching and fulfilling. They
don’t shy away from taking the stage. As a teacher or preacher,
they may enjoy managing entertainment events. Such mutual
aspects can also make someone a great stage or film director.
VENUSSATURN - The mutual aspect of Venus and Saturn is a
double edged sword. When Saturn shares its aspect with Venus,
the ability to form relationships becomes a bit cold and delayed.
Venus is the karaka of marriage, and Saturn is the delayer of
things. This aspect of Saturn delays marriage to one’s late 20’s or

between the ages 32-36. Venus is creativity and Saturn is work.
This mutual aspect gives Venus tremendous creativity and ability
to work vigorously towards an artistic career. When Venus aspects
Saturn, it shows that one loves routine work. The person forms
relationships with older and more mature people who are stable
and content. They appreciate law and order, and find pleasure in
helping the elderly and in the non-profit sector. In some ways,
their work becomes their spouse.
MARSJUPITER - The mutual aspect between Mars and Jupiter
is one of an energetic warrior. When Jupiter shares his aspect with
Mars, the native's action and energy has higher meaning. They
become a warrior with the purpose of teaching. They want to
achieve something big in their life, but it must have a philosophical
and spiritual meaning. This can also make for an energized athlete
or soldier. They use their energy to learn more about the divine
self, and to motivate for a higher purpose. When Mars aspects
Jupiter, one can be a forceful and tough teacher. They become
powerful coaches, because it requires tough love and guidance to
make a good athlete. Jupiter is about the law and Mars is a soldier,
which can represent a very aggressive criminal lawyer who uses
his energy and forcefulness to win cases. When it comes to higher
wisdom, Jupiter may be too impulsive to fall for many gurus.
MARSSATURN - This mutual aspect is the one to watch out
for. It can be equally good and bad at the same time. Mars is fire
and Saturn is oil. When fire and oil mix, there is a massive
explosion. Such mutual aspects can create a power struggle and a
warzone in both houses. When Saturn aspects Mars, the energy of
Mars is tamed, focused, disciplined, and organized. When Mars
takes action, it is careful and willing to wait for the right
opportunity to strike. Saturn, the mechanic, gets intertwined with
engineering. Whenever these two look at each other, you have to
see which is strong by sign and house placement, because
whichever wins this aspect will have the most impact. In most
cases, you want Saturn to win because Saturn brings patience to

Mars and cools him down. Fighters, athletes, and martial artists
have such combinations.
JUPITER-SATURN - This mutual aspect is quite mature, serious,
and hard working. When Saturn shares its aspect with Jupiter,
one's optimism becomes cold and more realistic. They know
success in life doesn't stem from the power of attraction, but a
strong work ethic. They have tremendous determination,
organization, and discipline in their belief system and knowledge
base. Jupiter represents the law, and Saturn is the judge. Saturn's
aspect makes Jupiter judgmental, and they want balance in society.
This is one of the strongest mutual aspects for a lawyer, judge, and
a police officer. Jupiter’s aspect on Saturn makes one's career and
sense of duty a way to find optimism. Saturn eases up on the
native, and does not delay events in life. The native doesn't feel
tired or aged at work because of responsibility. Overall, Saturn
gets the better end of this aspect.

## RAHU/KETU Special Aspects On Planets:

These aspect are one sided, meaning neither Rahu nor Ketu are
being affected by such aspects except when Jupiter aspects them.
These aspects occur in their 5 and 9 marks.
Rahu aspect on Mercury: Rahu's aspect on Mercury can be a
tricky one. On one hand the native becomes a revolutionary,
business savvy, and a very clever communicator. On the other
hand, Rahu is a natural malefic who will do whatever it takes to
gain material wealth, even if it requires deceit. Mercury is the
karaka of speech and language, and Rahu makes one a
pathological liar if Mercury is not exalted. They will think outside
the box when it comes to business, and love learning foreign
languages. Such an aspect makes one a very dramatic, spotlight

driven, politician. If they are writers, they break the social norm in
their stories, and may write only science fiction. If one runs
through the mahadasha of Mercury, they will most likely pursue
education in distant lands.
Rahu aspect on Venus: One is obsessed with love, relationships,
and the sensual pleasures of life. Often Rahu's aspect on Venus
shows sudden luxuries and wealth in life. One marries a foreigner,
or someone of a different faith. They have an unusual way of
forming relationships. One may get married against the wishes of
their parents or society, and may only have a court marriage. If
Venus is in its own sign or exalted, Rahu's aspect will be very
positive. Rahu also gives an interest in the arts and performing.
Their performances can break social boundaries, like acting in
movies which are not socially accepted. If one becomes a singer,
their lyrics will be about breaking taboos.
Rahu aspect on Jupiter: Whenever Rahu aspects Jupiter with 5 or
9 aspect, Jupiter will always aspect Rahu, too. Rahu and Jupiter are
not enemies. When Rahu aspects Jupiter, one's higher belief
system and philosophical perspective of society moves towards
breaking laws, rules, and social norms. Rahu will challenge
Jupiter's belief; meaning the native does not want to take satwic
knowledge as is, but needs proof to follow any of the endeavors of
Jupiter. They would rather believe in a new religion, or a faith that
does not coexist with the same rules and laws as the present
establishment. When Jupiter aspects Rahu, it makes Rahu crave
preaching towards the house where it’s placed. Rahu wants to be
known as one who has divine knowledge and works hard to attain
it because Jupiter shares its positive aspect on Rahu. The native
usually finds gurus and teachers in foreign lands, and they
complete their studies abroad or in a different state from where
they were born.
Rahu aspect on Saturn: Rahu's aspect on Saturn might be
confusing. The native becomes obsessed with discipline, structure,

and organization skills. They can be specific and orderly to a point
where they may suffer from Obsessive Compulsive Disorder. Rahu
and Saturn are both natural malefic, and bring anxiety. This aspect
can create paranoia within the native, and fear of losing their focus
and organizational skills. This is why they are so adamant about
having everything neat and clean. Rahu loves to break the law and
gain material wealth quickly, and the aspect on lawmaker Saturn
does not help. The native could be forced to break the law for their
own materialism, and desire to climb the ladder of status. Saturn
wants to help society and the unprivileged. Rahu recognizes this,
and though it wants to help, it makes the native greedy to attain
wealth through such work. If Rahu is in the 12th House, and
Saturn is in the 6th House, one may be caught doing illegal drug
deals with the poor, or taking money from non-profit
organizations.
Ketu aspect on Mercury: One finds themselves disconnected
with the world because they do not want to communicate. Mercury
is the karaka of business, language, education, and writing. This
aspect will make the native quiet, distant from the world of
business, and their intelligence would be focused on the non-
material world. One's siblings will be distant from the native. If
Ketu is exalted, then the aspect will provide spiritual intelligence
and knowledge. One will communicate for the welfare of the
world. But Ketu is severe, and brings sudden events like Rahu. In
the mahadasha of Mercury, one could be separated from siblings
and friends, and may suffer from fear, anxiety, and panic attacks.
Ketu aspect on Venus: When Ketu aspects Venus, one's love for
fine dining, drinking, and material wealth may bring emptiness and
dissatisfaction. The native forms only spiritual relationships with
others. Whenever they try to look for material or physical love in a
relationship, they find themselves detached and unsatisfied. Such
an aspect can also make the native a stripper or nude artist because
they are unattached to relationships, love, art, and beauty. They are
not insulted by others for breaking taboos. Often spiritual sages

have either aspect or conjunction of Ketu with Venus. Only after
becoming detached from the material world will one find divine
happiness. This is especially true if Venus is debilitated, or in the
sign of Scorpio; two places which are very harsh for material
success and relationships, yet great for spiritual life.
Ketu aspect on Mars: One's action and energy levels may go
down. They are not eager to take action or fight. This is different if
Mars is in conjunction with Ketu, because Ketu is becoming Mars.
With this aspect, Ketu does not become Mars and it brings its own
qualities. Mars, the warrior and fighter, suddenly becomes
uninterested in meaningless fights. The native becomes a peaceful
warrior, someone who seeks to bring peace; someone who would
fight for freedom of spiritual expression. There is distance and
boredom through male friends and brothers. This aspect also
makes one an electrical or software engineer, as Ketu shows
electronics and Mars shows engineering. Mars also represents
land, so if the native acquires property, they are generally
dissatisfied.
Ketu aspect on Jupiter: Again, like Rahu, Jupiter can also aspect
Ketu if Ketu aspects Jupiter from its 5th and 9th aspect. One is
unattached to gurus, traditional education, and mundane
philosophical discussions. They search for isolation, and receive
moksha from all religions and rituals. There is no desire to take
part in religious festivals unless Jupiter is in his own sign or
exalted. In this case, the native will be educated, traditional,
spiritual, and intuitive. One may not have any attachment to the
laws of the land and may break them; not because they are a
criminal, but because they believe no man should enforce any laws
upon another. Jupiter's aspect on Ketu makes Ketu believe in the
power of spirit, intuition, and meditation. They are extremely
peaceful, and may become teachers of yoga, astrology, or
spirituality.

Ketu aspect on Saturn: One has no attachment to status and
work. They are dissatisfied with their position in the world, and
have no desire to have a job. Rather, Ketu brings fear from
working and having a routine. They lack discipline and order, and
all they want to do is find themselves in the jungle of authority,
status, and rank. Saturn also represents order and the law. The
native has fear of the law because they are on the wrong side of it,
or they are cautious about breaking it. This aspect can be found in
monks or in the occult world of tarot and astrology. Ketu makes
one numb to their losses. Saturn can bring losses in its mahadasha,
but Ketu's aspect shows that one is not attached to having losses in
life, and understands karma. If such an aspect occurs while Saturn
is in 6th, 8th, or 12th House, someone may be willing to break the
law because they have no fear of it.
Nakshatra Parivartan yogas: (Sukshma Parivartan)
This is a unique concept you may not have heard about. This is
about exchange between two nakshatra lords. To even understand
the most simplest things in this chapter you must know what
Nakshatras are. They are the 27 constellation working behind the
12 zodiac sign, which each zodiac sign having 2 1/2 nakshatra
working behind the scenes, and each nakshatra is controlled by a
certain planet. I can't list everything here as I have already done
that in my first book: Astrology @ The speed of light and made
videos on each nakshatra on Youtube.
For those who are familiar with the basics of nakshatra this will be
something you will need to know. Rohini nakshatra is ruled by the
Moon, while Mula is ruled by the sound node Ketu. If in your
horoscope Moon is in the nakshatra of Mula and Ketu is in the
nakshatra of Rohini, in such case you have what's called
"Nakshatra Parivartan". There is a exchange of energy between
these two planets. During the mahadasha and antar dasha of Moon
and Ketu will activate each other's energy including the house

where they sit. If Moon is in the 2nd house that means Ketu is in
the 7th house, and during their activated mahadasha the native
would let go of attachment with relationships yet will suddenly
find interest in other people who are on a moksha path while at the
same time there would be sudden loss of savings or assets because
Moon is sitting in one the most trouble nakshatra of Mula. But
major loss will be felt in Ketu's mahadasha more so than Moon's
mahadasha. In Moon's mahadasha the native may lose some
savings by marriage, but only in period of Ketu's antar dasha. But
since Marriage is the most important part of any person's life,
Ketu's mahadasha would be worry some. Ketu would not only
effect the 7th house but also activate the 2nd house where Moon is,
which shows separation from family and mother, perhaps due to
marriage or moving to foreign lands.
If Jupiter was in the nakshatra of Pushya ruled by Saturn in the
sign of Cancer and Saturn was in the Nakshatra ruled by Vishaka
in the sign of Libra, they both are now in what is called sambandha
or "Connection". If for example this took place in Cancer
ascendant horoscope, that means Saturn is in the 1st house and
Jupiter is in the 4th house. In each others maha and antar dasha
