they will activate themselves that will show one will be involved
in the matter of property, land and mother related issues. Both
Jupiter and Saturn are functional malefic for Cancer ascendant,
hence will show sudden and chronic health problems related to gal
bladder and liver.
But such things can happen in each other maha and antar dasha
regardless if they are in each others nakshatra, then why such
yoga? This occurs because both planets are also acting like each
other. Saturn will act like Jupiter and will try to show optimism
and rise of self esteem even though he isn't supposed as a natural
malefic, the native's self efforts and working on marriage will feel
like a task filled with hope while Jupiter will show tremendous
hard work needed in order to deal with the issues of family and

property which will happen with delay and may even have sudden
unexpected results.
Any planet will change his or her result based on the nakshatra
they are placed in, especially in their activated time periods.
Jupiter sitting in Ketu's nakshatra will not only give sudden urges
for philosophy, higher leaning and spirituality but also sudden
interest in occult, magic and things of other world.
One must know the meaning and symbolism behind each
nakshatra when studying a chart. I am currently working on the
book which will focus just on planets and nakshatra.

## Planets in Nakshatra and their Aspects:

When a planet throws its rays upon other planets, its aspect not
only has the main significance of the planet, but also of the zodiac
sign which clarifies its dignity and the nakshatra it's placed in. If
you really want to quantify astrology you must understand
nakshatras. I am sure you read about nakshatra from my first book
and watched my videos on how Moon nakshatra becomes very
important in terms of setting your personality and thinking pattern.

In the same way each planet will be effected through the naksahtra
it sits in at the time of your birth. Like in regular astrology each
planet's dignity and personality is molded by the sign it sits in, in
the same manner nakshatra also mold the dignity and characteristic
of a planet.

## Example of Nakshatra aspect 1:

This chart shows Mars in the ascendant in the sign of Cancer and
Venus in the 7th house in the sign of Capricorn. Mars is at twenty
five degrees and Venus is at twenty degrees. Now, by looking at
the degrees and knowing the basics of nakshatras you will see
Mars is in the nakshatra of Ashlesha ruled by Mercury which
symbolizes serpent or snake. Venus is in the nakshatra of Saravan
which is about emotions and nourishment and a very soft nakshatra
of holding lot of emotions within. Mars aspects Venus with his 7th
aspect of reflection and intense desire for union. Mars is also
reaching towards his most debilitated state which occurs at 28
degrees. Mars is in the ascendant showing that personality of the
native is very much of a serpent who will do whatever it takes to
get things in life through clever means. The individual is short
tempered and manipulative through their communication since
Mercury is the ruler of Mars' naksahtra. Venus is in the 7th house

of relationship and marriage while Venus herself is the karaka of
marriage and spouse. When Mars aspects Venus, he not only
throws his own natural rays of desire, passion and energy towards
the marriage but his rays are now mixing up with the sign of
Cancer where he is debilitated and his dignity is very low. This
shows that such an aspect is filled with mostly sexual desire and
desire to fulfill ones own physical needs. Mars being the nakshatra
of Ashlesha shows that the person would not only manipulate the
spouse for their own needs but will also cheat and have affairs
because the nakshatra placement of Mars is changing his dignity
further. There will be lot of arguments, power struggle and even
physical violence in the marriage if there are no positive aspect on
Mars or Venus.
Since Mars aspects Venus and the 7th house, both of whom show
marriage; the spouse of the native will also have certain qualities
of a manipulator and cheater. This is where the power struggle will
come in. Both husband and wife will try to take advantage of each
other. Venus, too, is in mutual aspect to Mars, but Mars won't be
effected by Venus so much because Mars is physically more
powerful than Venus and he is in the 1st house of self. But Venus
aspect on Mars while sitting in the sign of Capricorn and in the
nakshatra of Saravan will show a person who is emotionally torn
in their personality and do not know how to respond emotionally
to others or understand others emotions, especially spouse.

## Example of Nakshatra aspect 2:

In the same chart Jupiter is sitting in the 9th house in the sign of
Pisces while in the nakshatra of Revati, again controlled by
Mercury. Jupiter in the 9th house closely aspects Mars in the
ascendant with this fifth aspect. Jupiter being the guru and a divine
teacher while in the 9th house of higher learning, sitting in the
nakshatra of deep spirituality is now controlling Mars' behavior.
Now the individual is more calm and spiritual on his approach
towards spouse and other people. The manipulation has been
decreased by 70%, although some attributes will always be present
throughout life, you can safely say that the individual will be a
much better person who will provide spiritual and loving passion
to the spouse.

## Example of Nakshatra aspect 3:

In example 3 you can see there is Saturn sitting in the 10th house
at eight degrees which is the nakshatra of Ashiwini controlled by
Ketu in the zodiac sign of Aries. Saturn in the 10th house aspects
the 7th house with his 10th aspect. Saturn and Mars are both
natural malefic planets no matter if they are exalted or well placed.
In this case Saturn is not debilitated because Saturn is truly
debilitated at 20 degrees Aries. But, Saturn is in the nakshatra of
Ketu, which is about curing others and digging deep into healing
people. But, the sign of Aries is tamasic sign which is about
aggression, competition and leadership. Now you have two of the
most natural malefic planets looking at soft Venus in the 7th house
while one is acting like Mercury and the other like Ketu. Saturn is
brining detachment with his aspect and more discipline and
organization needing in marriage including delay in marriage.
In conclusion, although the personality of the native is very
spiritual and easy going, marriage will still suffer due to no benefic
aspect on Venus. If the individual marries late in life, like after 36,
there will be far less complication in life than if they were to get
married early when both Saturn and Mars are immature. Such
immaturity is strongly causing physical harm to the spouse but not
the native.

## Example of Nakshatra aspect 4:

In example 4 you and see how Saturn and Mars both aspect the
ascendant of the native, this can show harm to the individual
physically through mother and the spouse not matter if it's a male
or female. First house is the house of self, which means threat to
self not others.

## Example of Nakshatra aspects 5:

In example 5 the native has far lesser threat from the spouse than
the mother, because Mars's aspect is being molded and changed by
spiritual Jupiter. Only Saturn poses a great threat, but threat of
Mars remains by 30%. If Jupiter's aspect was more than 10-15
degrees away from Mars then the changes of a bad spouse
increases.
Always check the Navamsa (D9) chart to give a final prediction.
I can't talk about all planets in nakshatra because that will take far
more time and space in this book. I will write a separate book
planets in nakshatra.

## Case Studies of Aspects:

Here are 3 examples of people who have suffered from mental
illness, anger, or severe depression.

## Case Study 1:

## Date:

February 9th, 1980
Time: 19:45 pm
Place: New Delhi, India
This is a chart is of a woman who suffers from multiple personality
disorder and anger issues. There are 3 hot planets in the ascendant
in the sign of Leo, which is the hottest of all. The Moon receives a
sandhi aspect from Saturn, because the Moon is about to enter into
Scorpio; it's most debilitated state, while receiving an aspect of
Saturn, which is looking at the 4th House at 2 degrees. Most
people will not see this unless they are familiar with the dance of
degrees. This shows that the mind is going towards its most
intense, emotional state. By receiving an aspect from Saturn in the
4th House, the mind is quite depressed and held hostage from
reality. Saturn’s aspect on the 4th House shows that peace of mind

is lost due to responsibility at home and some serious issues with
one’s mother. Mars’ 4th aspect is also on the 4th House, bringing
power struggles and differences of opinion with her.
Mercury, which represents logic and the nervous system, is in
conjunction with Ketu, bringing unnecessary fear and anxiety
through partnerships and marriage. Jupiter shares its aspect on the
7th House trying to protect the marriage. Before we use Jupiter's
aspect we have to see the conjunction. Jupiter and Rahu are at
conjunction, meaning Rahu is becoming Jupiter and Mars, and
taking away their positive qualities. Now, when Jupiter shares its
aspect with the 7th House, it's less than half of its power. Mars
sharing its aspect on the 7th House reflects that there are power
struggles and arguments in the marriage due to ego.
Mercury in conjunction with Ketu is becoming malefic. After
becoming malefic, Mercury shares its 7th aspect upon the
ascendant, making the native clever and intelligent. Since it's in
aspect with Ketu, the intelligence will create fear and war in the
lives of others. The native may have a partner who distantly
communicates with them.

## Case Study 2:

## Date:

June, 28th 1997
Time: 7:46 am
Place: Confidential
Here is another classic example of someone with major anger
problems and bipolar disorder. First, Saturn and the Moon are in
conjunction, and are about 6 degrees apart from each other. Such a
conjunction doesn't mean mental illness all the time. Why is this a
classic case? Because the conjunction is occurring in the
ascendant, which is the head. Notice Saturn is a functional malefic
for Pisces ascendant, as its moolatrikona sign rules the 12th House.
The 12th House represents losses, imagination, spirits, and other
worlds. Such a conjunction of the Moon shows that the mind is
filled with illusion, anxiety, and depression. Before judging
Saturn's conjunction, you also have to see if Saturn receives any
aspect from a planet. In this case, it does. Saturn receives a direct
aspect form Mars in the 7th House. Not only is Saturn a functional
and natural malefic, it receives an aspect from its enemy, Mars,
from the 7th House. The Moon also receives an aspect from hot
and fiery Mars.

The mind is stuck in a warzone of aspects. Mars rules two good
houses of wealth and higher learning. However, Mars is a natural
malefic. This aspect on the ascendant brings luck and wealth
through other males including one’s spouse. However, there is a
major power struggle happening in the house of marriage. This is
because the functional and natural malefic Saturn aspects Mars
with its 7th aspect as well. However, marriage is sustainable.
Why? First, although debilitated Jupiter aspects the 7th House of
marriage with its 9th aspect, it brings a saving grace. Second,
Jupiter and Saturn are in parivartan yoga with each other. Saturn
rules the 11th House where Jupiter sits, and Jupiter rules the
ascendant where Saturn sits. This shows that the native had
responsibilities in life when they were young, and there was
pressure upon the mother as well. She had to take on the role of the
father, as the Sun is in the 4th House of mother. The native was
introduced to her husband through their network circle, and he is a
guru-like figure, who brought optimism, taught her about higher
values, and spiritual learning. Lord of the 7th House Mercury is
also strong in the 4th House of its own sign of Gemini. The
husband is a civil engineer, while the female was a homemaker.
The husband is seen from Jupiter. Jupiter is debilitated in the sign
of Capricorn. Jupiter is not only in parivartan yoga with Saturn,
but it’s also receiving a neecha banga raja yoga from Saturn as
well. Venus in the 5th House aspects Jupiter with its 7th aspect,
which is a natural benefic aspect. The husband is also a lover of
classical music.
There are arguments between the couple, but at the end it works
out. The wife’s emotions still fluctuate, but it balances out with
time. Remember, Jupiter in the upachaya house grows better with
time even if debilitated.

## Case Study 3:

## Date:

December 6th 1951

Time: 11:50 pm
Place: Eugene, OR
In Case Study 3, the native has a Leo ascendant. He suffers from
schizophrenia. He hears voices and hallucinates. He has been
heavily medicated which caused a brain seizure. After analyzing
this chart, I concluded that the individual was not schizophrenic,
but had a sixth sense to see entities that may be seeking his help.
Anyone in this circumstance without proper spiritual guidance
would be afraid, and declare him mentally ill.
First, Ketu is in the ascendant in the sign of Leo, which means the
individual has given up on ego and lacks self-esteem. I also see
that Ketu is in its own nakshatra of Magha. This means Ketu is
behaving as it normally would, and is unaffected by any aspect or
conjunction. This depicts that the individual was spiritual and
gifted at birth to have the ability to see things beyond a human
perspective. Ketu is the karaka of occult knowledge. The Lord of
Ketu, or the Sun, is average in the 4th House in the sign of
Scorpio. This is a sign of transformation. The Sun is directionless
because the individual is born close to midnight. The Sun also

receives an aspect from Saturn in the 2nd House, which is at
planetary war with Mars. The aspect of Saturn shows that one's
peace of mind in the 4th House is troubled, as is his relationship
with his mother. She was under tremendous pressure, as any
mother would be if their child is suffering this way. The father is
under a lot of mental stress as well because of the native. The Sun,
the karaka of father, is in the 4th House with an aspect of warlike
Saturn.
Why is the native seeing and hearing things? Why is it real and not
imaginary? The Moon is the mind, intuition, and our senses. The
Moon in this horoscope is Lord of the 12th House of the spirit
world, other dimensions, and spirituality. The Moon is also the
functional malefic for the chart. The Moon is placed in the 8th
house of secrets, occult, death, and transformation with the Lord of
the 8th House, Jupiter. This depicts that the mind of the native is
linked to other worlds, secrets, and death-like experiences. Since
Jupiter, the divine guru, is with the Moon, the experiences of the
12th and 8th House are spiritual and saintly. Jupiter is also the
Lord of the 5th House, which represents education and past life
deeds. The individual was a master of occult knowledge, and
studied death and transformation. In this life, he is meant to finish
this task.
Mars and Saturn both aspect the Moon and Jupiter in the 8th
House. There is a war happening in the 2nd House of family due to
Mars and Saturn. It reflects verbal and physical abuse in the family
by an uncle, brother, or father. Saturn is the Lord of the 6th House
of diseases, and Mars is the Lord of 4th and 9th House. Mars is the
yoga karaka for the individual, and its aspect is mostly positive.
Since Mars is in a warzone with Saturn, it shows that Mars aspect
is troublesome. Both are natural malefic. Saturn is also the karaka
of death, rebirth, and the underworld because Saturn is the natural
karaka of 6th, 8th and 12th House. Saturn is also the karaka of
chemicals, while Mars is the karaka of poisons. Both look at the
Moon in the 8th House of death. This means one will face death-

like instances through chemical substances or sharp objects. In
today's world, chemicals and poisons are used in making
pharmaceuticals. Through the lagna chart, the mind would greatly
be affected by drugs. Jupiter and the Moon are at exact
conjunction, meaning the Moon is being influenced by the 9th and
8th Lord.
Ketu also aspects Mercury. Ketu is a natural malefic, and yet for
this chart, Ketu is also very spiritual and brings the knowledge of
other worlds. Ketu's aspect shows that one's brain function is
affected by its spiritual bliss. It can bring fear and anxiety within
the native. The logical thinking of the native is greatly affected by
the spirit world. The Lord of Mercury is placed in the 8th House of
Pisces, while in the nakshatra of Uttra Bhadrapada, controlled by
Saturn, showing spirituality and the underworld.
Let's review the chart through Moon ascendant.

## Example 1:

The image shows a chart placing the Moon in the ascendant which
is another impact in Vedic Astrology.
We get a spiritual picture from this perspective. The Moon and
Jupiter are two best friends in exact conjunction. Look at the
aspect of Mars and Saturn. Saturn rules the 12th House of the spirit
world and other dimensions. It is also the house of hospitals and
asylums. Rahu, the co-ruler of the 12th House is sitting in the 12th
house, bringing the ability to see into the spirit realm. Saturn being
the Lord of the 11th, looks at both the Moon and ascendant Lord,
showing that the native is heavily impacted by the spiritual world.
Mars, being the Lord of 2nd and 9th House, shows that one is not
only receiving a divine power, but is also struggling with their
mind and the knowledge being received. Saturn and Mars are still
in a warzone in the 7th House. In marriage, fighting and arguing
are prevalent, but Saturn sustains the marriage.
When you look at Mercury, you can see that it receives the aspect
of Ketu from the 6th House and Mars from the 7th House. Both
Mars and Ketu act the same way, and are known to bring conflicts.
Ketu in the 6th House of disease shows that one's nervous system
will be impacted by unknown things which doctors will not be able

to diagnose. Notice Ketu is the co-Lord of Scorpio, and Scorpio
rules two good places in the lagna and moon rashi chart. The 9th
House represents divine grace and higher knowledge. One can tell
that Ketu's aspect, although troubled with health problems, brings
the karma of divine grace. Both co-Lords of the 9th House aspect
Mercury.
After looking at the chart thoroughly, you can see that the native is
not mentally ill, but being visited by an entity that is creating fear
and anxiety. In a past life, the native must have provoked trapped
spirits that want his help to be released.

## Example 2:

Chart of previous client who suffered a heart attack during Jupiter-
Mars-Saturn period.
The ascendant is not only filled with malefic, but also receives
aspect from malefic. Mercury is known to be neutral, but changes
in tone when sitting with a natural malefic of benefic. In this case,
Mercury is sitting with two heavy natural malefics, so naturally
Mercury becomes malefic. Saturn from the 11th House aspects the
ascendant, and Rahu is already on the 1st and 7th axis. Anytime

you have all malefics sitting or aspecting the ascendant, one will
have chronic battles with health. Not only will the native suffer a
heart attack, but also many physical fights, including a stabbing, as
Mars is the karaka of knives, bloods, cuts, and wounds. Mars is
debilitated and aspects the 8th House of death-like scenarios, while
both co-Lords of the 8th House Rahu and Saturn are looking at the
ascendant. Saturn's aspect on Mars agitates the debilitated Mars
while sitting on the ascendant’s head. The native is known to have
a short fuse and fights, while also a semi-pro baseball player. Mars
aspects the 4th House of peace of mind while debilitated, including
the 7th House of marriage and 8th House of secrets. The native has
been divorced 3 times in his life, and has many affairs.

## Example 3:

How would you read this chart?
Do a chart analysis before reading the details of this example.
### 1. First see who is in aspect to who
### 2. What mutual aspects are occurring?
### 3. Which aspects are good and bad?

1. In Example 3: Ketu aspects Saturn, Mars aspects Saturn, Rahu
aspects Jupiter, Jupiter aspects Rahu, Saturn aspects Rahu, and
Saturn aspects Mars.
2. Saturn and Mars are in mutual aspect. Rahu and Jupiter are in
mutual aspect.
3. Jupiter’s aspect on Rahu is good, while the rest are difficult
aspects. The mutual aspect between Mars and Saturn show that
one will have a power struggle and difficulty creating structure in
their married life and career. They seek structure and organization,
which will make them run into disputes with their spouse and legal
partnerships. In their career, one will look for authority and routine
work, but face a constant challenge due to competition. When
Saturn and Mars have a mutual aspect, the native wants to conquer
both houses which are receiving the aspect. They want to control
their spouse and their co-workers. Ketu aspects Saturn from the
6th House, which depicts that the native is completely dissatisfied
by their career status. They distance themselves from work and
may suffer from excessive travel due to their job. The native will
be married late in life due to Saturn's aspect on the 7th House and
the karaka of marriage, Venus.
Saturn’s aspect on Rahu in the 12th House shows that one will face
responsibility and stress in foreign lands, and when dealing with
foreigners. There is demand upon the native when traveling to
foreign places. Saturn's ability to delay spiritual progress causes
frustration.

## Example 4:

Let’s review empty aspects from the same chart. Empty means
aspects on the houses with no planets.
First, we see that Jupiter aspects the 2nd House of family. Jupiter
for Libra ascendant rules the 3rd and 6th House, both of which are
malefic. The 3rd House is a mild malefic because it's 8th from the
8th. Jupiter is a natural benefic while sitting in the sign of Taurus
which is about value, wealth, and luxury of family. Jupiter’s aspect
shows that one will suddenly gain wealth through joint assets of
marriage, and through the help of in-laws. The native may
suddenly become wealthy through enemies and the unprivileged as
well. There can also be sudden debt, and the native must put effort
into sustaining married life and joint assets. There will also be
secret dealings with the enemy and mother's family, and also secret
dealings of wealth with younger siblings. You must be thinking
that Rahu's aspect on Jupiter is taking away its positive quality,
which is true. But Jupiter is part of a harsha vipreet raj yoga. This
raj yoga gives sudden wealth, name, and fame in life through
enemies, and by overcoming obstacles. The native's enemy won't
be able to harm them.

Saturn in the 10th House aspects the 4th House. Saturn rules the
4th and 5th House, and is the yogakaraka of Libra ascendant. In
other words, Saturn rules two of the most powerful house
combinations: kendra and trikona. Saturn is a natural malefic
planet, so no matter what house it rules, it will always bring fear,
anxiety, restriction, and discipline. Saturn aspects its own 4th
House and rules the 5th House. The 4th House represents the
mother, nourishment, happiness, peace of mind, and homeland.
The 5th House represents children, education, performing arts, and
political discussions. In the 10th House of Cancer, Saturn has no
emotion towards a career. Saturn needs status and security, and
wants routine jobs, perhaps taking care of the elderly or the poor.
This also shows one might be in the residential building business,
as Saturn rules the 4th House, and is the karaka of construction and
building homes.
Before we begin analyzing what the aspect is doing, we should see
the condition of Saturn. Saturn is in a strong house, which is the
10th House, and receives an aspect from Mars, which is strong in
its own sign in the 7th House. Ketu's 5th House from the 6th
House. Now, Saturn is not only hard working, disciplined, and
organized, but also power hungry, competitive, forceful,
impulsive, and at the same time disconnected from co-workers and
has difficulty completing tasks due to Ketu's aspect. One received
tough love from their mother. She was a professional who
demanded organization and structure in the house. She was always
busy with work, which is why she was away from home or distant
from the native.
Saturn also rules the 5th House, and from the 5th House Saturn is 6
places away, meaning 6th from the 5th. This shows that the native
will also be buried in conflicts with their children, and they will
put a lot of responsibility on the native, which will hinder their
professional life. They will be equally demanding of their children
as their mother once was.

If you notice, Saturn, Rahu, and Jupiter aspect the 4th House of
mother, childhood, homeland, nourishment, peace of mind, and
conveniences.

## Example 5:

In the above chart, all three planets are looking at the 4th House.
Without looking at the Moon, who is the karaka of the 4th House?
How would you judge this?
In conclusion, we would say that there would be lot many
fluctuations in the home despite the fact that one’s mother provides
motivation and optimism. Work and foreign travel distance the
native from their mother, and their mother may become an

her mother. This conclusion is due to the aspect of Jupiter, which
is like a Ganga jal of the horoscope.

## Example 6:

Why is it that certain houses are protected? When the Lord of
houses aspects that house, it is protected. In the chart above, you
can see how Jupiter aspects its own 2nd House, Saturn aspects its
own 3rd House, and Mars aspects its own 6th House.

## Example 6A:

Above are the aspect lines.
When a planet aspects its own house, it provides protection,
meaning the planet will not let the house be injured, especially
during its mahadasha. Jupiter's aspect on the 2nd House shows that
even though Saturn brings sorrow, delay, frustration, discipline
and hard work towards family and wealth, Jupiter’s aspect nullifies
it by 50-60%. Malefics are not welcomed in the 2nd House,
especially knowing it's a maraka house. Now, Saturn is being
judged by Jupiter. It's like a landlord constantly staring at a tenant,
and when a tenant knows they are being observed, they are on their
best behavior. In this case, the mix of Jupiter and Saturn can show
that one grew up in a very professional family that was career
oriented, disciplined, and organized enough have consistent
wealth. Gains for the native will be built slowly and steadily, yet
far better than if Saturn wasn't receiving an aspect from Jupiter.
Saturn, aspects its own 4th House while sitting in the 2nd House.
This shows that one's home will be protected and guarded. There is
a serious approach to building a home. The native gains a house
during Saturn's mahadasha and does not lose their parents

property. The mother will bring discipline and organization into
the family. There is maternal nourishment, yet she demands a
serious work ethic.
Mars in the 11th House aspects the 6th House. This shows that one
will always gain through their enemies, and will defeat them in
court cases, disputes, or even physical fights. There are
tremendous gains through male friends, brothers, and colleagues.
The native is protected from having any accidents. However,
accidents are not just controlled by Mars, who is the karaka of
accidents, or the 6th and 8th House. You have to see the karaka of
cars, which is Venus. In this case, Venus receives a conjunction of
Rahu and Mercury in the sign of Scorpio, or the sign of
transformation through dramatic events. Anytime Saturn, Mars, or
Rahu go over Venus through transit, one can face accidents but
they are not life threatening.

## Example 6B:

You might have missed the mutual aspect between Saturn and
Mars. How would this effect the final result?

Mars and Saturn are creating a warzone by mutual aspect and
stubbornness. This shows that the native has fighting ability, and is
willing to do anything and everything in life to protect their assets
and gains. This can also lead to the native doing illegal things or
going behind the scenes to make sure their assets and gains are
protected. Such a mutual aspect between the 2nd and 11th House
can show quick transformation of wealth, as money comes and
goes. There is never a time where the native has constant assets.
There are always fluctuations. There will be disputes with male
friends and brothers because Mars represents both, while Saturn
controls the 3rd House of siblings and 4th House of home. Mars’
aspect is sharing the energy of the 6th and 11th House since his
moolatrikona sign is of the 6th House. Mars is a functional malefic
for Scorpio ascendant, so disputes regarding property and family
assets are evident. But Mars' aspect on the 6th House shows that
one will have victory over legal disputes in Saturn and Mars
mahadasha.

## Example 6C:

We can see that Jupiter’s aspect is protecting the 2nd House, and
the 2nd house is the sign of Sagittarius, which is a friendly sign to

This shows that even though there may be disputes with friends,
uncles, and family members regarding assets, the native would still
be able to protect some assets from disappearing; including their
home.

## Example 6D:

Jupiter aspects the 4th House and 6th House.
This shows that Jupiter brings protection to assets, home, debts,
diseases, and disputes. Some of you might be thinking that
Jupiter's depositor, the Sun, is debilitated in the 12th House, so
why is Jupiter still doing this? First, Jupiter is a natural benefic, so
it will always try to be positive, no matter how it’s situated.
Second, we do not know the degree of the Sun. Without knowing
these facts, we can simply say Jupiter is strong in the 10th House
and in the sign of his friend, the Sun.
The result will depend on each individual mahadasha. Before
judging mahadasha, we have to see what yogas are being formed.
We have several dhan yogas in this chart.

1. Parivartan yoga between ascendant Lord Mars and 11th Lord
Mercury. One will gain through property, male friends, and their
older brother.
2. Mutual aspect between Saturn and Mars which is creating a Raj
yoga due to being lords of the ascendant and 4th House, while
sitting in wealth houses, and 11th from their respective houses,
because Saturn is 11th from the 4th House while Mars is 11th from
the ascendant.
3. Jupiter's aspect on his own 2nd House creates another dhan yoga
by aspect.
4. The Moon sitting in the 9th House creates another dhan yoga,
especially since Rahu aspects a strong moon. Rahu is amplifying
the Moon's positive energy. One always feels fortunate in life no
matter what the circumstances are.

## Result:

In Mars mahadasha, the native gets into disputes over property and
family assets, but will emerge victorious. They will gain through
siblings and businesses of real state and finance. However, they
may be more spending than saving.
In Saturn mahadasha, the native gets into property disputes and
will be separated from family and maybe their homeland. Yet, they
will receive gains through land and property without disputes. If
Saturn mahadasha comes early on in life, then one will experience
an average to barely below average gain. If the mahadasha of
Saturn comes later in life, after age 30, then Saturn will give good
results with wealth and property. One will become stingy and
fearful of losing savings, so they will guard their money during
this period.

In Jupiter mahadasha, one will have an excellent reputation at
work and at home. They will gain through their status, and build
savings. They will also receive benefits through foreign
government if the native settles permanently in foreign lands. If
the Sun is really close to its debilitated point in degrees, then one
will get in trouble with foreign governments.
In Mercury mahadasha, one will try to pursue education or
develop and market their real estate business. Real estate is very
prominent in their horoscope due to Saturn and Mars’ influence.
The native would gain tremendously due to Mercury being in a
dhan yoga while sitting with its two friends, Rahu and Venus.
Mercury mahadasha can bring sudden transformations as well, but
it could be a good transformation, as Gemini is a rajasic sign while
the 8th Lord is in a parivartan yoga with the ascendant lord. One
will suddenly gain in real estate through victory over disputed
property, or even win a lottery.
The 8th House is not always bad. If a natural benefic sign rules the
8th House while the 8th Lord is in a good spot forming a positive
yoga, then one will gain through the resources of the 8th House.
In Venus mahadasha, one can run into heavy expenses and health
issues due to Venus moolatrikona sign being in the 12th House,
making it a functional malefic and in the axis of Rahu and Ketu.

## Example 6E:

Venus aspects its own 7th House providing protection.
Though Ketu is there, it is being looked at by landlord Venus, so
Ketu will behave. One can safely say that the native will have a
foreign spouse due to Rahu's conjunction. The spouse will also be
spiritual, which is the good side of Ketu.

## Final result:

This is an excellent chart without knowing the degrees. The native
does have fluctuations in some dashas like Mars, Sun, Ketu, and a
touch of Saturn, yet protection is there with the family houses.
The best mahadasha for the native would be the Moon, Jupiter, and
Mercury. If Mercury had no yoga forming with Mars, then you can
say the native would run into health issues and can experience
sudden losses in Mercury mahadasha. Since Mercury is in
conjunction with the 12th Lord Venus, and in the axis of Rahu and
Ketu, major health issues will only arise in Mars mahadasha. Mars
is not only the lord of the physical body (ascendant), but also the
lord of diseases (6th House).

We only have 1 conjunction in this horoscope, which is of Rahu,
Venus, and Mercury, but we have no aspects on them. How can
you combine the lessons of my book "Conjunctions @ the Speed
of Light" and "Aspects @ The speed of Light"?

## 1. The conjunction:

## Mercury + Venus + Rahu conjunction:

Mercury: intelligence, communication, speech, logic, education,
analytical skills, calculative skills, education, business, trading and
language skills
Venuslove, relationship, desire, pleasure, luxurious foods,
culture, family history, beauty, comfort, art, creativity, lust and
sexuality

Rahuillusion, taboo breaker, unconventional, deceiver, magician,
foreign products and places, thief, politician, wide communication,
and computer related products
Unusual creative expression comes to mind with this conjunction.
There is a great need for sexual, romantic, and lustful
communication in all aspects of life. The native takes great
pleasure in communicating unusual and taboo breaking subjects
such as sexual orientation, interracial marriage, sex therapy, exotic
dancing, and perhaps even the more dark side of human nature.
The native becomes overly obsessive in romantic relationships and
has a tremendous urge to constantly communicate with their
partner. This can create a very controlling individual due to their
overprotectiveness, lust, and sexual needs. These natives are not
ashamed to talk about sexual behavior, and will most likely engage
in all types of sexual activities.
Their sense of pleasure comes from relating and communicating
with people of different cultural backgrounds, especially from
foreign lands. Rahu creates lots of interest in finding creativity, art,
and beauty in foreign things. Mercury gives Venus exclusive
access to communicate about foreign things. On a good note, this
can make for a historian, anthropologist, or archeologist. Creativity
is amplified with Rahu, and speech is used for mass
communication. If such conjunctions occur in 6th, 8th or 12th
House, it can lead to secret love affairs, pornography, and
prostitution, especially if Venus is debilitated or in the sign of
Scorpio. Such a sign placement creates a deep rooted sexual past,
which could include sexual abuse or growing up in a household
where sex and drugs were common. Rahu loves indulging in
substance abuse, and when sitting with Venus, the sense of
pleasure is derived from consuming exotic foods, drinks, and
illegal substances. Mercury's curiosity and youthfulness combined
with Rahu's nature of breaking rules leads Venus to places where it
never thought it would be. These natives are quite adventurous,
and will try anything once.

## Aspect of Saturn on the 4th House (Repeated 170/171):

Saturn in the 4th House aspects the 6th, 10th and 1st House. The
4th house represents the mother, home, homeland, mother's love,
early education, and convenience. Saturn in the 4th House makes
the relationship with the native’s mother disciplined, organized,
and structured. The mother might be cold towards the native unless
4th Lord is sitting with Saturn or aspects the 4th House. The
mother is controlling and might act like a general at home. There is
a delay in attaining property, but one does get a home after the age
of 35, since Saturn is the karaka of construction on land. They are
able to build a custom home after Saturn's restrictive period has
passed. During the mahadasha of Saturn, one has to fully indulge
in responsibilities towards the mother, home, and maintaining the
land. Saturn's placement might bring a cold, business-like
approach at home, but the environment is stable. This can produce
a soldier, policeman, or security guard who is forced to bare the
responsibility of protecting their homeland. This position brings
slow but steady growth in life and gains through property.
Saturn in the 4th House aspects the 6th House of debts, enemies,
diseases, small animals, everyday work life, and obstacles. Saturn's
3rd aspect brings victory over enemies through a steady work
ethic. The 6th House also represents disagreements and divorce.
Saturn's aspect delays or restricts the native in getting a divorce
due to the responsibility that comes with it. One may suffer from
chronic diseases of the chest, lungs, and joints. They need to have
a routine work life in order to handle obstacles. Saturn restricts one
from going into debt. If they do, Saturn helps them legally rid
themselves of it.
Saturn in the 4th House aspects the 10th House of career, status,
father, and government. This aspect brings responsibility and
leadership. They are forced to become a leader of the workforce
which is a source of more daily responsibility. They need routine

at work and steadiness no matter what they do. They like work to
be long term and constant. One is able to cope with the burden of
work due to their mother’s teachings. An appreciation of their
mother’s teachings comes later in life. They also benefit through
the government, and may even become a government
administrator or politician.
Saturn in the 4th House aspects the 1st House of the physical body,
personality, life force, and health. This aspect brings a very
disciplined and organized personality. There is a sense of
following the rules and abiding by the law. Their persona is
influenced by the mother, and is very serious and powerful. They
have a laser-like focus on what they do in life. As far as health is
concerned, the native should beware of joint pains, bone density,
heart, and lung issues. Depending upon Saturn's sign placement,
pinpointing the cause of disease is possible.
Final result example 10 chart?
The conjunction in the ascendant shows that the native is
extremely charming, sexual, communicative, and charismatic. The
native has exceptional speaking skills. There is a strict and direct
form of speaking, even when the native is being charming. They
are very serious towards their approach to other people. If they are
charming someone and being friendly, there is a purpose behind it.
Their personality is all about being involved with large group
settings, and having a tremendous work ethic which helps them
succeed in gaining land, career, and creative endeavors. When it
comes to sex, the native is very serious and goes deep into the
darkness of sexual activity.
Being in the parivartan yoga of Mars and Mercury, the native is
extremely sexual, but patient enough to wait for their chance to get
the opposite or same sex companion. This can be someone who is
a playboy, or someone who loves the thrill of sex and real estate.

## Example 8:

Saturn and Mars both aspect the ascendant. How will this change

The chart will be more energized. The native's sexual craving will

company. They are tough bosses who look out for their own
benefit before anyone else's. They may suffer from major health
issues, and physical attacks on the body with metal weapons. Both
Saturn and Mars are in mutual aspect, creating a very stubborn
person in terms of professionalism and private life. Having 4
malefics influencing the ascendant can make a dictator-type
personality, who is hated by enemies. This is because Scorpio is
the sign of dark transformation, and with Rahu, Saturn, Mars and
the axis of Ketu, someone can be a very forceful individual who
does not understand rejection. Though Mars' aspect on the
ascendant protects the ascendant, Mars is still a natural and
functional malefic planet for a Scorpio ascendant. For this native,
death doesn't bring fear; defeat does. In Ketu, Mars, and Mercury
mahadasha, the native feels such things because all three are part

of being functional malefic, or lord of the 8th House in the sign of
the 8th House of transformation, or receiving aspects of all major
malefics like Mercury.
Wealth is still prominent, but in Saturn dasha, because Jupiter and
Mars are creating a beautiful Raj and Dhana yoga, while Saturn is
also creating a Raj Yoga. Such a native can be an excellent
politician, which requires being cunning and unafraid of anything.
Even the Sun period is manageable because two of its best friends
(Jupiter and Mars) sitting in the sign of Leo, are in the powerful
10th House. The 8th Lord is in the ascendant, while the ascendant
lord is in the 10th House; meaning someone will expose secrets of
the government, and gain fame through it. In Sun mahadasha, they
may go to jail for treason.

## Example 9:

Let's talk about exact aspects. How do they affect a person and
why do they matter?
If you read my first book "Astrology @ The Speed of Light", I talk
about the effect of functional malefic and functional benefic

planets in astrology. Whenever a planet's moolatrikona sign comes
in the 6th, 8th and 12th House, it can bring more harm than good
to the native even if the planet is a natural benefic planet. In
Example 9, you can see the rising sign degrees. The chart is a
Cancer rising chart at 12 degrees. Any planet in any house that is
close to that 12 degree mark will have the most impact in the chart,
even if it's 3 degrees within the orb of the 12 degrees. If you
notice, Saturn and Jupiter are both very close to the ascendant
degree with Jupiter at the exact degree of the ascendant. The thing
to notice here is that both Jupiter and Saturn are functional malefic
planets, and they both are aspect to the ascendant. Jupiter in the 5th
House aspects the 9th, 11th and 1st House. Saturn in the 4th House
aspects the 6th, 10th and 1st House.

## Example 9A:

You can see the aspect lines of both planets.
Jupiter and Saturn are functional malefic because they rule the 6th
and 8th House. This reflects great health problems for the native,
not because they aspect the ascendant, but because they aspect the
ascendant at the exact degree. What kind of health problem does
the native have? Jupiter represents the liver, diabetes, fat, and even

small veins in the body. Saturn controls teeth, bones, joints, and
chronic diseases like arthritis. In this chart, Jupiter also aspects
Mercury in the 11th House, while Saturn aspects Venus and the
Sun in the 10th House. Saturn is in the 4th House of chest, heart,
and ribs. Jupiter is in the 5th House of stomach and upper intestine.
The Sun is the karaka of heart, and Venus is the karaka of the
reproductive system, kidneys, and hormones. Such an aspect can
show problems with the heart, breathing, nervous system, and
digestive track. But more than anything, it shows chronic health
problems because two functional malefic planets aspect the
ascendant at the exact degree.

## Example 9B:

You can see the ascendant degrees have changed. The ascendant is
now rising at 25 degrees, while Saturn and Jupiter both are at 12
degrees.
This shows that health problems will not be devastating, nor will
the negative impact of these two planets be severe. Even though
they will still cause problems in their respective mahadasha, it will
not be traumatic. You also have to see the degree of the Sun,
Venus, and Mercury. They may not be impacted by Saturn and

Jupiter as much if their degrees are more or less than 10 degrees
orb of these functional malefic planets. Jupiter controls the 9th
house of luck and fortune as well, and in its mahdasha, there will
be good times along with bad times. If Jupiter is at the exact same
degree as the ascendant, then bad times can overshadow the good.

## Jaimini Astrology aspects:

Jaimini astrology is another major branch of Prashara astrology
which was lost for 3000 years and was only revived in the 20th
century by gurus like Sri Sri K.N. Rao who is considered the best
Vedic astrology in the world including the best Jaimini astrologer
in the world; but this science never gained as much attention as
regular Vedic Astrology until recent years. Sri Rao accounts his
understanding and introduction to Jamini astrology through a
hidden gem known as "Vemuri Rama Murthy" in Andhra Pradesh,
which is described in his book "Jaimini's Chara dasha". Many
astrologers who came on my Youtube channel were also experts in
Jaimini and always emphasized using both system together side by
side to confirm events from the past. This is called composite
approach. When looking at a horoscope through aspects of
Prashara which are 80% of the whole game, you should also give a
quick glance to Jamini aspects including Chara dasha. I will
explain Chara dasha in this chapter. I have made understanding
Jamini aspects very easy through my videos and will do the same
in this book.
The most important thing to remember is never use just one
method in your astrological analysis, always use Prashara and
Jamini to confirm events in life. One doesn't work without the
other for a common man unless you're a gifted saint.
One important fact to remember is that planets really become
active in Jaimini when their sign based dasha becomes active, but
in any chara dasha they will give their results based on how far
they are from the sign dasha.
In Jamini astrology signs, only sign aspect other signs. Planets do
not have aspects. But if a planet is sitting in a certain sign, that

planet, too, will aspect all the houses that the sign is looking
towards. In such a method a planet will have multiple aspects
combining Prashara and Jamini together. If Saturn has 3 aspects in
Prashara then in Jamini it may have additional 3. This is why in
one single mahadasha Saturn can put delay on many things but no
matter what mahadasha you are running, there will always be some
"Chara dasha" running simultaneously with Vimshotri dasha. If
Mahadasha of Saturn is running while the chara dasha of the sign
where Saturn is placed that's when Saturn will began to aspect all
the other respective houses.
In Jamini all fixed signs aspects all cardinal signs except the one
next to them and all cardinal signs aspect all the fixed signs accept
the one next to them. Then, all the mutable sign aspect each other.
confused yet? let me make it easy.
What are fixed, cardinal and mutable signs in astrology? This is
one of the most basic concept of astrology like elements of planets
(fire, water, earth, air).
Fixed signs: Taurus, Leo, Scorpio, Aquarius
Cardinal signs: Aries, Cancer, Libra, Capricorn
Mutable signs: Gemini, Virgo, Sagittarius, Pisces
Remember what I said. All fixed signs aspect all the cardinal signs
except the one next to it.
The sign of Taurus will aspect Cancer, Libra and Capricorn but not
Aries. Why not Aries? because Aries is right next to Taurus in
ascending order. Aries and Taurus are neighbors and since they are
neighbors they do not aspect each other in Jamini.

Leo as a fixed sign will aspect Aries, Libra and Capricorn, but not
Cancer. Why not Cancer? because Cancer is right next to Leo in
the zodiac belt.
Scorpio will only aspect Aries, Cancer and Capricorn in the birth
chart but not Libra. Libra and Scorpio are neighbors in the zodiac
belt.
Aquarius will only aspect Aries, Cancer and Libra but not
Capricorn because Aquarius and Capricorn are right next to each
other in the zodiac belt.
All cardinal signs aspect all fixed signs accept the one next to
them.
Aries will aspect Leo, Scorpio and Aquarius but not Taurus
because they are right next to each other. The same will apply to
Cancer, Libra and Capricorn just like the fixed signs. I think you
get the picture.
When it comes to all mutable signs, they aspect each other.
The sign of Gemini will aspect Virgo, Sagittarius and Pisces.
Virgo will aspect Gemini, Sagittarius and Pisces. Sagittarius will
aspect Gemini, Virgo and Pisces. Pisces will aspect Gemini, Virgo
and Sagittarius.
See how simple and easy Jaimini aspects are. Let me show you in
a example.

## Fixed sign aspect example:

This is a Aries ascendant chart. The # 5 is obviously shows the 5th
sign of the zodiac Leo which is sitting in the 5th house. Leo is a
fixed sign and all fixed sign aspect all the cardinal signs except the
one next to them. The check mars shows all the aspect for Leo and
the X shows where it does not have a Jamini aspect.
Since there is a planet sitting in the sign of Leo (Rahu), that planet
will also aspect all the houses which sign of Leo is looking
towards. In this case Rahu also aspects 7th, 10th and 1st house.

## Cardinal sign aspect:

We again have Aries rising horoscope with Saturn in the 4th house
in the sign of Cancer. Since Cancer is a cardinal sign it will aspect
all the fixed sign except the one next to it. This means Cancer is
looking at the 8th, 11th and 2nd house but doesn't look at the 5th
house where Leo is because Leo and Cancer are always next to
each other. Since Saturn is in the sign of Cancer, it too, will aspect
the same houses as the sign.

## Mutable sign aspect:

We have Gemini ascendant, as all the mutable signs are inside
kendra. All the mutable houses are looking at each other and since
Rahu and Ketu are sitting in those houses they will look at all the
kendra houses in addition to their regular Prashara aspect.
When you are reading a normal chart always look at both aspect
approach. Look at the chart from the eye of regular Prashara Vedic
astrology and Jamini Astrology. But aspect of Prashara only
become active when the sign based dasha is active in the chart
including sign based antar dasha. Just like how regular aspect of
planets are only active during their Maha, antar and Pariantar
dasha.
If You are running through Cancer chara dasha and Saturn is
sitting in Cancer then Saturn will start effecting the houses that
Cancer is currently looking at.

When reading a regular Vimshorti mahadasha for any planet, read
their regular aspects like Mars has 4, 7, 8, Jupiter has 5, 7, 9 etc..
but when you are looking at chara dasha that's when you will use
sign based aspects for all planets and not the normal aspects. If you
are running the chara dasha of Capricorn while Saturn is sitting
directly opposite of it then Saturn will effect the situation of that
char dasha regarding whatever situation you are trying judge.
Prashara aspect are extremely important but Jamini aspect are just
as important.
When you are studying your chart through eye of Jamini never use
basic Vedic astrology aspects.

## Jamini chart example 1:

If you were reading the chart above for Jamini how would you
study the aspects?
One thing you will not do is say Saturn aspects the 2nd, 6th and
9th house in this chart. That's the Prashara aspect. The correct way
to address is by saying Saturn aspects the 3rd, 6th and 9th house.

Venus and Rahu aspects the 5th, 8th and 11th house. Mars, Moon
and Jupiter aspect the 12th, 3rd and 6th house. Sun and Mercury
aspect the 1st, 4th and 7th house while Ketu aspects the 11th, 2nd
and 5th house. The proper way of address the aspect in this chart
would be by saying, "The sign of Cancer aspects Scorpio,
Aquarius and Taurus." The conclusion of this chart by eye of
Jamini should be studied by regular Prashara aspect. When you
study Chara dasha do not think of Prashara aspect, only Jamini
aspect. When you study Prashara aspect only use regular aspects
you got to know in this book.

## Jamini chart example 2:

In the chart above you can see Venus and Saturn have a mutual
aspect which is effecting the native in terms of personality, spouse,
marriage and health. But, this is only Prashara aspect and quite
important. But, when you look at this chart through eyes of Jamini,
there is no mutual aspect between Venus and Saturn since they are
both in cardinal sign. In Jamini these two planets will play a
different role, perhaps of career and siblings, they may play a part

of education and personality depending upon their degree. Because
planet become karaka of different situation by their degree.
Planet with the highest degree is called Atmakaraka.
Planet with second highest degree is called Amatyakaraka.
Planet with third highest degree is called Bhatrikaraka.
Planet with 4th highest degree is called Matrikaraka.
Planet with 5th highest degree is called Putrakaraka.
Planet with sixth highest degree is called Gnatikaraka.
Planet with lowest degree is called Darakaraka.
If you are eager to learn about Jamini. I have made 3 videos
already on Youtube. You can search the video by typing, "Jamini
Astrology" or "Chara dasha in Jamini", and my videos will show
up.
What is Chara Dasha?
Chara dasha is a sign based dasha that always starts from the sign
of your ascendant and may either move forward or backwards
depending upon your ascendant. Either the dasha wil move
forward in counter clockwise motion or clockwise motion in a
chart.
Signs like Aries, Leo Virgo, Libra, Aquarius and Pisces move
counterclockwise. This means if you were an Aries ascendant your
first Chara dasha started with the sign of Aries and move to Taurus
in the second house, then Gemini in third house and would repeat
after Pisces.
If you were born with the ascendant Taurus, Gemini, Cancer,
Scorpio, Sagittarius and Capricorn then your Chara dasha will
move backwards. If you were born with a Taurus Chara dasha then
you will start with Taurus as your first dasha period followed by

Aries, Pisces, Aquarius all the way to Gemini and repeat there
after.
How do you calculate Chara dasha? It's a simple step, you count
how far the lord of each sign is from itself, count that many places
and substrate it by one. But, I won't go any further than this;
although it's always advised to calculate your tables and dasha
manually, yet with the help of new software all the work is done
for you. But be careful as some software will miscalculate your
Chara Dasha. I will soon be implementing a full PDF horoscope
service on my website Astrologykrs.com where you will get the
most accurate calculation of any form of astrology including Chara
dasha.
Chara dasha answered many questions for me in the past five
months. Why is that someone who goes through one of their best
Vimshotri mahadasha suddenly losses their assets or fall sick or
loses their job when they weren't supposed to. This is because
chara dasha of a weak karaka in the sign activated itself in middle
of your normal mahadasha. The good thing is sometimes Chara
dasha only lasts for one year and sometimes twelve years, which is
the max years allowed for any Chara dasha. A good Chara dasha
should obviously last longer than bad dasha but that's not how
Universe works.
In conclusion there is lot more depth than meets the eye of Jamini
aspects. Since I just started learning Jamini I have no authority to
write anymore than what I have. I've only tested these aspects,
karaka and Chara dasha system on celebrities, only, which does
seem to work quite effectively. Until I test Jamini on a regular Joe
I can't write or say anything about what works and what doesn't
work. I have been learning Jamini for 4 months now and I still
have lot to learn before I can even start practicing my skills on
others. Once I have done enough research and testing, I will write
a whole book on it including Jamini Raj yogas and Dhan yoga.

Prashara Astrology is excellent tool on its own. I only worked with
Prashara my whole life and never felt empty. Jamini is like cherry
on cake.

## Remedy for bad aspects:

First and foremost there are no remedies in the world of astrology.
This is truly my own experience and testing of over 12 years. No
gemstones, or throwing wheat in the river or burying something in
the ground helped me. Some gemstones work but only for health
reasons but they are not $1500 per ct. Those stone are no more
than 10-20 per ct. Please do not be fooled if someone says they can
change your fate by wearing some yantra, kavach or ring.
Only less than 1% of all self-proclaimed saints have the power to
change your fate or at least mold it with a much easier path.
Now, the best remedy for any of your life's hardship is pouring
milk, honey and water on a Shivling in a temple. Pour milk every
month or 1st of every month and water on 15th of every month but
also give some money from your pay check each time you do it.
You can also add organic or pure honey in the milk as well.
But, through my own experience the best time to do this activity is
when no one is around the Shivling, or the least amount of people
are present. When you go during rush hour in a temple your entire
vibration is distorted and distracted. Go at a time when it's just you
and the mystical power of this universe residing inside the
Shivling. while pouring milk or water connected with it and ask for
prosperity.
No bad planetary alignment can harm you once you are connected
to the source. The source energy is the manager of all the planets.
